quotes.sanskritText:
न दानं न तपो नेज्या न शौचं न व्रतानि च ।
प्रीयतेऽमलया भक्त्या हरिरन्यद् विडंबनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: God is not pleased with charity, penance, rituals, cleanliness, or fasting. He is pleased only by pure devotion. Everything else is a mere joke.
quotes.transliteration:
na dānaṃ na tapo nejyā na śaucaṃ na vratāni ca |
prīyateऽmalayā bhaktyā hariranyad viḍaṃbanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महता पुण्ययोगेन मानुषं जन्म लभ्यते ।
तत्प्राप्य न कृतो धर्मः कीदृशं हि मयाकृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: After much difficulty does one get a human life (in this endless cycle of life and deaths). Even after getting one, I did not live it by Dharma (the moral code of life). So laments a man who just realizes that he can not end the cycle with his life.
quotes.transliteration:
mahatā puṇyayogena mānuṣaṃ janma labhyate |
tatprāpya na kṛto dharmaḥ kīdṛśaṃ hi mayākṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नन्वाश्रयस्थिरियं तव कालकूट
केनोत्तरोत्तरविशिष्टपदोपदिष्टा ।
प्रागर्णवस्य हृदये वृषलक्ष्मणोऽथ
कण्टेऽधुना वससि वाचि पुनः खलानाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Indeed you find varied hosts at different times, Oh deadly poison Kalakuta. Wonder on whose command do you act? Earlier you were hidden in the heart of the ocean, then in the throat of Lord Shiva. Now you reside in the speech of the wicked.
quotes.transliteration:
nanvāśrayasthiriyaṃ tava kālakūṭa
kenottarottaraviśiṣṭapadopadiṣṭā |
prāgarṇavasya hṛdaye vṛṣalakṣmaṇoऽtha
kaṇṭeऽdhunā vasasi vāci punaḥ khalānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तपः परं कृतयुगे त्रेतायां ज्ञानमुच्यते ।
द्वापरे यज्ञमेवाहुर्दानमेव कलौ युगे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Penance is the best way to live in the Kruta Yuga. In Treta yuga, attaining knowledge was the best way. In Dwapara Yuga, performing relegious rituals was the way to live. In Kali Yuga (present day) donating generously to the needy is the only right way to live.
quotes.transliteration:
tapaḥ paraṃ kṛtayuge tretāyāṃ jñānamucyate |
dvāpare yajñamevāhurdānameva kalau yuge ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य नास्ति स्वयं प्रज्ञा शास्त्रं तस्य करोति किं ।
लोचनाभ्यां विहीनस्य दर्पणः किं करिष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The scriptures can not help anyone who does not himself. What good is a mirror for one who has no eyes.
quotes.transliteration:
yasya nāsti svayaṃ prajñā śāstraṃ tasya karoti kiṃ |
locanābhyāṃ vihīnasya darpaṇaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा तालं विना रागः यथा मानं विना नृपः ।
यथा दानं विना हस्ती तथा ज्ञानं विना यतिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A seer without knowledge is as useless as music without timing, king without dignity or an elephant without rut.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā tālaṃ vinā rāgaḥ yathā mānaṃ vinā nṛpaḥ |
yathā dānaṃ vinā hastī tathā jñānaṃ vinā yatiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अजरामरवत् प्राज्ञो विद्यामर्थं च साधयेत् |
गृहीत इव केशेषु मृत्युना धर्ममाचरेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Virtuous men go about collecting knowledge and wealth as if they will live for ever. When it comes to following Dharma, they never procrastinate. They perform their duties as if death is holding them by their hair.
quotes.transliteration:
ajarāmaravat prājño vidyāmarthaṃ ca sādhayet |
gṛhīta iva keśeṣu mṛtyunā dharmamācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लाभपूजानिमित्तं हि व्याख्यानं शिष्यसंग्रहः |
एते चान्ये च बहवः प्रपंचाः कुतपस्विनां ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There are a many rogues posing as mendicants. They sole aim to gather as many followers as possible so that it maximizes the profit they can make from rituals.
quotes.transliteration:
lābhapūjānimittaṃ hi vyākhyānaṃ śiṣyasaṃgrahaḥ |
ete cānye ca bahavaḥ prapaṃcāḥ kutapasvināṃ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तावत्प्रीतिर्भवेल्लोके यावद्दानं प्रदीयते ।
वत्सः क्षीरक्षयं दृष्ट्वा परित्यजति मातरं ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: As long as people are generous, there will be love and affection in this world. Once people stop being generous, love vanishes. A calf will abandon the cow once the cow stops giving it milk.
quotes.transliteration:
tāvatprītirbhavelloke yāvaddānaṃ pradīyate |
vatsaḥ kṣīrakṣayaṃ dṛṣṭvā parityajati mātaraṃ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देशः कालः पात्रं वितरति वित्तस्य कमपि महिमानं ।
अब्धितले शुक्तिपुटे स्नातं घनतोयमेति मणिभावम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one distributes his wealth in charity, the world (space, time and forces) seems to be working towards making him achieve greatness. A drop of fresh water from the clouds that enters the ocean selflessly gets trapped in a oyster and becomes a pearl.
quotes.transliteration:
deśaḥ kālaḥ pātraṃ vitarati vittasya kamapi mahimānaṃ |
abdhitale śuktipuṭe snātaṃ ghanatoyameti maṇibhāvam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शब्दः स्पर्शश्च रूपं च रसो गंधश्च पञ्चमः ।
एकैकमलमेतेषां विनाशप्रतिपत्तये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sound, touch, form (beauty), taste and smell are the five senses. Any one of them alone is capable of destroying you completely.
quotes.transliteration:
śabdaḥ sparśaśca rūpaṃ ca raso gaṃdhaśca pañcamaḥ |
ekaikamalameteṣāṃ vināśapratipattaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकृतात्मानमासाद्य राजानमनये रतम् |
समृद्धानि विनश्यन्ति राष्ट्राणि नगराणि च ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person who has not overcome his senses, one who believes in sensory pleasures is made the king, then in spite of all the wealth, such nations and cities will get destroyed.
quotes.transliteration:
akṛtātmānamāsādya rājānamanaye ratam |
samṛddhāni vinaśyanti rāṣṭrāṇi nagarāṇi ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो न वेत्ति गुणान्यस्य न तं सेवेत पण्डितः |
न हि तस्मात्फलं तस्य सुकृष्टा दूषरादिव ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man must never serve a master who can not value his qualities. Serving him will be like trying to cultivate on barren land. No matter how hard you plough the land and sow seeds, there will be no crop.
quotes.transliteration:
yo na vetti guṇānyasya na taṃ seveta paṇḍitaḥ |
na hi tasmātphalaṃ tasya sukṛṣṭā dūṣarādiva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
समरभरः सुभटानां फलानि कर्णेजपैस्तु भोग्यानि |
करिदशना इव नृपतेर्बाह्याः क्लेशाय खादनेंऽतस्थाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The brave soldiers win the war outside the country. But the benefits are enjoyed by rumormongers and ear biters with in the state. Just like all the hard work is done by the tusks of the elephant and the internal molars enjoy biting the food.
quotes.transliteration:
samarabharaḥ subhaṭānāṃ phalāni karṇejapaistu bhogyāni |
karidaśanā iva nṛpaterbāhyāḥ kleśāya khādaneṃऽtasthāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अवगच्छति मूढचेतनः प्रियनाशं हृदि शल्यमर्पितम् ।
स्थिरधीस्तु तदेव मन्यते कुशलद्वारतया समुद्धृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one loses his loved ones, a fool drowns himself in grief as if a spear went through his heart. But a stable minded person would consider the same as an opportunity to let go of attachments (and there by attain salvation) and would be unfazed by the situation.
quotes.transliteration:
avagacchati mūḍhacetanaḥ priyanāśaṃ hṛdi śalyamarpitam |
sthiradhīstu tadeva manyate kuśaladvāratayā samuddhṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न सीदन्नपि धर्मेण मनोऽधर्मे निवेशयेत् ।
अधार्मिकाणां पापानं आशुपश्यन् विपर्ययम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not employ his mind in immoral activities even after he sees virtuous people having to suffer (shed tears) and rogues enjoying all the riches (and leading a luxurious life).
quotes.transliteration:
na sīdannapi dharmeṇa manoऽdharme niveśayet |
adhārmikāṇāṃ pāpānaṃ āśupaśyan viparyayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकार्यवारणोद्युक्तो मूढे यः परिखिद्यते ।
वाग्विस्तरो वृथा तस्य भस्मन्याज्याहुतिर्यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: No matter how well you explain to an idiot how to avoid a disaster, it does not bear fruits. He will walk straight into the disaster. It is like pouring ghee over ashes in a pyre (it can not kindle fire, neither is it useful).
quotes.transliteration:
akāryavāraṇodyukto mūḍhe yaḥ parikhidyate |
vāgvistaro vṛthā tasya bhasmanyājyāhutiryathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आचार्यश्च पिता चैव माता भ्राता च पूर्वजः ।
नार्ते नाप्यवमंतव्या सज्जनेन विशेषतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when we are faced with lot of misery, we should not speak ill about our father, mother, brother or any ancestors. A virtous person whould take extra care not to do so.
quotes.transliteration:
ācāryaśca pitā caiva mātā bhrātā ca pūrvajaḥ |
nārte nāpyavamaṃtavyā sajjanena viśeṣataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मे मतिर्भवतु वः सततोत्थितानां
स ह्येक एव परलोकगतस्य बंधुः ।
अर्थाः स्त्रियश्च निपुणैरपि सेव्यमाना
नैवाप्तभावमुपयान्ति न च स्थिरत्वम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: dharma matir bhavatu satata utthitAnAm If you want to grow always, then keep thinking about Dharma (moral code of life) always. Dharma is the only thing that follows you in the after life. Wealth and women will never be yours for ever. They are very fickle.
quotes.transliteration:
dharme matirbhavatu vaḥ satatotthitānāṃ
sa hyeka eva paralokagatasya baṃdhuḥ |
arthāḥ striyaśca nipuṇairapi sevyamānā
naivāptabhāvamupayānti na ca sthiratvam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आकारश्छाद्यमानोऽपि न शक्यो विनिगूहितुम् ।
बलाद्धि विवृणोत्येव भावमंतर्गतं नृणाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if someone tries, he will not be able to prevent the inner feelings from being reflected in his facial expressions. It will always show up.
quotes.transliteration:
ākāraśchādyamānoऽpi na śakyo vinigūhitum |
balāddhi vivṛṇotyeva bhāvamaṃtargataṃ nṛṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रुतमप्यधिगम्य ये रिपून् विनयन्ते न शरीरजन्मनः ।
जनयन्त्यचिराय संपदां अयशस्ते खलु चापलाश्रयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person is very well read and yet not able to conquer the enemies with in his own body - the senses, then he shall get the ill fame as a person who lost all his wealth. Indulging in sensory pleasures will deplete one of all his wealth and underscore the fact that such pleasures are all temporary.
quotes.transliteration:
śrutamapyadhigamya ye ripūn vinayante na śarīrajanmanaḥ |
janayantyacirāya saṃpadāṃ ayaśaste khalu cāpalāśrayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अधर्मस्तु महांस्तात भवत्तस्य महीपतेः |
यो हरेद् बलिषड्भागं न च रक्षति पुत्रवत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king does not protect his subjects like his own sons in spite of taking one sixth of their earnings as tax, then his act shall attract a lot of sin.
quotes.transliteration:
adharmastu mahāṃstāta bhavattasya mahīpateḥ |
yo hared baliṣaḍbhāgaṃ na ca rakṣati putravat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अवधारय धर्मेषु प्रधनमवधानतः ।
निर्भरानंदाय गोविंदाय मनोऽर्पय ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Behold the most important of all the scriptures. Surrender your mind to Govinda who is the never ending source of happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
avadhāraya dharmeṣu pradhanamavadhānataḥ |
nirbharānaṃdāya goviṃdāya manoऽrpaya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भगवद्गीता किंचिदधीता
गंगाजलवकणिका पीता ।
सकृदपि यस्य मुरारिसमर्चा
कुरुते तस्य यमोऽपि न चर्चाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if you read the Bhagavadgita even a little, drink a little water from Ganga, worshiped Vishnu a little - Yama (God of death) will not think about you.
quotes.transliteration:
bhagavadgītā kiṃcidadhītā
gaṃgājalavakaṇikā pītā |
sakṛdapi yasya murārisamarcā
kurute tasya yamoऽpi na carcām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संसारजसुखासक्तं ब्रह्मज्ञोस्मीति वादिनम् |
कर्मब्रह्मोभयभ्रष्टं संत्यजेदंत्यजं यथा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person showing lot of interest in sensory pleasure claims that he has the knowledge of the universe, then he neither knows the real meaning of the universe nor that of karma (actions). He must be considered as a rogue and ostracized
quotes.transliteration:
saṃsārajasukhāsaktaṃ brahmajñosmīti vādinam |
karmabrahmobhayabhraṣṭaṃ saṃtyajedaṃtyajaṃ yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अप्रगल्भस्य या विद्याकृपणस्य च यद्धनम् ।
यच्च बाहुबलं भीरोर्व्यर्थमेतत्त्रयं भुवि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Eduction is waste when possessed by a person without courage. Money owned by a miser is waste. Muscle strenght of a coward is a waste. These three things on this earth are a complete waste.
quotes.transliteration:
apragalbhasya yā vidyākṛpaṇasya ca yaddhanam |
yacca bāhubalaṃ bhīrorvyarthametattrayaṃ bhuvi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वद्रव्येषु विद्यैव द्रव्यमाहुरनुत्तमम् ।
अहार्यत्वादनर्घ्यत्वादक्षयत्वाच्च सर्वदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Among all valuables, it is said that education is most important because it can not be stolen, it does not depreciate, it does not reduce over time.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvadravyeṣu vidyaiva dravyamāhuranuttamam |
ahāryatvādanarghyatvādakṣayatvācca sarvadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बहूनामप्यसाराणां समवायो हि दुर्जयः |
तृणैरावेष्ट्यते रज्जुर्येन नागोपि बध्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When many seemingly powerless beings unite, they can not be defeated easily. Just like a rope made of grass (many of them) can tie down an elephant
quotes.transliteration:
bahūnāmapyasārāṇāṃ samavāyo hi durjayaḥ |
tṛṇairāveṣṭyate rajjuryena nāgopi badhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वैराग्यमिव वृद्धानामौचित्यं महतामिव |
शुचिशीलमिवार्याणां सत्यं राज्ञां वुभूषणम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: To be free from worldly desires is an old man's duty. To be humble is the duty of the virtuous. To be clean and chaste is the duty of any person who expects respect. To be truthful to his responsibilities is the fore most duty of a king.
quotes.transliteration:
vairāgyamiva vṛddhānāmaucityaṃ mahatāmiva |
śuciśīlamivāryāṇāṃ satyaṃ rājñāṃ vubhūṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निद्राति स्नाति भुंक्ते चलति कचभरं शोषयत्यंतरास्ते
दीव्यत्यक्षैर्न चायं गदितुमवसरो भूय आयाहि याहि ।
इत्युद्दंडैः प्रभूणामसकृदधिकृतैर्वारितान् द्वारि दीनान्
अस्मान् पश्याभ्धकन्ये सरसिहरुचामंतरंगैरपांगैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When we go to the king, the sentries holding a stick stop (poor) us at the gate. They say that the king is sleeping, taking bath, eating, walking, drying his hair, wants to be alone, is gambling. Now is not a time to talk. They ask us to go away now and come back later. Oh! daughter of the ocean (Lakshmi) please look at us with the corner of your lotus like eyes (as pleasant as).
quotes.transliteration:
nidrāti snāti bhuṃkte calati kacabharaṃ śoṣayatyaṃtarāste
dīvyatyakṣairna cāyaṃ gaditumavasaro bhūya āyāhi yāhi |
ityuddaṃḍaiḥ prabhūṇāmasakṛdadhikṛtairvāritān dvāri dīnān
asmān paśyābhdhakanye sarasiharucāmaṃtaraṃgairapāṃgaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नयस्य विनयो मूलं विनयः शास्त्रनिश्चयः ।
विनयो हींद्रियजयस्तद्युक्तः शास्त्रमृच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Humility is the root of all morality. Humility comes from understanding and sticking to the scriptures. Humility is also the result of controlling one's senses. One who is able to do that, he will be able to study the scriptures also.
quotes.transliteration:
nayasya vinayo mūlaṃ vinayaḥ śāstraniścayaḥ |
vinayo hīṃdriyajayastadyuktaḥ śāstramṛcchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदि नात्मनि पुत्रेषु न चेत्पुत्रेषु नप्तृषु |
न त्वेव तु कृतोऽधर्मः कर्तुर्भवति निष्फलः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The consequences of one's mis deeds has to be suffered by him, or his children, or his grand children or his great grand children. There is no way that it will go unaccounted for.
quotes.transliteration:
yadi nātmani putreṣu na cetputreṣu naptṛṣu |
na tveva tu kṛtoऽdharmaḥ karturbhavati niṣphalaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महतस्तरसा विलङ्घय -
न्निजदोषेण कुधीर्विनश्यति |
कुरुते न खलु स्वयेच्छया
शलभानिन्धनमिद्धदीधितिः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When a rogue tries to trouble a noble man, it is the rogue who gets destroyed by his own actions. The fire will not hunt down the moth. But it is the moth that tries to conquer fire, and gets burnt in the process.
quotes.transliteration:
mahatastarasā vilaṅghaya -
nnijadoṣeṇa kudhīrvinaśyati |
kurute na khalu svayecchayā
śalabhānindhanamiddhadīdhitiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विपदः सन्तु नः शश्वत्तत्र तत्र जगद्गुरो ।
भवतो दर्शनं यत्स्यादपुनर्भवदर्शनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh God! let us be getting more and more troubles every day. Only then we will have a chance to see you. Only by seeing you can we get out of this eternal cycle of re-births.
quotes.transliteration:
vipadaḥ santu naḥ śaśvattatra tatra jagadguro |
bhavato darśanaṃ yatsyādapunarbhavadarśanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्यागो गुणो वित्तवतां वितं त्यागवतां गुणः ।
परस्पर वियुक्तौतु वित्तत्यागौ विडंबना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Generosity is the only quality that decorates the rich. Wealth is the only quality that decorates the generous. Both wealth and generosity are useless (laughable) without the other.
quotes.transliteration:
tyāgo guṇo vittavatāṃ vitaṃ tyāgavatāṃ guṇaḥ |
paraspara viyuktautu vittatyāgau viḍaṃbanā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्प्रातः संस्कृतं भोज्यं सायं तच्च विनश्यति ।
तदन्नरससंपुष्टे काये का नाम नित्यता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when food is very well cooked in the morning, it gets spoilt by evening. How can a body that is built from the essence of such food be permanent?
quotes.transliteration:
yatprātaḥ saṃskṛtaṃ bhojyaṃ sāyaṃ tacca vinaśyati |
tadannarasasaṃpuṣṭe kāye kā nāma nityatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दिनमपि रजनी सायंप्रातः
शिशिरवसन्तौ पुनरायातः ।
कालः क्रीडति गच्छत्यायुः
तदपि न मुञ्चति आशावायुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Morning, night, dawn, dusk, fall, spring come and go. Times plays tricks on us. Our lives get eroded. But even then, desire does not die.
quotes.transliteration:
dinamapi rajanī sāyaṃprātaḥ
śiśiravasantau punarāyātaḥ |
kālaḥ krīḍati gacchatyāyuḥ
tadapi na muñcati āśāvāyuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनित्यानि शरीराणि विभवो नैव शाश्वतः |
नित्यं संनिहितो मृत्युः कर्तव्यो धर्मसंग्रहः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: This body does not last for ever. Wealth is temporary. Death is approaching you every day. Hence get involved in following Dharma as if there is no tomorrow. Do not put off noble duties to tomorrow
quotes.transliteration:
anityāni śarīrāṇi vibhavo naiva śāśvataḥ |
nityaṃ saṃnihito mṛtyuḥ kartavyo dharmasaṃgrahaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सन्तप्तायसि संस्थितस्य पयसो नामापि न श्रूयते
मुक्ताकारतया तदेव नळिनीपत्रस्थितं दृश्यते ।
स्वात्यां सागरशुक्तिमध्यपतितं तन्मौक्तिकं जायते
प्रायेणाधममध्यमोत्तमगुणः संसर्गतो जायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A drop of water on a hot iron plate immediately looses existence. The same drop takes up the shape of a pearl when dropped on a lotus leaf. But, even in the middle of the ocean, a drop of water that enters the oyster achieves its destiny by turning into a real pearl. In time, a person becoming a wretched one, an average one or an extra-ordinary one depends on the company he keeps.
quotes.transliteration:
santaptāyasi saṃsthitasya payaso nāmāpi na śrūyate
muktākāratayā tadeva naळinīpatrasthitaṃ dṛśyate |
svātyāṃ sāgaraśuktimadhyapatitaṃ tanmauktikaṃ jāyate
prāyeṇādhamamadhyamottamaguṇaḥ saṃsargato jāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहन्यहनि दातव्यं अदीनेनांतरात्मना ।
स्तोकादपि प्रयत्नेन दानमित्यभिधीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It should not matter if you are poor. If you do not feel self-pity and help others even with the little that you have, then it is called real donation. [There is nothing great in donating excess money. It is one's duty to do so]
quotes.transliteration:
ahanyahani dātavyaṃ adīnenāṃtarātmanā |
stokādapi prayatnena dānamityabhidhīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रायः संप्रति कोपाय सन्मार्गस्योपदेशनम् |
विलूननासिकस्येव यद्वदादर्शदर्शनम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When one is in anger any advice (to do the right thing) will only increase it. It is just like showing a mirror to a person who has just now cut his nose.
quotes.transliteration:
prāyaḥ saṃprati kopāya sanmārgasyopadeśanam |
vilūnanāsikasyeva yadvadādarśadarśanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकार्य करणात् भीतः कार्याणां च विवर्जनात् ।
अकाले मन्त्रभेदात् च येन माद्येन्न तत् पिबेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Such is the effect of alcohol. It makes you do wrong things. It makes you miss doing the right things. It makes you let out secrets.
quotes.transliteration:
akārya karaṇāt bhītaḥ kāryāṇāṃ ca vivarjanāt |
akāle mantrabhedāt ca yena mādyenna tat pibet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दातुः प्रतिग्रहीतुश्च यौ हस्तावुत्तराधरौ ।
तयोरप्यौत्तराधर्यं ताभ्यामेवोपपाद्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A generous person gives away his stuff to the needy. In the process, his hand is on top and those of the needy are down. So is the social status of the generous and needy.
quotes.transliteration:
dātuḥ pratigrahītuśca yau hastāvuttarādharau |
tayorapyauttarādharyaṃ tābhyāmevopapādyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भेतव्यं न तथा शत्रोर्नाग्नेर्नाहेर्न चाशनेः ।
इंद्रियेभ्यो यथा स्वेभ्यस्तैरजस्रं हि हन्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One need not be fearful of his enemies, fire, snake or the lightning. One must very afraid of his senses. It is these senses that can drag him to doing deeds that are harmful himself. They are acting on such plans all the time.
quotes.transliteration:
bhetavyaṃ na tathā śatrornāgnernāherna cāśaneḥ |
iṃdriyebhyo yathā svebhyastairajasraṃ hi hanyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ध्यानमेव परो धर्मो ध्यनमेव परं तपः ।
ध्यानमेव परं शैचं तस्माद्ध्यानपरो भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Meditation is the greatest code of life. It is the best penance. It is the best cleansing. Hence learn to (and do)meditate.
quotes.transliteration:
dhyānameva paro dharmo dhyanameva paraṃ tapaḥ |
dhyānameva paraṃ śaicaṃ tasmāddhyānaparo bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अमंत्रमक्षरं नास्ति वनस्पतिरनौषधम् ।
अयोग्यः पुरुषो नास्ति योजकस्तत्र दुर्लभः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no letter in the alphabet that never occurs in any verse. No herb exists that is not part of some medicine. Likewise, no person is completely useless. It is however difficult to find a manager who can use him appropriately.
quotes.transliteration:
amaṃtramakṣaraṃ nāsti vanaspatiranauṣadham |
ayogyaḥ puruṣo nāsti yojakastatra durlabhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मं भजस्व सततं त्यज लोकधर्मान्
सेवस्व साधुपुरुषान् जहि कामतृष्णाम् ।
अन्यस्य दोषगुणचिन्तनमाशु मुक्त्वा
सेवाकथरसमहो नितरां पिब त्वम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: dharmam bhajasva satatam tyaja lokadharmAn Always follow Dharma in its purest form. Do not follow what is defacto which changes from time to time and from place to place. Serve noble men (mendicants). Give up greed to get more and lust. Stop finding faults in others. Spend your time in serving others and listening to tales about God.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmaṃ bhajasva satataṃ tyaja lokadharmān
sevasva sādhupuruṣān jahi kāmatṛṣṇām |
anyasya doṣaguṇacintanamāśu muktvā
sevākatharasamaho nitarāṃ piba tvam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पूर्वे वयसि यः शान्तः स शान्त इति मे मतिः ।
धातुषु क्षीयमाणेषु शमः कस्य न जायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only a person who is peaceful at young age can really be called as a 'peace and calm'. What is the big deal in being calm and peaceful when your body has weakened?
quotes.transliteration:
pūrve vayasi yaḥ śāntaḥ sa śānta iti me matiḥ |
dhātuṣu kṣīyamāṇeṣu śamaḥ kasya na jāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भूयो विनयमास्थाय भव नित्यं जितेंद्रियः ।
कामक्रोधसमुत्थानि त्यजेथा व्यसनानि च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You must be humble always. You must control your senses and not become their salves. You must give up all habits that will increase lust and anger in you. (As told by Dasharatha to Sri Rama)
quotes.transliteration:
bhūyo vinayamāsthāya bhava nityaṃ jiteṃdriyaḥ |
kāmakrodhasamutthāni tyajethā vyasanāni ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षोणीकोणशतांशपालनकलादुर्वारगर्वानल
क्षुभ्यत् क्षुद्रनरेन्द्रचाटुरचनाधन्यान् न मन्यामहे ।
देवं सेवितुमेव निश्चनुमहे योऽसौ दयाळुः पुरा
धानामुष्टिमुचे कुचेलमुनये दत्ते स्म वित्तेशताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: We will not respect any scholar who finds satisfaction in praising a king who rules one small corner of this huge world. We will serve only that Lord Krishna who gave wealth equal to that of Kubera (God of wealth) to Kuchela in return for handful of parched rice.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣoṇīkoṇaśatāṃśapālanakalādurvāragarvānala
kṣubhyat kṣudranarendracāṭuracanādhanyān na manyāmahe |
devaṃ sevitumeva niścanumahe yoऽsau dayāळuḥ purā
dhānāmuṣṭimuce kucelamunaye datte sma vitteśatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भूतानां प्राणिनः श्रेष्ठाः प्राणिनां बुद्धिजीवनः
बुद्धिमत्सु नराः श्रेष्ठा नराणां सात्त्विकाः स्मृताः |
सात्त्विकेषु च विद्वांसः विद्वत्सु कृतबुद्धयः
कृतबुद्धिषु कर्तारः कर्तृषु ब्रह्मवेदिनः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world of beings, the animals are better than other (plants, microbes etc..). Among the animals, those who think are better. Man is the best among those animals that can think. Among all men, those who follow honest (and other virtues) are the best. Among honest people, those who are knowledgeable are better. Among those who have knowledge, only those who practice are better than the rest. Among such people, the do-er is the best. It is no use just talking about knowledge. Among all the do-ers, the person who has the realization of the oneness in the universe is the best.
quotes.transliteration:
bhūtānāṃ prāṇinaḥ śreṣṭhāḥ prāṇināṃ buddhijīvanaḥ
buddhimatsu narāḥ śreṣṭhā narāṇāṃ sāttvikāḥ smṛtāḥ |
sāttvikeṣu ca vidvāṃsaḥ vidvatsu kṛtabuddhayaḥ
kṛtabuddhiṣu kartāraḥ kartṛṣu brahmavedinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उक्तो भवति यः पूर्वं गुणवानिति संसदि ।
तस्य दोषो न वक्तव्यः प्रतिज्ञाभंगभीरुणा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Once a person is declared as virtuous in an assembly, we must not openly talk about his faults lest we would be breaking the vow taken in front of the assembly. Such matters must be dealt in person and private.
quotes.transliteration:
ukto bhavati yaḥ pūrvaṃ guṇavāniti saṃsadi |
tasya doṣo na vaktavyaḥ pratijñābhaṃgabhīruṇā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अक्षरद्वयमभ्यस्तं नास्ति नास्तीति यत्पुरा ।
तदिदं देहि देहीति विपरीतमुपस्थितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Long ago, you were used to telling "No! No!" (when people asked for help/alms). The same habit has intensified and is making you tell "More! More!". Greed grows and times change.
quotes.transliteration:
akṣaradvayamabhyastaṃ nāsti nāstīti yatpurā |
tadidaṃ dehi dehīti viparītamupasthitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सक्तं ग्राम्येषु भोगेषु कामवृत्तं महीपतिम् |
लुब्धं न बहु मन्यन्ते श्मशानाग्निमिव प्रजाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If king muses himself with the coarse sensory pleasures only and is greedy also, then his subjects will not honor him. They will treat him with same disdain as a fire in a cemetery (burning bodies).
quotes.transliteration:
saktaṃ grāmyeṣu bhogeṣu kāmavṛttaṃ mahīpatim |
lubdhaṃ na bahu manyante śmaśānāgnimiva prajāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शैवा वयं न खलु तत्र विचारणीयम्
पञ्चाक्षरीजपपरा नितरां तथापि ।
चतो मदीयमतसीकुसुमावभासं
स्मेराननं स्मरति गोपवधूकिशोरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: We are the followers of Shiva. There is no doubt. We spend our time chanting the five syllable hymn - Om namah shivaya. But my mind always cherishes the memory of the lovely dark flower like face of the son of a cattle grazer (Sri Krishna).
quotes.transliteration:
śaivā vayaṃ na khalu tatra vicāraṇīyam
pañcākṣarījapaparā nitarāṃ tathāpi |
cato madīyamatasīkusumāvabhāsaṃ
smerānanaṃ smarati gopavadhūkiśoram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
माता शत्रुः पिता वैरी बालो येन न पाठितः ।
न शोभते सभा मध्ये हंसमध्ये बको यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Both the mother and father are enemies of a child if they do not educate him. He will be a misfit in any gathering - like a duck among swans.
quotes.transliteration:
mātā śatruḥ pitā vairī bālo yena na pāṭhitaḥ |
na śobhate sabhā madhye haṃsamadhye bako yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तमःपरिवृतं वेश्म यथा दीपेन दीप्यते ।
तथा बुद्धिप्रदीपेन शक्य आत्मा निरीक्षितुम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Darkness in our homes is killed by lamps lighting other lamps. Just like that, knowledge will let us search our souls in the darkness of ignorance.
quotes.transliteration:
tamaḥparivṛtaṃ veśma yathā dīpena dīpyate |
tathā buddhipradīpena śakya ātmā nirīkṣitum ||

quotes.sanskritText:
छन्नं दोषमुदाहरन्ति कुपिता न्यायेन दूरीकृताः
स्वान् दोषान् कथयन्ति नाधिकरणे सन्तोऽपि नष्टा
ध्रुवम् ।
ये पक्षापरपक्षदोषसहिताः पापानि संकुर्वते
संक्षेपादपवाद एव सुलभो द्रष्टुर्गुणो दूरतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When people are angry they hide their shortcomings (the truth). This often misleads the judge. More over, even a noble man will come up to the judge and admit his mistakes since they are certain that it will only cause harm. In many cases, both the defendant and the plaintiff are guilty (to some extent). The job of the judge is really thankless. It can only attract slander but never total praise. There is atleast one party that accuses the judge of partiality.
quotes.transliteration:
channaṃ doṣamudāharanti kupitā nyāyena dūrīkṛtāḥ
svān doṣān kathayanti nādhikaraṇe santoऽpi naṣṭā
dhruvam |
ye pakṣāparapakṣadoṣasahitāḥ pāpāni saṃkurvate
saṃkṣepādapavāda eva sulabho draṣṭurguṇo dūrataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकः क्षमावतां दोषो द्वितीयो नोपपद्यते
यदेनं क्षमया युक्तमशक्तं मन्यते जनः ।
सोऽस्य दोषो न मन्तव्यः क्षमा हि परमं बलं
क्षमा गुणो ह्यशक्तानां भूषणं क्षमा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People who forgive have one and only one drawback - people see them as lacking abilities (the sting). In fact, you can not hold it against them. Forgiveness is not a weakness, it is the greatest strength. It is a noble quality when found in the weak; a decoration (ornament) when found in the mighty.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaḥ kṣamāvatāṃ doṣo dvitīyo nopapadyate
yadenaṃ kṣamayā yuktamaśaktaṃ manyate janaḥ |
soऽsya doṣo na mantavyaḥ kṣamā hi paramaṃ balaṃ
kṣamā guṇo hyaśaktānāṃ bhūṣaṇaṃ kṣamā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्वेष्योऽपि संमतः शिष्टः तस्यार्तस्य यथौषधम् ।
त्याज्यो दुष्टः प्रियोऽप्यासीत् अंगलीवोरगक्षता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A sick person must take medicine even if it is not tasty. Even though a person is rough he may be required for the state machinery to run smoothly. A rogue must be kept at a distance even though he is a close relative - Just like a finger bitten by a snake must be cut off to spare the rest of the body.
quotes.transliteration:
dveṣyoऽpi saṃmataḥ śiṣṭaḥ tasyārtasya yathauṣadham |
tyājyo duṣṭaḥ priyoऽpyāsīt aṃgalīvoragakṣatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निवस्तां वा कृत्तिं निवसतु चिरं वा पितृवने
शिवस्तावद्धत्तामहिगणमथाप्येष सुभगः ।
परीतं शैलालैरुषितमपि पंकेषु मलिना
मलीनां बिभ्राणं किमिह कमनीयं न कमलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Lord Shiva wears animal hide, lives in a cemetery, has a snake around the neck - still he is so attractive and auspicious. The Lotus plant may be covered with moss and lichen, lives/blooms in mire, sprayed with discards of the bees - still it does looks beautiful.
quotes.transliteration:
nivastāṃ vā kṛttiṃ nivasatu ciraṃ vā pitṛvane
śivastāvaddhattāmahigaṇamathāpyeṣa subhagaḥ |
parītaṃ śailālairuṣitamapi paṃkeṣu malinā
malīnāṃ bibhrāṇaṃ kimiha kamanīyaṃ na kamalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शरीरं ज्ञानजननं रोगो विष्णुस्मृतिप्रदः ।
विपद्वैराग्यजननी त्रयं सुख़करं सताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These three are helpful to noble men - a body as a means to attain knowledge, a disease to help get closer to God and troubles to push towards austerity.
quotes.transliteration:
śarīraṃ jñānajananaṃ rogo viṣṇusmṛtipradaḥ |
vipadvairāgyajananī trayaṃ suk͟hakaraṃ satām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कोऽन्धो योऽकार्यरतः
को बधिरो यो हितानि न श्रुणोति ।
को मूको यः काले
प्रियाणि वक्तुं न जानाति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is blind who is busy in wrong deeds. He is deaf who does not heed to good advice. He is dumb who does not know to tell good words at the right time.
quotes.transliteration:
koऽndho yoऽkāryarataḥ
ko badhiro yo hitāni na śruṇoti |
ko mūko yaḥ kāle
priyāṇi vaktuṃ na jānāti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मातृवत्परदारेषु परद्रव्येषु लोष्ठवत् ।
आत्मवत्सर्वभूतेषु यः पश्यति स पण्डितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man will treat other people's wives as his own mother. He treats other people's wealth as a lump of mud (stone). He will treat every being in this world with compassion as he would treat himself.
quotes.transliteration:
mātṛvatparadāreṣu paradravyeṣu loṣṭhavat |
ātmavatsarvabhūteṣu yaḥ paśyati sa paṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुर्बलस्य बलं राजा बालानां रोदनं बलम् ।
बलं मूर्खस्य मौनित्वं चौराणामनृतम् बलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king is the strength of the weak. He protects them. Ability to cry is the strength of a baby. Ability to stay silent is the strength of a fool. Likewise, a theif finds his strength in his ability to lie.
quotes.transliteration:
durbalasya balaṃ rājā bālānāṃ rodanaṃ balam |
balaṃ mūrkhasya maunitvaṃ caurāṇāmanṛtam balam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपदिशति लोकवृत्तं वितरति वित्तं विनोदयति चित्तं ।
उत्तंभयति महत्वं विद्या हृद्या सुराजसेवेव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Education gives you experience to face the world. It gives you money. It will give you happiness. It will bring you honor. Good education will bring you all the wealth just like serving a good king.
quotes.transliteration:
upadiśati lokavṛttaṃ vitarati vittaṃ vinodayati cittaṃ |
uttaṃbhayati mahatvaṃ vidyā hṛdyā surājaseveva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नष्टं मृतमतिक्रांतं नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ।
पण्डितानां च मूर्खाणां विशेषोऽयं यतः स्मृतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men do not spend time grieving over a loss or death. This is the distinction between wise men and fools. </A
quotes.transliteration:
naṣṭaṃ mṛtamatikrāṃtaṃ nānuśocanti paṇḍitāḥ |
paṇḍitānāṃ ca mūrkhāṇāṃ viśeṣoऽyaṃ yataḥ smṛtaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतिसाहसमतिदुष्करमत्याश्चर्यं च दानमर्थानाम् |
यो हि ददाति शरीरं न ददाति स वित्तलेशमपि ||
quotes.englishTranslation: It is a very surprising, difficult and huge deal to see people donating money. It is so unnatural. People are rather to give up their body than part with a little money.
quotes.transliteration:
atisāhasamatiduṣkaramatyāścaryaṃ ca dānamarthānām |
yo hi dadāti śarīraṃ na dadāti sa vittaleśamapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षुध्र तृट् आशाः कुटुम्बिन्य
मयि जीवति न अन्यगाः।
तस्मात् आशा महासाध्वी
कदाचित् मां न मुञ्चति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Hunger, thirst and desire are like a man's wives. They do not desert a man through out his life. Of them, desire is the most faithful. Others leave at least temporarily after eating and drinking.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣudhra tṛṭ āśāḥ kuṭumbinya
mayi jīvati na anyagāḥ|
tasmāt āśā mahāsādhvī
kadācit māṃ na muñcati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाग्निं मुखेनोपधमेत् नग्नां नेक्षेत च स्त्रियम् ।
नामेध्यं प्रक्षिपेदग्नौ न च पादौ प्रतापयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This verse explains how to deal with fire. One must not blow at the fire directly from his mouth. There is always a risk of fire snarling back at you. Use a pipe to blow at fire. One must not look at naked ladies. One must not throw trash in the fire. It could cause various other chemical hazards. One must not stretch his legs towards the fire (to warm them). There is a danger of clothes catching fire. It is notable that the sages have talked about women along side fire.
quotes.transliteration:
nāgniṃ mukhenopadhamet nagnāṃ nekṣeta ca striyam |
nāmedhyaṃ prakṣipedagnau na ca pādau pratāpayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हस्ति स्थूलतरः स चांकुशवशः किं हस्तिमात्रोंऽकुशः
दीपे प्रज्वलिते प्रणश्यति तमः किं दीपमात्रं तमः ।
वज्रेणापि हताः पतन्ति गिरयः किं वज्रमात्रो गिरिः
तेजो यस्य विराजते स बलवान् स्थूलेषु कः प्रत्ययः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: An elephant is very big. But is controlled by a very small hook. Are they both of the same size? A small lamp destroys mighty darkness. Are they both of same size? A diamond can bring down a mountain. Are they both of the same size? He who has courage will become a winner. What has size got to with success?
quotes.transliteration:
hasti sthūlataraḥ sa cāṃkuśavaśaḥ kiṃ hastimātroṃऽkuśaḥ
dīpe prajvalite praṇaśyati tamaḥ kiṃ dīpamātraṃ tamaḥ |
vajreṇāpi hatāḥ patanti girayaḥ kiṃ vajramātro giriḥ
tejo yasya virājate sa balavān sthūleṣu kaḥ pratyayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आचार्यपुत्रः शुश्रूषुः ज्ञानदो धार्मिकः शुचिः ।
आप्तः शक्तोऽर्थदः साधुः स्वोऽध्याप्या दश धर्मितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A teacher must always teach the following students: his teacher's son, his servants, other teachers who will teach him other skills, people who know and live by Dharma, who live clean, relatives, anyone who can analyze by thinking, who can give money in return to knowledge, a well-wisher and other knowledgeable people.
quotes.transliteration:
ācāryaputraḥ śuśrūṣuḥ jñānado dhārmikaḥ śuciḥ |
āptaḥ śaktoऽrthadaḥ sādhuḥ svoऽdhyāpyā daśa dharmitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा दृष्टिः शरीरस्य नित्यमेव प्रवर्तते |
तथा नरेन्द्रो राष्ट्रस्य प्रभवः सत्यधर्मयोः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like eyes help us to perceive the outside world and there by determine what is good or bad for us (the body), the king is the custodian of honesty and Dharma (moral code of life) for the entire kingdom.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā dṛṣṭiḥ śarīrasya nityameva pravartate |
tathā narendro rāṣṭrasya prabhavaḥ satyadharmayoḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बालास्तावत् क्रीडासक्तस्तरुणस्तावत्तरुणीसक्तः ।
वृद्धास्तावच्चिंतासक्तः परे ब्रह्मणि कोऽपि न सक्तः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Little boys are always interested in playing. Young men are always interested in women. Old people are engrossed in worries. No one is interested in the creator.
quotes.transliteration:
bālāstāvat krīḍāsaktastaruṇastāvattaruṇīsaktaḥ |
vṛddhāstāvacciṃtāsaktaḥ pare brahmaṇi koऽpi na saktaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न वारयामो भवतीं विशंतीं
वर्षानदी स्रोतसि जह्नुजायाः ।
न युक्तमेतत्तु पुरो यदस्याः
तरङ्गभङ्गान् प्रकटीकरोषि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh rain-fed-river, you are going to join the mighty river Ganga. Good for you. But we definitely do not like the way you are creating rapids in your little water while Ganga herself is so calm. Empty vessels make more noise.
quotes.transliteration:
na vārayāmo bhavatīṃ viśaṃtīṃ
varṣānadī srotasi jahnujāyāḥ |
na yuktametattu puro yadasyāḥ
taraṅgabhaṅgān prakaṭīkaroṣi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भद्रं भद्रमिति ब्रूयात् भद्रमित्येव वा वदेत् |
शुष्कवैरं विवादं च न कुर्यात् केनचित् सह ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must always talk auspiciously. One must not talk negatively about anybody's endeavor. One must not quarrel just for the sake of quarreling. One must not pickup unnecessary arguments
quotes.transliteration:
bhadraṃ bhadramiti brūyāt bhadramityeva vā vadet |
śuṣkavairaṃ vivādaṃ ca na kuryāt kenacit saha ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इन्द्रियाण्येव तत्सर्वं यत्स्वर्गनरकाविभौ ।
निगृहीतविसृष्टानि स्वर्गाय नरकाय च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The key to heaven or hell is in how we manage our senses. If you let them take control of you then it is hell. If you control them then it is heaven.
quotes.transliteration:
indriyāṇyeva tatsarvaṃ yatsvarganarakāvibhau |
nigṛhītavisṛṣṭāni svargāya narakāya ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्यौतिषं जलदे मिथ्या मिथ्या श्वासिनि वैद्यकं ।
योगो बह्वशने मिथ्या मिथ्या ज्ञानं च मद्यपे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Astrology is irrelevant when one tries to predict the movement of clouds (and there by the rains). The entire field of medicine is irrlevant when it comes to gastic problems. Yoga can not do any good if a person routinely over eats. Knowledge is useless for a drunkard.
quotes.transliteration:
jyautiṣaṃ jalade mithyā mithyā śvāsini vaidyakaṃ |
yogo bahvaśane mithyā mithyā jñānaṃ ca madyape ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुर्लभं त्रयमेवैतद्दैवानुग्रहहेतुकं ।
मनुष्यत्वं मुमुक्षुत्वं महापुरुषसंश्रयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A human birth, liberation from the cycle of re-birth and kinship of virtuous men - these three things are very hard to obtain. Only God can help you get them.
quotes.transliteration:
durlabhaṃ trayamevaitaddaivānugrahahetukaṃ |
manuṣyatvaṃ mumukṣutvaṃ mahāpuruṣasaṃśrayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाप्यं स्नाति विचक्षणो द्वजवरो मूर्खोऽपि वर्णाधमः
फुल्लां नाम्यति वायसोऽपि हि लतां या नामिता बर्हिणा |
ब्रह्यक्षत्रविशस्तरंति च यया नावा तयैवेतेरे
त्वं वापीव लतेव नौरिव जनं वेश्याऽपि सर्वं भज ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In a pond all kinds of people take bath - the learned, fools, brahmins, common men, people of lower caste, so on. A creeper full of flowers will bend if a peacock sits on it. It does also bend if a crow sits like wise. While crossing a river, you can find all kinds of people on a boat. Just like a boat, or the pond or the creeper a prostitute does serve everyone of her clients equally favoring none.
quotes.transliteration:
vāpyaṃ snāti vicakṣaṇo dvajavaro mūrkhoऽpi varṇādhamaḥ
phullāṃ nāmyati vāyasoऽpi hi latāṃ yā nāmitā barhiṇā |
brahyakṣatraviśastaraṃti ca yayā nāvā tayaivetere
tvaṃ vāpīva lateva nauriva janaṃ veśyāऽpi sarvaṃ bhaja ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आचार्यं च प्रवक्तारं पितरं मातरं गुरुम् ।
न हिंस्यात्सज्जनान् गाश्च सर्वांश्चैव तपस्विनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never hurt a teacher, an advisor, father, mother, virtuous people and any sage.
quotes.transliteration:
ācāryaṃ ca pravaktāraṃ pitaraṃ mātaraṃ gurum |
na hiṃsyātsajjanān gāśca sarvāṃścaiva tapasvinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत् श्रुतं न विरागाय न धर्माय न शान्तये ।
सुबद्धमपि शब्देन काकवाशितमेव तत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any thing that is heard must help us any one of these - detachment, Dharma or peace. If not, it is same as a crow blabbering no matter how sweetly it is composed.
quotes.transliteration:
yat śrutaṃ na virāgāya na dharmāya na śāntaye |
subaddhamapi śabdena kākavāśitameva tat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यांति न्यायप्रवॄत्तस्य तिर्यंचोऽपि सहायतां |
अपंथानं तु गच्छंतं सोदरोपि विमुंचति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When a man is righteous (like Rama) even animals (like the monkeys) help him achieve his goals. If a man is a rogue (like Ravana) even his own brother (like Vibhishana) will desert him.
quotes.transliteration:
yāṃti nyāyapravṝttasya tiryaṃcoऽpi sahāyatāṃ |
apaṃthānaṃ tu gacchaṃtaṃ sodaropi vimuṃcati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नैर्गुण्यमेव साधीयो दिगस्तु गुणगौरवम् |
शाखिनोऽन्ये विराजन्ते खण्ड्यन्ते चन्दनद्रुमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: It is better not to have any virtues than dealing with lot of respect that virtue attracts. Look at the other trees in the forest that grow without any hindrances. It is only the sandalwood tree that gets cut down due to its superior quality
quotes.transliteration:
nairguṇyameva sādhīyo digastu guṇagauravam |
śākhinoऽnye virājante khaṇḍyante candanadrumaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्कर्म कुर्वतोऽस्य स्यात् परितोषोंतरात्मनः ।
तत्प्रयत्नेन कुर्वीत विपरीतं तु वर्जयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must do what pleases one's conscience even if it is harder. One must never do anything that troubles the conscience.
quotes.transliteration:
yatkarma kurvatoऽsya syāt paritoṣoṃtarātmanaḥ |
tatprayatnena kurvīta viparītaṃ tu varjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनेकसंशयोच्छेदि परोक्षार्थस्य दर्शकम् ।
सर्वस्य लोचनं शास्त्रं यस्य नास्त्यंध एव सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Scriptures will solve many doubts. It will show truths that do not usually meet the eye. Scriptures are the eyes for all people. Any person who can not read the scriptures, is blind indeed.
quotes.transliteration:
anekasaṃśayocchedi parokṣārthasya darśakam |
sarvasya locanaṃ śāstraṃ yasya nāstyaṃdha eva saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यं ब्रूयात् प्रियं ब्रूयात् न ब्रूयात् सत्यमप्रियम्।
प्रियञ्च नानृतम् ब्रूयात् एष धर्म सनातनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You should always tell the truth. You should also be pleasant. So, if the hard truth hurts, you should tell it pleasantly as long as you are not lying. This is the age old rule of virtue.
quotes.transliteration:
satyaṃ brūyāt priyaṃ brūyāt na brūyāt satyamapriyam|
priyañca nānṛtam brūyāt eṣa dharma sanātanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वर्जयेत् प्रमदागाथामगृहस्थो बृहद्व्रतः ।
इंद्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरंत्यपि यतेर्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who is not a married man, some one who has taken up a big mission should give up talking about women. These sensory pleasures have the ability to corrupt and destroy any one's mind; even a saint's.
quotes.transliteration:
varjayet pramadāgāthāmagṛhastho bṛhadvrataḥ |
iṃdriyāṇi pramāthīni haraṃtyapi yatermanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यपि भेदापगमे नाथ तवाहं न मामकीनस्त्वम् ।
सामुद्रो हि तरंगः क्वचन समुद्रो न तारंगः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even though we are one and the same (same universal matter, Gos is the whole of which we are the parts), there is a difference between you and me. I belong to you. But you don't belong to me. Just like the waves and the ocean are the same. The waves belong to the ocean. But the ocean does not belong to the wave.
quotes.transliteration:
satyapi bhedāpagame nātha tavāhaṃ na māmakīnastvam |
sāmudro hi taraṃgaḥ kvacana samudro na tāraṃgaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वलक्षणहीनोऽपि यः सदाचारवान्नरः ।
श्रद्धधानोऽनसूयश्च शतं वर्षाणि जीवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when a person who does not have any other qualities, a man will live for hundred years if he is virtuous, has sincerity in all his dealings and never feels jealous.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvalakṣaṇahīnoऽpi yaḥ sadācāravānnaraḥ |
śraddhadhānoऽnasūyaśca śataṃ varṣāṇi jīvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाणां प्राणाः संस्थितिहेतवः ।
तन्निघ्नता किं न हतं रक्षता किं न रक्षितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The four goals of life being Dharma, Wealth, Attachment (Kama) and liberation (via knowledge) are possible only because of the body we are in. Hence it is very important that we protect this body. If you let it waste away, then you have let everything down. If you protect the body, you would have protected the path to get everything else.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmārthakāmamokṣāṇāṃ prāṇāḥ saṃsthitihetavaḥ |
tannighnatā kiṃ na hataṃ rakṣatā kiṃ na rakṣitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वार्ता च कौतुकवती विमला च विद्या
लोकोत्तरः परिमलश्च कुरङ्गनाभेः |
तैलस्य बिन्दुरिव वारिणि दुर्निवारं
एतत्त्रयं प्रसरति स्वयमेव भूमौ ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Sensational news, pure education (knowledge) and the smell of the musk deer's secretion - these three will spread all around the world without any help from others. Much like a drop of oil on th surface of water.
quotes.transliteration:
vārtā ca kautukavatī vimalā ca vidyā
lokottaraḥ parimalaśca kuraṅganābheḥ |
tailasya binduriva vāriṇi durnivāraṃ
etattrayaṃ prasarati svayameva bhūmau ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अशुभपुषि कलावप्यप्रमत्ताः स्वधर्मा
दनुदिनमुपकारानाचरन्तो बुधानाम् ।
बहुजनपरिपुष्टौ बद्धदीक्षास्त एते
तनुसुखमपि हित्वा तन्वते देशसेवाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even in the times of Kali (present age) when sin is on the rise there will be virtuous people who will not abandon their traditions. They will not lose their mind. They will continue to serve wise men every day. They will always strive to help as many people as possible. They do this as if they owe it to them. They do not care about their own bodily luxuries. Their sole aim will be to serve the country.
quotes.transliteration:
aśubhapuṣi kalāvapyapramattāḥ svadharmā
danudinamupakārānācaranto budhānām |
bahujanaparipuṣṭau baddhadīkṣāsta ete
tanusukhamapi hitvā tanvate deśasevām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अविद्येयं विद्या भवति भगवद्भक्तिरहिता
न चेद्विष्णोः प्रेम प्रसृमरमहानंदमहिता ।
न तावच्चांचल्यं त्यजति च मनोवृत्ति विहगी
न यावच्छ्रीकृष्णस्मरणरससिंधौ निपतति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any knowledge is not knowledge if it does not give you happiness of being in love with God. One will not get eternal happiness if he is not in love with Vishnu. Unless the bird (our mind) immerses itself in the river (of bhakti), it will not leave its restlessness.
quotes.transliteration:
avidyeyaṃ vidyā bhavati bhagavadbhaktirahitā
na cedviṣṇoḥ prema prasṛmaramahānaṃdamahitā |
na tāvaccāṃcalyaṃ tyajati ca manovṛtti vihagī
na yāvacchrīkṛṣṇasmaraṇarasasiṃdhau nipatati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्वत्वं च नृपत्वं च नैव तल्यं कदाचन ।
स्वदेशे पूज्यते राजा विद्वान् सर्वत्र पूज्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never compare intellectual wealth and royalty. A king is respected only in his kingdom while a scholar is respected all over the world.
quotes.transliteration:
vidvatvaṃ ca nṛpatvaṃ ca naiva talyaṃ kadācana |
svadeśe pūjyate rājā vidvān sarvatra pūjyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तुलाशासनमानानां कूटकृन्नाणकस्य च ।
एभिश्च व्यवहर्ता यः स दाप्यो दममुत्तमम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People who cheat when dealing with balances, documents, other measuring devices, people who counterfeit money all must be punished harshly. Such punishment must be meted out to people who deal with them also.
quotes.transliteration:
tulāśāsanamānānāṃ kūṭakṛnnāṇakasya ca |
ebhiśca vyavahartā yaḥ sa dāpyo damamuttamam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाख्यं य इच्छेच्छेय आत्मनः ।
एकमेव हरेस्तत्र कारणं पादसेवनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharma, Wealth, Lust and salvation are the four most important goals of human life. But all of these can be achieved only by the grace of God. Hence we must serve him all our life.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmārthakāmamokṣākhyaṃ ya iccheccheya ātmanaḥ |
ekameva harestatra kāraṇaṃ pādasevanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुमहान्त्यपि शास्त्राणि धारयन्तो बहुश्रुताः |
छेत्तारः संशयानां च क्लिश्यन्ते लोभमोहिताः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: People with lot of wisdom and very learned, people who routinely clear other people's doubts also get into lot of difficulties because of greed.
quotes.transliteration:
sumahāntyapi śāstrāṇi dhārayanto bahuśrutāḥ |
chettāraḥ saṃśayānāṃ ca kliśyante lobhamohitāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विपर्यस्तमनश्चेष्टैः शिलाशकलवर्ष्मभिः |
मांसवृक्षैरियं मूर्खैः भारक्रान्ता वसुन्धरा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There are huge people walking this earth with weird mind and behavior, whose body is as hard as stone (indestructible) and whose only purpose in this earth is to increase the weight on earth. They are the fools.
quotes.transliteration:
viparyastamanaśceṣṭaiḥ śilāśakalavarṣmabhiḥ |
māṃsavṛkṣairiyaṃ mūrkhaiḥ bhārakrāntā vasundharā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
को धर्मो भूतदया किं सौख्यमरोगिता जगति जन्तोः ।
कः स्नेहः सद्भावः किं पाण्डित्यं परिच्छेदः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is dharma? Having compassion towards all beings. What is happiness? Being healthy is happiness. What is friendship? Not having hard feelings towards any one is friendship. What is scholarly wisdom? The art of dissection (ability to logically breakdown a problem and its intricacies).
quotes.transliteration:
ko dharmo bhūtadayā kiṃ saukhyamarogitā jagati jantoḥ |
kaḥ snehaḥ sadbhāvaḥ kiṃ pāṇḍityaṃ paricchedaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मरणं प्रकृतिः शरीरिणां विकृतिर्जीवितमुच्यते बुधैः ।
क्षणमप्यवतिष्ठते श्वसन् यदि जन्तुर्ननु लाभावानसौ ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Death is the natural steady state of nature. Life is an aberration. Wise men realize this. Even if one is alive for just a second, he must consider himself in profit.
quotes.transliteration:
maraṇaṃ prakṛtiḥ śarīriṇāṃ vikṛtirjīvitamucyate budhaiḥ |
kṣaṇamapyavatiṣṭhate śvasan yadi janturnanu lābhāvānasau ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदि नामास्य कायस्य यदंतस्तद्बहिर्भवेत् ।
दण्डमादाय लोकोऽयं शुनः काकांश्च वारयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If what is there in this body is turned inside out, then the entire world would be chasing dogs and crows away. For them, body is just meat.
quotes.transliteration:
yadi nāmāsya kāyasya yadaṃtastadbahirbhavet |
daṇḍamādāya lokoऽyaṃ śunaḥ kākāṃśca vārayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विषमां हि दशां प्राप्य दैवं गर्हयते नरः ।
आत्मनः कर्मदोषांस्तु नैव जानात्यपण्डितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a fool is in trouble, he will start blaming fate. A fool will never understand that his present state is the result of his past actions.
quotes.transliteration:
viṣamāṃ hi daśāṃ prāpya daivaṃ garhayate naraḥ |
ātmanaḥ karmadoṣāṃstu naiva jānātyapaṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्पृशन्ति शरवत्तीक्ष्णाः स्तोकमन्तर्विशन्ति च |
बहुस्पृशाऽपि स्थूलेन स्थीयते बहिरश्मवत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men are like arrows. They touch very lightly and penetrate by making the slightest slit. Even when explained with few words, they understand the entire picture. But idiots are like stones. They make huge impact but can not penetrate. Even after repeated explaining, they still can not understand.
quotes.transliteration:
spṛśanti śaravattīkṣṇāḥ stokamantarviśanti ca |
bahuspṛśāऽpi sthūlena sthīyate bahiraśmavat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यमस्तेयमक्रोधो ह्रीः शौचं धीर्धृतिर्दमः ।
संयतेंद्रियता विद्या धर्मः सर्व उदाहृतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following aspects living constitute Dharma - Truth, never stealing, no anger, remorse, cleanliness, intelligence to separate good and bad, calm mind, austerity, control over senses and good education
quotes.transliteration:
satyamasteyamakrodho hrīḥ śaucaṃ dhīrdhṛtirdamaḥ |
saṃyateṃdriyatā vidyā dharmaḥ sarva udāhṛtaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पश्यन्नपि प्रस्खलति शृण्वन्नपि न बुद्ध्यति ।
पठन्नपि न जानाति देवमायाविमोहितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People trip even after seeing the obstacle. People don't understand the scriptures even after listening to them. Even of studying, knowledge still eludes them. This can only be attributed to providence.
quotes.transliteration:
paśyannapi praskhalati śṛṇvannapi na buddhyati |
paṭhannapi na jānāti devamāyāvimohitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कः पथ्यतरो धर्मः कः शुचिरिह यस्य मानसं शुद्धम् ।
कः पंडितो विवेकी किं विषमवधीरणा गुरुषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is the ideal way to live? By following dharma (moral code of life). Who can be called clean? He whose mind is clean. Who is wise? He who is patient and logical. Which act is despicable? Showing disrespect to elders and teachers.
quotes.transliteration:
kaḥ pathyataro dharmaḥ kaḥ śuciriha yasya mānasaṃ śuddham |
kaḥ paṃḍito vivekī kiṃ viṣamavadhīraṇā guruṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सा रसना ते नयने तामेव करौ स एव कृतकृत्यः ।
या ये यौ यो भर्गं वदतीक्षेते सदार्चतः स्मरति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not a tongue unless it utters the God's names always. It is not an eye unless it sees God in everything. It is not a hand until it is folded while worshiping God. One can claim he has done some work only when he remembers God always.
quotes.transliteration:
sā rasanā te nayane tāmeva karau sa eva kṛtakṛtyaḥ |
yā ye yau yo bhargaṃ vadatīkṣete sadārcataḥ smarati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो बन्धनवधक्लेशान् प्राणिनां न चकीर्षति ।
न सर्वस्य हितप्रेप्सुः सुखमत्यन्तमश्नुते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who does not trouble animals by tying (captivating), killing or any other way and wish good of all beings will live happily.
quotes.transliteration:
yo bandhanavadhakleśān prāṇināṃ na cakīrṣati |
na sarvasya hitaprepsuḥ sukhamatyantamaśnute ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्विजेन्द्रो वा कलाढ्यो वा कामाचारी भवेद्यदि ।
नित्यं निमज्जन्नप्यब्धौ नांतश्युद्धो भवत्यलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: No one can clean his inner self by taking bath in the ocean every day. It does not matter even if he is a brahmin, or an accomplished artist or a lustful person.
quotes.transliteration:
dvijendro vā kalāḍhyo vā kāmācārī bhavedyadi |
nityaṃ nimajjannapyabdhau nāṃtaśyuddho bhavatyalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चित्तस्य शुद्धये कर्म न तु वस्तूपलब्धये ।
वस्तुसिद्धिविचारेण न किंचित् कर्मकोटिभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must do actions with self satisfaction in mind - not with a spiritual goal. Spiritual goals are attained by contemplation and not by successful completion of crores of tasks.
quotes.transliteration:
cittasya śuddhaye karma na tu vastūpalabdhaye |
vastusiddhivicāreṇa na kiṃcit karmakoṭibhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तेनाधीतं श्रुतं तेन तेन सर्वमनुष्ठितम् ।
येनाशाः पृष्ठतःकृत्वा नैराश्यमवलंबितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is nothing more to hear (listen and learn), nothing more to implement for a man who has given up on greed and completely dependant on his lack of desire. (This is not to be confused with pessimism or lack of hope). The only aim of all learning and rituals is to overcome greed.
quotes.transliteration:
tenādhītaṃ śrutaṃ tena tena sarvamanuṣṭhitam |
yenāśāḥ pṛṣṭhataḥkṛtvā nairāśyamavalaṃbitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अन्धः स एव श्रुतवर्जितो यः
शठः स एवार्थिनिरर्थको यः |
मृतः स एवास्ति यशो न यस्य
धर्मे न धीर्यस्य स एव शोच्यः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He who gives up education is blind. He is a cheater who does not give alms to a beggar. He is dead who has not done enough good deeds in his life to give him fame. He is pitiable who does not spare a thought about Dharma (moral code of life).
quotes.transliteration:
andhaḥ sa eva śrutavarjito yaḥ
śaṭhaḥ sa evārthinirarthako yaḥ |
mṛtaḥ sa evāsti yaśo na yasya
dharme na dhīryasya sa eva śocyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणानामन्तरं प्रायस्तज्ञो वेत्ति न चापरः ।
मालतीमल्लिकामोदं घ्राणं वेत्ति न लोचनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only a wise man can appreciate good qualities - not every one. Only the nose can know the difference between jasmine and imitations (jaji flower) and not the eyes.
quotes.transliteration:
guṇānāmantaraṃ prāyastajño vetti na cāparaḥ |
mālatīmallikāmodaṃ ghrāṇaṃ vetti na locanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भूतिर्नीचगृहेषु विप्रसदने दारिद्र्यकोलाहलः
नाशो हंत सतामसत्पथजुषामायुः शतानां शतम् |
दुर्नीतिं तव वीक्ष्य कोपदहनज्वालाजटलोऽपिसन्
किं कुर्वे जगदीश यत्पुनरहं दीनो भवानीश्वरः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The bad people always have plenty. Poverty dances in the homes of people who lead a honest life. Virtuous people die early while the rogues live for hundred years. When I see such atrocities in Your creation, I get all fumed up in anger. But what can I do, I am helpless and you are all powerful.
quotes.transliteration:
bhūtirnīcagṛheṣu viprasadane dāridryakolāhalaḥ
nāśo haṃta satāmasatpathajuṣāmāyuḥ śatānāṃ śatam |
durnītiṃ tava vīkṣya kopadahanajvālājaṭaloऽpisan
kiṃ kurve jagadīśa yatpunarahaṃ dīno bhavānīśvaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नायमत्यन्तसंवासः कस्यचित् केनचित् सह ।
अपि स्वस्य शरीरेण किमुतान्यैः पृथग्जनैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never get too attached to anyone or anything. One must not get too attached to one's own body; what can one say about other people.
quotes.transliteration:
nāyamatyantasaṃvāsaḥ kasyacit kenacit saha |
api svasya śarīreṇa kimutānyaiḥ pṛthagjanaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्तुवन्ति श्रान्तास्याः क्षितिपतिमभूतैरपि गुणैः |
प्रवाचः कार्पण्याद्यदवितथवाचोऽपि पुरुषाः |
प्रभावस्तृष्णायाः स खलु सकलः स्यादितरथा
निरीहाणामीशस्तृणमिव तिरस्कारविषयः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Even the most articulate people, honest people lie in front of a king by praising about qualities that he does not have. This is the effect that greed has on people. There is another alternative. If a person can win over his greed, then to him even a king is as insignificant a blade of grass.
quotes.transliteration:
stuvanti śrāntāsyāḥ kṣitipatimabhūtairapi guṇaiḥ |
pravācaḥ kārpaṇyādyadavitathavācoऽpi puruṣāḥ |
prabhāvastṛṣṇāyāḥ sa khalu sakalaḥ syāditarathā
nirīhāṇāmīśastṛṇamiva tiraskāraviṣayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्या विवादाय धनं मदाय
शक्तिः परेषां परिपीडनाय ।
खलस्यः साधोः विपरीतं
एतद् ज्ञानाय दानाय च रक्षणाय ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wicked man uses his education for needless argument, money to indulge and strength to hurt others. In contrast a virtuous person uses them for aquiring new knowledge, charity and protecting the weak reaspectively.
quotes.transliteration:
vidyā vivādāya dhanaṃ madāya
śaktiḥ pareṣāṃ paripīḍanāya |
khalasyaḥ sādhoḥ viparītaṃ
etad jñānāya dānāya ca rakṣaṇāya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुःखं च जन्मदुरितं च दृढामविद्यां
हा हन्त हन्ति परमा हरिभक्तिरेका ।
एकोऽपि राघवशरः किल सप्त सालान्
शैलं रसातलमपि त्वरया बिभेद ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Devotion towards God is alone enough to mitigate misery, endless cycle of births and ignorance. One arrow from Lord Rama was enough to penetrate seven Sal trees as well as the underworlds all the way down to Rasatala. Such is the power of God and seeking His refuge is enough to get us out of misery.
quotes.transliteration:
duḥkhaṃ ca janmaduritaṃ ca dṛḍhāmavidyāṃ
hā hanta hanti paramā haribhaktirekā |
ekoऽpi rāghavaśaraḥ kila sapta sālān
śailaṃ rasātalamapi tvarayā bibheda ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मार्थं यस्य वित्तेहा वरं तस्य निरेहता ।
प्रक्षालनाद्धि पंकस्य श्रेयो न स्पर्शनं नृणाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one wants wealth only so that he can spend it on Dharma (charity and other noble deeds), then it is better to give up the longing. It is better keep your feet clean by staying away from mud than clean it after getting dirty.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmārthaṃ yasya vittehā varaṃ tasya nirehatā |
prakṣālanāddhi paṃkasya śreyo na sparśanaṃ nṛṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तयोर्नित्यं प्रियं कुर्यात् आचार्यस्य च सर्वदा ।
तेष्वेव त्रिषु तुष्टेषु तपः सर्वं समाप्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must do all he can to keep his parents and teacher happy. If they are satisfied it is equivalent to any (all) penance.
quotes.transliteration:
tayornityaṃ priyaṃ kuryāt ācāryasya ca sarvadā |
teṣveva triṣu tuṣṭeṣu tapaḥ sarvaṃ samāpyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुलस्यार्थे त्यजेदेकम् ग्रामस्यार्थे कुलम् त्यजेत् ।
ग्रामम् जनपदस्यार्थे आत्मार्थे पृथिवीम् त्यजेत्॥
quotes.englishTranslation: For the sake of the family, we may have to sacrifice one person. Sacrifice a family to save a village. Sacrifice a village to save a state. But to save one-self, sacrifice the entire world.
quotes.transliteration:
kulasyārthe tyajedekam grāmasyārthe kulam tyajet |
grāmam janapadasyārthe ātmārthe pṛthivīm tyajet||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वभूतेषु यः पश्येद्भगवद्भावमात्मनः ।
भूतानि भगवत्यात्मन्येष भागवतोत्तमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is the best devotee who is capable of seeing God in all beings and seeing all beings in God.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvabhūteṣu yaḥ paśyedbhagavadbhāvamātmanaḥ |
bhūtāni bhagavatyātmanyeṣa bhāgavatottamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सामादिदण्डपर्यन्तो नयः प्रोक्तः स्वयंभुवा |
तेषां दण्डस्तु पापीयान् तं पश्चाद्विनियोजयेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The creator (of the world) has enumerated many ways deal with adversaries - starting from patience to punishment. Among them punishing/penalizing some one has some sin attached to it. Hence one must resort to punishment only as a last resort.
quotes.transliteration:
sāmādidaṇḍaparyanto nayaḥ proktaḥ svayaṃbhuvā |
teṣāṃ daṇḍastu pāpīyān taṃ paścādviniyojayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बालस्यापि रवेः पादाः पतत्युपरि भूभृतां ।
तेजसा सह जातानां वयः कुत्रोपयुज्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a very young Sun (early morning Sun) trots over mighty mountains. Those who are born virtous need not wait till old age to command respect.
quotes.transliteration:
bālasyāpi raveḥ pādāḥ patatyupari bhūbhṛtāṃ |
tejasā saha jātānāṃ vayaḥ kutropayujyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मादर्थः प्रभवते धर्मात् प्रभवते सुखम् ।
धर्मेण लभते सर्वं धर्मसारमिदं जगत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth follows dharma. Dharma leads to happiness. Everything can be attained by following dharma. Dharma supports everything in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmādarthaḥ prabhavate dharmāt prabhavate sukham |
dharmeṇa labhate sarvaṃ dharmasāramidaṃ jagat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तरुणीप्रभुता गेहे श्यालको बुद्धिदायकः ।
कन्याविक्रयिणो लोभाद्दंपतीनां च कल्कनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the time of Kali (the present age) the following sins will be prevalent: women will have control of the family, the wife will listen to husband's brother more than her husband, girls will be sold for money, husband and wife will fight with each other a lot due to greed.
quotes.transliteration:
taruṇīprabhutā gehe śyālako buddhidāyakaḥ |
kanyāvikrayiṇo lobhāddaṃpatīnāṃ ca kalkanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कर्म लोकविरुद्धं तु कुर्वाणं क्षणदचर |
तीक्ष्णं सर्वजनो हन्ति सर्पं दुष्टमिवागतम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Nocturnal being (demon)! If you continue your actions that are harmful to the general public, those people will finally kill you. They will treat you exactly like a harmful snake that entered their house.
quotes.transliteration:
karma lokaviruddhaṃ tu kurvāṇaṃ kṣaṇadacara |
tīkṣṇaṃ sarvajano hanti sarpaṃ duṣṭamivāgatam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शमार्थं सर्वशास्त्राणि विहितानि मनीषिभिः ।
स एव सर्वशास्त्रज्ञः यस्य शांतं मनः सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All scriptures are written with the sole intention of helping one find inner peace. He who has found and maintained inner tranquility can thus be called a scholar who knows all scriptures.
quotes.transliteration:
śamārthaṃ sarvaśāstrāṇi vihitāni manīṣibhiḥ |
sa eva sarvaśāstrajñaḥ yasya śāṃtaṃ manaḥ sadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परवानर्थसंसिद्धौ नीचवृत्तिरपत्रपः |
अविधेयन्द्रियः पुंसां गौरिवैति विधेयताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One who does surrender himself for money, one who does take up immoral occupation for money, one who does not control his senses will finally end up losing his freedom and be domesticated like a cow.
quotes.transliteration:
paravānarthasaṃsiddhau nīcavṛttirapatrapaḥ |
avidheyandriyaḥ puṃsāṃ gaurivaiti vidheyatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा ह्यनुदका नद्यो यथा वाप्यतृणं वनं |
अगोपाला यथा गावस्तथा राष्ट्रमराजकम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Subjects without their king are like a river without water, a forest without grass or cattle without the herdsmen. They are neither safe nor productive.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā hyanudakā nadyo yathā vāpyatṛṇaṃ vanaṃ |
agopālā yathā gāvastathā rāṣṭramarājakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दानं वित्तादृतं वाचः कीर्तिधर्मौ तथायुषः ।
परोपकरणं कायादसारात्सारमुद्धरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Money must be used for charity. Speech must be used for truth. Life must be used to uphold Dharma and earn fame. Body must be used for helping others. Worthless things must always be used to create some thing worthwhile.
quotes.transliteration:
dānaṃ vittādṛtaṃ vācaḥ kīrtidharmau tathāyuṣaḥ |
paropakaraṇaṃ kāyādasārātsāramuddharet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकं हन्यान्न वा हन्यात् इषुर्मुक्तो धनुष्मता ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतः सृष्टा हन्ति राष्ट्रं सनायकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: An arrow may or may not kill the target. But an intelligent plot can destroy an entire nation along with its leaders.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaṃ hanyānna vā hanyāt iṣurmukto dhanuṣmatā |
buddhirbuddhimataḥ sṛṣṭā hanti rāṣṭraṃ sanāyakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्वयि रक्षति रक्षकैः किमन्यैः
त्वयि चारक्षति रक्षकैः किमन्यैः ।
इति निश्चितधीः श्रयामि नित्यं
नृहरे वेगवतीतटाश्रयं त्वाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: iti nishcita dhIH shrayAmi nityam If you protect, then what is the use of any other protector? If you do not protect, then what is the use of other protectors? So, I will only seek refuge under you.
quotes.transliteration:
tvayi rakṣati rakṣakaiḥ kimanyaiḥ
tvayi cārakṣati rakṣakaiḥ kimanyaiḥ |
iti niścitadhīḥ śrayāmi nityaṃ
nṛhare vegavatītaṭāśrayaṃ tvām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा नदीनां प्रभवः समुद्रः
यथाऽऽहुतीनां प्रभवः हुताशनः ।
यथेन्द्रियाणां प्रभवं मनोऽपि
तथा प्रभुर्नो भगवानुपेन्द्रः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All rivers flow towards the sea. All offerings end at the pyre (Yajneshwara). All sense organs are controlled and work for the mind. Just like that, the master of all of us is Upendra (Vishnu)
quotes.transliteration:
yathā nadīnāṃ prabhavaḥ samudraḥ
yathāऽऽhutīnāṃ prabhavaḥ hutāśanaḥ |
yathendriyāṇāṃ prabhavaṃ manoऽpi
tathā prabhurno bhagavānupendraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गोपाल इति मत्वा त्वां प्रचुरक्षीरवांछया ।
श्रितो मातुः स्तनक्षीरमपि लब्धुं न शक्नुयाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I thought you are a cowherd and came to your refuge looking forward to drink as much milk as I want. But your acquaintance has robbed me of the chance to drink milk from my own mother again. (by giving salvation and hence excused from being born again)
quotes.transliteration:
gopāla iti matvā tvāṃ pracurakṣīravāṃchayā |
śrito mātuḥ stanakṣīramapi labdhuṃ na śaknuyām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत् स्यादहिंसासंयुक्तं स धर्म इति निश्चयः ।
अहिंसार्थाय भूतानां धर्मप्रवचनं कृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That which believes in non-violence, compassion and kindness is said to in accordance with Dharma. No doubt about that. In fact all scriptures that preach Dharma have always preached against violence.
quotes.transliteration:
yat syādahiṃsāsaṃyuktaṃ sa dharma iti niścayaḥ |
ahiṃsārthāya bhūtānāṃ dharmapravacanaṃ kṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा नेच्छति निरोगः कदाचित्सुचिकित्सकं |
तथापद्रहितो राजा सचिवं नाभिवाछति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a healthy person never wishes to go to a doctor, a king would not wish to consult a minister unless there is some trouble. But when there is trouble, a minister is as important as a doctor during illness.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā necchati nirogaḥ kadācitsucikitsakaṃ |
tathāpadrahito rājā sacivaṃ nābhivāchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विविधनिगमसारे विश्वरक्षैकधीरे
वृषशिख़रिविहारे वक्षसाश्मिष्टदारे ।
भगवति यदुवीरे भक्तबुद्धेरदूरे
भवतु चिरमुदारे भावना निर्विकारे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Krishna is the essence of all the Vedas. He is the lone protector of the world. He wanders in the Sheshashaila. He has Lakshmi in his heart. He is never far away from the mind of the devotees. He is the only one with no defects. Let my devotion in you be for ever.
quotes.transliteration:
vividhanigamasāre viśvarakṣaikadhīre
vṛṣaśik͟harivihāre vakṣasāśmiṣṭadāre |
bhagavati yaduvīre bhaktabuddheradūre
bhavatu ciramudāre bhāvanā nirvikāre ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अदण्ड्यान् दण्डयन् राजा दंड्यांश्चैवाप्यदण्डयन् |
अयशो महदाप्नोति नरकं चैव गच्छति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king punishes the innocent and fails to punish the guilty, he will become very infamous. In addition to that, he will go to hell.
quotes.transliteration:
adaṇḍyān daṇḍayan rājā daṃḍyāṃścaivāpyadaṇḍayan |
ayaśo mahadāpnoti narakaṃ caiva gacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वथा व्यवहर्तव्यं कुतोह्यवचनीयता |
यथा स्त्रीणां तथा वाचां साधुत्वे दुर्जनो जनः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: How can one be silent in the face of adversary? One has to talk (and defend himself). When it is the matter of some one accusing you of something, or hurting the ladies - inaction is not an option. One is called noble only when wickedness is paid back in the same coin.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvathā vyavahartavyaṃ kutohyavacanīyatā |
yathā strīṇāṃ tathā vācāṃ sādhutve durjano janaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मातापितृसहस्राणि पुत्रदाराशतानि च ।
संसारेष्वनुभूतानि कस्य ते कस्य वा वयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There were thousands of fathers and mothers, hundreds of children and wives. All of them came and went into this world before us. How is any one related to another? Should any one be attached to any one else?
quotes.transliteration:
mātāpitṛsahasrāṇi putradārāśatāni ca |
saṃsāreṣvanubhūtāni kasya te kasya vā vayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शिष्यो भार्या शिशुर्भ्राता पुत्रो दासः समाश्रितः |
यस्यैतानि विनीतानि तस्य लोके हि गौरवम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In a house, only if the student, the wife, the kid brother, the son, the servant and any dependent is obedient to you, then you will have respect in the society.
quotes.transliteration:
śiṣyo bhāryā śiśurbhrātā putro dāsaḥ samāśritaḥ |
yasyaitāni vinītāni tasya loke hi gauravam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नारायणपराः सर्वे न कुतश्चन बिभ्यति ।
स्वर्गापवर्गनरकेष्वपि तुल्यार्थदर्शिनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Real devotees of God do not fear anything. They treat heaven, misery or hell likely.
quotes.transliteration:
nārāyaṇaparāḥ sarve na kutaścana bibhyati |
svargāpavarganarakeṣvapi tulyārthadarśinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा जानन्ति बहवो यथा वक्ष्यन्ति दातरी ।
तथा धर्मं चरेत् सर्वं वृथा धर्ममाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one follows Dharma, he must let every one know about it. The donor must be praised in order to ensure future donations. Without these, it is not worth following Dharma. Such will be the times in the epoch of Kali (present times).
quotes.transliteration:
yathā jānanti bahavo yathā vakṣyanti dātarī |
tathā dharmaṃ caret sarvaṃ vṛthā dharmamācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मूर्खाणां पण्डिता द्वेष्या निर्धनानां महाधनाः |
व्रतिनः पापशीलानामसतीनां कुलस्त्रियः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very natural that the fools hate the wise people, poor hate rich, corrupt hate the righteous and promiscuous hate the chaste.
quotes.transliteration:
mūrkhāṇāṃ paṇḍitā dveṣyā nirdhanānāṃ mahādhanāḥ |
vratinaḥ pāpaśīlānāmasatīnāṃ kulastriyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहन्यहनि भूतानि गच्छन्तीह यमालयम् ।
शेषाः स्थावरमिच्छन्ति किमाश्चर्यमतःपरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Every day; day after day, people see others going to death's home. Still, the rest long for immortality. Is there any bigger irony than this?
quotes.transliteration:
ahanyahani bhūtāni gacchantīha yamālayam |
śeṣāḥ sthāvaramicchanti kimāścaryamataḥparam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नास्तिक्यमनृतं क्रोधं प्रमादं दीर्घसूत्रताम् |
अदर्शनं ज्ञानवतामालस्यं पञ्चवृत्तिताम् ||
एकचिंतनमर्थानामनर्थज्ञैश्च मंत्रणम् |
निश्चितानामनारंभं मंत्रस्यापरिरक्षणम् ||
मंगलस्याप्रयोगं च प्रत्युत्थानं च सर्वतः |
कच्चित्त्वं वर्जयस्येतान् राजदोषांचतुर्दश ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the fourteen sins that a king must stay clear of - disbelief (atheism), dishonesty, anger, lack of patience (or logical thinking), procastination, not consulting the wise men, laziness, giving in to sensory pleasures. taking decisions without consulting others, consulting people without experience, not starting the projects that are agreed upon, not keeping state secrets. Not undertaking any auspicious work, attacking all enemies at the same time.
quotes.transliteration:
nāstikyamanṛtaṃ krodhaṃ pramādaṃ dīrghasūtratām |
adarśanaṃ jñānavatāmālasyaṃ pañcavṛttitām ||
ekaciṃtanamarthānāmanarthajñaiśca maṃtraṇam |
niścitānāmanāraṃbhaṃ maṃtrasyāparirakṣaṇam ||
maṃgalasyāprayogaṃ ca pratyutthānaṃ ca sarvataḥ |
kaccittvaṃ varjayasyetān rājadoṣāṃcaturdaśa ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्रुतस्य भगवद्धर्मात् धारावाहिकतां गता ।
सर्वेशे मनसो वृत्तिः भक्तिरित्यभिधीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is devotion? When one's mind dissolves itself (and flows like a torrent) in God thinking about the immeasurable qualities (like compassion) of Him - it is called Devotion.
quotes.transliteration:
drutasya bhagavaddharmāt dhārāvāhikatāṃ gatā |
sarveśe manaso vṛttiḥ bhaktirityabhidhīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चारो यस्य विचारश्च राज्ञो नास्तीक्षणद्वयम् |
तस्यांधदुग्धवद्राज्यं मंत्रिमार्जालगोचरम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king who does not have an elaborate spy network and a sane logical mind (for analysis) will lose his kingdom to the minister. This is the same fate that happens to milk kept in front of a blind man. The cat will silently drink it all.
quotes.transliteration:
cāro yasya vicāraśca rājño nāstīkṣaṇadvayam |
tasyāṃdhadugdhavadrājyaṃ maṃtrimārjālagocaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अणुभ्यश्च महद्भ्यश्च शास्त्रेभ्यः कुशलो नरः ।
सर्वतः सारमादद्यात् पुष्पेभ्य इव षट्पदः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man does not differentiate between knowledge - small as an atom or mighty. There are things to learn from everything. He will extract value from all like a bee visiting all flowers for nectar.
quotes.transliteration:
aṇubhyaśca mahadbhyaśca śāstrebhyaḥ kuśalo naraḥ |
sarvataḥ sāramādadyāt puṣpebhya iva ṣaṭpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पर्जन्यमिव भूतानि महाद्रुममिवांडजाः |
नराः समुपजीवन्ति नृपं सर्वार्थसाधकम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like animals depend on the rain, the birds depend on the tree - ordinary subjects depend on the king for achieving anything.
quotes.transliteration:
parjanyamiva bhūtāni mahādrumamivāṃḍajāḥ |
narāḥ samupajīvanti nṛpaṃ sarvārthasādhakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चीयते बालिशस्यापि सत्क्षेत्रप्रतिता कृषिः ।
न शालेः स्तंबकरिता वप्तुर्गुणमपेक्षते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If an idiot sows seed in a fertile land, the crop will still be excellent. For the crop to grow well and give good yeild does not depend on the character (or intelligence) of the sower.
quotes.transliteration:
cīyate bāliśasyāpi satkṣetrapratitā kṛṣiḥ |
na śāleḥ staṃbakaritā vapturguṇamapekṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परिव्राट् कामुकशुनामेकस्यां प्रमदातनौ ।
कुणपः कामिनी भक्ष्यमिति तिस्रो विकल्पनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A female body is lying there. A dos sees it as food. A pervert sees it as a means to satisfy his wanton. A mendicant considers it as a dead body.
quotes.transliteration:
parivrāṭ kāmukaśunāmekasyāṃ pramadātanau |
kuṇapaḥ kāminī bhakṣyamiti tisro vikalpanāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न हायनैर्नपलितैः न वित्तेन न बंधुभिः ।
ॠषयश्चक्रिरे धर्मं योऽनूचानः स नो महान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharma is not to elope from everyday life. Neither it is what ever old people say. Dharma is not run by money. Dharma is not what your relatives tell. Dharma is practicing and living by the moral code of life (just as the sages lived). He who studies the scriptures (about dharma) will attain greatness.
quotes.transliteration:
na hāyanairnapalitaiḥ na vittena na baṃdhubhiḥ |
ṝṣayaścakrire dharmaṃ yoऽnūcānaḥ sa no mahān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वस्य हि शास्त्रस्य कर्मणो वाऽपि कस्यचित् |
यावत् प्रयोजनं नोक्तं तावत् तत् केन गृह्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must always tell the use (or applications) of any knowledge, wisdom or any action. If people do not see applications, they will not pay attention to it.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvasya hi śāstrasya karmaṇo vāऽpi kasyacit |
yāvat prayojanaṃ noktaṃ tāvat tat kena gṛhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रज्ञावृद्धं धर्मवृद्धं स्वबंधुं
विद्यावृद्धं वयसा चापि वृद्धं |
कार्याकार्ये पूजयित्वा प्रसाद्य
यः संपृच्छेन्न स मुह्येत् कदाचित् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There are five kinds of people who one must listen to and respect - knowledgeable people, pious people, close friends, well educated people and the elderly. Such a person will never tread along wrong path due to ignorance.
quotes.transliteration:
prajñāvṛddhaṃ dharmavṛddhaṃ svabaṃdhuṃ
vidyāvṛddhaṃ vayasā cāpi vṛddhaṃ |
kāryākārye pūjayitvā prasādya
yaḥ saṃpṛcchenna sa muhyet kadācit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकमप्यक्षरं यस्तु गुरुः शिष्ये निवेदयेत् ।
पृथिव्यां नास्ति तद्द्रव्यं यद्दत्वा ह्यनृणीभवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if the teacher has taught you just one letter he has helped you a lot. This help can not be repaid by giving any gift in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
ekamapyakṣaraṃ yastu guruḥ śiṣye nivedayet |
pṛthivyāṃ nāsti taddravyaṃ yaddatvā hyanṛṇībhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
षट्कर्णो भिद्यते मंत्रश्चतुष्कर्णः स्थिरो भवेत् ।
तस्मात्सर्वप्रयत्नेन षट्कर्णं वर्जयेत्सुधीः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a secret falls over six ears, it will be spread like wild fire. If it falls on just four ears, it will become more strong. So a wise man must use all his resources to avoid his secrets falling over six ears. If you confide in a friend he will not betray you because, he knows that if the secret becomes public you will know that he has failed you. If you tell it more than one person, they will not keep it to themselves because both of them knows that you will have no idea who spread it.
quotes.transliteration:
ṣaṭkarṇo bhidyate maṃtraścatuṣkarṇaḥ sthiro bhavet |
tasmātsarvaprayatnena ṣaṭkarṇaṃ varjayetsudhīḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आयुर्नष्यति पश्यतां प्रतिदिनं याति क्षयं यौवनं
प्रत्ययान्ति गताः पुनर्न दिवसाः कालो जगद्भक्षकः ।
लक्ष्मीस्तोयतरंगभंगचपला विद्युच्चलं जीवितं
तस्मान्मां शरणागतं करुणया त्व रक्ष रक्षाधुना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Lifetime erodes every day right in front of our eyes. Youth is always decreasing. Days that go past can never be got back. In time everything gets destroyed. Wealth is as temporary and fickle as a wave on water front. Life itself is as short as a lightning. So, I have come to your refuge. Please save me today.
quotes.transliteration:
āyurnaṣyati paśyatāṃ pratidinaṃ yāti kṣayaṃ yauvanaṃ
pratyayānti gatāḥ punarna divasāḥ kālo jagadbhakṣakaḥ |
lakṣmīstoyataraṃgabhaṃgacapalā vidyuccalaṃ jīvitaṃ
tasmānmāṃ śaraṇāgataṃ karuṇayā tva rakṣa rakṣādhunā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संरोहति शरैर्विद्धं वरं परशुना हतम् ।
वाचा दुरुक्तं भीभत्सं न संरोहति वाक्क्षतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person is hit by an arrow or hurt by an axe; he will survive to get better. But if a person is hurt by harsh words, then he will never recover from the anguish.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃrohati śarairviddhaṃ varaṃ paraśunā hatam |
vācā duruktaṃ bhībhatsaṃ na saṃrohati vākkṣatam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तत्तदुत्प्रेक्षमाणानां पुराणैरागमैर्विना ।
अनुपासितवृद्धानां विद्या नातिप्रसीदति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Knowledge can not be attained by a person who does not put in the hard work to understand the vedas, the puranas, the reasons behind our rituals, serve our elders. Such a person will only spend time in exaggerating facts and pretending to be well-educated.
