quotes.transliteration:
tattadutprekṣamāṇānāṃ purāṇairāgamairvinā |
anupāsitavṛddhānāṃ vidyā nātiprasīdati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्यक्तव्यो ममकारस्त्यक्तुं यदि शक्यते नासौ ।
कर्तव्यो ममकारः किं तु स सर्वत्र कर्तव्यः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must denounce all kinds of attachment. It is not possible he must develop attachment towards all objects alike.
quotes.transliteration:
tyaktavyo mamakārastyaktuṃ yadi śakyate nāsau |
kartavyo mamakāraḥ kiṃ tu sa sarvatra kartavyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शिलं किमनलं भवेदनलमौदरं बाधितुं
पयः प्रसृतिपूरकं किमुन धारकं सारसं ।
अयत्न मलवल्लिकं पथिपटच्चरं कच्चरं
भजन्ति विबुधा मुधा ह्यहह कुक्षितः कुक्षितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Can the grains collected not enough to douse the fire of hunger in the stomach? Can the water in the lakes not quench the thirst? Can the rags found on the streets not enough to make clothes to cover us? Even when all the life necessities are taken care of by nature, why do these scholars serve the King?
quotes.transliteration:
śilaṃ kimanalaṃ bhavedanalamaudaraṃ bādhituṃ
payaḥ prasṛtipūrakaṃ kimuna dhārakaṃ sārasaṃ |
ayatna malavallikaṃ pathipaṭaccaraṃ kaccaraṃ
bhajanti vibudhā mudhā hyahaha kukṣitaḥ kukṣitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाकृत्वा प्राणिनां हिंसां मांसमुत्पाद्यते क्वचित् ।
न च प्राणिवधः स्वर्ग्यः तस्मान्मांसं विवर्जयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no way one can get meat without killing animals. Killing anything is a sin. Hence one must give up meat eating.
quotes.transliteration:
nākṛtvā prāṇināṃ hiṃsāṃ māṃsamutpādyate kvacit |
na ca prāṇivadhaḥ svargyaḥ tasmānmāṃsaṃ vivarjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तृष्णया चेत्परित्यक्तो को दरिद्रः कः ईश्वरः ।
तस्याश्चेत्प्रसरो दत्तो दास्यं च शिरसि स्थितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one does give up greed, it does not matter (to him) if he is poor or rich. On the other hand, if one lets greed thrive, then it is certain that he will end up being its slave.
quotes.transliteration:
tṛṣṇayā cetparityakto ko daridraḥ kaḥ īśvaraḥ |
tasyāścetprasaro datto dāsyaṃ ca śirasi sthitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यन्मातापितरौ वृत्तं तनये कुरुतः सदा ।
न सुप्रतिकरं तत्तु मात्रा पित्रा च यत्कृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one considers the efforts of parents in bringing up a child, then it is fairly obvious that one never be able to repay the debt.
quotes.transliteration:
yanmātāpitarau vṛttaṃ tanaye kurutaḥ sadā |
na supratikaraṃ tattu mātrā pitrā ca yatkṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वनाशे समुत्पन्ने ह्यर्धं त्यजति पण्डितः ।
अर्धेन कुरुते कार्यं सर्वनाशः सदुःसहः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When wise men are faced with big trouble (total destruction) then they give up half of their belongings - instead of trying to protect all. They will work with only half and get their work done. It is very difficult to protect your entire wealth in the face of destruction.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvanāśe samutpanne hyardhaṃ tyajati paṇḍitaḥ |
ardhena kurute kāryaṃ sarvanāśaḥ saduḥsahaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भवेत्स्व परराष्ट्राणां कार्यकार्यविलोकने |
चारचक्षुर्महीभर्तुर्यस्य नास्त्यांध एव सः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: "What is going on?" and "What must go on?" in his country and in his neighbouring country are the questions that a king must know. To do this he must have a good network of spies.
quotes.transliteration:
bhavetsva pararāṣṭrāṇāṃ kāryakāryavilokane |
cāracakṣurmahībharturyasya nāstyāṃdha eva saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इन्द्रियाणि पुरा जित्वा त्रिभुवनं त्वया ।
स्मरद्भिरिव तद्वैरमिन्द्रियैरेव निर्जितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: At one time you had overcome your senses and conquered the three worlds. Today, as if a revenge, the same senses have got you by their grip and caused your defeat (and death). [As told by Mandodari to Ravana after his death]
quotes.transliteration:
indriyāṇi purā jitvā tribhuvanaṃ tvayā |
smaradbhiriva tadvairamindriyaireva nirjitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गभीरे कासारे विशति विजने घोरविपिने
विशाले शैले च भ्रमति कुसुमार्थं जडमतिः ।
समर्पैकं चेतः सरसिजमुमानाथ भवतो
सुखेनावस्थातुं जन इह न जानाति किमहो ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Stupid people roam through the deep lakes, high mountains or forests deviod of people to get flowers for your worship. Oh Lord of Uma! why can not these people understand that the best offering is to offer their own mind to you which is better than any lotus.
quotes.transliteration:
gabhīre kāsāre viśati vijane ghoravipine
viśāle śaile ca bhramati kusumārthaṃ jaḍamatiḥ |
samarpaikaṃ cetaḥ sarasijamumānātha bhavato
sukhenāvasthātuṃ jana iha na jānāti kimaho ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शक्तिवैकल्यनम्रस्य निःसारत्वाल्लघीयसः |
जन्मिनो मानहीनस्य तृणस्य च समा गतिः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A blade of grass bends due to lack of strength. It flies away due to lack of weight. A person without honour is just like a grass - without inner strength or weight.
quotes.transliteration:
śaktivaikalyanamrasya niḥsāratvāllaghīyasaḥ |
janmino mānahīnasya tṛṇasya ca samā gatiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न चन्द्रेण न औषध्या न सूर्येण न वह्निना |
साम्नैव विलयं याति विद्वेष्रभवं तमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The darkness caused by hatred can not be overcome by moon (moonlight), nor by the sun, also not by any fire. It can only be dealt with by pacifying patiently.
quotes.transliteration:
na candreṇa na auṣadhyā na sūryeṇa na vahninā |
sāmnaiva vilayaṃ yāti vidveṣrabhavaṃ tamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संतोषार्थं विमृशति मुहुः स्द्भिरध्यात्मविद्यां
नित्यं ब्रूते निशमयति छ स्वादुसुव्याहृतानि ।
अंगीकुर्वन्ननघललितां वृत्तिमादेहपातात्
दृष्टादृष्टस्वभरविगमे दत्त दृष्टिः प्रसन्नः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A devotee amuses himself by indulging in philosophical discussions. Everyday, he talks and hears good words. He goes about his business by not hurting those people who are good, who have not greed in this world or the next.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃtoṣārthaṃ vimṛśati muhuḥ sdbhiradhyātmavidyāṃ
nityaṃ brūte niśamayati cha svādusuvyāhṛtāni |
aṃgīkurvannanaghalalitāṃ vṛttimādehapātāt
dṛṣṭādṛṣṭasvabharavigame datta dṛṣṭiḥ prasannaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पानं स्त्री मृगया द्यूतं अर्थदूषणमेव च |
वाग्दण्डयोश्च पारुष्यं व्यसनानि महीभुजाम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: These are the bad habits that should not be found in a king - alcoholism, promiscuous, interest in hunting, gambling, wasting money, harsh words and rough demeanor
quotes.transliteration:
pānaṃ strī mṛgayā dyūtaṃ arthadūṣaṇameva ca |
vāgdaṇḍayośca pāruṣyaṃ vyasanāni mahībhujām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अगुणस्य हतं रूपं अशीलस्य हतं कुलम् ।
असिद्धेस्तु हता विद्या अभोगस्य हतं धनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Beauty is of no use if you do not have right qualities. Being born in a good family is useless unless you develop character. If not practiced, education is of no use. If not used (enjoyed) wealth is of no use.
quotes.transliteration:
aguṇasya hataṃ rūpaṃ aśīlasya hataṃ kulam |
asiddhestu hatā vidyā abhogasya hataṃ dhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दूरस्थानामविद्यानां मोक्षधर्मानुयायिनाम् ।
शूराणां निर्धनानां च न देय कन्यका बुधैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man will not give his daughter in marriage to these people - some living very far away, an uneducated person, a man who is only interested in seeking salvation, a madly courageous person or a poor person. While they may not have any glaring shortcomings the chances of long lasting happy married life is less with each of the above grooms.
quotes.transliteration:
dūrasthānāmavidyānāṃ mokṣadharmānuyāyinām |
śūrāṇāṃ nirdhanānāṃ ca na deya kanyakā budhaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लाङ्गूलचालनमधश्चरणावपातं
भूमौ निपत्य वदनोदरदर्शनं च |
श्वा पिण्डदस्य कुरुते गजपुंगवस्तु
धीरं विलोकयति चाटुशतैश्च भुङ्क्ते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Look at the difference between a dog and an elephant. A dog shakes its tail, falls at the feet, shows a sorry face and does belly up antics in front of the person who gives it food. But an elephant maintains its composure, majestic look. On the other hand, the mahout has to cajole the elephant with hundred nice words in order to make it eat. We all have choices whether to behave like a dog or an elephant.
quotes.transliteration:
lāṅgūlacālanamadhaścaraṇāvapātaṃ
bhūmau nipatya vadanodaradarśanaṃ ca |
śvā piṇḍadasya kurute gajapuṃgavastu
dhīraṃ vilokayati cāṭuśataiśca bhuṅkte ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नापेक्षा न च दाक्षिण्यं न प्रीतिर्नच सङ्गतिः ।
तथाऽपि हरते तापं लोकानमुन्नतो घनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The mighty clouds cool the world. They do not expect anything in return. They have no obligations. They are neither friends nor have affection on any one. Yet, they help people by shielding them from hot Sun. Such is the greatness of the virtuous people.
quotes.transliteration:
nāpekṣā na ca dākṣiṇyaṃ na prītirnaca saṅgatiḥ |
tathāऽpi harate tāpaṃ lokānamunnato ghanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुत्रमित्रवसुबंधुपुरंध्री-
ग्रामधामरहिताः किल लोकाः ।
हन्त तेऽपि न भवन्ति विरक्ताः
तेन मे भवति चेतसि चित्रम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: putra mitra vasu bandhu puraM dhrI hanta te api na bhavanti viraktAH People don't have children, friends, money, relatives, wife, town that he call his own, a refuge - nothing. In spite of the situation, they can not still follow austerity.
quotes.transliteration:
putramitravasubaṃdhupuraṃdhrī-
grāmadhāmarahitāḥ kila lokāḥ |
hanta teऽpi na bhavanti viraktāḥ
tena me bhavati cetasi citram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इज्याध्ययनदानानि तपः सत्यं धृतिः क्षमा ।
अलोभ इति मार्गोऽयं धर्मस्याष्टविधः स्मृतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the eight ways to lead your life following Dharma (moral code of life): holy rituals, study, charity, penance, truth, courage, forgiveness, lack of greed.
quotes.transliteration:
ijyādhyayanadānāni tapaḥ satyaṃ dhṛtiḥ kṣamā |
alobha iti mārgoऽyaṃ dharmasyāṣṭavidhaḥ smṛtaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अत्येति रजनी यातु सा न प्रनिवर्तते ।
यत्येव यमुना पूर्णा समुद्रं लवणार्णवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Night passes. It never comes back. Yamuna also flows forward towards the sea and becomes one with the sea (salty). It will never come back (it will not become sweet again). Time also passes and never comes back.
quotes.transliteration:
atyeti rajanī yātu sā na pranivartate |
yatyeva yamunā pūrṇā samudraṃ lavaṇārṇavam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अज्ञानाद्यदि वा ज्ञानात्कृत्वा कर्म विगर्हितम् |
तस्माद्विमुक्तिमिन्विच्छन् द्वितीयं न समाचरेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One may have committed a sin by chance or intentionally. If he wants redemption from it, then he must never repeat it.
quotes.transliteration:
ajñānādyadi vā jñānātkṛtvā karma vigarhitam |
tasmādvimuktiminvicchan dvitīyaṃ na samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो न निर्गत्य निःशेषां विलोकयति मेदिनीं |
अनेकाद्भुतवृत्तांतां स नरः कूपदर्दुरः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person never gets out of his home (home town) and sees the world full of surprises, then he is to be considered as a frog in a well.
quotes.transliteration:
yo na nirgatya niḥśeṣāṃ vilokayati medinīṃ |
anekādbhutavṛttāṃtāṃ sa naraḥ kūpadarduraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
य उपेक्षेत शत्रुं स्वं प्रसरन्तं यदृच्छया ||
रोगं चालस्यसंयुक्तं स शनैस्तेन हन्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A person who ignores an enemy who is growing from strength to strength, who ignores an ailment due to laziness - both share the same fate. They will both be, in time, killed by what they ignored.
quotes.transliteration:
ya upekṣeta śatruṃ svaṃ prasarantaṃ yadṛcchayā ||
rogaṃ cālasyasaṃyuktaṃ sa śanaistena hanyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रद्धेया विप्रलब्धारः प्रिया विप्रियकारिणः |
सुदुस्त्यजाः त्यजन्त्योऽपि कामाः कष्टा हि शत्रवः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Sensory pleasures are worse than conventional enemies. They will corrupt a person who has till then lead a righteous life. If a person embraces them with love, it will destroy him in return. It will not let go of him easily even if he wants to. Indeed, traditional enemies are better.
quotes.transliteration:
śraddheyā vipralabdhāraḥ priyā vipriyakāriṇaḥ |
sudustyajāḥ tyajantyoऽpi kāmāḥ kaṣṭā hi śatravaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परद्रव्येष्वभिध्यानं मनसानिष्टचिंतनम् |
वितथाभिनिवेशश्च त्रिविधं कर्म मानसम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The following three are the sins committed by our mind (not our actions) - Longing for other people's treasures, thinking ill of others and having firm belief in the untruth.
quotes.transliteration:
paradravyeṣvabhidhyānaṃ manasāniṣṭaciṃtanam |
vitathābhiniveśaśca trividhaṃ karma mānasam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धनेन किं यो न ददाति नाश्नुते
बलेन किं येन रिपुं न भाधते ।
श्रुतेन किं येन न धर्ममाचरेत्
किमात्मना यो न जितेन्द्रियो भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is the use of wealth that is not spent either in charity or on himself? What is the use of strength that is not used to control enemies? What is the use of reading all the scriptures that does not make you follow Dharma? What is the use of knowing thyself when you can not over come your senses?
quotes.transliteration:
dhanena kiṃ yo na dadāti nāśnute
balena kiṃ yena ripuṃ na bhādhate |
śrutena kiṃ yena na dharmamācaret
kimātmanā yo na jitendriyo bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तमांसि ध्वंसन्ते परिणमति भूयानुपशमः
सकृत्संवादेऽपि प्रथत इह चामुत्र च शुभम् ।
अथ प्रत्यासङ्गः कमपि महिमानं वितरति
प्रसन्नानां वाचः फलमपरिमेयं प्रसुवते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The benefits of association with virtuous people is immense. It destroys all your bad habits. They have a calming effect on your mind. Even if they speak to you once, it will help you in this world and after life too. Their association will spread greatness to every body involved. If they happily talk to you, it will bear lot of sweet fruits.
quotes.transliteration:
tamāṃsi dhvaṃsante pariṇamati bhūyānupaśamaḥ
sakṛtsaṃvādeऽpi prathata iha cāmutra ca śubham |
atha pratyāsaṅgaḥ kamapi mahimānaṃ vitarati
prasannānāṃ vācaḥ phalamaparimeyaṃ prasuvate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वहिंसानिवृत्ता ये नराः सर्वं सहाश्च ये ।
सर्वस्याश्रयभूताश्च ते नराः स्वर्गगामिनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only those people go to heaven who give up all kinds of violence, tolerant towards every one and always give refuge to any one who seeks help.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvahiṃsānivṛttā ye narāḥ sarvaṃ sahāśca ye |
sarvasyāśrayabhūtāśca te narāḥ svargagāminaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नोपेक्षितव्यो विद्वद्भिः शत्रुरल्पोऽप्यवज्ञया |
वह्निरल्पोऽपि संवृद्धः कुरुते भस्मसाद्वनम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man must not ignore his enemies even if they are weak and small. Even if the spark is too small, it can grow into a raging fire and burn down a forest.
quotes.transliteration:
nopekṣitavyo vidvadbhiḥ śatruralpoऽpyavajñayā |
vahniralpoऽpi saṃvṛddhaḥ kurute bhasmasādvanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महद्दिः स्पर्धमानस्य विपदेव गरीयसी |
दन्तभङ्गोऽपि नागानां श्लाघ्यो गिरिविदारणे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When you are competing with the mighty, even if you face deep trouble you still have your honor. If an elephant breaks its tusk while it tries to break the mountain (by ramming into it) it still looks majestic.
quotes.transliteration:
mahaddiḥ spardhamānasya vipadeva garīyasī |
dantabhaṅgoऽpi nāgānāṃ ślāghyo girividāraṇe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उत्पाद्य पुत्राननृणांश्च कृत्वा
वृत्तिं च तेभ्योऽनुविधाय कांचित् ।
स्थाने कुमारीः प्रतिपाद्य सर्वाः
अरण्यसंस्थोऽथ मुनिर्बुभूषेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must beget children. He must make his son free from any debt. Teach him something using which he can earn some money. He must get all his daughters married and settled in good. After all that he must go to a forest and desire to become a saint.
quotes.transliteration:
utpādya putrānanṛṇāṃśca kṛtvā
vṛttiṃ ca tebhyoऽnuvidhāya kāṃcit |
sthāne kumārīḥ pratipādya sarvāḥ
araṇyasaṃsthoऽtha munirbubhūṣet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न अभिषेको न संस्कारः सिंहस्य क्रियते वने ।
विक्रमार्जित सत्वस्य स्वयमेव मृगेंद्रता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no coronation or other ceremonies to declare the Lion as the "King of the forest". Real winners do not need any such ceremonies.
quotes.transliteration:
na abhiṣeko na saṃskāraḥ siṃhasya kriyate vane |
vikramārjita satvasya svayameva mṛgeṃdratā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अक्रोधेन जयेत् क्रोधमसाधुं साधुना जयेत् |
जयेत् कदर्यं दानेन जयेत् सत्येन चानृतम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must show patience to win over anger, virtue to win over the ignoble, benevolence to win over the miserly and truth over deceit.
quotes.transliteration:
akrodhena jayet krodhamasādhuṃ sādhunā jayet |
jayet kadaryaṃ dānena jayet satyena cānṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनिर्लोडितकार्यस्य वाग्जलं वाग्मिनो वृथा ।
निमित्तादपराद्धेषोर्धानुष्कस्येव वल्गितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a job is not done after fully understanding it, it is going to fail. No amount of flowery words is going to make up for lack of preparation. An arrow that leaves the bow can not be made to hit the target by the words of the archer.
quotes.transliteration:
anirloḍitakāryasya vāgjalaṃ vāgmino vṛthā |
nimittādaparāddheṣordhānuṣkasyeva valgitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पञ्चेंद्रियस्य मर्त्यस्य छिद्रं चेदेकमिंद्रियम् ।
ततोऽस्य स्रवति प्रज्ञा दृतेः पात्रादिवोदकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Man has five sense organs. Even if he loses one of them, his mind will deteriorate - just like water in a leather bag with a hole.
quotes.transliteration:
pañceṃdriyasya martyasya chidraṃ cedekamiṃdriyam |
tatoऽsya sravati prajñā dṛteḥ pātrādivodakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकः स्वादु न भुंजीत नैकः कार्यं विचिन्तयेत् ।
एको न गच्छेदध्वानं नैकः सुप्तेषु जागृयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not eat a savory alone. One must not deliberate on important matters alone. One must not go alone on a (dangerous)path. One must not be awake when every one else is sleeping.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaḥ svādu na bhuṃjīta naikaḥ kāryaṃ vicintayet |
eko na gacchedadhvānaṃ naikaḥ supteṣu jāgṛyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रागो नाम मनश्यल्यं गुणद्रविणतस्करः |
राहुर्विद्याशशांकस्य तपोवनहुताशनः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Desire is the nail that drills into your mind and hurts it. It is the thief who steals all your good qualities. He is the Rahu who eats the moon of knowledge (your wisdom). He is the fire that burns the garden of penance.
quotes.transliteration:
rāgo nāma manaśyalyaṃ guṇadraviṇataskaraḥ |
rāhurvidyāśaśāṃkasya tapovanahutāśanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भिषज्ञ्मिथ्याचरन् दंड्यस्तिर्यक्षु प्रथमं दमम् |
मानुषे मध्यमं राजपुरुषेषूत्तमं दमम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a doctor administers wrong medicine, then he must be punished in increasing order of severity based on who he gave the medecine to - animals, common man and royalty
quotes.transliteration:
bhiṣajñmithyācaran daṃḍyastiryakṣu prathamaṃ damam |
mānuṣe madhyamaṃ rājapuruṣeṣūttamaṃ damam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाप्राप्यमभिवांछन्ति नष्टं नेच्छन्ति शोचितुम् ।
आपत्सु च न मुह्यन्ति नराः पण्डितबुद्धयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men will not long for the things that can not be attained. They do not grieve for what is lost. They are not distracted when they are in trouble.
quotes.transliteration:
nāprāpyamabhivāṃchanti naṣṭaṃ necchanti śocitum |
āpatsu ca na muhyanti narāḥ paṇḍitabuddhayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
योऽसाधुभ्योऽर्थमादाय साधुभ्यः संप्रयच्छति |
स कृत्वा प्लवमात्मानं संतारयति तावुभौ ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person takes away from the bad people and gives it to the virtuous people, then he becomes boat using which both can cross this world of misery.
quotes.transliteration:
yoऽsādhubhyoऽrthamādāya sādhubhyaḥ saṃprayacchati |
sa kṛtvā plavamātmānaṃ saṃtārayati tāvubhau ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संपदा सुस्थिरंमन्यो भवति स्वल्पयाऽपि यः |
कृतकृत्यो विधिर्मन्ये न वर्धयति तस्य ताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person is happy with little wealth, providence will assume that his job is done. Hence He will not provide him with riches. This is a view point that says one must aim for greater wealth and only then would he be able to achieve it. However, there is no mention about what kind of wealth it is. If it is the wealth of knowledge then it is good to aim for more wealth. But there are countless other verses that say it is futile to wish for more wealth in the material worldly sense.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃpadā susthiraṃmanyo bhavati svalpayāऽpi yaḥ |
kṛtakṛtyo vidhirmanye na vardhayati tasya tām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा मधु समादत्ते रक्षन् पुष्पाणि षट्पदः |
तद्वदर्थान् मनुष्येभ्यः आदद्यादविहिंसया ||
पुष्पं पुष्पं विचिन्वीत मूलच्छेदं न कारयेत् |
मालाकार इवारामे न यथांगारकारकः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must extract tax from his subjects just as painlessly as a bee extracting nectar from a flower. He must act like florist who plucks flowers but takes good care of the plants in return. He must not be like a coal miner who destroys the source of his income day by day.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā madhu samādatte rakṣan puṣpāṇi ṣaṭpadaḥ |
tadvadarthān manuṣyebhyaḥ ādadyādavihiṃsayā ||
puṣpaṃ puṣpaṃ vicinvīta mūlacchedaṃ na kārayet |
mālākāra ivārāme na yathāṃgārakārakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बालो वा यदि वा वृद्धो युवा वा गृहमागतः ।
तस्य पूजा विधातव्या सर्वत्राभ्यागतो गुरुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must treat a guest with lot o respect and affection irrespective of whether he is a child, or youth or an old person. A guest in the house should be treated with the same respect as a teacher.
quotes.transliteration:
bālo vā yadi vā vṛddho yuvā vā gṛhamāgataḥ |
tasya pūjā vidhātavyā sarvatrābhyāgato guruḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नास्ति सत्यात् परो धर्मः संतुष्टिर्नात्मजात् परा ।
नान्नदानात् परं दानं वन्दनान्नोपचारकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no grander way of following Dharma than being truthful. There is no greater joy than at seeing your child (son) born. No better charity than giving food. There is no better hospitality than paying reverence.
quotes.transliteration:
nāsti satyāt paro dharmaḥ saṃtuṣṭirnātmajāt parā |
nānnadānāt paraṃ dānaṃ vandanānnopacārakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्ने कृते यदि न सिद्ध्यति कोऽत्र दोषः
को वा न सिद्ध्यति ममेति करोति कर्मः ।
यत्नैः शुभैः पुरुषता भवतीह नृणां
दैवं विधानमनुगच्छति कार्यसिध्दिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you are trying and still not accomplishing, who is to blame? Who does work, not to accomplish? People become heroes even when they try to accomplish something noble. It is up to GOD whether those attempts success
quotes.transliteration:
yatne kṛte yadi na siddhyati koऽtra doṣaḥ
ko vā na siddhyati mameti karoti karmaḥ |
yatnaiḥ śubhaiḥ puruṣatā bhavatīha nṛṇāṃ
daivaṃ vidhānamanugacchati kāryasidhdiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
योऽरक्षन् बलिमादत्ते करं शुल्कं च पार्थिवः |
प्रतिभागं च दण्डं च स सद्यो नरकं व्रजेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king who collects varieties of taxes, tolls, shares, fines without actually protecting the subjects or their properties will surely end in hell.
quotes.transliteration:
yoऽrakṣan balimādatte karaṃ śulkaṃ ca pārthivaḥ |
pratibhāgaṃ ca daṇḍaṃ ca sa sadyo narakaṃ vrajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परमं यत्नमातिष्ठेत् स्तेनानां निग्रहे नृपः |
स्तेनानां निग्रहादस्य यशो राष्ट्रं च वर्धते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: It is one of the foremost duties of a king to nab thieves and keep them behind bars. Doing so will spread good word about him and the state. It will also increase the wealth of the state.
quotes.transliteration:
paramaṃ yatnamātiṣṭhet stenānāṃ nigrahe nṛpaḥ |
stenānāṃ nigrahādasya yaśo rāṣṭraṃ ca vardhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
युक्तः प्रमाद्यसि हितादपेतः परितप्यसे ।
यदि नेष्टात्मनः पीडा मा संजी भवता जने ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you get what you long for, you will become happy. You will feel sad when you don't get it. Since you do not like to be hurt, you must not hurt others too.
quotes.transliteration:
yuktaḥ pramādyasi hitādapetaḥ paritapyase |
yadi neṣṭātmanaḥ pīḍā mā saṃjī bhavatā jane ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपाध्यायं पितरं मातरं च
येऽभिद्रुह्यन्ते मनसा कर्मणा वा ।
तेषां पापं भ्रूणहत्याविशिष्टं
तस्मान्नान्यः पापकृदस्ति लोके ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The sin attributed to hurting teacher, father or mother - mentally or by actions is worse than killing a fetus. There is no sin more heinous than hurting those who gave you a life.
quotes.transliteration:
upādhyāyaṃ pitaraṃ mātaraṃ ca
yeऽbhidruhyante manasā karmaṇā vā |
teṣāṃ pāpaṃ bhrūṇahatyāviśiṣṭaṃ
tasmānnānyaḥ pāpakṛdasti loke ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपार्जितानामर्थानां त्याग एव हि रक्षणम् ।
तडागोदरसंस्थानां परीवाह इवांभसाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The only way one must protect the wealth accumulated is by donating a part of it to the needy. That way those who have no money will not be antagonistic towards him. A water in the tank is protected (during rainy seasons) by creating small channels that lead water out of the tank.
quotes.transliteration:
upārjitānāmarthānāṃ tyāga eva hi rakṣaṇam |
taḍāgodarasaṃsthānāṃ parīvāha ivāṃbhasām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उद्यन्तु शतमादित्या उद्यन्तु शतमिन्दवः ।
न विना विदुषां वाक्यैर्नश्यत्याभ्यंतरं तमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let hundreds of Suns rise or hundreds of moon. They still not be able to illuminate the inner darkness. Such darkness is eliminated only by preachings of a wise man.
quotes.transliteration:
udyantu śatamādityā udyantu śatamindavaḥ |
na vinā viduṣāṃ vākyairnaśyatyābhyaṃtaraṃ tamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एतवानेव लोकेऽस्मिन् पुंसां धर्मः परः स्मृतः ।
भक्तियोगो भगवति तन्नामग्रहणादिभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world there is one dharma that sets itself way above others - it is to be devoted to God by means such as chanting his name at all times. If you have him in your mind at all time, how will you commit any sin?
quotes.transliteration:
etavāneva lokeऽsmin puṃsāṃ dharmaḥ paraḥ smṛtaḥ |
bhaktiyogo bhagavati tannāmagrahaṇādibhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हंतुं देवकतनयां वसुदेवेनोद्यतायुधः कंसः |
मधुरोक्तिभिरनुनीतः साम्ना मूर्खं वशे कुर्यात् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When Kamsa lifted his sword to kill the Devaki's baby, Vasudeva placated him with pleasing words. It is the only way an angry idiot can be placated.
quotes.transliteration:
haṃtuṃ devakatanayāṃ vasudevenodyatāyudhaḥ kaṃsaḥ |
madhuroktibhiranunītaḥ sāmnā mūrkhaṃ vaśe kuryāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्वचिद्रुदंत्यच्युतचिंतया क्वचिद्
हसन्ति नंदन्ति वदन्त्यलौकिकाः ।
नृत्यन्ति गायंत्यनुशीलयंत्यजं
भवन्ति तूष्णीं परमेत्यनिर्वृताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kvachid rudanti achyuta chintayA kvachid When people realize that these worldly pleasures are all a mere illusion, they become fully engrossed in thoughts about God (Achyuta). When they are in such a state, some people cry, some laugh, revel, talk, dance, sing. In doing so, they will become one with God and attain salvation.
quotes.transliteration:
kvacidrudaṃtyacyutaciṃtayā kvacid
hasanti naṃdanti vadantyalaukikāḥ |
nṛtyanti gāyaṃtyanuśīlayaṃtyajaṃ
bhavanti tūṣṇīṃ parametyanirvṛtāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गंगादितीर्थेषु वसन्ति मत्स्याः
देवालये पक्षिगणाश्च नित्यम् ।
ते ज्ञानहीना न फलं लभन्ते
तीर्थानि देवायतनानि भावः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fishes and other beings live in river Ganga. Many birds make nests and live in temples. That does not mean that they are all enlightened. They continue to be ignorant. Holiness (river or temples) must in essence (mind) and not be attached to material things (water and buildings).
quotes.transliteration:
gaṃgāditīrtheṣu vasanti matsyāḥ
devālaye pakṣigaṇāśca nityam |
te jñānahīnā na phalaṃ labhante
tīrthāni devāyatanāni bhāvaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तस्मादेताः सदा पूज्याः भूषणाच्छादनाशनैः ।
भूतिकामैर्नरैर्नित्यं सत्कारेषूत्सवेषु च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any one who wants to prosper will keep women of the house happy by giving them ornaments, new dresses during special days (festivals) and treats them with respect.
quotes.transliteration:
tasmādetāḥ sadā pūjyāḥ bhūṣaṇācchādanāśanaiḥ |
bhūtikāmairnarairnityaṃ satkāreṣūtsaveṣu ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पण्डिते चैव मूर्खे च बलवत्यपि दुर्बले ।
ईश्वरे च दरिद्रे च मृत्योः सर्वत्र तुल्यता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: For death all are equal; scholar or an idiot, strong or weak, wealthy or poor.
quotes.transliteration:
paṇḍite caiva mūrkhe ca balavatyapi durbale |
īśvare ca daridre ca mṛtyoḥ sarvatra tulyatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्थानेषु शिष्यनिवहैर्विनियुज्यमाना
विद्या गुरुं हि गुणवत्तरमातनोति ।
आदाय शुक्तिषु बलाहकविप्रकीर्णैः
रत्नाकरो भवति वारिभिरंबुराशिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If the students make good use of the education they got from the teacher, the teacher has to be given credit for it. It only shows his greatness. If a drop of pure water from the clouds falls as rain into the sea, it is up to the sea to see that it reaches an oyster and becomes a pearl. Only because the sea is able to do this, he is also called a gem-maker.
quotes.transliteration:
sthāneṣu śiṣyanivahairviniyujyamānā
vidyā guruṃ hi guṇavattaramātanoti |
ādāya śuktiṣu balāhakaviprakīrṇaiḥ
ratnākaro bhavati vāribhiraṃburāśiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हतं ज्ञानं क्रियाशून्यं हता चाज्ञानिनः क्रिया ।
धावन्नप्यन्धको नष्टः पश्यन्नपि च पङ्गुकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Knowledge is lost when not acted up on. Action without knowledge is meaningless. Even if a blind man can run fast, it will only lead to his loss. Even if a cripple can see, it is of no use.
quotes.transliteration:
hataṃ jñānaṃ kriyāśūnyaṃ hatā cājñāninaḥ kriyā |
dhāvannapyandhako naṣṭaḥ paśyannapi ca paṅgukaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुण्येन कायः सुखितः पाण्डित्येन मनःसुखं ।
तिष्ठन् परार्थं संसारे कृपालुः केन खिद्यन्ते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: By doing good deeds when living will keep us happy physically. Wisdom keeps us happy mentally. When one lives for the sake of others, no one can make him unhappy at any time.
quotes.transliteration:
puṇyena kāyaḥ sukhitaḥ pāṇḍityena manaḥsukhaṃ |
tiṣṭhan parārthaṃ saṃsāre kṛpāluḥ kena khidyante ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनो हि हेतुः सर्वेषामिन्द्रियाणां प्रवर्तने ।
शुभाशुभास्ववस्थासु तच्च मे सुव्यवस्थितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Mind is the driver of all senses. It is the cause of all good and bad. When my mind is under my control, why should I worry? (as told by Hanumantha on seeing all the ladies in Ravana's harem in Lanka)
quotes.transliteration:
mano hi hetuḥ sarveṣāmindriyāṇāṃ pravartane |
śubhāśubhāsvavasthāsu tacca me suvyavasthitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुत्रा इव पितुर्गेहे विषये यस्य मानवाः |
निर्भया विचरिष्यन्ति स राजा रजसत्तमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He is a good king in whose kingdom the subjects are as free to roam about (physically and intellectually) as a son in his father's home.
quotes.transliteration:
putrā iva piturgehe viṣaye yasya mānavāḥ |
nirbhayā vicariṣyanti sa rājā rajasattamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रे रे रासभ वस्त्रभारवहनात् कुग्रासमश्नासि किं
राजाश्वावसथं प्रयाह्जि चणकभ्यूषान् सुखं भक्षय |
सर्वान् पुच्छवतो हयानभिवदन्त्यत्राधिकारे स्थिताः
राजा तैरुपदिष्टमेव मनुते सत्यं तटस्थाः परे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Oh Donkey! Why are you eating garbage after having carried so much weight? Go to the royal stable where they keep the horses. Don't mind the officers there. They consider anything with a tail as a horse. That place is filled with yes-men who consider the kings words to be the ultimate truth. The others (who can know right from wrong) will not mind (do not care) you eating there.
quotes.transliteration:
re re rāsabha vastrabhāravahanāt kugrāsamaśnāsi kiṃ
rājāśvāvasathaṃ prayāhji caṇakabhyūṣān sukhaṃ bhakṣaya |
sarvān pucchavato hayānabhivadantyatrādhikāre sthitāḥ
rājā tairupadiṣṭameva manute satyaṃ taṭasthāḥ pare ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वस्य हि परीक्ष्यन्ते स्वभावा नेतरे गुणाः |
अतीत्य हि गुणान्सर्वान् स्वभावो मूर्ध्नि वर्तते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Everybody has to be tested on his innate character than their acquired behavior. In case of distress (or any other pressure) it is the innate character that stands out in the open without any masks (of acquired behavior)
quotes.transliteration:
sarvasya hi parīkṣyante svabhāvā netare guṇāḥ |
atītya hi guṇānsarvān svabhāvo mūrdhni vartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तृष्णे त्वमसि रागान्धा त्रिषु स्थानेषु रज्यसि ।
व्याधितेष्वनपत्येषु जरापरिणितेषु च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh greed!! you are usually found in people who are blinded by passion. In the fore most are - the sick people, the childless and the old. These people can go to unbelievable heights to get their wish fulfilled (to get better, to conceive and to live longer)
quotes.transliteration:
tṛṣṇe tvamasi rāgāndhā triṣu sthāneṣu rajyasi |
vyādhiteṣvanapatyeṣu jarāpariṇiteṣu ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धनक्षयः शिष्टगर्हा सदाचरविवर्जनम् ।
शतृभिः पीडनं चैव द्यूतासक्तधियां नृणाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Being addicted to gambling will definitely have these effects on a person - loss of wealth, censure by virtuous people, shunning any virtuous conduct and even wrath from enemies.
quotes.transliteration:
dhanakṣayaḥ śiṣṭagarhā sadācaravivarjanam |
śatṛbhiḥ pīḍanaṃ caiva dyūtāsaktadhiyāṃ nṛṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षेषु नेच्छा मम कदाचन ।
त्वत्पादपंकजास्वादजीवितं दीयताम् मम ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh God! I am not interested in the four aspirations of mankind namely - Dharma, Wealth, Lust and salvation. All I want is to live by your lotus feet. Please grant this wish of mine.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmārthakāmamokṣeṣu necchā mama kadācana |
tvatpādapaṃkajāsvādajīvitaṃ dīyatām mama ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लशुने कुसुमाधिवासनं पिशुने सज्जनताप्रसंजनम् |
शुनि किंच शुचित्वकल्पनं न विधेरप्यधिकारगोचरम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are impossible even by order of fate - making perfumes from the flowers of garlic, making a virtuous man out of a rumor monger, teaching a dog to be clean.
quotes.transliteration:
laśune kusumādhivāsanaṃ piśune sajjanatāprasaṃjanam |
śuni kiṃca śucitvakalpanaṃ na vidherapyadhikāragocaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तच्छस्त्रैः न नागेंद्रैर्न हयैः न पदातिभिः ।
कार्यं संसिद्धिमभ्येति यथा बुद्ध्या प्रसाधितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One can be achieve a lot by using wisdom. The same is not true about weapons, elephants, horses and cavalry (army in total).
quotes.transliteration:
na tacchastraiḥ na nāgeṃdrairna hayaiḥ na padātibhiḥ |
kāryaṃ saṃsiddhimabhyeti yathā buddhyā prasādhitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अप्रणोद्योऽतिथिः सायं सूर्योढो गृहमेधिना |
काले प्राप्तस्त्वकाले वा नास्यानश्नन् गृहे वसेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When a guest arrives in the evening, one must insist they spend the night in their house. When a guest arrives during lunch (dinner) time or any odd time, he must be served food.
quotes.transliteration:
apraṇodyoऽtithiḥ sāyaṃ sūryoḍho gṛhamedhinā |
kāle prāptastvakāle vā nāsyānaśnan gṛhe vaset ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आचारः परमो धर्मः श्रुत्युक्तः स्मार्त एव च ।
तस्मादस्मिन् सदा युक्तः नित्यं स्यादात्मवान्नरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The code of life as described in the scriptures (otherwise known as dharma) is the way one must lead his life. A man must try to attain salvation by living so.
quotes.transliteration:
ācāraḥ paramo dharmaḥ śrutyuktaḥ smārta eva ca |
tasmādasmin sadā yuktaḥ nityaṃ syādātmavānnaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न कामकर्मबीजानां यस्य चेतसि संभवः ।
वासुदेवैकनिलयः स वै भागवतोत्तमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is a real devotee whose mind does neither entertain seeds of greed nor worry about the consequences of one's actions. His mind is completely occupied by thoughts about God.
quotes.transliteration:
na kāmakarmabījānāṃ yasya cetasi saṃbhavaḥ |
vāsudevaikanilayaḥ sa vai bhāgavatottamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दानं प्रियवाक्सहितं ज्ञानमगर्वं क्षमान्वितं शौर्यम् ।
त्यागसहितं च वित्तं दुर्लभमेतच्चतुष्टयं लोके ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Charity with sweet words, knowledge without pride, bravery with forgiveness, wealth with generosity - all the above four are very rare in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
dānaṃ priyavāksahitaṃ jñānamagarvaṃ kṣamānvitaṃ śauryam |
tyāgasahitaṃ ca vittaṃ durlabhametaccatuṣṭayaṃ loke ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रद्धधानः शुभां विद्यामाददीतावरादपि ।
अंत्यादपि परं धर्मं स्त्रीरत्नं दुष्कुलादपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must have good dedication even when learning from a person younger than him. One must be open to learn subtleties of Dharma from any one. One should bring a virtuous lady into home (as daughter-in-law) even if she is from bad family.
quotes.transliteration:
śraddhadhānaḥ śubhāṃ vidyāmādadītāvarādapi |
aṃtyādapi paraṃ dharmaṃ strīratnaṃ duṣkulādapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाक्षैः क्रीडेत् कदाचित्तु स्वयं नोपानहौ हरेत् ।
शयनस्थो न भुंजीतेन न पाणिस्थं न चासने ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never play at the wheels (roulette). One must never hold his own foot wear in his hands - always wear it. Do not eat while on the bed. Do not eat holding the plate in one hand. Do not eat while sitting on a chair. Most of these may not be relevant to the way we live now. But, it only goes to show how Manu Smriti detailed out the way to live in that age.
quotes.transliteration:
nākṣaiḥ krīḍet kadācittu svayaṃ nopānahau haret |
śayanastho na bhuṃjītena na pāṇisthaṃ na cāsane ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतिरूपाद् हृता सीताऽतिगर्वाद्रावणो हतः ।
अतिदानाद् बलिर्बद्धः अति सर्वत्र वर्जयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Too much beauty is what lead to the kidnapping of Sita. Ravana got killed because of too much pride. Bali's progress was curtailed because of too much charity. One should refrain from 'too much' of anything.
quotes.transliteration:
atirūpād hṛtā sītāऽtigarvādrāvaṇo hataḥ |
atidānād balirbaddhaḥ ati sarvatra varjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रीरंगे शोभते यस्य श्रीरंगे शोभते च या ।
नमोऽहं कलये तस्मै न मोहं कलये ततः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The Goddess of wealth glows in him and he glows in Sriranga (a holy city). I salute to that God. After that, my desires no longer exist.
quotes.transliteration:
śrīraṃge śobhate yasya śrīraṃge śobhate ca yā |
namoऽhaṃ kalaye tasmai na mohaṃ kalaye tataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्या ददाति विनयं विनयाददातिपात्रतां ।
पात्रत्वाद्धनमाप्नोति धनाद्धर्मं ततः सुखम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Education brings about humility in a person. Humility will make him eligible for many activities. Such activities will bring him wealth. From wealth he will be able to follow Dharma and hence attain happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
vidyā dadāti vinayaṃ vinayādadātipātratāṃ |
pātratvāddhanamāpnoti dhanāddharmaṃ tataḥ sukham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रूयतां धर्मसर्वं श्रुत्वा चैवावधार्यताम् ।
आत्मनः प्रतिकूलानि परेषां न समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must read about all the ways of Dharma. After learning about Dharma one must follow it. One must not treat others in any way he does not want to be treated.
quotes.transliteration:
śrūyatāṃ dharmasarvaṃ śrutvā caivāvadhāryatām |
ātmanaḥ pratikūlāni pareṣāṃ na samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बलादत्तं बलाद्भुक्तं बलाद्यच्चापि लेखितम् |
सर्वान् बलकृतानर्थान् अकृतान् मनुरब्रवीत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Charity, eating, writing (learning) or any other work - if done only when forced up on you, is as good as not done.
quotes.transliteration:
balādattaṃ balādbhuktaṃ balādyaccāpi lekhitam |
sarvān balakṛtānarthān akṛtān manurabravīt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असंकल्पाज्जयेत् कामं क्रोधं कामविवर्जनात् ।
अर्थानर्थेक्षया लोभं भयं तत्त्वावमर्शनात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must get over lust by teaching one self not to feel the need. By giving up lust, one can give up anger. One will give up greed once he realises that wealth is the source of so many miseries. Fear can be let go by proper understanding of philosophy.
quotes.transliteration:
asaṃkalpājjayet kāmaṃ krodhaṃ kāmavivarjanāt |
arthānarthekṣayā lobhaṃ bhayaṃ tattvāvamarśanāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुखं न कृषितोऽन्यत्र यदि धर्मेण वर्तते |
अवस्त्रत्वं निरन्नत्वं कृषितो नैव जायते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A farmer will always be happy as long as he leads his life following Dharma (moral code of life). He should respect the land and not try to exploit. If he lives like that, he will never go hungry or without clothes.
quotes.transliteration:
sukhaṃ na kṛṣitoऽnyatra yadi dharmeṇa vartate |
avastratvaṃ nirannatvaṃ kṛṣito naiva jāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मः श्रुतो वा दृष्टो वा स्मृतो वा कथितोऽपि वा ।
संमोदितो वा राजेन्द्र पुनाति पुरुषं सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh King! one attains some good karma every time he hears, sees, remembers, speaks about or follows dharma (the moral code of life).
quotes.transliteration:
dharmaḥ śruto vā dṛṣṭo vā smṛto vā kathitoऽpi vā |
saṃmodito vā rājendra punāti puruṣaṃ sadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तथासिस्तथा तीक्ष्ण सर्पो वा दुरधिष्ठितः ।
यथा क्रोधो हि जन्तूनां शरीरस्थो विनाशकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Every body knows that when you step on knife or snake in the dark, it will hurt you much. But it is not very apparent how anger with in will hurt a person.
quotes.transliteration:
na tathāsistathā tīkṣṇa sarpo vā duradhiṣṭhitaḥ |
yathā krodho hi jantūnāṃ śarīrastho vināśakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नृणां जन्मसहस्रेण भक्तौ प्रीतिर्हि जायते ।
कलौ भक्तिः कलौ भक्तिः भक्त्या कृष्णः पुरः स्थितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! King, after thousands of births will one get to understand the true meaning of devotion towards God. Devotion alone is the means to please him in the epoch of Kali. Only through devotion can one see Krishna standing in front of them.
quotes.transliteration:
nṛṇāṃ janmasahasreṇa bhaktau prītirhi jāyate |
kalau bhaktiḥ kalau bhaktiḥ bhaktyā kṛṣṇaḥ puraḥ sthitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तेन वृद्धो भवति येनास्य पलितं शिरः ।
यो वै युवाप्यधीयानस्तं देवाः स्थविरं विदुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is not considered old (and wise) just because he has gray hair. One must be well educated to be considered so. The Gods consider even a young man as matured if he is well read and educated.
quotes.transliteration:
na tena vṛddho bhavati yenāsya palitaṃ śiraḥ |
yo vai yuvāpyadhīyānastaṃ devāḥ sthaviraṃ viduḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पानं दुर्जनसंसर्गः पत्या च विरहोऽटनम् |
स्वप्नमन्यगृहे वासः नारीणां दूषणानि षट् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Habitual drinking (of alcohol), friendship with bad people, living away from her husband, wandering aimlessly, sleeping or living in some one else's home - these are the six bad habits that are forbidden for women.
quotes.transliteration:
pānaṃ durjanasaṃsargaḥ patyā ca virahoऽṭanam |
svapnamanyagṛhe vāsaḥ nārīṇāṃ dūṣaṇāni ṣaṭ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सभा वा न प्रविष्टव्यं वक्तव्यं वा समंजसम् |
अब्रुवन् विब्रुवन् वाऽपि नरो भवति किल्बिषी ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not go to a gathering without proper preparation. Even when one does, one should not speak inappropriately. It does not matter whether your bad words were intentional or accidental. You will be guilty.
quotes.transliteration:
sabhā vā na praviṣṭavyaṃ vaktavyaṃ vā samaṃjasam |
abruvan vibruvan vāऽpi naro bhavati kilbiṣī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थी करोति दैन्यं लब्धार्थो गर्वमपरितोषं च ।
नष्टधनोऽस्ति सशोकः सुखमास्ते निःस्पृहः पुरुषः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who want wealth and not have it will make themselves pitiable in order to get it from others. Those who have wealth become restless and unhappy because they want more. Those who lose wealth will drown in grief. If you want to be happy, one must not long for wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
arthī karoti dainyaṃ labdhārtho garvamaparitoṣaṃ ca |
naṣṭadhanoऽsti saśokaḥ sukhamāste niḥspṛhaḥ puruṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आपद्युन्मार्गगमने कार्यकालात्ययेषु च ।
अपृष्टेनापि वक्तव्यं भृत्येन हितमिच्छता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When the master is going down a dangerous path, or not done any work that is long pending a loyal servant must tell him otherwise, He must do this even when he is not spoken to.
quotes.transliteration:
āpadyunmārgagamane kāryakālātyayeṣu ca |
apṛṣṭenāpi vaktavyaṃ bhṛtyena hitamicchatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
का दुर्लभा नराणां हरिभक्तिः, पातकं च किं हिंसा ।
को हि भगवत्प्रियः स्यात् योऽन्यं नोद्वेजयेदनुद्विग्नः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is very rare among people? Devotion towards God. What is sin? any form of cruelty. Who does God like? He who does not get anxious himself as well as not cause others be anxious.
quotes.transliteration:
kā durlabhā narāṇāṃ haribhaktiḥ, pātakaṃ ca kiṃ hiṃsā |
ko hi bhagavatpriyaḥ syāt yoऽnyaṃ nodvejayedanudvignaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वपुः कुब्जीभूतं गतिरपि तथा यष्टिशरणा
विशीर्णा दंतालिः श्रवणविकलं श्रोत्रयुगलम् ।
शिरः शुक्लं चक्षुस्तिमिरपटलैरावृतमहो
मनो मे निर्लज्जं तदपि विषयेभ्यः स्पृहयति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: My body is shrinked. I need a stick to move aroung. My teeth are all gone. Both the ears have stopped hearing. My hair has turned white. Darkness covers my eyes. But my mind is still shameless. It is still longing for sensory pleasures.
quotes.transliteration:
vapuḥ kubjībhūtaṃ gatirapi tathā yaṣṭiśaraṇā
viśīrṇā daṃtāliḥ śravaṇavikalaṃ śrotrayugalam |
śiraḥ śuklaṃ cakṣustimirapaṭalairāvṛtamaho
mano me nirlajjaṃ tadapi viṣayebhyaḥ spṛhayati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अविद्यमानोऽप्यवभाति हि द्वयो
ध्यातुर्धिया स्वप्नमनोरथौ यथा ।
तत्कर्म संकल्पविकल्पकं मनो
बुधो निरुन्ध्यादभयं ततः स्यात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: avidyamAno api avabhAti hi dvayo tat karma saMkalpa vikalpakam mano Even though there is only one truth, it may appear as many. Just like a person who sits to meditate sees dreams and wishes as separate even though they are the same. Hence one must contain the mind that thinks in both directions - positive and negative. Only then can one be free from fear.
quotes.transliteration:
avidyamānoऽpyavabhāti hi dvayo
dhyāturdhiyā svapnamanorathau yathā |
tatkarma saṃkalpavikalpakaṃ mano
budho nirundhyādabhayaṃ tataḥ syāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुष्कैः काष्ठैर्भवेत् कार्यंं लोष्टैरपि च पांसुभिः |
न तु स्थानात् परिभ्रष्टैः कार्यं स्यावसुधाधिपैः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There is use for dry twigs fallen from a tree. There are uses for lump of mud, or just dust. But a king who has been displaced from his throne is utterly and completely useless.
quotes.transliteration:
śuṣkaiḥ kāṣṭhairbhavet kāryaṃṃ loṣṭairapi ca pāṃsubhiḥ |
na tu sthānāt paribhraṣṭaiḥ kāryaṃ syāvasudhādhipaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्तु संचरते देशान् यस्तु सेवेत पण्डितान् ।
तस्य विस्तारिता बुद्धिः तैलबिंदुरिवांभसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who wanders various countries and serves wise men there will expand his knowledge just like oil on water.
quotes.transliteration:
yastu saṃcarate deśān yastu seveta paṇḍitān |
tasya vistāritā buddhiḥ tailabiṃdurivāṃbhasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यावंतो विषयाः प्रेष्ठास्त्रिलोक्यामजितेंद्रियम् ।
न शक्नुवन्ति ते सर्वे प्रतिपूरयितुं नृप ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh King, if a person has not conquered his senses - then all the pleasures from all three worlds can not satiate him.
quotes.transliteration:
yāvaṃto viṣayāḥ preṣṭhāstrilokyāmajiteṃdriyam |
na śaknuvanti te sarve pratipūrayituṃ nṛpa ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रज्ञाविवेकं लभते विभिन्नागमदर्शनैः ।
कियद्वा शक्यमुन्नेतुं स्वयत्नमनुधावता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Presence of mind and wisdom can be attained by studying various scriptures on rituals and contemplation. How much can one learn solely on his experiences?
quotes.transliteration:
prajñāvivekaṃ labhate vibhinnāgamadarśanaiḥ |
kiyadvā śakyamunnetuṃ svayatnamanudhāvatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न हीदृशं संवननं त्रिषु लोकेषु विद्यते ।
दया मैत्री च भूतेषु दानं च मधुरा च वाक् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There can not be better qualities found in the beings of all three worlds than these - compassion, friendship, selfless charity and sweet words.
quotes.transliteration:
na hīdṛśaṃ saṃvananaṃ triṣu lokeṣu vidyate |
dayā maitrī ca bhūteṣu dānaṃ ca madhurā ca vāk ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य नास्ति स्वयं प्रज्ञा केवलं तु बहुश्रुतः ।
न स जानाति शास्त्रार्थं दर्वी सूपरसानिव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One can not be called a wise man if he does not understand himself. He can only be called well read. He can not understand the deeper meanings of any scriptures - just like the spoon does not know the taste of the dish.
quotes.transliteration:
yasya nāsti svayaṃ prajñā kevalaṃ tu bahuśrutaḥ |
na sa jānāti śāstrārthaṃ darvī sūparasāniva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भये वा यदि वा हर्षे संप्राप्ते यो विमर्शयेत् ।
कृत्यं न कुरुते वेगान्न स संतापमाप्नुयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He will always prosper who pauses to analyze when ever he feels fear as well as happiness. Such people will never hurry any of their work.
quotes.transliteration:
bhaye vā yadi vā harṣe saṃprāpte yo vimarśayet |
kṛtyaṃ na kurute vegānna sa saṃtāpamāpnuyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आयुः श्रियं यशो धर्मं लोकानाशिष एव च ।
हन्ति श्रेयांसि सर्वाणि पुंसो महदतिक्रमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a man insults a virtuous person, then he will see it all decline - his lifespan, wealth, fame, dharma, his world and all his good karma.
quotes.transliteration:
āyuḥ śriyaṃ yaśo dharmaṃ lokānāśiṣa eva ca |
hanti śreyāṃsi sarvāṇi puṃso mahadatikramaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न साहसैकान्तरसानुवर्तिना
न चायुपायोपहतान्तरात्मना ।
विभूतयः शक्यमवाप्तुमूर्जिताः
नये च शौर्ये च वसन्ति संपदः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not possible to accomplish great things by people who rely on courage alone, or intellect alone. Prosperity will be attained by those who can exhibit both these traits.
quotes.transliteration:
na sāhasaikāntarasānuvartinā
na cāyupāyopahatāntarātmanā |
vibhūtayaḥ śakyamavāptumūrjitāḥ
naye ca śaurye ca vasanti saṃpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बलवानपि कोपजन्मनः
तमसो नाभिभवं रुणद्धि यः |
क्ष्यपक्ष इवैंदवीः कलाः
सकला हन्ति स शक्तिसंपदः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must destroy the darkness that emanates from anger as early as possible. If unchecked, it will destroy everything. The darks spots on the moon will engulf the entire moon during the waning phase.
quotes.transliteration:
balavānapi kopajanmanaḥ
tamaso nābhibhavaṃ ruṇaddhi yaḥ |
kṣyapakṣa ivaiṃdavīḥ kalāḥ
sakalā hanti sa śaktisaṃpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अश्वं नैव गजं नैव व्याघ्रं नैव च ।
अजापुत्रं बलिं दधात् देवो दुर्बलघातकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Not a horse, neither elephant, nor a tiger - it is a goat that is offered as a sacrifice. Seems like even god does not spare the weak.
quotes.transliteration:
aśvaṃ naiva gajaṃ naiva vyāghraṃ naiva ca |
ajāputraṃ baliṃ dadhāt devo durbalaghātakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्तोतारः के भविष्यन्ति मूर्खस्य जगतीतले |
न स्तौति चेत् स्वयं च स्वं कदा तस्यास्तु निवृतिः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a fool does not priase himself, who else will do it for him in this entire world? Praising himself is the only pass time that he knows about.
quotes.transliteration:
stotāraḥ ke bhaviṣyanti mūrkhasya jagatītale |
na stauti cet svayaṃ ca svaṃ kadā tasyāstu nivṛtiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मानाद्वा यदि वा लोभात् क्रोधाद्वा यदि वा भयात् ।
यो न्यायमन्यथा ब्रूते स याति नरकं नरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person (judge) gives bad judgment due to prejudice, greed or fear he will go to hell.
quotes.transliteration:
mānādvā yadi vā lobhāt krodhādvā yadi vā bhayāt |
yo nyāyamanyathā brūte sa yāti narakaṃ naraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्वद्भिः सेवितः सद्भिः नित्यमद्वेषरागिभिः ।
हृदयेनाभ्यनुज्ञातो यो धर्मस्तं निभोधत ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Please listen. I will tell you the essence of Dharma - moral code of life. This is what has been followed by the wise men after thinking long and hard without prejudice. This is what has kept their conscience clean.
quotes.transliteration:
vidvadbhiḥ sevitaḥ sadbhiḥ nityamadveṣarāgibhiḥ |
hṛdayenābhyanujñāto yo dharmastaṃ nibhodhata ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अलं वृतैरलं तीर्थैरलं योगैरलं मखैः ।
अलं ज्ञानकथालापैर्भक्तिरेकैव मुक्तिदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Nothing is going to be benefited from doing ritualistic fasting, visiting holy places, performing various kinds of yoga, performing yajnas (holy pyres), listening to moral stories. Absolute devotion is the only way to attain salvation.
quotes.transliteration:
alaṃ vṛtairalaṃ tīrthairalaṃ yogairalaṃ makhaiḥ |
alaṃ jñānakathālāpairbhaktirekaiva muktidā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यानि मिथ्याभिशस्तानां पतंत्यस्राणि राघव |
तानि पुत्रपशून् घ्नंति प्रीत्यर्थमनुशासतः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Bharatha! If a king rules a kingdom for his own sake then his subjects will suffer. Tears from such subjects will destroy the kings wealth, progeny (sons) and husbandry (cattle, horses and others).
quotes.transliteration:
yāni mithyābhiśastānāṃ pataṃtyasrāṇi rāghava |
tāni putrapaśūn ghnaṃti prītyarthamanuśāsataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वैर्गुणैर्विहीनोऽपि वीर्यवान् हि तरेद्रिपून् |
सर्वैरपि गुणैर्युक्तो निर्वीर्यः किं करिष्यति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If one does not have lofty virtues but is courageous, he can conquer his enemies. What good is all the virtues if one does not have the courage to confront and conquer your adversaries?
quotes.transliteration:
sarvairguṇairvihīnoऽpi vīryavān hi taredripūn |
sarvairapi guṇairyukto nirvīryaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संभूय वणिजां पण्यं अनर्घेणोपरुन्धताम् |
विक्रीणतां वा विहितो दन्ड उत्तम साहसः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If all the vendors get together and hoard merchandise to create artificial scarcity so that they can sell them at higher price, then all of them must be punished.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃbhūya vaṇijāṃ paṇyaṃ anargheṇoparundhatām |
vikrīṇatāṃ vā vihito danḍa uttama sāhasaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आसने शयने याने पानभोजनवस्तुषु |
दृष्टादृष्टप्रमत्तेषु प्रहरन्त्यरयोऽरिषु ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Enemies can strike you at any time - sitting, sleeping, traveling, eating, drinking, lost in thought about material and philosophical issues. Hence one must never let down his guard.
quotes.transliteration:
āsane śayane yāne pānabhojanavastuṣu |
dṛṣṭādṛṣṭapramatteṣu praharantyarayoऽriṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुनरपि जननं पुनरपि मरणं
पुनरपि जननीजठरे शयनम् ।
इह संसारे बहु दुस्तारे
कृपयाऽपारे पाहि मुरारे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: punarapi jananam punarapi maraNam iha samsAre bahu dustAre To be born again, to die again, to sleep once again in the womb of the mother - this is the rule of the world. There is lot of misery in this world. Oh Murari, please release me from this cycle.
quotes.transliteration:
punarapi jananaṃ punarapi maraṇaṃ
punarapi jananījaṭhare śayanam |
iha saṃsāre bahu dustāre
kṛpayāऽpāre pāhi murāre ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अत्यंतमतिमेधावी त्रयाणां एकमश्नुते ।
अल्पायुर्वा दरिद्रो वा ह्यनपत्यो न संशयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A very highly intellectual scholar will have at least one of the below three misfortunes - short life, or poverty, or childless. Fate some how ensures that his wisdom is not carried forward to future generations.
quotes.transliteration:
atyaṃtamatimedhāvī trayāṇāṃ ekamaśnute |
alpāyurvā daridro vā hyanapatyo na saṃśayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शरणं किं प्रपन्नानि विषवन्मारयन्ति वा |
न त्यज्यन्ते न भुज्यन्ते कृपणेन धनानि यत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A miser does not let go of his money. He will not spend it. He will not give it away in charity. Has he conquered wealth? Or wealth gotten into him like poison and does not let go of him until he is dead?
quotes.transliteration:
śaraṇaṃ kiṃ prapannāni viṣavanmārayanti vā |
na tyajyante na bhujyante kṛpaṇena dhanāni yat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृतैः संप्राप्यते स्वर्गो जीवद्भिः कीर्तिरुत्तमा |
तदुभावपि शूराणां गुणावेतौ सुदुर्लभौ ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Speaking about soldiers fighting a war - the dead attain heaven. Those who live will enjoy fame. Brave courageous men will enjoy both. It is very difficult for common man to attain both.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtaiḥ saṃprāpyate svargo jīvadbhiḥ kīrtiruttamā |
tadubhāvapi śūrāṇāṃ guṇāvetau sudurlabhau ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दारिद्र्येण कुलं मदेन कुशलं द्वेषेण विद्याफलं
शीलं दुर्जनसंगेमेन मलिनाचारेण शुक्लं यशः ।
आलस्येन धनं प्रयाति निधनं लौल्येन मानोन्नतिः
याज्ञादैन्यपरिग्रहेण च गुणग्रामः समग्रो नृणाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Misery hurts the family. Intoxication destroys well-being. The fruits of education are spoiled by hatred. Character is spoiled by having friendship with rogues. By doing sinful acts one destroys his fame. Laziness makes you lose wealth. Greed destroys honor. But beggary destroys all qualities that a man has.
quotes.transliteration:
dāridryeṇa kulaṃ madena kuśalaṃ dveṣeṇa vidyāphalaṃ
śīlaṃ durjanasaṃgemena malinācāreṇa śuklaṃ yaśaḥ |
ālasyena dhanaṃ prayāti nidhanaṃ laulyena mānonnatiḥ
yājñādainyaparigraheṇa ca guṇagrāmaḥ samagro nṛṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्रयी सांख्यं योगः पशुपतिमतं वैष्णवमिति
प्रभिन्ने प्रस्थाने परमिदमदः पथ्यमिति च ।
रुचीनां वैचित्र्यात् ऋजुकुटिलनानापथजुषां
नृणामेको गम्यस्त्वमसि पयसामर्णव इव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh God! there are many scriptures like vedas, saankhya and yoga. People read them and form their own philosophies. They think that their way is the right way and others are wrong. They call one method as straight and others are crooked. But all the paths actually lead to you only. Just like water from all rivers reach the sea following their own paths.
quotes.transliteration:
trayī sāṃkhyaṃ yogaḥ paśupatimataṃ vaiṣṇavamiti
prabhinne prasthāne paramidamadaḥ pathyamiti ca |
rucīnāṃ vaicitryāt ṛjukuṭilanānāpathajuṣāṃ
nṛṇāmeko gamyastvamasi payasāmarṇava iva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राज्ञि धर्मिणि धर्मिष्ठाः पापे पापपराः सदा |
राजानमनुवर्तन्ते यथा राजा तथा प्रजाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The subjects will be as righteous as the king himself. If the king is a sinner, so will be the subjects. People always follow their king. Like King, like subjects.
quotes.transliteration:
rājñi dharmiṇi dharmiṣṭhāḥ pāpe pāpaparāḥ sadā |
rājānamanuvartante yathā rājā tathā prajāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तुल्येऽपराधे स्वर्भानुर्भानुमंतं चिरेण यत् ।
हिमांशुमाशुग्रासते तन्म्रदिम्नःस्फुटं फलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Mythology believes eclipses are means of punishing the sun and the moon for some sins they committed long ago. Both committed the same sin. But, solar eclipses are much rarer than lunar eclipse. It clearly shows what people get if they are soft.
quotes.transliteration:
tulyeऽparādhe svarbhānurbhānumaṃtaṃ cireṇa yat |
himāṃśumāśugrāsate tanmradimnaḥsphuṭaṃ phalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः समुत्पतितं क्रोधं क्षमयैव निरस्यति ।
यथोरगस्त्वचं जीर्णां स वै पुरुष उच्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A snake grows out of its skin, thereby shedding it. He alone can be called a complete man who can grow out of his anger using his patience.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ samutpatitaṃ krodhaṃ kṣamayaiva nirasyati |
yathoragastvacaṃ jīrṇāṃ sa vai puruṣa ucyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मन्दोऽप्यमन्दतामेति संसर्गेण विपश्चितः |
पङ्कच्छिदः फलस्येव निकषेणाविलं पयः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a fool can learn some thing (and attain greatness) if he spends some time with the virtuous wise men. Even muddy water becomes clear when it comes in contact with a particular fruit - Strychnos Potatorum (that acts like a coagulant).
quotes.transliteration:
mandoऽpyamandatāmeti saṃsargeṇa vipaścitaḥ |
paṅkacchidaḥ phalasyeva nikaṣeṇāvilaṃ payaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्त्वे पुलीनाः स्वर्यान्ति नरलोकं तु राजसाः ।
तमोलयास्तु निरयं यान्ति मामेव निर्गुणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The people who have sattva guna (good qualities) will attain heaven. The people who have rajas guna (enjoying pleasures) will get the earth. The people who have tamas guna (demonic qualities). He who does not have any quality (does not care for qualities), will attain me (supreme being).
quotes.transliteration:
sattve pulīnāḥ svaryānti naralokaṃ tu rājasāḥ |
tamolayāstu nirayaṃ yānti māmeva nirguṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शर्करास्वादमत्तेन मक्षिका चेदुपेक्ष्यते |
साकं प्रविश्य जठरं विपत्तिं तनुते न किम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In a hurry to taste the sugar, if you ignore the fly sitting on top of it - it is bound to create trouble in your stomach. Hence one must not hurry at the sight of anything pleasant. One must be patient.
quotes.transliteration:
śarkarāsvādamattena makṣikā cedupekṣyate |
sākaṃ praviśya jaṭharaṃ vipattiṃ tanute na kim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कदाचित् प्रसादे क्वचिदपि च सौधेषु धनिनां
कदाचित् शैलेषु क्वचिदपि च कूलेषु सरिताम् ।
कुटीरे दान्तानां मुनिजनवराणामपि वसन्
मुनिर्न व्यामोहं भजति गुरुदीक्षाक्षततमाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: They live sometimes in the palace of a king, in the houses of rich people, on mountain top, on river banks with cool water by their side, among the huts of other mendicants and saints. In spite of seeing so much the minds of the saints are unmoved by the comforts and luxury. This must be due to the learnings on detachment they got from their teachers.
quotes.transliteration:
kadācit prasāde kvacidapi ca saudheṣu dhanināṃ
kadācit śaileṣu kvacidapi ca kūleṣu saritām |
kuṭīre dāntānāṃ munijanavarāṇāmapi vasan
munirna vyāmohaṃ bhajati gurudīkṣākṣatatamāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्याज्यं धैर्यं विधुरेऽपि काले
धैर्यात्कदाचिद्गतिमाप्नुयात्सः ।
यथा समुद्रेऽपि च पोतभङ्गे
सांयात्रिको वांछति तर्तुमेव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when one is faced with adversity, he must not abandon courage. If one has courage, he will one day meet a good end. Even when a ship is sinking, the sailors still look forward to being saved (or swim to the shore).
quotes.transliteration:
tyājyaṃ dhairyaṃ vidhureऽpi kāle
dhairyātkadācidgatimāpnuyātsaḥ |
yathā samudreऽpi ca potabhaṅge
sāṃyātriko vāṃchati tartumeva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकं विषरसो हन्ति शस्त्रेणैकश्च बाध्यते ।
सराष्ट्रं सप्रजं हन्ति राजानं मन्त्रविस्रवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Poison could kill one person. You could kill only one person using a weapon. But using a weapon of thought, one can destroy not just the king, but an entire kingdom including the state and its subjects.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaṃ viṣaraso hanti śastreṇaikaśca bādhyate |
sarāṣṭraṃ saprajaṃ hanti rājānaṃ mantravisravaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
युज्यते वेष्टनं वायोः आकाशस्य च खंडनम् ।
ग्रथनं तरंगाणां आस्था नायुषि युज्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One may be able to surround and lock air, or cut the sky into pieces, or weave the waves into a chain. But one can not put any trust in your longevity. You cannot do anything to be assured to live longer.
quotes.transliteration:
yujyate veṣṭanaṃ vāyoḥ ākāśasya ca khaṃḍanam |
grathanaṃ taraṃgāṇāṃ āsthā nāyuṣi yujyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भवन्ति नरकाः पापात् पापं दारिद्र्यसंभवम् ।
दारिद्र्यमप्रदानेन तस्माद्दानपरो भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one commits sin, he goes to hell. Sin is committed by people usually to escape poverty. Poverty is the result of greed because of which people do not give money to others. Hence one must generously give money to the needy (to indirectly prevent going to hell).
quotes.transliteration:
bhavanti narakāḥ pāpāt pāpaṃ dāridryasaṃbhavam |
dāridryamapradānena tasmāddānaparo bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्ररोहिशाखिनां शाखास्कन्दसर्वविदारणे |
उपजीव्यद्रुमाणां च विंशतेर्द्विगुणो दमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must be punished severely if he cuts the branches and trunk of a tree that was growing well (freely and happily). The punishment must be doubled if the tree was being useful to people.
quotes.transliteration:
prarohiśākhināṃ śākhāskandasarvavidāraṇe |
upajīvyadrumāṇāṃ ca viṃśaterdviguṇo damaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मात्वं गिरिजा मतिः परिजनाः प्राणाः शरीरं गृहं
पूजा ते विषयोपभोगरचना निद्रासमाधिस्थितिः ।
संचारस्तु पदोः प्रदक्षिणविधिः स्तोत्राणि सर्वा गिरो
यद्यत्कर्म करोमि तत्तदखिलं शंभो तवाराधनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Shambu! you are my soul. Goddess Girija is my mind. The other Gods in your court are my life. My body is a temple. My indulgence in sensory pleasures are ways of me worshiping you. My sleep is meditation. Everywhere I walk, I think of it as circling you. Every word I utter is a way of praising you. What ever I do is part of your worship.
quotes.transliteration:
ātmātvaṃ girijā matiḥ parijanāḥ prāṇāḥ śarīraṃ gṛhaṃ
pūjā te viṣayopabhogaracanā nidrāsamādhisthitiḥ |
saṃcārastu padoḥ pradakṣiṇavidhiḥ stotrāṇi sarvā giro
yadyatkarma karomi tattadakhilaṃ śaṃbho tavārādhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न नर्मयुक्तं वचनं हिनस्ति
न स्त्रीषु राजन् न विवाहकाले ।
प्राणात्यये सर्वधनापहारे
पंचानृतान्याहुरपातकानि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a lie is told as a joke (to invoke happiness) it does not attract sin. There are four more circumstances in life when telling a lie does not attract sin. They are - when you are with a woman, during a wedding (so that a wedding may go on smoothly), when your life is in danger or when all your wealth is being stolen.
quotes.transliteration:
na narmayuktaṃ vacanaṃ hinasti
na strīṣu rājan na vivāhakāle |
prāṇātyaye sarvadhanāpahāre
paṃcānṛtānyāhurapātakāni ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तीक्ष्णमल्पदातारः प्रमत्तं गर्वतं शठम् |
व्यसने सर्वभूतानि नाभिधावन्ति पार्थिवम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king is harsh, miserly, eccentric, pompous, deceiving then none of his subjects will come to help him if he is in trouble.
quotes.transliteration:
tīkṣṇamalpadātāraḥ pramattaṃ garvataṃ śaṭham |
vyasane sarvabhūtāni nābhidhāvanti pārthivam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धृतिः क्षमा दमोऽस्तेयं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः ।
धीर्विद्या सत्यमक्रोधो दशकं धर्मलक्षणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These are the ten qualities of Dharma (moral code of life): Courage, forgiveness, austerity, never stealing, cleanliness, control of senses, a balanced mind, good education, truth, being never angry.
quotes.transliteration:
dhṛtiḥ kṣamā damoऽsteyaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ |
dhīrvidyā satyamakrodho daśakaṃ dharmalakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न मित्रकारणाद्राजा विपुलाद्वा धनागमात् |
समुत्सृजेत्साहसिकान् सर्वभूतभयावहान् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must always punish people who cause fear in other people using their friends (influence), lot of money, ill gotten wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
na mitrakāraṇādrājā vipulādvā dhanāgamāt |
samutsṛjetsāhasikān sarvabhūtabhayāvahān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वयसः कर्मणोऽर्थस्य श्रुतस्याभिजनस्य ।
वेषवाग्वृत्ति सारूप्यमाचरन्विचरेदिह ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must dress, talk and conduct himself according to his age, occupation, wealth, education and family. That is the thinking and order of this world.
quotes.transliteration:
vayasaḥ karmaṇoऽrthasya śrutasyābhijanasya |
veṣavāgvṛtti sārūpyamācaranvicarediha ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृगयाक्षो दिवास्वप्नः परिवादः स्त्रियो मदः |
तौर्यत्रिकं वृथाट्य च कामजो दशको गणः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the ten types of sin a person commits due to his desires - hunting, gambling, sleeping during day (laziness), flirting with women, intoxication, triple symphony of song, dance, and instrumental music and wandering aimlessly.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛgayākṣo divāsvapnaḥ parivādaḥ striyo madaḥ |
tauryatrikaṃ vṛthāṭya ca kāmajo daśako gaṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परं कूपशताद्वापि परं वापीशतात्कृतुः |
परं कृतुशतात् पुत्रः सत्यं पुत्रशतात् परम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A lake is more useful than a hundred wells. If we do not have the right apparatus to get the water out, a well is useless. Doing a Yajna (rituals) is more useful than finding hundred lakes. By performing Yajna one can get pure water in the form of rain. Having a son is better than performing hundred Yajnas. He shall perform many more Yajnas for you and help you accumulate good Karma. Always acting truthfully is better than all of them since it will eliminate the need for any of them.
quotes.transliteration:
paraṃ kūpaśatādvāpi paraṃ vāpīśatātkṛtuḥ |
paraṃ kṛtuśatāt putraḥ satyaṃ putraśatāt param ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अन्यथा परमं तत्त्वं जनाः क्लिश्यन्ति चान्यथा ।
अन्यथा शास्त्रसद्भावो व्याख्यां कुर्वन्ति चान्यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The ultimate truth says one thing. People go in search of other and get worked up. The scriptures say one thing and the discourses say another.
quotes.transliteration:
anyathā paramaṃ tattvaṃ janāḥ kliśyanti cānyathā |
anyathā śāstrasadbhāvo vyākhyāṃ kurvanti cānyathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुक्षेत्रे वापयेद्बीजं सुपुत्रे दापयेद्धनम् |
सुक्षेत्रे च सुपुत्रे च यत्क्षिप्रं नैव नश्यति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A farmer must sow seeds in a fertile land. A father must entrust money to an able son. Seed or money when handed over to able recipients will always prosper
quotes.transliteration:
sukṣetre vāpayedbījaṃ suputre dāpayeddhanam |
sukṣetre ca suputre ca yatkṣipraṃ naiva naśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जातमात्रं न यः शत्रुं व्याधिं च प्रशमं नयेत् |
अतिपुष्टांगयुक्तोऽपि स पश्चात्तेन हन्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Be it a disease or an enemy - it must be put out when it first notices. If it is not done, even if you have the strongest body (and a strong army) you will be killed by the enemy (or disease)
quotes.transliteration:
jātamātraṃ na yaḥ śatruṃ vyādhiṃ ca praśamaṃ nayet |
atipuṣṭāṃgayuktoऽpi sa paścāttena hanyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संप्रसन्ने भगवति पुरुषः प्राकृतैर्गुणैः ।
विमुक्तो जीवनिर्मुक्तो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only if the God is pleased, then man shall leave his innate qualities and attain salvation and become one with the universe.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃprasanne bhagavati puruṣaḥ prākṛtairguṇaiḥ |
vimukto jīvanirmukto brahmanirvāṇamṛcchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नास्ति विद्यासमं चक्षुर्नास्ति सत्यसमं तपः ।
नास्ति रागसमं दुःखं नास्ति त्यागसमं सुखम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no better eye than education. No better penance than be honest. No worse grief than greed. No greater happiness than in sacrifice (giving).
quotes.transliteration:
nāsti vidyāsamaṃ cakṣurnāsti satyasamaṃ tapaḥ |
nāsti rāgasamaṃ duḥkhaṃ nāsti tyāgasamaṃ sukham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नृणां निःश्रेयसार्थाय व्यक्तिर्भगवतो नृप ।
अव्ययस्याप्रमेयस्य निर्गुणस्य गुणात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king should be working selflessly only with the best interest of the subjects in his mind. He has to behave as if people can see indestructible, immeasurable, perfect (without any bad qualities) God through him.
quotes.transliteration:
nṛṇāṃ niḥśreyasārthāya vyaktirbhagavato nṛpa |
avyayasyāprameyasya nirguṇasya guṇātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शब्दादिभिः पंचभिरेव पंचपंचत्वमापुः स्वगुणेन बद्धाः ।
कुरंग मातंग पतंग मीन भृंगा नरः पंचभिरंचितः किम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The five senses are sound, touch, sight, taste and smell. Animals that are slaves of any one of them often pay the price by their life. A deer follows the sound (of its calf) and becomes a prey. An elephant is enslaved by its vulnerability of touch behind the head. A moth is driven to the flame by its sight. A fish is baited by its longing for taste. A bee loses its life when it gets trapped in the lotus which attracted it by smell. What can now be told of a man who is driven by all the five senses?
quotes.transliteration:
śabdādibhiḥ paṃcabhireva paṃcapaṃcatvamāpuḥ svaguṇena baddhāḥ |
kuraṃga mātaṃga pataṃga mīna bhṛṃgā naraḥ paṃcabhiraṃcitaḥ kim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एतावानव्ययो धर्मः पुण्यश्लोकैरुपासितः ।
यो भूतशोकहर्षाभ्यां आत्मा शोचति हर्षति च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharma will be kept alive for ever by the virtuous people. These people find their happiness and grief in the happiness and grief of the world.
quotes.transliteration:
etāvānavyayo dharmaḥ puṇyaślokairupāsitaḥ |
yo bhūtaśokaharṣābhyāṃ ātmā śocati harṣati ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तुङ्गत्वमितरा नाद्रौ नेदं सिंढावगाधता ।
अलङ्घनीयताहेतुरुभयं तन्मनस्विनि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A mountain is very high, but is not deep. An ocean is very deep, but not high. Even if you have one such quality (high or deep), it becomes very difficult for people to overcome you. People who are mentally strong have both of them. They thoughts can be very high (in ideals) at the same time being deep (in sensitivity).
quotes.transliteration:
tuṅgatvamitarā nādrau nedaṃ siṃḍhāvagādhatā |
alaṅghanīyatāheturubhayaṃ tanmanasvini ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पैशुन्यं साहसं द्रोह ईर्ष्यासूयार्थदूषणम् |
वाग्दण्डजं च पौरुष्यं क्रोधजोऽपि गणोष्टकः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Calumny, haste, deceit, jealousy, greed, insincerity while dealing with money, attacking people, hurting people with harsh words - these are the eight vices that accompany anger.
quotes.transliteration:
paiśunyaṃ sāhasaṃ droha īrṣyāsūyārthadūṣaṇam |
vāgdaṇḍajaṃ ca pauruṣyaṃ krodhajoऽpi gaṇoṣṭakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पादाहतं यदुत्थाय मूर्धानमधिरोहति |
स्वस्थादेवापमानेऽपि देहिनस्तद्वरं रजः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When kicked with foot, even dust fights back and ultimately sits on top of your head. Such is its power of revenge. A person who does nothing when insulted is worse than dust.
quotes.transliteration:
pādāhataṃ yadutthāya mūrdhānamadhirohati |
svasthādevāpamāneऽpi dehinastadvaraṃ rajaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा काष्ठमयो हस्ती यथा चर्ममयो मृगः |
पुमान्यस्चानधीयानः त्रयस्ते नाम बिभ्रति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: An elephant made out of sticks, a deer made out of just skin (not speed) and a person without education are all useless. Their only accomplishment is to be counted as an elephant, deer or a man.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā kāṣṭhamayo hastī yathā carmamayo mṛgaḥ |
pumānyascānadhīyānaḥ trayaste nāma bibhrati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भूयो दोषैरपि परिवृतः पूरुषः सात्विकानां
प्रायः पूज्यो भवति भगवद्भक्तिमासाद्य हृद्याम् ।
आक्रांतापि प्रबलभुजगैः कंटकैरप्यनल्पैः
किं नादृत्या सुरभिकुसुमा केतकी भाग्यभाजाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A man may have many defects. But if he has pure devotion towards God in his heart, then even he will be revered and respected by other people. The ketaki plant is considered very sacred even when it is surrounded by poisonous snakes and big thorns.
quotes.transliteration:
bhūyo doṣairapi parivṛtaḥ pūruṣaḥ sātvikānāṃ
prāyaḥ pūjyo bhavati bhagavadbhaktimāsādya hṛdyām |
ākrāṃtāpi prabalabhujagaiḥ kaṃṭakairapyanalpaiḥ
kiṃ nādṛtyā surabhikusumā ketakī bhāgyabhājām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निदानज्ञस्य भिषजो रुग्णहिंसां प्रयुंजतः ।
न किंचदपि नैर्घृण्यं घृणैवात्र प्रयोजिका ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A doctor diagnoses a problem correctly and makes the patient go through the pain of surgery. There is no sin attached to this. In fact, he gets a lot of good karma credit for this.
quotes.transliteration:
nidānajñasya bhiṣajo rugṇahiṃsāṃ prayuṃjataḥ |
na kiṃcadapi nairghṛṇyaṃ ghṛṇaivātra prayojikā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अन्यमाश्रयते लक्ष्मीस्त्वन्यमन्यंच मेदिनी ।
अनन्यगामिनी पुंसां कीर्तिरेका पतिव्रता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth is very fickle. It will goto different people every day. So is land. Today it may be with you, tomorrow it is with someone else. But fame is not like that. It will not go to anyone after you. It will remain yours for ever. It is like a chaste wife.
quotes.transliteration:
anyamāśrayate lakṣmīstvanyamanyaṃca medinī |
ananyagāminī puṃsāṃ kīrtirekā pativratā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्रयाणामेकभावानां यो न पश्यति वै भिदां ।
सर्वभूतात्मनां ब्रह्मन् स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one stops noticing the difference between the three - Creator, Preserver and Destroyer; and sees the universal cosmic spirit in everyone/everything - then he naturally attains peace.
quotes.transliteration:
trayāṇāmekabhāvānāṃ yo na paśyati vai bhidāṃ |
sarvabhūtātmanāṃ brahman sa śāntimadhigacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अंकोलं निजबीजसंततिरयस्कान्तोपलं सूचिका
साध्वी नैजविभुं लता क्षितिरुहं सिंधुः सरिद्वल्लभं ।
प्राप्नोतीह यथा तथा पशुपतेः पादारविन्दद्वयं
चेतोवृत्तिरुपेत्य तिष्ठति सदा सा भक्तिरुच्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The seeds of ankola tree always follow the tree. A needle follows the magnet. A chaste wife always follows her husband. A creeper follows the tree. A river seeks the ocean. Just like that my mind always seeks the pair of lotus feet of you - Oh Lord of all animals (Shiva). After getting there, let it be there for ever. This longing to be at God's presence is called devotion.
quotes.transliteration:
aṃkolaṃ nijabījasaṃtatirayaskāntopalaṃ sūcikā
sādhvī naijavibhuṃ latā kṣitiruhaṃ siṃdhuḥ saridvallabhaṃ |
prāpnotīha yathā tathā paśupateḥ pādāravindadvayaṃ
cetovṛttirupetya tiṣṭhati sadā sā bhaktirucyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भुक्ता बहवो दारा लब्धाः पुत्राश्च पौत्राश्च ।
नीतं शतमप्यायुः सत्यं वद मर्तुमस्ति मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: After enjoying many wives, having lots of children and grand children, having lived over hundred years - tell me the truth: Do you now wish to die?
quotes.transliteration:
bhuktā bahavo dārā labdhāḥ putrāśca pautrāśca |
nītaṃ śatamapyāyuḥ satyaṃ vada martumasti manaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तत् समाचरेद्धीरो यत् परोऽस्य विगर्हयेत् ।
यथात्मनस्तथान्येषां दारा रक्ष्या विपश्चिता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A noble man will never do any deeds that will attract criticism (honest). Such a person will protect other people's wives just as he would have if it were his wife in trouble.
quotes.transliteration:
na tat samācareddhīro yat paroऽsya vigarhayet |
yathātmanastathānyeṣāṃ dārā rakṣyā vipaścitā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संपदो जलतरंगविलोल
यौवनं त्रिचतुराणि दिनानि ।
शारदाभ्रपरिपेलवमायुः
किं धनैः परहितानि कुरुध्वम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth is as temporary as a wave on still water. Youth is just a matter of few years. Our life it self is as uncertain as a cloud of Sharat month (where clouds could get formed and dispersed in a matter of minutes. No rain.) What is the use of all the wealth that you accumulate? Spend them in a way that is helpful to others.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃpado jalataraṃgavilola
yauvanaṃ tricaturāṇi dināni |
śāradābhraparipelavamāyuḥ
kiṃ dhanaiḥ parahitāni kurudhvam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नामुत्र हि सहायार्थं पिता माता च तिष्ठतः ।
न पुत्रदारा न ज्ञातिः धर्मस्तिष्ठति केवलः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the after life none of whom you love here will help you - not father, not mother, not relatives, not son, not wife, not friends. It is only fruits of following Dharma that help you there.
quotes.transliteration:
nāmutra hi sahāyārthaṃ pitā mātā ca tiṣṭhataḥ |
na putradārā na jñātiḥ dharmastiṣṭhati kevalaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आर्षं धर्मोपदेशं च वेदशास्त्राविरोधिना ।
यस्तर्केणानुसंधत्ते स धर्मं वेद नेतारः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The preachings of sages regarding the moral code of conduct are on one side. The ancient scriptures are the other side. Both supplement each other. But only the person who reconciles both of them with sound logic can be called as "righteous". If not, he would just be another blind follower.
quotes.transliteration:
ārṣaṃ dharmopadeśaṃ ca vedaśāstrāvirodhinā |
yastarkeṇānusaṃdhatte sa dharmaṃ veda netāraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मधु पश्यति मूढात्मा प्रपातं नैव पश्यति |
करोति निंदितं कर्म नरकान्न बिभेति च ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When he looks at the honey comb hanging off the cliff, a fool sees only honey. He does not see the danger of falling into the valley below. Just like that people only see the short term pleasures and not their consequences (like miserable time in hell)
quotes.transliteration:
madhu paśyati mūḍhātmā prapātaṃ naiva paśyati |
karoti niṃditaṃ karma narakānna bibheti ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतस्करकरग्राह्यमराजकवशंवदम् ।
अदायादविभागार्हं धनमार्जय सुस्थिरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Put your efforts in accumulating wealth that can not be stolen by thieves, confiscated by government (king), or split among siblings like ancestral property. There is only one such wealth - Education.
quotes.transliteration:
ataskarakaragrāhyamarājakavaśaṃvadam |
adāyādavibhāgārhaṃ dhanamārjaya susthiram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
युध्यन्ति पशवः सर्वे पठन्ति शुकसारिकाः |
दातुं जानाति यो वित्तं स शूरः स च पण्डितः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: All animals know to fight. Even birds like parrots can talk. Ability to fight and talk do not make us any better than them. He who has the ability to give away money (or any other wealth, like knowledge) should alone be called as brave or wise.
quotes.transliteration:
yudhyanti paśavaḥ sarve paṭhanti śukasārikāḥ |
dātuṃ jānāti yo vittaṃ sa śūraḥ sa ca paṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वेदाः त्यागश्च यज्ञाश्च नियामाश्च तपांसि च |
वि विप्रदुष्टभावस्य सिद्धिं गच्छन्ति कर्हिचित् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The vedas, mindset for sacrifice, rituals of offerings to God, prayers, penances can never be mastered by any one with a corrupted mind - a mind that chases sensory pleasures.
quotes.transliteration:
vedāḥ tyāgaśca yajñāśca niyāmāśca tapāṃsi ca |
