vi vipraduṣṭabhāvasya siddhiṃ gacchanti karhicit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आयासशतलब्धस्य प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसः ।
गतिरेकैव वित्तस्य दानं शेषा विपत्तयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth is hard-earned (by hundred difficult ways) and dearer than life. There is only one way it must be spent - by donating to the needy and worthy. All other ways are only inviting trouble.
quotes.transliteration:
āyāsaśatalabdhasya prāṇebhyoऽpi garīyasaḥ |
gatirekaiva vittasya dānaṃ śeṣā vipattayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दवदहनजटालज्वालाहतानां
परिगलितलतानां म्लायतां भूरुहाणाम् ।
अयि जलधर शैलश्रेणिशृङ्गेषु तोयं
वितरसि बहु कोऽयं श्रीमदस्तावकीनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The high and indiscreet flames of the forest fire have burnt down many trees. The creepers that were clinging on to those trees are lying on the ground. It is these places that are in need of rain. But still, oh rain!! you pour water only over the cold mountains that are very high. Is this not an example of your arrogance? (misusing your power)
quotes.transliteration:
davadahanajaṭālajvālāhatānāṃ
parigalitalatānāṃ mlāyatāṃ bhūruhāṇām |
ayi jaladhara śailaśreṇiśṛṅgeṣu toyaṃ
vitarasi bahu koऽyaṃ śrīmadastāvakīnaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्यसनस्य च मृतोश्च व्यसनं कष्टमुच्यते |
व्यसन्यधोऽधो व्रजति स्वर्यात्यव्यसनी मृतः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Between addiction and death, death is better. By death one may rise up to heaven. But with addiction there is only one way - down and down.
quotes.transliteration:
vyasanasya ca mṛtośca vyasanaṃ kaṣṭamucyate |
vyasanyadhoऽdho vrajati svaryātyavyasanī mṛtaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भारोऽविवेकिनः शास्त्रं भारो ज्ञानं च रागिणः ।
अशान्तस्य मनो भारः भारोऽनात्मविदो वपुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: An idiot would find the scriptures heavy. A person who enjoys worldly pleasures finds knowledge heavy and unbearable. A restless man finds his mind heavy. If one does not know his self, he will find his body heavy.
quotes.transliteration:
bhāroऽvivekinaḥ śāstraṃ bhāro jñānaṃ ca rāgiṇaḥ |
aśāntasya mano bhāraḥ bhāroऽnātmavido vapuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सम्पदा संपरिष्वक्तो विद्यया चानवद्यया ।
नरो न शोभते लोके हरिभक्तिरसं विना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A man may have good education and lot of wealth. But if he does not have devotion towards God then he will not achieve much in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
sampadā saṃpariṣvakto vidyayā cānavadyayā |
naro na śobhate loke haribhaktirasaṃ vinā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पायपानं भुजङ्गानां केवलं विषवर्धनम् |
उपदेशो हि मूर्खाणां प्रकोपाय न शान्तये ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If you give milk to a snake it will only result in more venom. Just like that advice given to a fool (in anger) will only increase his
quotes.transliteration:
pāyapānaṃ bhujaṅgānāṃ kevalaṃ viṣavardhanam |
upadeśo hi mūrkhāṇāṃ prakopāya na śāntaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं तर्केण वितर्कितेन शतशो ज्ञातेन किं छंदसा
किं पीतेन सुधारसेन बहुधा स्वाध्यायपाठेन किम् ।
अभ्यस्तेन च लक्षणेन किमहो ध्यानं न चेत् सर्वथा
लोकालोकविलोकनैककुशलज्ञाने हृदि ब्रह्मणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is the use of logic that is only used to argue? What is the use of having mastery over meters of poetry? What is the use of drinking elixir? What is the use of reciting Vedas many times? What is the use of practicing the craft of charm? If one does not have devotion towards God at heart, where there is ability to know everything in all worlds.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ tarkeṇa vitarkitena śataśo jñātena kiṃ chaṃdasā
kiṃ pītena sudhārasena bahudhā svādhyāyapāṭhena kim |
abhyastena ca lakṣaṇena kimaho dhyānaṃ na cet sarvathā
lokālokavilokanaikakuśalajñāne hṛdi brahmaṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
या सृष्टिः स्रष्टुराद्या वहति विधिहुतं या हविर्या च होत्री
ये द्वे कालं विधत्तः श्रुतिविषयगुणा याः स्थिता व्याप्य विश्वम् ।
यामाहुः सर्वबीजप्रकृतिरिति यया प्राणिनः प्राणवन्तः
प्रत्याक्षाभिः प्रपन्नः तनुभिरवतु वस्ताभिरष्टाभिरीशः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the God who is composed of all eight factors protect us - that which was created first (water), that which will take our ritualistic offerings to Gods (fire), that which is the owner of itself (energy), that which splits day and night (sun and moon), that which is the meaning of all words, that which has enveloped entire world (sky), that which causes the seeds to sprout (earth), that which gives life to all beings (air).
quotes.transliteration:
yā sṛṣṭiḥ sraṣṭurādyā vahati vidhihutaṃ yā haviryā ca hotrī
ye dve kālaṃ vidhattaḥ śrutiviṣayaguṇā yāḥ sthitā vyāpya viśvam |
yāmāhuḥ sarvabījaprakṛtiriti yayā prāṇinaḥ prāṇavantaḥ
pratyākṣābhiḥ prapannaḥ tanubhiravatu vastābhiraṣṭābhirīśaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न मांसभक्षणे दोषो न मद्ये न च मैथुने ।
प्रवृत्तिरेषा भूतानां निवृत्तिस्तु महाफला ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not a sin to eat non-veg food, not a sin to drink alcohol and not a sin to make love. These are only natural to all beings. If one can abstain from all these, then one can achieve great results.
quotes.transliteration:
na māṃsabhakṣaṇe doṣo na madye na ca maithune |
pravṛttireṣā bhūtānāṃ nivṛttistu mahāphalā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथाग्निः सुसमृद्धार्चिः करोत्येधांसि भस्मसात् ।
तथा मद्विषया भक्तिरुद्धवैनांसि कृत्स्नशः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like fire burning well can turn wood to ash, complete devotion towards me (Krishna) will burn any sin.
quotes.transliteration:
yathāgniḥ susamṛddhārciḥ karotyedhāṃsi bhasmasāt |
tathā madviṣayā bhaktiruddhavaināṃsi kṛtsnaśaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुखार्थिनः कुतो विद्या नास्ति विद्यार्थिनः सुखम् ।
सुखार्थी वा त्यजेद्विद्यां विद्यार्थी वा त्यजेत् सुखम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Where is education (wisdom) for pleasure seekers? There is no pleasure for those who seek knowledge. If you are a pelasure seeker, you must give up any quest for knowledge and vice-versa.
quotes.transliteration:
sukhārthinaḥ kuto vidyā nāsti vidyārthinaḥ sukham |
sukhārthī vā tyajedvidyāṃ vidyārthī vā tyajet sukham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रत्नाकरः किं कुरुते स्वरत्नैः
विन्ध्याचलः किं करिभिः करोति |
श्रीखण्डखण्डैः मलयाचलः किं
परोपकाराय सतां विभूतयः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: What is the use of gems to an ocean? What do the forests of Vindhya have to gain from the famous elephants that roam them? Does the Malaya forests gain by the sandalwood trees that grow there? Noble men are always like that. Their actions always help others.
quotes.transliteration:
ratnākaraḥ kiṃ kurute svaratnaiḥ
vindhyācalaḥ kiṃ karibhiḥ karoti |
śrīkhaṇḍakhaṇḍaiḥ malayācalaḥ kiṃ
paropakārāya satāṃ vibhūtayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शरतल्पमधिशयनाद् भीष्मादाकर्ण्य धर्मजो धर्मान् ।
दुःखं जहौ दुरन्तं प्रष्टव्याः सत्पथं वृद्धाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharmaraja was himself very pious. But he also listened to Bheeshma sleeping on the death bed (of arrows). This helped him alleviate the pain caused by all the turmoil of the war before. Hence one must always listen to the old men who have led their life virtuously.
quotes.transliteration:
śaratalpamadhiśayanād bhīṣmādākarṇya dharmajo dharmān |
duḥkhaṃ jahau durantaṃ praṣṭavyāḥ satpathaṃ vṛddhāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आम्रं छित्वा कुठारेण निंबं परिचरेत्तु यः ।
यश्चैवं पयसा संचेन्नैवास्य मधुरो भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One cannot change the innate nature of any being. If you cut down a neighbouring mango tree and treat the neem tree very well by pouring milk at it, it will not become any sweeter.
quotes.transliteration:
āmraṃ chitvā kuṭhāreṇa niṃbaṃ paricarettu yaḥ |
yaścaivaṃ payasā saṃcennaivāsya madhuro bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाधर्मश्चरितो राजन् सद्यः फलति गौरिव ।
शनैरावर्त्यमानो हि कर्तुर्मूलानि कृतन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you stray from path of Dharma, you will not know its repercussions right then. It is not like raising cattle whose benefits are there to be seen immediately. The consequences of unlawful acts will slowly act to cut your very roots.
quotes.transliteration:
nādharmaścarito rājan sadyaḥ phalati gauriva |
śanairāvartyamāno hi karturmūlāni kṛtanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वर्गो धनं वा धान्यं वा विद्याः पुत्रास्सुखानि च ।
गुरुवृत्तनुरोधेन न किञ्चिदपि दुर्लभम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A place in heaven, wealth, peaceful mind, education, a good son - none of this is difficult to get for a person who serves his teacher well.
quotes.transliteration:
svargo dhanaṃ vā dhānyaṃ vā vidyāḥ putrāssukhāni ca |
guruvṛttanurodhena na kiñcidapi durlabham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आजन्मनः शाठ्यमशिक्षितो यः
तस्याप्रमाणं वचनं जनस्य ।
परातिसंधानमधीयते यैः
विद्येति ते सन्तु किलाप्तवाचः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What has become of this world? People do not believe the words of sages who live in hermitage and have never been exposed (or taught) deceit. On the other hand, they believe words of those who have learnt the art of deception as part of their formal education (the kings).
quotes.transliteration:
ājanmanaḥ śāṭhyamaśikṣito yaḥ
tasyāpramāṇaṃ vacanaṃ janasya |
parātisaṃdhānamadhīyate yaiḥ
vidyeti te santu kilāptavācaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाग्वैखरि शब्दझरी शास्त्रव्याख्यानकौशलम् ।
वैदुष्यं विदुषां तद्वद् भुक्तये न तु मुक्तये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Being articulate, free flow of words, ability to explain intricacies of scriptures, knowledge of the scholars are all for enjoyment (mental gratification) not for salvation.
quotes.transliteration:
vāgvaikhari śabdajharī śāstravyākhyānakauśalam |
vaiduṣyaṃ viduṣāṃ tadvad bhuktaye na tu muktaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वसुखनिरभिलाषः खिद्यसे कस्य हेतोः
प्रतिदिनमथवा ते वृत्तिरेवं विधैव ।
अनुभवति हि मूर्ध्ना पादपस्तीव्रमुष्णं
शमयति परितापं छायया संश्रितानाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Why are you doing these things even if it hurts your own interest? Perhaps, this is what it is to be a king. Just like a tree bears all the heat during an afternoon to provide shade to those stand under it.
quotes.transliteration:
svasukhanirabhilāṣaḥ khidyase kasya hetoḥ
pratidinamathavā te vṛttirevaṃ vidhaiva |
anubhavati hi mūrdhnā pādapastīvramuṣṇaṃ
śamayati paritāpaṃ chāyayā saṃśritānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्वः श्वः प्राणप्रयाणेपि न स्मरन्ति स्मरद्विषः ।
मग्नाः कुटुम्बजम्बाले बालिशा यदुपासिनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This life will end tomorrow or the day after. Even at that time, fools will not remember God. Instead they run behind the sensory pleasures which are like mire.
quotes.transliteration:
śvaḥ śvaḥ prāṇaprayāṇepi na smaranti smaradviṣaḥ |
magnāḥ kuṭumbajambāle bāliśā yadupāsinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वितरति गुरुः प्राज्ञे विद्यां यथैव तथा जडे
न तु खलु तयोर्ज्ञाने शक्तिं करोत्यपहन्ति वा ।
भवति हि पुनर्भूयान् भेदः फलं प्रति तद्यथा
प्रभवति शुचिर्बिंबग्राहे मणिर्न मृदादयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A teacher teaches his lessons to a intelligent student just like he does to a dull one. The lessons themselves do not have the ability to make or break one's fortune. However we see a big difference in the way the same lessons impact either students. It is the same difference we see in the ability to reflect radiance between a gem (crystal) and mud.
quotes.transliteration:
vitarati guruḥ prājñe vidyāṃ yathaiva tathā jaḍe
na tu khalu tayorjñāne śaktiṃ karotyapahanti vā |
bhavati hi punarbhūyān bhedaḥ phalaṃ prati tadyathā
prabhavati śucirbiṃbagrāhe maṇirna mṛdādayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्तु हित्वा मतं भर्तुः स्वमतं संप्रभाषते |
अनुक्तवादी दूतः सन् स दूतो वधमर्हति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: It is a messenger's duty to stay true to his masters message (and intentions). Instead of that, if he conveys his own feelings, or tries to convey some thing never told by his master - then he deserves to be killed.
quotes.transliteration:
yastu hitvā mataṃ bhartuḥ svamataṃ saṃprabhāṣate |
anuktavādī dūtaḥ san sa dūto vadhamarhati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
येषां चित्ते वसेद्भक्तिः सर्वदा प्रेमरूपिणी ।
न ते पशन्ति कीनाशं स्वप्नेऽप्यमलमूर्तयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those whose mind is always filled with devotion towards God, those pure minds will not even see death in their dreams.
quotes.transliteration:
yeṣāṃ citte vasedbhaktiḥ sarvadā premarūpiṇī |
na te paśanti kīnāśaṃ svapneऽpyamalamūrtayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सकृदपि दृष्ट्वा विबुधा जानन्ति सारतां तस्य ।
हस्ततुलयापि निपुणाः पलप्रमाणं विजानन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men can know the character of a person by meeting him only once. Just like skilled traders can measure minute weights just by their hands.
quotes.transliteration:
sakṛdapi dṛṣṭvā vibudhā jānanti sāratāṃ tasya |
hastatulayāpi nipuṇāḥ palapramāṇaṃ vijānanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोभाविष्टो नरो वित्तं वीक्षते नैव चापदम् |
दुग्धं पश्यति मार्जारो यथा न लगुडाहतिम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A person blinded with greed can only see ways acquiring wealth - not the dangers associated with them. A cat sees the milk but can not see the danger of getting beaten with a stick when caught.
quotes.transliteration:
lobhāviṣṭo naro vittaṃ vīkṣate naiva cāpadam |
dugdhaṃ paśyati mārjāro yathā na laguḍāhatim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तस्मात् पुत्रेषु दारेषु मित्रेषु च धनेषु च ।
नातिप्रसंगः कर्तव्यो विप्रयोगो हि तैर्ध्रुवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not attach himself too much to children, wives, friends and wealth. All the above are bound to depart from you sooner or later.
quotes.transliteration:
tasmāt putreṣu dāreṣu mitreṣu ca dhaneṣu ca |
nātiprasaṃgaḥ kartavyo viprayogo hi tairdhruvam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकृपणमरुक् श्रान्तं जीव्याज्जनः पुरुषायुषं
भवतु भगवद्भक्तिः द्वैतं विना पुरुषोत्तमे ।
दयितभुवनो विद्वद्बंधुर्गुणेषु विशेषवित्
सततसुकृती भूयाद्भूपः प्रसाधितमण्डलः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the people be generous, healthy and never tired. Let them all live to their full lives. Let the devotion in God be unwavering. Let the king give ample support to the scholars. Let the king understand his special responsibility and always do good for his subjects.
quotes.transliteration:
akṛpaṇamaruk śrāntaṃ jīvyājjanaḥ puruṣāyuṣaṃ
bhavatu bhagavadbhaktiḥ dvaitaṃ vinā puruṣottame |
dayitabhuvano vidvadbaṃdhurguṇeṣu viśeṣavit
satatasukṛtī bhūyādbhūpaḥ prasādhitamaṇḍalaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भाग्योदयेन बहुजन्मसमार्जितेन
सत्संगमं च लभते पुरुषो यदा वै ।
अज्ञानहेतुकृतमोहमदांधकार
नाशं विधाय हि तदोदयते विवेकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one accumulates a lot of good karma over lot of births, he come to a fortune of knowing virtuous people. With such association, he can learn to defeat the darkness called - attachment and pride. Once the darkness is lifted, the path to wisdom is illuminated.
quotes.transliteration:
bhāgyodayena bahujanmasamārjitena
satsaṃgamaṃ ca labhate puruṣo yadā vai |
ajñānahetukṛtamohamadāṃdhakāra
nāśaṃ vidhāya hi tadodayate vivekaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धनानि जीवितं चैव परार्थे प्राज्ञ उत्सृजेत् ।
सन्निमित्तं वरं त्यागो विनाशे नियते सति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth and one's life must be used to help others. So think the wise. Both are bound to be destroyed. So it is best used to help others.
quotes.transliteration:
dhanāni jīvitaṃ caiva parārthe prājña utsṛjet |
sannimittaṃ varaṃ tyāgo vināśe niyate sati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वभावो नोपदेशेन शक्यते कर्तुमन्यथा |
सुतप्तमपि पानीयं पुनर्गच्छति शीतताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Objects or people will always follow their innate disposition. It can not be changed by any amount of advice. No matter how much heat water, left to itself, it will cool down.
quotes.transliteration:
svabhāvo nopadeśena śakyate kartumanyathā |
sutaptamapi pānīyaṃ punargacchati śītatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्ञानमेव परंब्रह्म ज्ञानं बंधाय कल्पते ।
ज्ञानात्मकमिदं विश्वं न ज्ञानाद्विद्यते परम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Knowledge is the ultimate truth. Knowledge enables us to relate to all other things. The whole world is realized only through knowledge. There is no parallel to knowledge.
quotes.transliteration:
jñānameva paraṃbrahma jñānaṃ baṃdhāya kalpate |
jñānātmakamidaṃ viśvaṃ na jñānādvidyate param ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वेदान्देषु यमाहुरेकपुरुशं व्याप्य स्थितं रोदसी
यस्मिन्नीश्वर इत्यनन्यविशयः शब्दो यथार्थाक्षरः ।
अन्तर्यश्च मुमुक्षुभिर्नियमितप्राणादिभिर्मृग्यते
सस्थाणुः स्थिरभक्तियोगसुलभो निःश्रेयसायास्तु वः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The Upanishads call him as the only person who expands beyond the earth and the skies. For him, the word "Eshwara" (owner) does not refer to any one else. It is apt for him only. People who wish to be released from the cycle of births control their lives and want to see him. He is only obligated to strong devotion. Let such a God help me in attaining salvation.
quotes.transliteration:
vedāndeṣu yamāhurekapuruśaṃ vyāpya sthitaṃ rodasī
yasminnīśvara ityananyaviśayaḥ śabdo yathārthākṣaraḥ |
antaryaśca mumukṣubhirniyamitaprāṇādibhirmṛgyate
sasthāṇuḥ sthirabhaktiyogasulabho niḥśreyasāyāstu vaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुष्प्राप्तं मकराकरे करतलाद्रत्नं निमग्नं यथा
संसारेऽत्र तथा नरत्वमथ तत्प्राप्तं मया निर्मलम् ।
भ्रातः पश्य विमूढतां मम हहा नीतं यदेतन्मुदा
कामक्रोध कुबोध मत्सर कुधीमायामहामोहतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Once a gem slips from hand into the sea, it is impossible to get it back. Similarly, one can if you lose the gem of human life in worldly affairs if he is stupid and gets drawn into lust, anger, ill-advice, jealousy, greed.
quotes.transliteration:
duṣprāptaṃ makarākare karatalādratnaṃ nimagnaṃ yathā
saṃsāreऽtra tathā naratvamatha tatprāptaṃ mayā nirmalam |
bhrātaḥ paśya vimūḍhatāṃ mama hahā nītaṃ yadetanmudā
kāmakrodha kubodha matsara kudhīmāyāmahāmohataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुराचारो हि पुरुषः लोके भवति निंदितः ।
दुःखभागी च सततं व्याधितोऽल्पायुरेव च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wicked person will definitely reap the results of his actions. He will always be scorned up on by virtuous people. He will always languish in misery. He will fall sick. He will definitely die early.
quotes.transliteration:
durācāro hi puruṣaḥ loke bhavati niṃditaḥ |
duḥkhabhāgī ca satataṃ vyādhitoऽlpāyureva ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शास्त्राण्यधीत्यापि भवन्ति मूर्खाः
यस्तु क्रियावान् पुरुषः स विद्वान् ।
सुचिन्तितं औषधमातुराणां
न नाममात्रेण करोत्यरोगम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not enough to know what is written in the scriptures. Only he will be called wise who puts those learnings into action. Even a very well thought out medicine will not cure the illness by just uttering its name.
quotes.transliteration:
śāstrāṇyadhītyāpi bhavanti mūrkhāḥ
yastu kriyāvān puruṣaḥ sa vidvān |
sucintitaṃ auṣadhamāturāṇāṃ
na nāmamātreṇa karotyarogam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बाह्यमाध्यात्मिकं वाऽपि दुःखमुत्पाद्यते परैः ।
न कुप्यति न चाहंति दम इत्यभिधीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Others may give you grief mentally or bodily. One is called self restraint when he neither gets angry or vows revenge.
quotes.transliteration:
bāhyamādhyātmikaṃ vāऽpi duḥkhamutpādyate paraiḥ |
na kupyati na cāhaṃti dama ityabhidhīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्रकुत्र कुले वासो येषु केषु भवोSस्तु मे ।
तव दास्यैकभावे स्यात् सदा सर्वत्र मे रतिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: My birth could be any where, any family, any caste. Let my happiness be only in surrendering to you.
quotes.transliteration:
yatrakutra kule vāso yeṣu keṣu bhavoSstu me |
tava dāsyaikabhāve syāt sadā sarvatra me ratiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यात्येकतोऽस्तशिखरं पतिरोषधीनां
आविष्कृतोऽरुणपुरःसर एकतोऽर्कः ।
तेजोद्वयस्य युगपद्व्यसनोदयाभ्यां
लोको नियम्यत इवात्मदशान्तरेषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: On one side the moon is sinking. On the other side Sun is rising with his tender early morning rays. Both sun and moon give light (that they are meant to) at their own times and set when their time is up. This must teach us people something about dealing with our own inner turmoils.
quotes.transliteration:
yātyekatoऽstaśikharaṃ patiroṣadhīnāṃ
āviṣkṛtoऽruṇapuraḥsara ekatoऽrkaḥ |
tejodvayasya yugapadvyasanodayābhyāṃ
loko niyamyata ivātmadaśāntareṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
घातयितुमेव नीचः परकार्यं वेति न प्रसाधयितुम् ।
पातयितुमस्ति शक्तिर्वायोः वृक्षं न चोन्नमितुम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wicked person only knows to destroy other people's work, not to help them. Wind can uproot a tree, not to help it grow.
quotes.transliteration:
ghātayitumeva nīcaḥ parakāryaṃ veti na prasādhayitum |
pātayitumasti śaktirvāyoḥ vṛkṣaṃ na connamitum ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नित्यं कोपात्तपो रक्षेच्छ्रियं रक्षेच्च मत्सरात् ।
विद्यां मानावमानाभ्यामात्मानां च प्रमादतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must protect his penance from being disturbed by anger. Protect wealth from jealousy (about wealthier people). Protect education from ego. Protect self from being irresponsible.
quotes.transliteration:
nityaṃ kopāttapo rakṣecchriyaṃ rakṣecca matsarāt |
vidyāṃ mānāvamānābhyāmātmānāṃ ca pramādataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मजायासुतागारपशुद्रविणबंधुषु ।
निरूढमूलहृदय आत्मानं बहु मन्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Man gets too much attached to himself, his wife, children, house, pets, wealth and relatives. Once he feels for all these in his heart, he thinks himself to be very important for all these to exist.
quotes.transliteration:
ātmajāyāsutāgārapaśudraviṇabaṃdhuṣu |
nirūḍhamūlahṛdaya ātmānaṃ bahu manyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रीमानजननिंद्यश्च शूराश्चाप्यविकत्थनः |
समदृष्टिः प्रभुश्चैव दुर्लभाः पुरुषास्त्रयः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A rich person whom people do not loathe, a courageous person who does not boast about his bravado, a level headed king - all the three are very rare in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
śrīmānajananiṃdyaśca śūrāścāpyavikatthanaḥ |
samadṛṣṭiḥ prabhuścaiva durlabhāḥ puruṣāstrayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
य उद्धरेत्करं राजा राजा प्रजा धर्मेष्वशिक्षयन् ।
प्रजानां शमलं भुंक्ते भगं च स्वं जहाति सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king who collects taxes also must let the subjects understand the value of Dharma. If not, he will be no greater than some one protecting in exchange for money. So, if he does not teach them the value of Dharma, it is as of he is eating their waste.
quotes.transliteration:
ya uddharetkaraṃ rājā rājā prajā dharmeṣvaśikṣayan |
prajānāṃ śamalaṃ bhuṃkte bhagaṃ ca svaṃ jahāti saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अत्रामोदमरंदयोर्न कणिका कोषांतरस्थायिनी
पत्रच्छत्रपरंपराऽपि विफलारंभा कियद्वर्ण्यताम् ।
एतत्सर्वमपूर्वशिल्परचनाचातुर्यमुच्चावचं
किं मोहादलिपुत्र चित्रकमले भूयः परिभ्राम्यसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! baby bee. You are still very young and should be living in your cell. You do not know that this lotus is a mere painting and has neither the fragrance or the nectar. It does not have petals or the leaves. How can I say that your efforts will go waste? This lotus is only showing off the supreme craft of a painter. Why are you going around this painting which is a mere illusion. (World being the painting, God being the creator and we being the bees.)
quotes.transliteration:
atrāmodamaraṃdayorna kaṇikā koṣāṃtarasthāyinī
patracchatraparaṃparāऽpi viphalāraṃbhā kiyadvarṇyatām |
etatsarvamapūrvaśilparacanācāturyamuccāvacaṃ
kiṃ mohādaliputra citrakamale bhūyaḥ paribhrāmyasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्वानेव विजानाति विद्वज्जनपरिश्रमम् ।
न हि वंध्या विजानाति गुर्वीं प्रसववेदनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only a wise man will be able to appreciate another wise man's efforts. Only a mother would know about the labour pain - not any body else.
quotes.transliteration:
vidvāneva vijānāti vidvajjanapariśramam |
na hi vaṃdhyā vijānāti gurvīṃ prasavavedanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रजापीडनसंतापात्समुद्भूतो हुताशनः |
राज्ञः कुलं श्रियं प्राणान् चादग्ध्वा न निवर्तते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The heat from the hatred felt by the subjects will not relent until it burns down the king, his dynasty and his wealth. Such is the hatred that is caused by a king who builds his treasure by hurting his subjects.
quotes.transliteration:
prajāpīḍanasaṃtāpātsamudbhūto hutāśanaḥ |
rājñaḥ kulaṃ śriyaṃ prāṇān cādagdhvā na nivartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रुधिरत्रिधातुमज्जामेदोमांसास्थिसंहतिर्देहः ।
स बहिस्त्वचा पिनद्धस्तस्मान्नो भक्ष्यते काकैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This body is made up of all disgusting things like blood, air, bile, foam, flesh, marrow, muscles and bones. It is good that skin covers all these up so that we are not eaten up by crows. This body nothing to be attached with.
quotes.transliteration:
rudhiratridhātumajjāmedomāṃsāsthisaṃhatirdehaḥ |
sa bahistvacā pinaddhastasmānno bhakṣyate kākaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कर्तव्यो भ्रातृषु स्नेहो विस्मर्तव्याः गुणेतराः ।
संबंधो बंधुभिः श्रेयान् लोकयोरुभयोरपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must show love and affection towards brothers (relatives and friends in general). One must show leniency while dealing with their mistakes and shortcomings. Being so will bring good fortune both in this world as well as the other.
quotes.transliteration:
kartavyo bhrātṛṣu sneho vismartavyāḥ guṇetarāḥ |
saṃbaṃdho baṃdhubhiḥ śreyān lokayorubhayorapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाराजके जनपदे बीजमुष्टिः प्रकीर्यते |
नाराजके पितुः पुत्रो भार्या वा वर्तते वशे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When there is no king to monitor, the peasants will not sow seeds. Anarchy will lead to lawlessness. Children will not listen to their parents. Men will not be able to control their wives.
quotes.transliteration:
nārājake janapade bījamuṣṭiḥ prakīryate |
nārājake pituḥ putro bhāryā vā vartate vaśe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राजानां प्रथमं विन्देत्ततो भार्यां ततो धनम् |
राजन्यसति लोकस्य कुतो भार्या कुतु धनम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must first find a king (who will protect him). Only then he must find a wife and later wealth. If he can not find a benevolent king, where is the question of wife and wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
rājānāṃ prathamaṃ vindettato bhāryāṃ tato dhanam |
rājanyasati lokasya kuto bhāryā kutu dhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पूर्वे वयसि तत् कुर्यात् येन वृद्धः सुखं वसेत् ।
यावज्जीवेन तत् कुर्यात् येन प्रेत्य सुखं वसेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is required at old-age, one must strive for that in days of youth. In old age, one must strive for what will help him in afterlife.
quotes.transliteration:
pūrve vayasi tat kuryāt yena vṛddhaḥ sukhaṃ vaset |
yāvajjīvena tat kuryāt yena pretya sukhaṃ vaset ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मूर्खा न द्रष्टव्या द्रष्टव्यास्चेन्नतैस्तु सह तिष्ठेत् |
यदि तिष्ठेन्न कथयेद्यदि कथयेन्मूर्खवत्कथयेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not see a fool. Avoid him. If you happen to see one, then avoid standing next to him. If you happen to stand next to him, avoid talking to him. If you absolutely have to talk to him, then talk to him like a fool only. A fool will not understand any way of communicating other than his own foolish way.
quotes.transliteration:
mūrkhā na draṣṭavyā draṣṭavyāscennataistu saha tiṣṭhet |
yadi tiṣṭhenna kathayedyadi kathayenmūrkhavatkathayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाश्रमः कारणं धर्मे क्रियमाणो भवेद्धि सः ।
अतो यदात्मनोऽपथ्यं परेषां न तदाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not the hermitage that constitutes Dharma. It is the actions of people in a hermitage that qualifies as Dharma. Wise men know this. Hence they will not encourage people to rush to hermitages as a way to follow Dharma. Wise men will not advise to others what they know will not work.
quotes.transliteration:
nāśramaḥ kāraṇaṃ dharme kriyamāṇo bhaveddhi saḥ |
ato yadātmanoऽpathyaṃ pareṣāṃ na tadācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न सा सभा यत्र न सन्ति वृद्धा
न ते वृद्धा ये न वदन्ति धर्मम् ।
नासौ धर्मो यत्र न सत्यमस्ति
न तत् सत्यं यत्छलेनाभ्युपेतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no assembly of significance without wise old men. Old men are wise only when they follow Dharma. Dharma is supported only by truth. Truth is something that has no deceit.
quotes.transliteration:
na sā sabhā yatra na santi vṛddhā
na te vṛddhā ye na vadanti dharmam |
nāsau dharmo yatra na satyamasti
na tat satyaṃ yatchalenābhyupetam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अभ्यस्य वेदशास्त्राणि तत्त्वं ज्ञात्वाऽथ बुद्धिमान् ।
पलालमिव धान्यार्थी सर्वशास्त्राणि संत्यजेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must study all the veda and other scriptures. Understand the philosophy behind them. Once done, a wise man will leave the scriptures behind and take only the knowledge. Just like anyone interested in the grains will leave the husk behind. Scriptures are only a means and not the end.
quotes.transliteration:
abhyasya vedaśāstrāṇi tattvaṃ jñātvāऽtha buddhimān |
palālamiva dhānyārthī sarvaśāstrāṇi saṃtyajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हीयते न प्रदीपेन क्षीयते नैव वह्निना |
नाश्यते रविणा नापि क्रोधरूपं तमो महत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The darkness created by anger can not be reduced by a flame (like a candle), can not be burnt down by fire, can not be destroyed even by the sun-light. Hence one must stay away from anger.
quotes.transliteration:
hīyate na pradīpena kṣīyate naiva vahninā |
nāśyate raviṇā nāpi krodharūpaṃ tamo mahat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुत्र विषं दुष्टजने किमिहाशौचं भवेदृणं नृणाम् ।
किमभयमिह वैराग्यं भयमपि किं वित्तमेव सर्वेषाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Where is poison? In the mind of a rogue. What is unclean? A man who is under debt. What gives you protection? Lack of greed is the best protection. What is the greatest cause of fear? Possessing wealth; One is always under the fear of losing it.
quotes.transliteration:
kutra viṣaṃ duṣṭajane kimihāśaucaṃ bhavedṛṇaṃ nṛṇām |
kimabhayamiha vairāgyaṃ bhayamapi kiṃ vittameva sarveṣām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न वै स्वयं तदश्नीयात् अतिथिं यन्न भोजयेत् ।
धन्यं यशस्यमायुष्यं स्वर्ग्यं वातिथिपूजनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never eat food before the guest has eaten. Serving the guest will lead to great wealth, fame and one can attain heaven-like happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
na vai svayaṃ tadaśnīyāt atithiṃ yanna bhojayet |
dhanyaṃ yaśasyamāyuṣyaṃ svargyaṃ vātithipūjanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कच्चित् सहस्रान्मूर्खाणामेकमिच्छसि पण्डीतम् ।
पण्डीतो ह्यर्थकृच्छेषु कुर्यान्निःश्रेयसं महत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is better to hire one wise man than thousands of fools. It is the wise man who will help you when you are in trouble.
quotes.transliteration:
kaccit sahasrānmūrkhāṇāmekamicchasi paṇḍītam |
paṇḍīto hyarthakṛccheṣu kuryānniḥśreyasaṃ mahat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुर्लभं त्रयमेवैतद्दैवानुग्रहहेतुकम् ।
मनुष्यत्वं मुमुक्षुत्वं महापुरुषसंश्रयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Getting these three together is matter of great luck and god's grace - human life, longing for salvation and kinship of virtuous people.
quotes.transliteration:
durlabhaṃ trayamevaitaddaivānugrahahetukam |
manuṣyatvaṃ mumukṣutvaṃ mahāpuruṣasaṃśrayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राजभिर्धृतदण्डास्तु कृत्वा पापानि मानवाः |
निर्मलाः स्वर्गमायान्ति सन्तः सुकृतिनो यथा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a sinner gets the right punishment (from a king) then he becomes pure again. He shall go to heaven just like people who have always done only good work.
quotes.transliteration:
rājabhirdhṛtadaṇḍāstu kṛtvā pāpāni mānavāḥ |
nirmalāḥ svargamāyānti santaḥ sukṛtino yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोभः पितातिवृद्धो जननी माया सहोदरः कूटः |
भ्रुकुटीरचना च विद्या पुत्रो दम्भस्य हुंकारः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: This is the verse that enumerates the relatives of deceit. Greed is his father, illusion his mother, fraud is his brother, art of making eyebrows knot is his education and grunting is his son. Such is the nature of his company.
quotes.transliteration:
lobhaḥ pitātivṛddho jananī māyā sahodaraḥ kūṭaḥ |
bhrukuṭīracanā ca vidyā putro dambhasya huṃkāraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यद्भूतहितमत्यन्तं तत्सत्यमिति धारणा ।
विपर्ययकृतोधर्मः पश्य धर्मस्य सूक्ष्मताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That action (in its purest sense) which helps the animals has to treated as truth (in accordance with Dharma). Anything that hurts them is therefore against dharma. See how simple and subtle is the essence of Dharma. This was told by a hunter to a brahmin while explaining how he perceived Dharma to be.
quotes.transliteration:
yadbhūtahitamatyantaṃ tatsatyamiti dhāraṇā |
viparyayakṛtodharmaḥ paśya dharmasya sūkṣmatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तृष्णासूचीविनिर्भिन्नं सिक्तं विषयसर्पिषा ।
रागद्वेषानले पक्वं मृत्युरश्नाति मानवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Death cooks a man's body like this - pierce it by the needle of greed, fry it in the ghee of sensory pleasures and heat it in the fire of attachment and hatred. At the end man is finished with his life.
quotes.transliteration:
tṛṣṇāsūcīvinirbhinnaṃ siktaṃ viṣayasarpiṣā |
rāgadveṣānale pakvaṃ mṛtyuraśnāti mānavam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नादातव्यं न दातव्यं न कर्तव्यं च किंचन ।
सांत्वमेकं प्रयोक्तव्यं सर्वं तस्य वशे जगत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In order to rule the world, one need not be a miser, nor give away everything, need not do anything special. It is enough to keep doing good and the rest come automatically.
quotes.transliteration:
nādātavyaṃ na dātavyaṃ na kartavyaṃ ca kiṃcana |
sāṃtvamekaṃ prayoktavyaṃ sarvaṃ tasya vaśe jagat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नेह चात्यंतसंवासः कर्हिचित् केनचित् सह ।
राजन् स्वेनापि देहेन किमु जायात्मजादिभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh King, attachments in this world are far from being permanent. Even your own body leaves you one day. What more can be said about your wife, children and others.
quotes.transliteration:
neha cātyaṃtasaṃvāsaḥ karhicit kenacit saha |
rājan svenāpi dehena kimu jāyātmajādibhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
घर्मार्तं न तथा सुशीतलजलैः स्नानं मुक्तावली
न श्रीखण्डविलेपनं सुखयति प्रत्यंगमप्यर्पितम् ।
प्रीत्यै सज्जनभाषितं प्रभवति प्रायो यथा देहिनां
सद्युक्त्या च पुरस्कृष्टिमंत्रोपमम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A tired traveler will appreciate warm talk by a virtuous man which is as attractive and knowledgeable as the hymns. Other pleasantries like fresh cold water, a pearl neck-lace, or sandalwood paste applied to the whole body will not bring as much joy as the good words of a noble man.
quotes.transliteration:
gharmārtaṃ na tathā suśītalajalaiḥ snānaṃ muktāvalī
na śrīkhaṇḍavilepanaṃ sukhayati pratyaṃgamapyarpitam |
prītyai sajjanabhāṣitaṃ prabhavati prāyo yathā dehināṃ
sadyuktyā ca puraskṛṣṭimaṃtropamam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हीनांगानतिरिक्तांगान्विद्याहीनान् वयोऽधिकान् |
रूपद्रव्यविहीनांश्च जातिहीनांश्च नाक्षिपेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not treat any body badly even when they are physically handicapped, or have deformed growth (like a sixth finger), uneducated, the aged, ugly, poor people, people of lower castes. One must treat everyone as equals.
quotes.transliteration:
hīnāṃgānatiriktāṃgānvidyāhīnān vayoऽdhikān |
rūpadravyavihīnāṃśca jātihīnāṃśca nākṣipet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तपः परं कृतयुगे त्रेतायां ज्ञानमुच्यते ।
द्वापरे यज्ञमित्यूचुः दानमेकं कलौ युगे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Religious austerity is the way to attain salvation in Kruta yuga. Knowledge is the key in Treta yuga. Rituals is the convenient way in Dwapara yuga. In Kali yuga (present), charity is the only way to attain inner peace.
quotes.transliteration:
tapaḥ paraṃ kṛtayuge tretāyāṃ jñānamucyate |
dvāpare yajñamityūcuḥ dānamekaṃ kalau yuge ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धारणाद्धर्म इत्याहुर्धर्मो धारयते प्रजाः ।
यत् स्याद्धारणसंयुक्तं स धर्म इति निश्चयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This verse explains the etymology of the word "Dharma" in sanskrit. Dharma can be analyzed as 'some that supports'. Dharma clearly is the strength that supports the society and citizens. Anything that gives such honest strength can be thus called 'Dharma'.
quotes.transliteration:
dhāraṇāddharma ityāhurdharmo dhārayate prajāḥ |
yat syāddhāraṇasaṃyuktaṃ sa dharma iti niścayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोकान्तरसुखं पुण्यं तपोदानसमुद्भवम् |
सन्ततिः शुद्धवंश्या हि परत्रेह च शर्मणे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If you want to be happy in your afterlife, then you have to do a lot of penance and charity in this life. But it is a good son (or daughter) brought up in righteous upbringing that can give you happiness both in this world as well as the after life.
quotes.transliteration:
lokāntarasukhaṃ puṇyaṃ tapodānasamudbhavam |
santatiḥ śuddhavaṃśyā hi paratreha ca śarmaṇe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दश धर्मं न जानंति धृतराष्ट्र निबोध तान् ।
मत्तः प्रमत्तः उन्मत्तः श्रान्तः क्रुद्धो बुभुक्षितः ॥
त्वरमाणश्च लुब्धश्च भीतः कामी च ते दश ।
तस्मादेतेषु सर्वेषु न प्रसज्जेत पण्डितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Dhrutarashtra! the following ten kind of people will not be able to tell the right from wrong. They are someone mad with pride, insane, intoxicated, very tired, angry, hungry, someone in a hurry, greedy, frightened and the lascivious. Hence a wise man has to stay clear of them.
quotes.transliteration:
daśa dharmaṃ na jānaṃti dhṛtarāṣṭra nibodha tān |
mattaḥ pramattaḥ unmattaḥ śrāntaḥ kruddho bubhukṣitaḥ ||
tvaramāṇaśca lubdhaśca bhītaḥ kāmī ca te daśa |
tasmādeteṣu sarveṣu na prasajjeta paṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नंदंति मंदाः श्रियमप्यनित्यां
परं विषीदंति विपद्गृहीताः ।
विवेकदृष्ट्या चरतां नराणां
श्रियो न किंचित् विपदो न किंचित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: nandanti mandAH shriyamapi anityAm Idiots rejoice when they get wealth and grieve when they have to face troubles. For the wise men, what is wealth and what are troubles.
quotes.transliteration:
naṃdaṃti maṃdāḥ śriyamapyanityāṃ
paraṃ viṣīdaṃti vipadgṛhītāḥ |
vivekadṛṣṭyā caratāṃ narāṇāṃ
śriyo na kiṃcit vipado na kiṃcit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कृत्वा पापं हि सन्तप्य तस्मात्पात्प्रमुच्यते ।
नैवं कुर्यां पुनरिति निवृत्त्या पूयते तु सः
quotes.englishTranslation: A sinner will get his redemption from the sin and becomes pure if he truly feels remorse for his act and wows not to repeat it ever.
quotes.transliteration:
kṛtvā pāpaṃ hi santapya tasmātpātpramucyate |
naivaṃ kuryāṃ punariti nivṛttyā pūyate tu saḥ

quotes.sanskritText:
चीराणि किं पथि न संति दिशंति भिक्षां
नैवांघ्रिपाः परभृतः सरितोऽप्यशुष्यन् ।
रुद्धा गुहाः किमजितोऽवति नोपसन्नान्
कस्माद् भजन्ति कवयो धनदुर्मदांधान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Can we not find torn clothes on the road to cover ourselves? Don't the trees give us food to eat as alms? The good rivers; can they not give us water to drink? Have the caves been closed? Will the Lord not reach out to those who seek his refuge? In spite of all the basic needs taken care of by nature, why do the scholars have to praise the rich people who are blinded by the intoxication of wealth?
quotes.transliteration:
cīrāṇi kiṃ pathi na saṃti diśaṃti bhikṣāṃ
naivāṃghripāḥ parabhṛtaḥ saritoऽpyaśuṣyan |
ruddhā guhāḥ kimajitoऽvati nopasannān
kasmād bhajanti kavayo dhanadurmadāṃdhān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इतः कोन्वस्ति मूढात्मा यस्तु स्वार्थे प्रमाद्यति ।
दुर्लभं मानुषं देहं प्राप्य तत्रापि पौरुषम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is indeed a fool who does not contemplate on salvation in spite of obtaining a human birth (especially being a man).
quotes.transliteration:
itaḥ konvasti mūḍhātmā yastu svārthe pramādyati |
durlabhaṃ mānuṣaṃ dehaṃ prāpya tatrāpi pauruṣam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दाराः पुत्रेषु रताः पुत्राः पितृधनपरिग्रहव्यग्राः ।
रोदनशरणा जननी परलोकगतस्य को बंधुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The wives are worried about the sons. The sons are thinking about their inheritance. The mother is resigned to grief. Who is there to help the man who has gone to the after worlds?
quotes.transliteration:
dārāḥ putreṣu ratāḥ putrāḥ pitṛdhanaparigrahavyagrāḥ |
rodanaśaraṇā jananī paralokagatasya ko baṃdhuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
होमार्थैर्विधिवत्प्रदानविधिना सद्विप्रवृंदार्चनैः
यज्ञैर्भूरिसुदक्षिणैः सुविहितैः संप्राप्यते यत्फलम् |
सत्तीर्थाश्रमवास होमनियमैश्चान्द्रायणाद्यैः कृतैः
पुंभिस्तत्फलमाहवे एनिहतैः संप्राप्यते तत्क्षणात् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Soldiers who die in a war get the same credit of good karma as any of the below - religious rituals being performed by very learned brahmins, rituals that have been performed by generously giving money, visiting many holy places (temples and ashrams), performing various ritualistic penances. All these are comparable to a person being ready to lay down his life to protect other people of his state.
quotes.transliteration:
homārthairvidhivatpradānavidhinā sadvipravṛṃdārcanaiḥ
yajñairbhūrisudakṣiṇaiḥ suvihitaiḥ saṃprāpyate yatphalam |
sattīrthāśramavāsa homaniyamaiścāndrāyaṇādyaiḥ kṛtaiḥ
puṃbhistatphalamāhave enihataiḥ saṃprāpyate tatkṣaṇāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रसादो निष्फलो यस्य क्रोधश्चापि निरर्थकः |
न तं भर्तारमिच्छन्ति षण्डं पतिमिव स्त्रियः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A person is to be considered useless if his being pleased does not help you, or his wrath is not potent enough. He is as useless as an impotent husband for a wife. No will want to serve such a master
quotes.transliteration:
prasādo niṣphalo yasya krodhaścāpi nirarthakaḥ |
na taṃ bhartāramicchanti ṣaṇḍaṃ patimiva striyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थानामर्जनं कार्यं वर्धनं रक्षणं तथा ।
भक्ष्यमाणो निरादायः सुमेरुरपि हीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth must be earned, grown, protected. If one only consumes wealth without earning, then even the Mt Sumeru (mountain of gold) will not be enough.
quotes.transliteration:
arthānāmarjanaṃ kāryaṃ vardhanaṃ rakṣaṇaṃ tathā |
bhakṣyamāṇo nirādāyaḥ sumerurapi hīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आलस्यं यदि न भवेज्जगत्यनर्थः
को न स्याद् बहु धनको बहुश्रुतो वा ।
आलस्यादियमवनिः ससागरांता
संपूर्णा नरपशुभिश्च निर्धनैश्च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Alasyam yadi na bhavet jagati anarthaH If there were no such thing as laziness in this world, who would be poor? Every one would be either rich or very learned. Because of laziness, this world (including the seas) is filled with animal like men (uneducated) and penniless people.
quotes.transliteration:
ālasyaṃ yadi na bhavejjagatyanarthaḥ
ko na syād bahu dhanako bahuśruto vā |
ālasyādiyamavaniḥ sasāgarāṃtā
saṃpūrṇā narapaśubhiśca nirdhanaiśca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संगमयति विद्यैव नीचगापि नरं सरित् ।
समुद्रमपि दुर्दशं नृपं भाग्यमतः परम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like water of any river flows lower and lower towards the sea, a rogue even if he is well educated will seek a bad king to serve. The result of both actions is the same - they get destroyed (both river and the rogue).
quotes.transliteration:
saṃgamayati vidyaiva nīcagāpi naraṃ sarit |
samudramapi durdaśaṃ nṛpaṃ bhāgyamataḥ param ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनुचरति शशांकं राहुदोषेऽपि तारा
पतति च वनवृक्षे याति भूमि लता च |
त्यजति न च करेणुः पंकलग्नं गजेन्द्रं
व्रजतु चरतु धर्मं भर्तृनाथा हि नार्यः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The stars follow the moon even when Rahu engulfs him (eclipse). When a tree falls down in a forest, the creepers fall along with it. When the tusker gets its foot entangled with lotus plants, the female elephants do not abandon it. It is in the nature of a virtuous wife to follow her husband in glory or misery alike.
quotes.transliteration:
anucarati śaśāṃkaṃ rāhudoṣeऽpi tārā
patati ca vanavṛkṣe yāti bhūmi latā ca |
tyajati na ca kareṇuḥ paṃkalagnaṃ gajendraṃ
vrajatu caratu dharmaṃ bhartṛnāthā hi nāryaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुरुवो बहवः सन्ति शिष्यवित्तापहारकाः ।
दुर्लभः स गुरुर्लोके शिष्यचित्तापहारकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are many teachers in this world who can steal the student's money. It is difficult to find one who can capture their mind and imagination.
quotes.transliteration:
guruvo bahavaḥ santi śiṣyavittāpahārakāḥ |
durlabhaḥ sa gururloke śiṣyacittāpahārakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अयशस्यमनायुष्यं परदाराभिमर्शनम् ।
अर्थक्षयकरं घोरम् पापस्य च पुनर्भवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Touching another man's wife is a great sin. It will lead to infamy. It will diminish your life and wealth. It will be dealt with like any other heinous crime (in hell).
quotes.transliteration:
ayaśasyamanāyuṣyaṃ paradārābhimarśanam |
arthakṣayakaraṃ ghoram pāpasya ca punarbhavam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अयुक्तचारं दुर्दर्शनमस्वाधीनं नराधिपम् |
वर्जयन्ति नरा दूरान्नदीपङ्कमिव द्विपाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king does not have spies, offers ill advice to the subjects, not in control of himself - then his subjects will abandon him just like elephants stays clear of the mire in the river.
quotes.transliteration:
ayuktacāraṃ durdarśanamasvādhīnaṃ narādhipam |
varjayanti narā dūrānnadīpaṅkamiva dvipāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मूढ जहीहि धनागमतृष्णां
कुरु सद्बुद्धिं मनसि वितृष्णाम् ।
यल्लभसे निजकर्मोपत्तं
वित्तं तेन विनोदय चित्तम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: yallabhase nijakarmopAttam Oh fool! give up the greed for wealth. Keep faith in the truth. Make your mind free from greed. You will get what you are entitled to because of your prior actions. Be happy with that.
quotes.transliteration:
mūḍha jahīhi dhanāgamatṛṣṇāṃ
kuru sadbuddhiṃ manasi vitṛṣṇām |
yallabhase nijakarmopattaṃ
vittaṃ tena vinodaya cittam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
केशावधि नखराग्रादिदमंतः पूतिगन्धसंपूर्णम् ।
बहिरपि जानन् चंदनकर्पूराद्यैर्विलेपयति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: From the hair to the toenails; the body is filled with stink. Even after knowing so, people mask it by applying sandal and camphor. Stress is on the futility of changing external appearances when the inner core is rotten.
quotes.transliteration:
keśāvadhi nakharāgrādidamaṃtaḥ pūtigandhasaṃpūrṇam |
bahirapi jānan caṃdanakarpūrādyairvilepayati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भानुः सकृद्युक्ततुरंग एव
रात्रिं दिवं गन्धवहः प्रयाति ।
शेषः सदैवाहितभूमिभारः
षष्ठांशवृत्तेरपि धर्म एषः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no rest for the Sun who has taken it up on him to pour light on this world every day. The wind blows day and night. Adi Shesha supports the weight of the earth all the time. Like wise, there is no rest for a king who takes one-sixth of everyone's earnings. He has to work tirelessly towards protecting his subjects.
quotes.transliteration:
bhānuḥ sakṛdyuktaturaṃga eva
rātriṃ divaṃ gandhavahaḥ prayāti |
śeṣaḥ sadaivāhitabhūmibhāraḥ
ṣaṣṭhāṃśavṛtterapi dharma eṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शीतवातातपक्लेशान् सहंते ये पराश्रिताः ।
तदंशेनापि मेधावी तपस्तप्त्वा सुखी भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person may face a lot of miseries when he is dependent on others - cold, wind and heat. If he is willing to even withstand a fraction of that discomfort and perform penances, he can be very happy and independent.
quotes.transliteration:
śītavātātapakleśān sahaṃte ye parāśritāḥ |
tadaṃśenāpi medhāvī tapastaptvā sukhī bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतिवादांस्तितिक्षेत नावमन्येत कंचन |
न चेमं देहमाश्रित्य वैरं कुर्वीत केनचित् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must be patient even when some one provokes you with harsh words. One must never humiliate any one. There is no reason why one must beget enmity with any one for the sake of this impermanent body.
quotes.transliteration:
ativādāṃstitikṣeta nāvamanyeta kaṃcana |
na cemaṃ dehamāśritya vairaṃ kurvīta kenacit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शान्तिखड्गः करे यस्य दुर्जनः किं करिष्यति ।
अतृणे पतितो वह्निः स्वयमेवोपशाम्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What harm can a rogue inflict on someone who is armed with the sword of patience? When a fire falls over a barren land with no grass, it dies by itself. A wicked man's anger often feeds on the victim's impatience and reciprocation.
quotes.transliteration:
śāntikhaḍgaḥ kare yasya durjanaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati |
atṛṇe patito vahniḥ svayamevopaśāmyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यस्य वचनं श्रेयः सत्यादपि हितं वदेत् ।
यद्भूतहितमत्यन्तं एतत्सत्यं मतं मम ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is good to tell the truth. But it is better to tell what is good for others. I (Sanathkumar) believe that what is good for all people in the absolute sense has to be the truth as well.
quotes.transliteration:
satyasya vacanaṃ śreyaḥ satyādapi hitaṃ vadet |
yadbhūtahitamatyantaṃ etatsatyaṃ mataṃ mama ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शरीरस्य गुणानां च दूरमत्यंतमंतरम् ।
शरीरं क्षणविध्वंसि कल्पांतस्थायिनो गुणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is a lot of difference between body and the qualities it possesses. The body is short lived. But its nature does not die till the end of time. People still talk about the qualities (good or bad) of people long after they are dead.
quotes.transliteration:
śarīrasya guṇānāṃ ca dūramatyaṃtamaṃtaram |
śarīraṃ kṣaṇavidhvaṃsi kalpāṃtasthāyino guṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सस्यलंपटबलीवर्दो न शक्यो वारयितुं
अन्यप्रसक्तकलत्रं न शक्यं वारयितुम् |
द्यूतप्रसक्तमनुष्यो न शक्यो वारयितुं
योऽपि स्वाभाविकदोषो न शक्यो वारयितुम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One can not control a bull that is running towards green grass eager to eat it. One can not control a wife who is interested in some one else. One can not control a man who is addicted to gambling. One can not reform a person whose innate character is to be wicked. He is incorrigible.
quotes.transliteration:
sasyalaṃpaṭabalīvardo na śakyo vārayituṃ
anyaprasaktakalatraṃ na śakyaṃ vārayitum |
dyūtaprasaktamanuṣyo na śakyo vārayituṃ
yoऽpi svābhāvikadoṣo na śakyo vārayitum ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गिरीन्द्रशिखराकारो लीलयोन्मूलितद्रुमः ।
करिणीस्पर्शसंमोहादालानं याति वारणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A bull elephant that looks like a mountain and capable of uprooting trees just for fun can be chained (and tamed) because of his weakness - longing for the touch of the cow elephant.
quotes.transliteration:
girīndraśikharākāro līlayonmūlitadrumaḥ |
kariṇīsparśasaṃmohādālānaṃ yāti vāraṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकृत्वा पौरुषं या श्रीर्विकासिन्यपि किं तया ।
जरद्गवोऽपि चाश्नाति दैवादुपगतं तृणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you are accumulating wealth without any effort from your side, then what is there to be proud about. Even an old cow will graze happily if it come across a green pasture by luck.
quotes.transliteration:
akṛtvā pauruṣaṃ yā śrīrvikāsinyapi kiṃ tayā |
jaradgavoऽpi cāśnāti daivādupagataṃ tṛṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पथि धावन्निह पतितो रोदिष्यंबाकरावलंबाय ।
पतितोद्धारणसमये किं न स्मरसि त्वमात्मानम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! Krishna, you are playing the role of a small kid who has fallen down while running and is crying to get mother's attention. You want her to come and help you get up. Do you not remember your own plight when your devotees cry for help when they are stuck in this world?
quotes.transliteration:
pathi dhāvanniha patito rodiṣyaṃbākarāvalaṃbāya |
patitoddhāraṇasamaye kiṃ na smarasi tvamātmānam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नृशंसमनृशंसं वा प्रजारक्षणकारणात् |
पातकं वा सदोषं वा कर्तव्यं रक्षता सता ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king has to do his duty of protecting his subjects at any cost. He must not be bothered if his actions are cruel (towards enemies) or not, whether the action is a sin or not.
quotes.transliteration:
nṛśaṃsamanṛśaṃsaṃ vā prajārakṣaṇakāraṇāt |
pātakaṃ vā sadoṣaṃ vā kartavyaṃ rakṣatā satā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जडसंगेऽपिन लिप्ताः श्रीसद्भावेऽपि नोत्तरलाः ।
अंभोजकोरका इव विज्ञा विकसन्ति विश्वस्मै ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when wise men stay with idiots, they will stay sane. Even when they have wealth, they will not lose their mind. Just like a lotus bud will maintain its cleanliness even amidst mire. It will stay steady even when it has so much beauty. It will bloom beautifully when sun shines on it.
quotes.transliteration:
jaḍasaṃgeऽpina liptāḥ śrīsadbhāveऽpi nottaralāḥ |
aṃbhojakorakā iva vijñā vikasanti viśvasmai ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शिक्षाक्षयं गच्छति कालपर्यायात्
सुबद्धमूला निपतन्ति पादपाः ।
जलं जलस्थानगतं च शुष्यति
हुतं च दत्तं च तथैव शिष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Overtime one forgets what he has learned. Great trees may also fall one day. A great lake may also get dried. But the good karma earned by doing charity or sacrifice will never get diminished.
quotes.transliteration:
śikṣākṣayaṃ gacchati kālaparyāyāt
subaddhamūlā nipatanti pādapāḥ |
jalaṃ jalasthānagataṃ ca śuṣyati
hutaṃ ca dattaṃ ca tathaiva śiṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ध्यानाभ्यासवशीकृतेन मनसा तन्निर्गुणं निष्क्रियं
ज्योतिः किंछन योगिनो यदि परं पश्यन्ति पश्यन्तु ते ।
अस्माकं तु तदेव लोचनचमत्काराय भूयाच्चिरं
कालिंदीपुलिनोदरे किमपि यन्नीलं महो धावति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the saints see God in the form of a formless, quality-less, activity-less entity or a light. This may be possible since they have mastered the process of meditation. Let them have their ways. But for us, let God be always seen as that blue boy running and playing on the sands of Yamuna (Sri Krishna).
quotes.transliteration:
dhyānābhyāsavaśīkṛtena manasā tannirguṇaṃ niṣkriyaṃ
jyotiḥ kiṃchana yogino yadi paraṃ paśyanti paśyantu te |
asmākaṃ tu tadeva locanacamatkārāya bhūyācciraṃ
kāliṃdīpulinodare kimapi yannīlaṃ maho dhāvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृतोऽप्यर्थं न मोक्ष्यामि बद्ध्वा नेष्यामि मूर्धनि ।
इति चेत् सुदृढो लोभः पात्रे देयमशंकितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: As long as greed has control of me, I will be thinking of carrying all this wealth on my head even after death. Hence I must get rid of greed and start developing a charitable attitude.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtoऽpyarthaṃ na mokṣyāmi baddhvā neṣyāmi mūrdhani |
iti cet sudṛḍho lobhaḥ pātre deyamaśaṃkitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परनिंदासु पाण्डित्यं स्वेषु कार्येष्वनुद्यमः |
प्रद्वेषश्च गुणज्ञेषु पंथानो ह्यापदां त्रयः ||
सुभाषितसुधानिधि
quotes.englishTranslation: There are three ways one can invite trouble - by attaining mastery in criticizing others, by being lethargic in one's own work and by being an enemy of the virtuous.
quotes.transliteration:
paraniṃdāsu pāṇḍityaṃ sveṣu kāryeṣvanudyamaḥ |
pradveṣaśca guṇajñeṣu paṃthāno hyāpadāṃ trayaḥ ||
subhāṣitasudhānidhi

quotes.sanskritText:
यच्च कामसुखं लोके यच्च दिव्यं महत्सुखम् |
तृष्णाक्षयसुखस्यैते नार्हतः षोडशीयं कलाम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The happiness got by satisfying your desires, or the happiness got in heaven does not even match up to a sixteenth portion of the real happiness that is attained through limiting your desires.
quotes.transliteration:
yacca kāmasukhaṃ loke yacca divyaṃ mahatsukham |
tṛṣṇākṣayasukhasyaite nārhataḥ ṣoḍaśīyaṃ kalām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रायो मुमुक्षवस्तेषां केचनैव द्विजोत्तम ।
मुमुक्षूणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिन्मुच्येत सिध्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Of those who get old, only a few ever think of salvation. Oh good brahmin! of thousands of people who think about salvation, only a very few get enlightened and finally attain salvation.
quotes.transliteration:
prāyo mumukṣavasteṣāṃ kecanaiva dvijottama |
mumukṣūṇāṃ sahasreṣu kaścinmucyeta sidhyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दूरीकरोति कुमतिं विमलीकरोति
चेतश्चिरन्तनमघं चुलुकीकरोति ।
भूतेषु किञ्च करुणां बहुलीकरोति
सङ्गः सतां किमु मङ्गलमातनोति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Company of virtuous people will have the following effect on you: drives away rogue thoughts, cleanses the mind, engulfs the sins, amplifies the compassion. Their company will be beneficial for every one.
quotes.transliteration:
dūrīkaroti kumatiṃ vimalīkaroti
cetaścirantanamaghaṃ culukīkaroti |
bhūteṣu kiñca karuṇāṃ bahulīkaroti
saṅgaḥ satāṃ kimu maṅgalamātanoti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रामे प्रव्रजनं बलेर्नियमनं पाण्डोः सुतानां वनः
वृष्णीनां निधनं नलस्य नृपते राज्यात् परिभ्रंशनं ।
कारागारनिषेवणं च मरणं संचिंत्य लंकेश्वरे
सर्वः कालवशेन नश्यति नरः को वा परित्रायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Rama had to live in a forest. Bali had to go to the underworld. The Pandavas (sons of Pandu) had to live in exile. The Yadavas died due to infighting. Nala was made to leave his own kingdom and live like a destitute. Vibhishana was imprisoned. Death met Ravana, the king of Lanka. Everything is destroyed in time. Which human has been able to escape the fate?
quotes.transliteration:
rāme pravrajanaṃ balerniyamanaṃ pāṇḍoḥ sutānāṃ vanaḥ
vṛṣṇīnāṃ nidhanaṃ nalasya nṛpate rājyāt paribhraṃśanaṃ |
kārāgāraniṣevaṇaṃ ca maraṇaṃ saṃciṃtya laṃkeśvare
sarvaḥ kālavaśena naśyati naraḥ ko vā paritrāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
काकः कृष्णः पिकः कृष्णः को भेदः पिककाकयोः ।
वसन्तसमये प्राप्ते काकः काकः पिकः पिकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The crow is black. The nightingale is also black. Who can tell the differnce between them both? But when it is spring time, it becomes very clear that a crow is a crow and the nightingale is a nightingale.
quotes.transliteration:
kākaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pikaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ ko bhedaḥ pikakākayoḥ |
vasantasamaye prāpte kākaḥ kākaḥ pikaḥ pikaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वित्तमेव कलौ नृणां जन्माचारगुणॊदयः |
धर्मन्यायव्यवस्थायां कारणं बलमेव हि ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In the epoch of Kali (present age) money assumes too much importance. It is what determines the nobility of the family you are born in, your character and all your qualities. He who has the money will be considered noble, of good character and believed to have good qualities. In the global system of morality, economy and justice - the mighty will always have their way. They derive their might from the money they possess.
quotes.transliteration:
vittameva kalau nṛṇāṃ janmācāraguṇaॊdayaḥ |
dharmanyāyavyavasthāyāṃ kāraṇaṃ balameva hi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परस्मिन् बंधुवर्गे वा मित्रे द्वेष्ये रिपौ तथा ।
आत्मवद्वर्तितव्यं हि दयैषा परिकीर्तिता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who treats everyone like himself can be called as compassionate. The word 'everyone' includes strangers, relatives, friends, those who hate him and enemies
quotes.transliteration:
parasmin baṃdhuvarge vā mitre dveṣye ripau tathā |
ātmavadvartitavyaṃ hi dayaiṣā parikīrtitā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जन्मैस्वर्यश्रुतश्रीभिरेधमानमदः पुमान् ।
नैवार्हत्यभिधातुम् वै त्वामकिंचनगोचरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A good birth, wealth, education, money - all intoxicate men. Such a person can not see God. God can be seen only by the poor.
quotes.transliteration:
janmaisvaryaśrutaśrībhiredhamānamadaḥ pumān |
naivārhatyabhidhātum vai tvāmakiṃcanagocaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथामयोऽसाधुचिकित्सितो नृणां
पुनः पुनः सन्तुदति प्ररोहन् ।
एवं मनोऽपक्वकषायकर्म
कुयोगिनं विध्यति सर्वसंगम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A disease not treated completely, always grows back again and again. Just like that the mind of bad yogi is plagued with consequences of his actions from previous births. This leads him to be attached to everything material.
quotes.transliteration:
yathāmayoऽsādhucikitsito nṛṇāṃ
punaḥ punaḥ santudati prarohan |
evaṃ manoऽpakvakaṣāyakarma
kuyoginaṃ vidhyati sarvasaṃgam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाराजके जनपदे स्वकं भवति कस्यचित् |
मत्स्या इव नरा नित्यं भक्षयन्ति परस्परम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In the absence of the king, there is no body to settle the ownership of property (of the people). Everyday, people will eat(cheat) other people like the fish do.
quotes.transliteration:
nārājake janapade svakaṃ bhavati kasyacit |
matsyā iva narā nityaṃ bhakṣayanti parasparam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सेवध्वं विबुधास्तमंधकरिपुं माक्लिश्यतान्यश्रुते
यस्मादत्र परत्र च त्रिजगति त्राता स एकः शिवः।
आयाते नियतेर्वशात् स्वविषये कालात् करालाद्भये
कुत्र व्याकरणं क्व तर्ककलहः काव्यश्रमः क्वापि वा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh scholars! Serve the only Lord who is can get rid of all darkness. He is the only one who can help you in this world, in the afterlife and in all the three worlds. He is Lord Shiva. When death comes to take you away you will be really scared. How will your knowledge of grammar, logic or poetry help?
quotes.transliteration:
sevadhvaṃ vibudhāstamaṃdhakaripuṃ mākliśyatānyaśrute
yasmādatra paratra ca trijagati trātā sa ekaḥ śivaḥ|
āyāte niyatervaśāt svaviṣaye kālāt karālādbhaye
kutra vyākaraṇaṃ kva tarkakalahaḥ kāvyaśramaḥ kvāpi vā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
फलमूलाशनैर्मेध्यैः मुन्यन्नानां च भोजनैः ।
न तत्फलमवाप्नोति यन्मांसपरिवर्जनात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One gets more results from giving up meat and other non-veg food than by eating just fruits and roots like the saints.
quotes.transliteration:
phalamūlāśanairmedhyaiḥ munyannānāṃ ca bhojanaiḥ |
na tatphalamavāpnoti yanmāṃsaparivarjanāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्वलति चलितेन्धनोग्निः विप्रकृतः पन्नगः फणां
कुरुते ।
प्रायः स्वं महिमानं कोपात् प्रतिपद्यते जन्तुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one moves the wood in the ritual fire, it burns with greater vigor. If you provoke a serpent it will get agitated and open its hood. A little bit of anger becomes a catalyst for people to exhibit their power to the fullest extent.
quotes.transliteration:
jvalati calitendhanogniḥ viprakṛtaḥ pannagaḥ phaṇāṃ
kurute |
prāyaḥ svaṃ mahimānaṃ kopāt pratipadyate jantuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मानमनभिज्ञाय विवेक्तुं योऽन्यदिच्छति ।
तेन भौतेन किं वाच्यं प्रश्नेऽस्मिन् को भवानिति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One wants to learn about the world without understanding the self. What will you expect to hear from such a person if you ask "Who are you?".
quotes.transliteration:
ātmānamanabhijñāya vivektuṃ yoऽnyadicchati |
tena bhautena kiṃ vācyaṃ praśneऽsmin ko bhavāniti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
माता मित्रं पिता चेति स्वभावात् त्रितयं हितम् |
कार्यकारणतश्चान्ये भवन्ति हितबुद्धयः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Mother, friend and father - these are the only three people who will help you by nature. All other become well-wishers only to serve their own purpose.
quotes.transliteration:
mātā mitraṃ pitā ceti svabhāvāt tritayaṃ hitam |
kāryakāraṇataścānye bhavanti hitabuddhayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनंतपारं किल शब्दशास्त्रं
स्वल्पं तथायुर्बहवश्च विघ्नाः ।
सारं ततो ग्राह्यमपास्य फल्गु
हंसैर्यथा क्षीरमिवांबुमध्यात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The vastness of knowledge (written in scriptures) is huge. This life is short and filled with obstacles. Hence it is not possible to read them all. One must attempt to understand the scriptures with most value first and abandon the rest much like how a swan filters milk from water.
quotes.transliteration:
anaṃtapāraṃ kila śabdaśāstraṃ
svalpaṃ tathāyurbahavaśca vighnāḥ |
sāraṃ tato grāhyamapāsya phalgu
haṃsairyathā kṣīramivāṃbumadhyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सामवादाः सकोपस्य तस्य प्रत्युत दीपकाः |
प्रतप्तस्येव सहसा सर्पिषस्तोयबिन्दवः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When one is angry we should not try to placate him by talking sensibly and logically. Such talk will only aggravate his anger since it would show that his anger is not justified. If you sprinkle water over hot oil (or ghee) in the pan, it will agitate oil more.
quotes.transliteration:
sāmavādāḥ sakopasya tasya pratyuta dīpakāḥ |
prataptasyeva sahasā sarpiṣastoyabindavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न कामयेऽहं गतिमीश्वरात् परा-
मष्टर्द्धियुक्तामपुनर्भवं वा ।
आर्तिं प्रपद्येऽखिलदुःखभाजा-
मन्तः स्थितो येन भवन्त्यदुःखाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I do not ask for salvation, death or wealth from god. All I ask to be among people with misery so that I can shoulder their misery and alleviate their pain.
quotes.transliteration:
na kāmayeऽhaṃ gatimīśvarāt parā-
maṣṭarddhiyuktāmapunarbhavaṃ vā |
ārtiṃ prapadyeऽkhiladuḥkhabhājā-
mantaḥ sthito yena bhavantyaduḥkhāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न संशयं प्रपद्येत नाकस्मादप्रियं वदेत् ।
नाहितं नानृतं चैव न स्तेनः स्यान्न वार्धुषी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not create confusion. One must not utter harsh words even by mistake. Our words must not hurt others, but not be a lie. One must *not* make a living by stealing or lending money for interest.
quotes.transliteration:
na saṃśayaṃ prapadyeta nākasmādapriyaṃ vadet |
nāhitaṃ nānṛtaṃ caiva na stenaḥ syānna vārdhuṣī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लब्ध्वाऽपि सुबहून् लाभान् चिरं भुक्त्वा सुखान्यपि ।
रिक्तहस्तश्च नग्नश्च यास्यामि मुषितो यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You may get all your wishes and live your entire life in surplus. You may have enjoyed every pleasure. At the end, you go empty handed and naked - just like anybody who has lost everything.
quotes.transliteration:
labdhvāऽpi subahūn lābhān ciraṃ bhuktvā sukhānyapi |
riktahastaśca nagnaśca yāsyāmi muṣito yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निर्वनो वध्यते व्याघ्रो निर्व्याघ्रो छिद्यते वनम् |
तस्माद्व्याघ्रो वनं रक्षेत् वनं व्याघ्रं च पालयेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Without the forest, the tiger can not survive. Without the tiger in the forest, the forest will get destroyed (by the people). Hence the tiger must protect the forest and forest must protect the tiger (and other animals)
quotes.transliteration:
nirvano vadhyate vyāghro nirvyāghro chidyate vanam |
tasmādvyāghro vanaṃ rakṣet vanaṃ vyāghraṃ ca pālayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणेषु यत्नः पुरुषेण कार्यः
न किंचिदप्राप्यतमं गुणानाम् ।
गुणप्रकर्षादुडुपेन शंभोः
अलंघ्यमुल्लंघितमुत्तमाङ्गम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must strive to collect as many good qualities as possible. This is not easy and will involve lot of effort. But one must persevere. Look what the moon has achieved. By the virtue of all his good qualities, he is able to secure a place on top of the head of Lord Shiva (who by himself is insurmountable).
quotes.transliteration:
guṇeṣu yatnaḥ puruṣeṇa kāryaḥ
na kiṃcidaprāpyatamaṃ guṇānām |
guṇaprakarṣāduḍupena śaṃbhoḥ
alaṃghyamullaṃghitamuttamāṅgam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असंमाने तपोवृद्धिः संमानाच्च तपःक्षयः ।
पूजया पुण्यहानिः स्यान्निंदया सद्गतिर्भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you do penance in isolation your penance gets stronger. When you accept to be respected by followers, then the power of your penance decreases. When people worship you then your good karma erodes. When they do not, you are on your way to a good death.
quotes.transliteration:
asaṃmāne tapovṛddhiḥ saṃmānācca tapaḥkṣayaḥ |
pūjayā puṇyahāniḥ syānniṃdayā sadgatirbhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रकीर्णे विषयारण्ये धावन्तं विप्रमाथिनम् ।
ज्ञानांकुशेन कुर्वीत वश्यमिंद्रियदंतिनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the forest of sensory pleasures, the mad elephant of sense organs is running wild and destroying everything. One must control that elephant with the hook of knowledge.
quotes.transliteration:
prakīrṇe viṣayāraṇye dhāvantaṃ vipramāthinam |
jñānāṃkuśena kurvīta vaśyamiṃdriyadaṃtinam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अश्वमेधसहस्राणि सत्यं च तुलया धृतम् ।
अश्वमेधसहस्रस्य सत्यमेवातिरिच्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a thousand Ashvamedha yajna (where a king defeats all kingdoms to which his horse goes to) are weighed against the power of truth, truth it self will prove worthier.
quotes.transliteration:
aśvamedhasahasrāṇi satyaṃ ca tulayā dhṛtam |
aśvamedhasahasrasya satyamevātiricyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नितरां नीचोस्मीति त्वं खेदं कूप मा कदापि कृथाः |
अत्यन्तसरसहृदयो यतः परेषां गुणग्रहिताऽसि ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh dear well! Just because you are physically so low, don't feel bad. You are not without virtues. You are very soft/kind (moist) hearted and receive people well. A well gives out water to any one who drops down a rope.
quotes.transliteration:
nitarāṃ nīcosmīti tvaṃ khedaṃ kūpa mā kadāpi kṛthāḥ |
atyantasarasahṛdayo yataḥ pareṣāṃ guṇagrahitāऽsi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तर्किका घटपटेति रटेयुः
शब्दिकाः खफछठेति पठेयुः ।
मादृशां तु सततं स्मरणियौ
चन्द्रचूडचरणौ रमणीयौ ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the logicians shout from top of their voices to prove others wrong. Sweet talkers will use rhyming letters. I am not interested in either. People like me are happy just remembering the refreshing foot of the Lord (the one with Moon on his head - Shiva).
quotes.transliteration:
tarkikā ghaṭapaṭeti raṭeyuḥ
śabdikāḥ khaphachaṭheti paṭheyuḥ |
mādṛśāṃ tu satataṃ smaraṇiyau
candracūḍacaraṇau ramaṇīyau ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृदुलं नवनीतमीरितं नवनीतादपि सज्जनस्य हृत् ।
तदिदं द्रवति स्वतापनात् परतापा द्द्रवते सतां पुनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Butter is soft - so people say.Softer that that is the heart of the virtuous. Because, butter melts when it is heated. The other (heart of the virtuous)melts even when some one else is heated.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛdulaṃ navanītamīritaṃ navanītādapi sajjanasya hṛt |
tadidaṃ dravati svatāpanāt paratāpā ddravate satāṃ punaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नेक्षेतोद्यंतमादित्यं नास्तंयंतं कदाचन |
नोपसृष्टं न वारिस्थं न मध्यं नभसो गतम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not look at the sun directly just when it is rising, or setting, or at noon (directly above one's head), or its reflection in the water. Each of these have their own reasons not to be watched with naked eyes.
quotes.transliteration:
nekṣetodyaṃtamādityaṃ nāstaṃyaṃtaṃ kadācana |
nopasṛṣṭaṃ na vāristhaṃ na madhyaṃ nabhaso gatam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तत्कर्म यन्न बंधाय सा विद्या या विमुक्तये ।
आयासायापरं कर्म विद्यान्न शिल्पनैपुणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That which does not create attachments is a good action. All other actions only drain your energy. Only the education that liberates is the right kind. All other education is like sculpturing.
quotes.transliteration:
tatkarma yanna baṃdhāya sā vidyā yā vimuktaye |
āyāsāyāparaṃ karma vidyānna śilpanaipuṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अदृढं च हतं ज्ञानं प्रमादेन हतं श्रुतम् ।
संदिग्धो हि हतो मंत्रो व्यग्रचित्तो हतो जपः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That knowledge that is not firm is useless. Education is ruined if you indulge. If you doubt, you will not get hold on the hymn (spell). A restless mind can not meditate (penance).
quotes.transliteration:
adṛḍhaṃ ca hataṃ jñānaṃ pramādena hataṃ śrutam |
saṃdigdho hi hato maṃtro vyagracitto hato japaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पङ्कोद्भवं न च विमर्दसहं सदैव
शैवालजालसहवासविडंबितं च |
इन्दीवरं दिनकरेण महाशयेन
संभावितं भवति भाजनमिन्दिरायाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A lotus has its origin in slush. It is very gentle. It is easily hurt even by small force. It is subject to ridicule since it accompanies dirty algae all the time. But it is blessed by the Sun God. A lotus blooms when his rays fall over it. It becomes so beautiful and strong that Goddess Lakshmi sits on it. If you have blessings from a virtuous man, then it does help a lot.
quotes.transliteration:
paṅkodbhavaṃ na ca vimardasahaṃ sadaiva
śaivālajālasahavāsaviḍaṃbitaṃ ca |
indīvaraṃ dinakareṇa mahāśayena
saṃbhāvitaṃ bhavati bhājanamindirāyāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न स्नानमाचरेद्भुक्त्वा नातुरो न महानिशि ।
न वासोभिः सहाजस्रं ना विज्ञाते जलाशये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This is a verse about when a person can take bath. Not after having food, not in a hurry, not at mid night, not with the clothes on, not in a unknown lake (pond or well).
quotes.transliteration:
na snānamācaredbhuktvā nāturo na mahāniśi |
na vāsobhiḥ sahājasraṃ nā vijñāte jalāśaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न योगि नैव सिद्धो वा न ज्ञानी सत्क्रियो नरः ।
कलिदावानलेनाद्य साधनं भस्मतां गतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the age of Kali (present time), his evil influence will burn everything like a wild fire. There are no people who are connected with the universe (God), no one who has accomplished, no one who has the knowledge, no one who is virtuous - no such people left. All the necessary infrastructure enabling people to achieve greatness is also burnt to ashes.
quotes.transliteration:
na yogi naiva siddho vā na jñānī satkriyo naraḥ |
kalidāvānalenādya sādhanaṃ bhasmatāṃ gatam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विधाय वैरं सामर्षे नरोऽरौ य उदासते |
प्रक्षिप्योदर्चिषं कक्षे शेरते तेऽभिमारुतम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: After hurting an enemy, one must expect him to be filled with ideas for revenge. Hence one must not relax. If he does relax, he will be destroyed. Much similar to a person setting fire to a stretch of grass and sleeping against the direction of wind. The wind will soon carry the fire to him and burn him down.
quotes.transliteration:
vidhāya vairaṃ sāmarṣe naroऽrau ya udāsate |
prakṣipyodarciṣaṃ kakṣe śerate teऽbhimārutam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आर्तो हि भिषजं पृष्ट्वा तदुक्तमनुतिष्ठते ।
तस्मिन् सविचिकित्सस्तु व्याधेराधिक्यमाप्नुयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one is ill, he goes to doctor and takes the prescribed medicine. But while doing that if he doubts the doctor, then the illness will aggravate. This perhaps due the body subconsciously rejecting the medicine.
quotes.transliteration:
ārto hi bhiṣajaṃ pṛṣṭvā taduktamanutiṣṭhate |
tasmin savicikitsastu vyādherādhikyamāpnuyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आकारैरिंगितैर्गत्या चेष्टया भाषणेन च।
नेत्रवक्त्रविकारैश्च लक्ष्यते अंतर्गतं मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One's inner feelings are revealed thus - his form, his intentions, his mannerisms (posture, walk, etc), his antics, his words, what his eyes follows and his facial expressions.
quotes.transliteration:
ākārairiṃgitairgatyā ceṣṭayā bhāṣaṇena ca|
netravaktravikāraiśca lakṣyate aṃtargataṃ manaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहिंसयैव भूतानां कार्यं श्रेयोऽनुशासनम् ।
वाक्चैव मधुराश्लक्ष्णा प्रयोज्या धर्ममिच्छता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you are training (animals or students), one must not use physical force (or hurt them). They must be won over by nice words and not by a cane.
quotes.transliteration:
ahiṃsayaiva bhūtānāṃ kāryaṃ śreyoऽnuśāsanam |
vākcaiva madhurāślakṣṇā prayojyā dharmamicchatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मय्येव जीर्णतां यातु यत्त्वयोपकृतं मम ।
नरः प्रत्युपकारार्थी विपत्तिमनुकांक्षति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I do not wish to repay the help that you have done to me. Any person who wishes to do so, is actually wishing that you get into trouble so that he can help you back.
quotes.transliteration:
mayyeva jīrṇatāṃ yātu yattvayopakṛtaṃ mama |
naraḥ pratyupakārārthī vipattimanukāṃkṣati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दण्डः शास्ति प्रजाः सर्वाः दण्ड एवाभिरक्षति ।
दण्डः सुप्तेषु जागर्ति दण्डं धर्मं विदुर्बुधाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Punishment is what helps the king rule his subjects justly. It protects all the subjects (from miscreants). It protects even those who are sleeping (from thieves). Hence the wise men think that punishment is an integral part of Dharma (moral code of life)
quotes.transliteration:
daṇḍaḥ śāsti prajāḥ sarvāḥ daṇḍa evābhirakṣati |
daṇḍaḥ supteṣu jāgarti daṇḍaṃ dharmaṃ vidurbudhāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वित्तं बंधुर्वयः कर्म विद्या भवति पञ्चमी ।
एतानि मान्यस्थानानि गरीयो यद्यदुत्तरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Money, relatives or friends, youth, actions and education - all these five make other people respect you. Also, they are arranged in their increasing order of influence.
quotes.transliteration:
vittaṃ baṃdhurvayaḥ karma vidyā bhavati pañcamī |
etāni mānyasthānāni garīyo yadyaduttaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकस्यापि न यः शक्तो मनसः संनिबर्हणे ।
महीं सागरपर्यंतां स कथं विजिगीषते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one can not control one thing - his own mind, then how can he hope to conquer the whole world surrounded by seas.
quotes.transliteration:
ekasyāpi na yaḥ śakto manasaḥ saṃnibarhaṇe |
mahīṃ sāgaraparyaṃtāṃ sa kathaṃ vijigīṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत् करोति परं धर्मं मुनिर्मूलफलाशनः |
तत्र राज्ञश्चतुर्भागः प्रजा धर्मेण रक्षतः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If the king is protecting his subjects properly (according to the moral code of life) then he is entitled to one fourth of the 'good-will' earned by sadhus doing penance in his kingdom.
quotes.transliteration:
yat karoti paraṃ dharmaṃ munirmūlaphalāśanaḥ |
tatra rājñaścaturbhāgaḥ prajā dharmeṇa rakṣataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विषयान् ध्यायतश्चित्तं विषयेषु विषज्जते ।
मामनुस्मर तच्चित्तं मय्येव प्रविलीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The mind that gives importance to sensory pleasures gets lost in the same. He who channelizes his mind towards me (God) will become one with me one day.
quotes.transliteration:
viṣayān dhyāyataścittaṃ viṣayeṣu viṣajjate |
māmanusmara taccittaṃ mayyeva pravilīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थस्य निश्चयो दृष्टो विचारेण हितोक्तितः ।
न स्नानेन न दानेन प्राणायामशतेन वा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It only when one deliberates on wise sayings that his belief consolidates into a conviction. Not by reciting the same thing over and over again after taking bath, or by resorting to charity or doing rhythmic breathing hundred times. Practice is much better than recital.
quotes.transliteration:
arthasya niścayo dṛṣṭo vicāreṇa hitoktitaḥ |
na snānena na dānena prāṇāyāmaśatena vā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शरीर पोषणार्थि सन् यु आत्मानं दिदृक्षति ।
ग्राहं दारुधिया धृत्वा नदीं तुर्तुं स गच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Some people are interested in nurturing their body well assuming that it make their soul also happy. Such people are kidding themselves; trying to cross a river on a crocodile taking that to be a log.
quotes.transliteration:
śarīra poṣaṇārthi san yu ātmānaṃ didṛkṣati |
grāhaṃ dārudhiyā dhṛtvā nadīṃ turtuṃ sa gacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इदं शरीरं परिणामपेशलं
पतत्यवश्यं श्लथसंधिजर्झरम् ।
किमौषधैः क्लिश्यसि मूढ दुर्मते
निरामयं कृष्णरसायनं पिब ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This body is now very old. All the worldly pleasures have been destroyed. This body will fall one day or another. Why are suffering by taking so many medicines? You should be drinking the concoction made of devotion about Lord Krishna.
quotes.transliteration:
idaṃ śarīraṃ pariṇāmapeśalaṃ
patatyavaśyaṃ ślathasaṃdhijarjharam |
kimauṣadhaiḥ kliśyasi mūḍha durmate
nirāmayaṃ kṛṣṇarasāyanaṃ piba ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महताऽपि प्रयत्नेन तमिस्रायां परामृशन् |
कृष्णशुक्लविवेकं हि न कश्चिदधिगच्छति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In pitch darkness, one can not know the difference between white or black no matter how hard you try. The same difficulty exists when trying to know right from wrong if you are in the darkness of ignorance.
quotes.transliteration:
mahatāऽpi prayatnena tamisrāyāṃ parāmṛśan |
kṛṣṇaśuklavivekaṃ hi na kaścidadhigacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अचिराधिष्ठितराज्यः शत्रुः प्रकृतिष्वरूढमूलत्वात् ।
नवसंरोपणशिथिलस्तरुरिव सुकरः समुद्धर्तुम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very easy to defeat an enemy who has usurped your kingdom just a few days ago. He would not have made his presence felt in the hearts of the people. Defeating him is as easy as uprooting a plant that we just planted recently.
quotes.transliteration:
acirādhiṣṭhitarājyaḥ śatruḥ prakṛtiṣvarūḍhamūlatvāt |
navasaṃropaṇaśithilastaruriva sukaraḥ samuddhartum ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यच्छन् जलमपि जलदो वल्लभतामेति सकललोकस्य ।
नित्यं प्रसारितकरो मित्रोऽपि न वीक्षितुं शक्यः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All the cloud does is to sprinkle water on the earth once in a while. Yet, the entire world likes him a lot. The Sun on the other hand works tirelessly giving out life giving rays all through out the day, every day. Yet, no one even looks him in the eye. In this unfair world, it is not enough to be helping others but one must also be presentable to win friends.
quotes.transliteration:
yacchan jalamapi jalado vallabhatāmeti sakalalokasya |
nityaṃ prasāritakaro mitroऽpi na vīkṣituṃ śakyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मूलमर्थस्य संरक्ष्यमेष कार्यविदां नयः |
मूले हि सति सिध्यन्ति गुणाः पुष्पफलोद्गमाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The real essence (root) of an endeavor must not be lost. This is what all the successful people say. The root is the heart of the tree. If the root is protected, only then can one expect to see flowers and fruits.
quotes.transliteration:
mūlamarthasya saṃrakṣyameṣa kāryavidāṃ nayaḥ |
mūle hi sati sidhyanti guṇāḥ puṣpaphalodgamāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अभक्ष्यपरिहारश्च संसर्गश्चाप्यनिन्दितैः ।
आचारेषु व्यवस्थानं शौचमित्यभिधीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not eat bad food. One must befriend virtuous people. One must always follow the rules of moral conduct. These three habits constitute moral cleanliness.
quotes.transliteration:
abhakṣyaparihāraśca saṃsargaścāpyaninditaiḥ |
ācāreṣu vyavasthānaṃ śaucamityabhidhīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः प्राप्य दुष्प्रापमिदं नरत्वं
धर्मं न यत्नेन करोति मूढः ।
क्लेशप्रबंधेन स लुब्धमब्धौ
चिन्तामणिं पातयति प्रमादात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is a fool who does not follow dharma even after having got this human form in the life. In fact, getting a human form is itself a very difficult task. It is like throwing away a rare gem (chintamani, that can solve all your problems) back into the sea. It is sheer madness.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ prāpya duṣprāpamidaṃ naratvaṃ
dharmaṃ na yatnena karoti mūḍhaḥ |
kleśaprabaṃdhena sa lubdhamabdhau
cintāmaṇiṃ pātayati pramādāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पंकैर्विना सरो भाति सदः खलजनैर्विना ।
कटुवर्णैर्विना काव्यं मानसं विषयैर्विना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A pond without mire, an assembly without wicked men, a poem without harsh letters are all beautiful. Likewise a mind without indulging in sensory pleasures is beautiful.
quotes.transliteration:
paṃkairvinā saro bhāti sadaḥ khalajanairvinā |
kaṭuvarṇairvinā kāvyaṃ mānasaṃ viṣayairvinā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आपत्काले तु संप्राप्ते शौचाचारं न चिंतयेत् ।
स्वयं समुद्धरेत् पश्चात् स्वस्थो धर्मं समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you are faced with a calamity, one must not be adamant on following the cleanliness procedures. One must save himself first and only then the Dharma must be followed.
quotes.transliteration:
āpatkāle tu saṃprāpte śaucācāraṃ na ciṃtayet |
svayaṃ samuddharet paścāt svastho dharmaṃ samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
