मृतः कीर्तिं न जानाति जीवन् कीर्तिं समश्नुते |
मृतस्य कीर्तिर्मर्त्यस्य यथा माला गतायुषः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A dead man will not enjoy his fame. Fame can be relished only when he is alive. Fame for a dead man is as useless as the garland that is put over his dead body.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtaḥ kīrtiṃ na jānāti jīvan kīrtiṃ samaśnute |
mṛtasya kīrtirmartyasya yathā mālā gatāyuṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुमुदान्येव शशांकः सविता बोधयति पंकजान्येव ।
वशिनां हि परपरिग्रहसंश्लेषपराज्ञ्मुखी वृत्तिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Moon blooms water lilies. Sun blooms the lotuses. Both of them are in the same pond, yet one does not bloom the other flowers. Those who have their sense under control are like the sun and the moon; they have least interest with things that belong to others.
quotes.transliteration:
kumudānyeva śaśāṃkaḥ savitā bodhayati paṃkajānyeva |
vaśināṃ hi paraparigrahasaṃśleṣaparājñmukhī vṛttiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न यत्र शक्यते कर्तुं साम दानमथापि वा |
भेदस्तत्र प्रयोक्तव्यो यतः स वशकारकः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When the enemy can not be placated then we must exercise compromise (concede some ground). If that is not possible, then one must try to explain situation using a contrast - good v/s bad. This contrast is the only way he can be brought under control
quotes.transliteration:
na yatra śakyate kartuṃ sāma dānamathāpi vā |
bhedastatra prayoktavyo yataḥ sa vaśakārakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अमृतं शिशिरे वह्निरमृतं प्रियदर्शनम् ।
अमृतं राजसंमानं अमृतं क्षीरभोजनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In winter sitting beside the fire is like having elixir. When you see someone you like, it has the same feeling. Same is the feeling when you are decorated by the king. Very similar is the feeling when you eat rice baked in milk. One must consider all these very important in his life.
quotes.transliteration:
amṛtaṃ śiśire vahniramṛtaṃ priyadarśanam |
amṛtaṃ rājasaṃmānaṃ amṛtaṃ kṣīrabhojanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वे कंकणकेयूरकुण्डलप्रतिमा गुणाः ।
शीलं चाकृत्रिमं लोके लावण्यमिव भूषणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All other qualities of a noble man are like ornaments (can be acquired)- bangles, armlets or ear-rings. Only character is innate like charm.
quotes.transliteration:
sarve kaṃkaṇakeyūrakuṇḍalapratimā guṇāḥ |
śīlaṃ cākṛtrimaṃ loke lāvaṇyamiva bhūṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा ह्यवहितो वह्निर्दारुष्वेकः स्वयोनिषु ।
नानेव भाति विश्वात्मा भूतेषु च तथा पुमान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fire resides in wood and becomes one with it. Just like that the cosmic spirit even though alone, lives in all beings without being separate from any one of them.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā hyavahito vahnirdāruṣvekaḥ svayoniṣu |
nāneva bhāti viśvātmā bhūteṣu ca tathā pumān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुहृद्बिराप्तैरसकृद्विचारितं
स्वयं च बुद्ध्या प्रविचारिताश्रयम् ।
करोति कार्यं खलु यः स बुद्धिमान्
स एव लक्ष्म्या यशसां च भाजनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one does any work after listening to friends and well wishers and does his own thinking - he is bound to be successful, accumulate wealth and become famous.
quotes.transliteration:
suhṛdbirāptairasakṛdvicāritaṃ
svayaṃ ca buddhyā pravicāritāśrayam |
karoti kāryaṃ khalu yaḥ sa buddhimān
sa eva lakṣmyā yaśasāṃ ca bhājanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इन्द्रियाणां जये योगं समातिष्ठेद्दिवानिशम् ।
जितेन्द्रियो हि शक्नोति वशे स्थापयितुं प्रजाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must practice to keep his senses under control at all times - day and night. Only one who can win over his senses can rule over the subjects effectively.
quotes.transliteration:
indriyāṇāṃ jaye yogaṃ samātiṣṭheddivāniśam |
jitendriyo hi śaknoti vaśe sthāpayituṃ prajāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गृहवासः सुखार्थाय पत्नीमूलं गृहे सुखम् ।
सा पत्नी या विनीता स्याच्चित्तज्ञा वशवर्तिनी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People make homes (families) to be happy. At the root of such happiness is the wife. She is a good wife who is obedient and understands the mind of the man by herself.
quotes.transliteration:
gṛhavāsaḥ sukhārthāya patnīmūlaṃ gṛhe sukham |
sā patnī yā vinītā syāccittajñā vaśavartinī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अष्टादशपुराणां सारं व्यासेन कीर्तितम् ।
परोपकारः पुण्याय पापाय परपीडनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In eighteen lenthy episodes of puranas Vyasa (author of the epic Mahabharatha which has 18 episodes named puranas) has described one thing. Helping others is vitue - troubling others is sin. Show me one religious book that deals with something different.
quotes.transliteration:
aṣṭādaśapurāṇāṃ sāraṃ vyāsena kīrtitam |
paropakāraḥ puṇyāya pāpāya parapīḍanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रसने त्वं रसज्ञेति वृथैव स्तूयसे बुधैः ।
अपारमाधुरीधामरामनामपराञ्मुखी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! tongue, you are praised by scholars as being very tasteful. I find that praise a waste if you turn away from the one name that is the abode of all the sweetness - Rama.
quotes.transliteration:
rasane tvaṃ rasajñeti vṛthaiva stūyase budhaiḥ |
apāramādhurīdhāmarāmanāmaparāñmukhī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रसिद्धौ कार्याणां प्रवदति जनः पार्थिवबलं
विपत्तौ विस्पष्टं सचिवमतिदोषं जनयति |
अमात्या इत्युक्ताः श्रुतिसुखमुदारं नृपतिभिः
सुसूक्ष्मं दण्ड्यंते मतिबलविदग्धाः कुपुरुषाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When things work out for good, people give credit to the king (telling that it was his benevolence that got the work done). When things go wrong, the subjects usually blame the minister for bad administration. Even though the kings call the ministers as "amaatya" (meaning: a dear one), they punish the ministers in a subtle way. In fact a very learned and wise minister is also treated like a rogue.
quotes.transliteration:
prasiddhau kāryāṇāṃ pravadati janaḥ pārthivabalaṃ
vipattau vispaṣṭaṃ sacivamatidoṣaṃ janayati |
amātyā ityuktāḥ śrutisukhamudāraṃ nṛpatibhiḥ
susūkṣmaṃ daṇḍyaṃte matibalavidagdhāḥ kupuruṣāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वे क्षयान्ता निचयाः पतनांताः समुच्छ्रयाः ।
संयोगा विप्रयोगांता मरणांतं च जीवितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any accumulated wealth can dissolve in due time. Any position of power attained may come to an end. All the relations (children, wife) come to an end in death. There is nothing in this world that is permanent.
quotes.transliteration:
sarve kṣayāntā nicayāḥ patanāṃtāḥ samucchrayāḥ |
saṃyogā viprayogāṃtā maraṇāṃtaṃ ca jīvitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणग्रामाभिसंवादि नामापि हि महात्मनाम् ।
यथा सुवर्णश्रीखण्ड रत्नाकर सुधाकरः ।।
quotes.englishTranslation: Even the names of great men describe their great characters. They are all very aptly named. Gold is called Suvarna (meaning: the one with lustrous color). Sandalwood is called Srikhanda (meaning: wood filled with wealth). Ocean is called Ratnakara (meaning: he who has gems). Moon is called Sudhakara (meaning: he who has the elixir) Prasanna Raghava
quotes.transliteration:
guṇagrāmābhisaṃvādi nāmāpi hi mahātmanām |
yathā suvarṇaśrīkhaṇḍa ratnākara sudhākaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मो हि परमो लोके धर्मे सत्यं प्रतिष्ठितम् ।
धर्मसंश्रितमेतच्च पितुर्वचनमुत्तमम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Nothing is above the Dharma (the moral code of life). There is no greater truth than dharma. Following one's father's advice is hence the first thing to do (given that his advice is according to dharma).
quotes.transliteration:
dharmo hi paramo loke dharme satyaṃ pratiṣṭhitam |
dharmasaṃśritametacca piturvacanamuttamam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निर्धनत्वमसत्यत्वं विद्यायुक्तस्य कर्हिचित् |
अस्थानित्वमभाग्यत्वं न सुशीलस्य कर्हिचित् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: You may find an educated person being poor or corrupt. But you will never see a man with great character suffering from either poverty or misfortune. This shows that education alone can not make a man rich or upright. It is one's character that determines his fortunes.
quotes.transliteration:
nirdhanatvamasatyatvaṃ vidyāyuktasya karhicit |
asthānitvamabhāgyatvaṃ na suśīlasya karhicit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स गंगा स गया सेतुस्स काशी स च पुष्करं ।
जिह्वाग्रे वर्तते यस्य हरिरित्यक्षरद्वयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who has the name of 'Hari' at the tip of his toungue at all times is as holy as the Ganga, the Gaya, the Sethu (Rama sethu), the Kashi, the Pushkar.
quotes.transliteration:
sa gaṃgā sa gayā setussa kāśī sa ca puṣkaraṃ |
jihvāgre vartate yasya harirityakṣaradvayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भये वा यदि वा हर्षे संप्राप्ते यो विमर्शयेत् |
कृत्यं न कुरुते वेगात् न स संतापमाप्नुयात् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He who ponders to think (and deliberates) when confronted with fear or extreme happiness and who would never react in a hurry will never be sad.
quotes.transliteration:
bhaye vā yadi vā harṣe saṃprāpte yo vimarśayet |
kṛtyaṃ na kurute vegāt na sa saṃtāpamāpnuyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकं विषरसो हन्ति शस्त्रेणैकश्च हन्यते |
सबंधुराष्ट्रं राजानं हन्त्येको मंत्रविप्लवः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Poison kills only one person (the one who drinks it). Weapons can kill only one (or few). But a rogue thought in a king's mind can kill the king along with all his relatives, subjects and his kingdom.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaṃ viṣaraso hanti śastreṇaikaśca hanyate |
sabaṃdhurāṣṭraṃ rājānaṃ hantyeko maṃtraviplavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शय्या शाद्वलमासनं शुचिशिला सद्म द्रुमाणामधः
शीतं निर्झरवारि पानमशनं कंदाः सहाया मृगाः |
इत्यप्रार्थितलभ्यसर्वविभवे दोषोऽयमेको वने
दुष्प्रापार्थिनि यत् परार्थघटनावंध्यैर्वऋथा स्थीयते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A bed made of soft grass. Seats made of clean stones. Houses under the trees. Pure cold water to drink from the freely flowing streams. Various tubes (like potato) for food. There are animals that help (in their own way). All the luxuries that can be thought of are present in the forest. But it all the facilities lack only one thing - there is no body make use of it.
quotes.transliteration:
śayyā śādvalamāsanaṃ śuciśilā sadma drumāṇāmadhaḥ
śītaṃ nirjharavāri pānamaśanaṃ kaṃdāḥ sahāyā mṛgāḥ |
ityaprārthitalabhyasarvavibhave doṣoऽyameko vane
duṣprāpārthini yat parārthaghaṭanāvaṃdhyairvaṛthā sthīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शास्त्राय गुरुसंयोगः शास्त्रं विनयवृद्धये ।
विद्याविनीतो नृपतिर्न कृच्छ्रेष्ववसीदति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: To learn about the scriptures, one must go to an able teacher. The goal of scriptures is to teach us humility. A king who has learned about scriptures and remains humble can not be destroyed.
quotes.transliteration:
śāstrāya gurusaṃyogaḥ śāstraṃ vinayavṛddhaye |
vidyāvinīto nṛpatirna kṛcchreṣvavasīdati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परपरिवादः परिषदि न कथंचित्पण्डितेन वक्तव्यः |
सत्यमपि तन्न वाच्यं यदुक्तमसुखावहं भवति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man knows how to behave in a gathering. He knows well that he must not ridicule/talk harshly to any one (even when they are his opponents). Some times, even the truth must not be told. Especially when it is sure to hurt some one.
quotes.transliteration:
paraparivādaḥ pariṣadi na kathaṃcitpaṇḍitena vaktavyaḥ |
satyamapi tanna vācyaṃ yaduktamasukhāvahaṃ bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अधीयानः पण्डितं मन्यमानो
यो विद्यया हन्ति यशः परेषाम् ।
तस्यांतवंतश्च भान्ति लोका
न चास्य तद् ब्रह्म फलं ददाति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: tasyAntavantashcha bhavanti lokA One who wants to be educated must not think he is a scholar already. He must also not use his education to kill someone else's fame. If he does that, he may get the world, but it is not permanent. He will not attain the eternal peace. (because he will be constantly on the guard that someone else may kill his fame)
quotes.transliteration:
adhīyānaḥ paṇḍitaṃ manyamāno
yo vidyayā hanti yaśaḥ pareṣām |
tasyāṃtavaṃtaśca bhānti lokā
na cāsya tad brahma phalaṃ dadāti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोभमूलानि पापानि व्याधयो रसमूलकाः ।
स्नेहमूलानि दुःखानि त्रीणि त्यक्त्वा सुखी भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Greed is the cause of all sins, indulgence the cause of illness, attachment/friendship is the cause of sadness. If one lets go of the three, then he shall be happy.
quotes.transliteration:
lobhamūlāni pāpāni vyādhayo rasamūlakāḥ |
snehamūlāni duḥkhāni trīṇi tyaktvā sukhī bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोभेन बुद्धिश्चलति लोभो जनयति तृषाम् |
तृषार्तो दुःखमाप्नोति परत्रेह च मानवः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Greed forces wisdom to abandon you. It gives rise to various desires. Desires lead to sorrow - both in this world as well as the after life.
quotes.transliteration:
lobhena buddhiścalati lobho janayati tṛṣām |
tṛṣārto duḥkhamāpnoti paratreha ca mānavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनुभवेन विनाधिगतं श्रुतं
भवति नैव नॄणामुपकारकं ।
दधिनि वतर्त एव हविः पुनः
न मथनेन विना विना तदवाप्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All the knowledge that one may learn from others, or read in scriptures is of no use unless one ratifies them by experience. Curd does have ingredients of ghee - but it still requires a lot of processing.
quotes.transliteration:
anubhavena vinādhigataṃ śrutaṃ
bhavati naiva nṝṇāmupakārakaṃ |
dadhini vatarta eva haviḥ punaḥ
na mathanena vinā vinā tadavāpyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आचार्यात् पादमादत्ते पादं शिष्यः स्वमेधया ।
कालेन पादमादत्ते पादं सब्रह्मचारिभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A student gets one quarter of his education from his teacher. Another quarter by contemplation. Another quarter is learned over time by experience. The last quarter is learned from the fellow students.
quotes.transliteration:
ācāryāt pādamādatte pādaṃ śiṣyaḥ svamedhayā |
kālena pādamādatte pādaṃ sabrahmacāribhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाऽधर्मश्चरितो लोके सद्यः फलति गौरिव ।
शनैरावर्तमानस्तु कर्तुर्मूलानि कृंतति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The results of the deeds of cruelty (against dharma the code of life) are not immediately apparent unlike the benefits of domesticating a cow (you get milk on day one). Slowly the effects of mis-deeds surround the doer and cut the very roots of the source. Perpetrating cruelty will always end in the destruction of the culprit. ==== Update: Thanks Gayathri for pointing out the typo.
quotes.transliteration:
nāऽdharmaścarito loke sadyaḥ phalati gauriva |
śanairāvartamānastu karturmūlāni kṛṃtati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणेषु रागो व्यसनेष्वनादरो
रतिः सुभृत्येषु च यस्य भूपतेः |
चिरं स भुंक्ते चलचामरांशुकां
सितातपत्राभरणां नृपश्रियम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king who respects good qualities in people, who stays away from addictions, who appreciates a good servant will for ever enjoy the luxuries of royalty - the constant movement of the fans (with peacock feathers blowing cool air), the ornaments, the roayal umbrella and so on.
quotes.transliteration:
guṇeṣu rāgo vyasaneṣvanādaro
ratiḥ subhṛtyeṣu ca yasya bhūpateḥ |
ciraṃ sa bhuṃkte calacāmarāṃśukāṃ
sitātapatrābharaṇāṃ nṛpaśriyam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संगः सत्सु विधीयतां भगवतो भक्तिर्दृडा धीयतां
शान्त्यादिः परिचीयतां दृडतरं कर्माशु संत्यज्यतां ।
सद्विद्वानुपसर्प्यतां प्रतिदिनं तत्पादुका सेव्यतां
ब्रह्मैकाक्षरमर्थ्यतां शृतिशिरोवाक्यं समाकर्ण्यताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Befriend virtuous men. Have faith and devotion in God. Fill your mind with peaceful thoughts. Stop doing anything that would require applying force (on others). Follow wise men every day. Serve them with humility. Listen to the recitals of Veda from them. Try to understand the meaning of the word - Brahma (the creator).
quotes.transliteration:
saṃgaḥ satsu vidhīyatāṃ bhagavato bhaktirdṛḍā dhīyatāṃ
śāntyādiḥ paricīyatāṃ dṛḍataraṃ karmāśu saṃtyajyatāṃ |
sadvidvānupasarpyatāṃ pratidinaṃ tatpādukā sevyatāṃ
brahmaikākṣaramarthyatāṃ śṛtiśirovākyaṃ samākarṇyatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एवमाचारतो दृष्ट्वा धर्मस्य मुनयो गतिम् ।
सर्वस्य तपसो मूलं आचारं जगृहुः परम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Righteous living is the basis of dharma - the moral code of life. The sages have known this for ever. So they say that every penance has its root in righteous living.
quotes.transliteration:
evamācārato dṛṣṭvā dharmasya munayo gatim |
sarvasya tapaso mūlaṃ ācāraṃ jagṛhuḥ param ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सदा प्रहृष्टया भाव्यं गृहकार्येषु दक्षया ।
सुसंस्कृतोपस्करया व्यये चामुक्तहस्तया ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A good wife must always be happy. She must run the household efficiently. All the things (appliances, utensils, etc) in the house must be kept clean. She must exercise a tight grip on spending.
quotes.transliteration:
sadā prahṛṣṭayā bhāvyaṃ gṛhakāryeṣu dakṣayā |
susaṃskṛtopaskarayā vyaye cāmuktahastayā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुध्यति हि नान्तरात्मा कृष्णपदांभोजभक्तिमृते ।
वसनमिव क्षारोदैः भक्त्या प्रक्षाल्यते चेतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not possible to cleanse your mind without having complete devotion towards the lotus feet of Sri Krishna. Just like hard salt cleans a cloth, devotion cleans the mind.
quotes.transliteration:
śudhyati hi nāntarātmā kṛṣṇapadāṃbhojabhaktimṛte |
vasanamiva kṣārodaiḥ bhaktyā prakṣālyate cetaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दृष्यन्ते बहवः कलासु कुशलास्ते च स्फुरत्कीर्तये
सर्वस्वं वितरन्ति ये तृणमिव क्षुद्रैरपि प्रार्थिताः ।
धीरास्तेऽपि च ये त्यजन्ति झटिति प्राणान् कृते स्वामिनो
द्वित्रास्ते तु नरा मनः समरसं येषां सुहृद्वैरिणोः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You may find many people who are good at arts and whose fame has taken them places. You will find others who will give away all their wealth to the needy (even if they are undeserving) as if it were just a blade of grass. You will find courageous people who are ready to lay down their life without any hesitation for the sake of their masters. All these people are common. But you will find only two or three people in this entire world who treat both their enemies and well-wishers alike. That level of detachment is rare.
quotes.transliteration:
dṛṣyante bahavaḥ kalāsu kuśalāste ca sphuratkīrtaye
sarvasvaṃ vitaranti ye tṛṇamiva kṣudrairapi prārthitāḥ |
dhīrāsteऽpi ca ye tyajanti jhaṭiti prāṇān kṛte svāmino
dvitrāste tu narā manaḥ samarasaṃ yeṣāṃ suhṛdvairiṇoḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विक्रोशंतो यस्य राष्ट्राद्ध्रियंते दस्युभिः प्रजाः |
संपश्यतः सभृत्यस्य मृतः स न तु जीवति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king and his officers are as good as dead under whose watch the thieves (and other rogues) steal from and trouble subjects. It is the duty of a king to keep his subjects safe.
quotes.transliteration:
vikrośaṃto yasya rāṣṭrāddhriyaṃte dasyubhiḥ prajāḥ |
saṃpaśyataḥ sabhṛtyasya mṛtaḥ sa na tu jīvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं दातुरखिलैर्दोषैः किं लुब्धस्याखिलैर्गुणैः ।
न लोभादधिको दोषो न दानाधिको गुणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It does not matter if a generous person has some defects. It does not matter if a greedy person has all the good qualities. There is no greater vice than greed. There is no greater quality than being generous.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ dāturakhilairdoṣaiḥ kiṃ lubdhasyākhilairguṇaiḥ |
na lobhādadhiko doṣo na dānādhiko guṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न यत्रास्ति गतिर्वायो रश्मीनां च विवस्वतः।
तत्रापि प्रविशत्याशु बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतां सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man's wisdom can penetrate to such depths where even wind can not flow or can light reach.
quotes.transliteration:
na yatrāsti gatirvāyo raśmīnāṃ ca vivasvataḥ|
tatrāpi praviśatyāśu buddhirbuddhimatāṃ sadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वनेऽपि दोषाः प्रभवन्ति रागिणां
गृहेऽपि पंचेन्द्रियनिग्रहं तपः |
अकुत्सिते कर्मणि यः प्रवर्तते
निवृत्तरागस्य गृहं तपोवनम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A person with lot of desires will not at peace even in a forest. He will find something or the other to indulge in. On the other hand, if a person has over come greed and desires and can control his senses - he can find peace right in his home. If you are earning your daily food by a respectable occupation and give up all the desires - your own home will become a penance ground.
quotes.transliteration:
vaneऽpi doṣāḥ prabhavanti rāgiṇāṃ
gṛheऽpi paṃcendriyanigrahaṃ tapaḥ |
akutsite karmaṇi yaḥ pravartate
nivṛttarāgasya gṛhaṃ tapovanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मुक्तिमिच्छसि चेत्तात विषयान् विषवत्त्यजेः ।
क्षमार्जवदया शौचं सत्यं पीयूषवत् पिबेः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you want to attain salvation, then you should shun sensory pleasures as if it were poison. You must embrace forgiveness, simplicity, compassion, cleanliness, truth as if it were elixir.
quotes.transliteration:
muktimicchasi cettāta viṣayān viṣavattyajeḥ |
kṣamārjavadayā śaucaṃ satyaṃ pīyūṣavat pibeḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आदित्यचन्द्रावनिलौ च
द्यौर्भूमिरापो हृदयं यमश्च ।
अहश्च रात्रिश्च उभे च सन्ध्ये
धर्मश्च जानाति नरस्य वृत्तम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the witness to man's every action - the Sun, the Moon, air, fire, sky, earth, water, heart, Lord of Death, times (morning, night, dawn and dusk), the Lord of Dharma. One must never assume that he can get away with wrong doings just because there is nobody around.
quotes.transliteration:
ādityacandrāvanilau ca
dyaurbhūmirāpo hṛdayaṃ yamaśca |
ahaśca rātriśca ubhe ca sandhye
dharmaśca jānāti narasya vṛttam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जीर्णा तरिः सरिदियं च गभीरनीरा
नक्राकुला वहति वायुरतिप्रचंडः ।
तार्या स्त्रियश्च शिशवश्च तथैव वृद्धाः
तत्कर्णधारभुजयोर्बलमाश्रयामः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The boat is broken. The river (of samsaara) is deep. There are dangerous crocodiles in the river and storm is raging. We have to get the women, children and old people across. So, let us rely on the prowess of the boatman (Krishna).
quotes.transliteration:
jīrṇā tariḥ saridiyaṃ ca gabhīranīrā
nakrākulā vahati vāyuratipracaṃḍaḥ |
tāryā striyaśca śiśavaśca tathaiva vṛddhāḥ
tatkarṇadhārabhujayorbalamāśrayāmaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विदेशेषु धनं विद्या व्यसनेषु धनं मतिः ।
परलोके धनं धर्मः शीलं तु निखिलं धनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Education is wealth when one is traveling. One's intelligence is wealth when in trouble. The good karma accumulated by following dharma is wealth in the after-life. Good conduct is wealth in any where, any time.
quotes.transliteration:
videśeṣu dhanaṃ vidyā vyasaneṣu dhanaṃ matiḥ |
paraloke dhanaṃ dharmaḥ śīlaṃ tu nikhilaṃ dhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संतापं तनुते भिनत्ति विनयं सौहार्दमुत्सादय-
त्युद्वेगं जनयत्यवद्यवचनं सूते विधत्ते कलिम् |
कीर्तिं कृंतति दुर्मतिं वितरति व्याहन्ति पुण्योदयं
दत्ते यः कुगतिं स हातुमुचितो रोषः स दोषः सताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man must renounce anger. For anger has the following effect on people. It will cause distress. It will kill humility and friendship. It will beget anxiety. It will make you utter bad words (the unutterable). It will give rise to differences. It will cut short your fame. It will spread wickedness (among all of them who suffer from your anger). It will diminish all your collected good karma and let you die a horrible death.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃtāpaṃ tanute bhinatti vinayaṃ sauhārdamutsādaya-
tyudvegaṃ janayatyavadyavacanaṃ sūte vidhatte kalim |
kīrtiṃ kṛṃtati durmatiṃ vitarati vyāhanti puṇyodayaṃ
datte yaḥ kugatiṃ sa hātumucito roṣaḥ sa doṣaḥ satām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्र विद्वज्जनो नास्ति श्लाघ्यस्तत्राल्पधीरपि ।
निरस्तपादपे देशे एरण्डोऽपि द्रुमायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a little knowledgeable people are regarded with high respect in places which does not have any wise men. In a dry and arid place even castor-oil plants are treated as if they are great trees.
quotes.transliteration:
yatra vidvajjano nāsti ślāghyastatrālpadhīrapi |
nirastapādape deśe eraṇḍoऽpi drumāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अज्ञानात् या मया पूर्वं शाखापत्रैर्वियोजिता ।
छायार्थी ग्रीष्मसंतप्तस्तामेव पुनराश्रितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! Because of my ignorance I made this branch (of tree) devoid of all its leaves (looted it). Now, I have come to take refuge below it to escape from this summer Sun beating on me.
quotes.transliteration:
ajñānāt yā mayā pūrvaṃ śākhāpatrairviyojitā |
chāyārthī grīṣmasaṃtaptastāmeva punarāśritaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ख्यापनेनानुतापेन तपसाऽध्ययनेन च ।
पापकृन्मुच्यते पापात्तथा दानेन चापदि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A sinner will earn his redemption by doing the following: talking to others about his sin, feeling remorse, doing penance, studying and being generous in charity.
quotes.transliteration:
khyāpanenānutāpena tapasāऽdhyayanena ca |
pāpakṛnmucyate pāpāttathā dānena cāpadi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनागतविधाता च प्रत्युत्पन्नमतिस्तथा |
द्वावेतौ सुखमेधेते यद्भविष्यो विनश्यति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Two people will prosper and be happy - one who prepares ahead of troubled times and the one who is reacts well and quick to changing times. A person who takes on things as they come will soon perish.
quotes.transliteration:
anāgatavidhātā ca pratyutpannamatistathā |
dvāvetau sukhamedhete yadbhaviṣyo vinaśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उच्चैरुच्चरितव्यं यत्किंचिदजानतापि पुरुषेण ।
मूर्खा बहु मन्यन्ते विदुषामपि संशयो भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must talk loudly even when talking about whatever little you know. Fools will think you are right and the wise may also be put in doubt.
quotes.transliteration:
uccairuccaritavyaṃ yatkiṃcidajānatāpi puruṣeṇa |
mūrkhā bahu manyante viduṣāmapi saṃśayo bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न देवा दण्डमादाय रक्षन्ति पशुपालवत् ।
यं हि रक्षितुमिच्छन्ति बुद्ध्या संयोजयन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The Gods do not hold sticks in their hands like shepherds and protect us. They just give enough wisdom in each of our minds to protect ourselves.
quotes.transliteration:
na devā daṇḍamādāya rakṣanti paśupālavat |
yaṃ hi rakṣitumicchanti buddhyā saṃyojayanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यावद्वित्तोपार्जनसक्तः
तावन्निजपरिवारो रक्तः ।
पश्चाज्जर्जरभूते देहे
वार्तां कोऽपि न पृच्छति गेहे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: yavat vittopArjanasaktaH pashchAt jarjarabhUte dehe As long as you are earning money, your family loves you. When you stop earning and the body becomes weak, no one in the house want to hear about you.
quotes.transliteration:
yāvadvittopārjanasaktaḥ
tāvannijaparivāro raktaḥ |
paścājjarjarabhūte dehe
vārtāṃ koऽpi na pṛcchati gehe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अरे भज हरेर्नाम क्षेमधाम क्षणे क्षणे ।
बहिस्सरति निःष्वासे विश्वासः कः प्रवर्तते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One should always take the all benevolent God's name. What is the guarantee that the breath let out is ever going to come back?
quotes.transliteration:
are bhaja harernāma kṣemadhāma kṣaṇe kṣaṇe |
bahissarati niḥṣvāse viśvāsaḥ kaḥ pravartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनभिज्ञाय शास्त्रार्थान् पुरुषाः पशुबुद्धयः |
प्रागल्भ्याद्वक्तुमिच्छन्ति मंत्रिष्वभ्यंतरीकृताः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Some ministers have intellect of an animal. Without knowing the intricacies of scriptures, they advise the king with their theories framed by nice words. [A good king should either get rid of such ministers or at least not listen to them]
quotes.transliteration:
anabhijñāya śāstrārthān puruṣāḥ paśubuddhayaḥ |
prāgalbhyādvaktumicchanti maṃtriṣvabhyaṃtarīkṛtāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न हि पूरयितुं शक्यः लोभः प्रीत्या कथंचन ।
नित्यगंभीरतोयाभिरापगाभिरिवाम्बुधिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not possible to satiate desire by indulgence. All those big rivers may feed water into the ocean all year long - But can never fill up the ocean.
quotes.transliteration:
na hi pūrayituṃ śakyaḥ lobhaḥ prītyā kathaṃcana |
nityagaṃbhīratoyābhirāpagābhirivāmbudhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कः केन हन्यते जन्तुर्जन्तुः कः केन रक्ष्यते ।
हन्ति रक्षति चैवात्मा ह्यसत्साधु समाचरन् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who can kill any one? Who can save any one from being killed? To kill, or to be saved from being killed are all results of one's own actions.
quotes.transliteration:
kaḥ kena hanyate janturjantuḥ kaḥ kena rakṣyate |
hanti rakṣati caivātmā hyasatsādhu samācaran ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न स्थिरं क्षणमप्येकं उदकं तु यथोर्मिभिः ।
वाताहतं तथा चित्तं तस्मात्तस्य न विश्वसेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When there wind blowing the water does not stay still even for a second. Our mind is just like that. It will keep changing every second. Never trust it.
quotes.transliteration:
na sthiraṃ kṣaṇamapyekaṃ udakaṃ tu yathormibhiḥ |
vātāhataṃ tathā cittaṃ tasmāttasya na viśvaset ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इन्द्रियाणां प्रसंगेन दोषमृच्छत्यसंशयम् ।
संनियम्य तु तान्येव ततः सिद्धिं नियच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you listen to your senses alone, you are bound to commit sin that goes against dharma. On the other hand, if you control them you will definitely attain realization.
quotes.transliteration:
indriyāṇāṃ prasaṃgena doṣamṛcchatyasaṃśayam |
saṃniyamya tu tānyeva tataḥ siddhiṃ niyacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अयमुत्तमोऽयमधमो
जात्या रूपेण संपदा वयसा ।
श्लाघ्योऽश्लाघ्यो वेत्थं
न वेत्ति भगवाननुग्रहावसरे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: ayam uttamaH ayam adhamaH When it comes to showering favors God does not differentiate on caste, beauty, wealth or age. God only cares if the person deserves it or not.
quotes.transliteration:
ayamuttamoऽyamadhamo
jātyā rūpeṇa saṃpadā vayasā |
ślāghyoऽślāghyo vetthaṃ
na vetti bhagavānanugrahāvasare ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृतं शरीरमुत्सृज्य काष्ठलोष्टसमं क्षितौ ।
विमुखा बांधवा यान्ति धर्मस्तमनुगच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Relatives will leave the dead body just like they would leave dry wood or a lump of mud. No one will follow you to the world of after-life. It is only results of the Dharma you followed all your life that will follow you and help/hurt you in the after-life.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtaṃ śarīramutsṛjya kāṣṭhaloṣṭasamaṃ kṣitau |
vimukhā bāṃdhavā yānti dharmastamanugacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महेश्वरे वा जगतामधीश्वरे
जनार्दने वा जगदंतरात्मनि ।
न वस्तुभेद प्रतिपत्तिरस्ति मे
तथापि भक्तिस्तरुणेंदुशेखरे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: maheshvare vA jagatAmadhishvare na vastu bheda pratipattirasti me Lord Shiva, who is the owner of this world, or Lord Vishnu who is in every one's conscience - I do not differentiate between them. Both are equal to me. But when it comes to worshiping, I worship the God with young moon on his head (Shiva). A mendicant would definitely identify with Shiva because Lord Shiva himself is said to have led a very ascetic life.
quotes.transliteration:
maheśvare vā jagatāmadhīśvare
janārdane vā jagadaṃtarātmani |
na vastubheda pratipattirasti me
tathāpi bhaktistaruṇeṃduśekhare ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकस्य हि प्रसादेन कृत्स्नो लोकः प्रसीदति |
व्याकुलेनाकुलः सर्वो भवतीति विनिश्चयम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If one is happy, to him the whole world seems to be happy. If he is sad, then he is sure to see sadness every where.
quotes.transliteration:
ekasya hi prasādena kṛtsno lokaḥ prasīdati |
vyākulenākulaḥ sarvo bhavatīti viniścayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहिंसा सत्यमस्तेयं शौचमिंद्रियनिग्रहः ।
दानं दमो दया क्षान्तिः सर्वेषां धर्मसाधनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Non violence, truth, refraining from any stealing, cleanliness (morally as well as physically), control over senses, charity, self restraint, compassion, patience (and forgiveness) are the ways one can adopt to follow Dharma.
quotes.transliteration:
ahiṃsā satyamasteyaṃ śaucamiṃdriyanigrahaḥ |
dānaṃ damo dayā kṣāntiḥ sarveṣāṃ dharmasādhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न योजनशतं दूरं वाह्यमानस्य तृष्णया ।
संतुष्टस्य करप्राप्तेऽप्यर्थे भवति नादरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person who is interested in accumulating wealth will not find it hard to travel thousands of miles powered by greed. For a person who is satisfied with what he has, even money at hand will not interest him.
quotes.transliteration:
na yojanaśataṃ dūraṃ vāhyamānasya tṛṣṇayā |
saṃtuṣṭasya karaprāpteऽpyarthe bhavati nādaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्यजेत् कुलार्थे पुरुषं ग्रामस्यार्थे कुलं त्यजेत् ।
ग्रामं जनपदस्यार्थे आत्मार्थे पृथिवीं त्यजेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must disown a person to save a family. One family can be sacrificed to save a village. A village has to be abandoned to save a state. But if one needs to save his soul, he must abandon the entire world.
quotes.transliteration:
tyajet kulārthe puruṣaṃ grāmasyārthe kulaṃ tyajet |
grāmaṃ janapadasyārthe ātmārthe pṛthivīṃ tyajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अपि मानुष्यकं लब्ध्वा भवन्ति ज्ञानिनो नये ।
पशुतैव वरा तेषां प्रत्यवायाप्रवर्तनात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who are fortunate enough to get a human birth and stupid enough not to get some knowledge (about self, God, etc...) are better of as animals. At least, animals are free from the sins that arise out of their actions.
quotes.transliteration:
api mānuṣyakaṃ labdhvā bhavanti jñānino naye |
paśutaiva varā teṣāṃ pratyavāyāpravartanāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कामं क्रोधं मोहं लोभं त्यक्त्वात्मानं भावय कोऽहं ।
आत्मज्ञानविहीन मूढास्ते पच्यन्ते नरकनिगूढाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Give up lust, anger, attachement and greed. Stop and ask yourself "Who am I?". People without that knowledge will suffer in all kinds of hell.
quotes.transliteration:
kāmaṃ krodhaṃ mohaṃ lobhaṃ tyaktvātmānaṃ bhāvaya koऽhaṃ |
ātmajñānavihīna mūḍhāste pacyante narakanigūḍhāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यज्जातीयो यादृशो यत्स्वभावः
पादाच्छायं संश्रितो योSपि कोSपि ।
तज्जातीयस्तादृशस्तत्स्वभावः
श्लिष्यत्येनं सुन्दरो वत्सलत्वात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: God does not differentiate among his devotees. Whatever be his caste, his ideals, his nature - if a devotee falls at his feet with complete devotion, God will embrace him with love. He will make the devotee feel as though He is also of same caste, same ideals and same nature.
quotes.transliteration:
yajjātīyo yādṛśo yatsvabhāvaḥ
pādācchāyaṃ saṃśrito yoSpi koSpi |
tajjātīyastādṛśastatsvabhāvaḥ
śliṣyatyenaṃ sundaro vatsalatvāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
साम्नैव यत्र सिद्धिः न तत्र दण्डो बुधेन विनियोज्यः |
पित्तं यदि शर्करया शाम्यति कोऽर्थः पटोलेन ||
quotes.englishTranslation: What can be got by exercising reasonable patience must never be escalated to a full fledged conflict. The wise men know this. Sour sensation in the mouth can be mitigated by eating sugar. Why would one resort to bitter-gourd instead?
quotes.transliteration:
sāmnaiva yatra siddhiḥ na tatra daṇḍo budhena viniyojyaḥ |
pittaṃ yadi śarkarayā śāmyati koऽrthaḥ paṭolena ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दानं भोगो नाशस्तिस्रो गतयो भवन्ति वित्तस्य ।
यो न ददाति न भुंक्ते तस्य तृतीया गतिर्भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth has to be either given away in charity, or spent, or destroyed. These are the only three outcomes from wealth. If not given away in charity, not spent, it will attain the third result.
quotes.transliteration:
dānaṃ bhogo nāśastisro gatayo bhavanti vittasya |
yo na dadāti na bhuṃkte tasya tṛtīyā gatirbhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न भुंजीतोद्धृतस्नेहं नातिसौहित्यमाचरेत् ।
नातिप्रगे नातिसायं न सायं प्रातराशितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not eat too oily stuff. One should not indulge in too many pleasures. One should not eat at sunrise or at sunset. If you eat more in the morning, then you must skip dinner.
quotes.transliteration:
na bhuṃjītoddhṛtasnehaṃ nātisauhityamācaret |
nātiprage nātisāyaṃ na sāyaṃ prātarāśitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा दारुमयी नारी यथा यन्त्रमयो मृगः ।
एवं भूतानि मघवन्नीशतन्त्राणि विद्धि भोः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a lady made of wood (puppet), or a mechanical deer moves only on the commands and wish of its owner, the whole world moves and acts only the wish of the supreme cosmic spirit.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā dārumayī nārī yathā yantramayo mṛgaḥ |
evaṃ bhūtāni maghavannīśatantrāṇi viddhi bhoḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न जातु कामः कामानामुपभोगेन शाम्यति ।
हविषा कृष्णवर्त्मेव भूय एवाभिवर्तते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Desire does not get mitigated by yielding to it. Instead, it only grows just like the fire when added more fuel.
quotes.transliteration:
na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānāmupabhogena śāmyati |
haviṣā kṛṣṇavartmeva bhūya evābhivartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अंतस्सारैरकुटिलैरच्छिदैः सुपरीक्षितैः ।
मंत्रिभिर्धार्यते राज्यं सुस्तंभैरिव मंदिरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When constructing a building one must choose wood that is intrinsically strong, straight, without any cracks and well inspected so that they can be used as pillars that bear all the load. Similar qualities are to be expected out of minister to shoulder the weight of governance.
quotes.transliteration:
aṃtassārairakuṭilairacchidaiḥ suparīkṣitaiḥ |
maṃtribhirdhāryate rājyaṃ sustaṃbhairiva maṃdiram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मनो मुखदोषेण बध्यन्ते शुकसारिकाः ।
बकास्तत्र न बध्यन्ते मौनं सर्वार्थसाधनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Chirpy birds like the parrot get caught in the net because of their words. (because people like to have them as pet). A duck never gets caught because it does not make any sound. Anything can be achieved by being silent.
quotes.transliteration:
ātmano mukhadoṣeṇa badhyante śukasārikāḥ |
bakāstatra na badhyante maunaṃ sarvārthasādhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृद्वीका रसिता सिता समशिता स्फीतं निपीतं पयः
स्वर्यातेन सुधाप्यधायि कतिधा रम्भाधरः खंडितः ।
तत्त्वं ब्रूहि मदीयजीव भवता भूयो भवे भ्राम्यता
कृष्णेत्यक्षरयोरयं मधुरिमोद्गारः क्वचिल्लक्षितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: My dear life (and all past lives)! you have tasted grapes, sugar, creamy milk. You must have even tasted elixir on your visit to heaven. You would have already kissed Rambha (celestial beauty) on her lips. In this life also, you have traveled all world. Tell me, have you ever experienced any joy similar to what you get when you utter the name of 'Krishna'?
quotes.transliteration:
mṛdvīkā rasitā sitā samaśitā sphītaṃ nipītaṃ payaḥ
svaryātena sudhāpyadhāyi katidhā rambhādharaḥ khaṃḍitaḥ |
tattvaṃ brūhi madīyajīva bhavatā bhūyo bhave bhrāmyatā
kṛṣṇetyakṣarayorayaṃ madhurimodgāraḥ kvacillakṣitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बुद्धिवृद्धिकरण्याशु धनानि च हितानि च ।
नित्यं शास्त्राण्यवेक्षेत निगमांश्चैव वैदिकान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must read the scriptures that increase one's intelligence, wealth, well-being. Such practice is accordance with the Vedas as well as the followed rituals.
quotes.transliteration:
buddhivṛddhikaraṇyāśu dhanāni ca hitāni ca |
nityaṃ śāstrāṇyavekṣeta nigamāṃścaiva vaidikān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मा गच्छ त्वमितस्ततो गिरिश भो मय्येव वासं कुरु
स्वामिन्नादिकिरात मामकमनःकांतारसीमांतरे ।
वर्तंते बहुशो मृगा मदजुषो मात्सर्यमोहादयः
तान्हत्वा मृगयाविनोदरुचितालाभं च संप्राप्स्यसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Lord Shiva! Don't go any where else. Please stay with me inside my mind always. You are the first hunter-god. The forest of mind is filled with wild animals running amok - like greed, lust, jealousy. You will have good time hunting them down.
quotes.transliteration:
mā gaccha tvamitastato giriśa bho mayyeva vāsaṃ kuru
svāminnādikirāta māmakamanaḥkāṃtārasīmāṃtare |
vartaṃte bahuśo mṛgā madajuṣo mātsaryamohādayaḥ
tānhatvā mṛgayāvinodarucitālābhaṃ ca saṃprāpsyasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनाहूतः प्रविशति अपृष्टो बहु भाषते ।
अविश्वस्ते विश्वसिति मूढचेता नराधमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wretched fool can be recognized by the following three characteristics - he will go into a meeting uninvited, he will speak a lot even when not asked to and he will put his faith in a person who should not be believed.
quotes.transliteration:
anāhūtaḥ praviśati apṛṣṭo bahu bhāṣate |
aviśvaste viśvasiti mūḍhacetā narādhamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बहवो न विरोद्धव्या दर्जया हि महाजनाः ।
स्फुरन्तमपि नागेन्द्रं भक्षयन्ति पिपीलिकाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A strong person also must not fight with the weak when they are too many in number. A colony of small ants will devour a snake even if it is ferocious.
quotes.transliteration:
bahavo na viroddhavyā darjayā hi mahājanāḥ |
sphurantamapi nāgendraṃ bhakṣayanti pipīlikāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मा नदी भारत पुण्यतीर्था
सत्योदका शीलतट दयोर्मिः ।
अत्राभिषेकं कुरु पाण्डुपुत्र
न वारिणा शुध्यति चान्तरात्मा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The soul is a river. Good karma that one accumulates is the steps on the banks of the river. Truth is the water that flows in it. Character is the banks of that river. Compassion is a wave in it. Oh son of Pandu, take a bath in such a river. The inner mind is cleansed by such introspection but not by water.
quotes.transliteration:
ātmā nadī bhārata puṇyatīrthā
satyodakā śīlataṭa dayormiḥ |
atrābhiṣekaṃ kuru pāṇḍuputra
na vāriṇā śudhyati cāntarātmā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतः परीक्ष्य कर्तव्यं विशेषात्संगतं रहः ।
अज्ञातहृदयेष्वेवं वैरीभवति सौहृदम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a man and a woman decide to get married in secrecy, they must be really careful and examine each other thoroughly. If there keep secrets from each other, the love can soon turn to hatred.
quotes.transliteration:
ataḥ parīkṣya kartavyaṃ viśeṣātsaṃgataṃ rahaḥ |
ajñātahṛdayeṣvevaṃ vairībhavati sauhṛdam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पिताचार्यः सुहृन्माता भार्या पुत्रः पुरोहितः |
नादण्ड्यो नाम राज्ञोऽस्ति यः स्वधर्मे न तिष्ठति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king should stand by his principles and must not let go the guilty even if they are - his own father, his teacher, his well-wisher, his mother, his wife, his son or his spiritual guide
quotes.transliteration:
pitācāryaḥ suhṛnmātā bhāryā putraḥ purohitaḥ |
nādaṇḍyo nāma rājñoऽsti yaḥ svadharme na tiṣṭhati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कष्टं कर्मेति दुर्मेधाः कर्तव्याद्विनिवर्तते ।
न साहसमनारभ्य श्रेयः समुपलभ्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Idiots give up on a task assuming it would be tough to complete it. They do not understand that fame/success can come only when you start tasks (and complete it).
quotes.transliteration:
kaṣṭaṃ karmeti durmedhāḥ kartavyādvinivartate |
na sāhasamanārabhya śreyaḥ samupalabhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अलं दानेन विप्रेन्द यजनाध्ययनेन वा ।
इदं तत्स्वर्गसोपानममलं यत्प्रियं वचः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh great brahmin! stop all this charity, rituals, studying and all other attempts to go to heaven. Developing a habit of talking sweetly towards all under all circumstances is the key to going to heaven.
quotes.transliteration:
alaṃ dānena viprenda yajanādhyayanena vā |
idaṃ tatsvargasopānamamalaṃ yatpriyaṃ vacaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोभः स्वप्नोऽधृतिः क्रौर्यं नास्तिक्यं भिन्नवृत्तिका |
याचिष्णुता प्रमादश्च तामसं गुणलक्षणम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the qualities of darkness (found in a person): greed, laziness (always dreaming), fear, cruelty, disbelief, lacking righteousness in occupation, beggary, insanity.
quotes.transliteration:
lobhaḥ svapnoऽdhṛtiḥ krauryaṃ nāstikyaṃ bhinnavṛttikā |
yāciṣṇutā pramādaśca tāmasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यादृशं फलमाप्नोति कुप्लवैः संतरन् जलम् |
तादृशं फलमाप्नोति कुपुत्रैः संतरंस्तमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If you plan to navigate through darkness(miseries) of the world with the help of a bad son, then you will meet with the same fate as if you tried to cross the ocean using a bad boat (with holes).
quotes.transliteration:
yādṛśaṃ phalamāpnoti kuplavaiḥ saṃtaran jalam |
tādṛśaṃ phalamāpnoti kuputraiḥ saṃtaraṃstamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शत्रुपक्षं समृद्यंतं यो मोहात् समुपेक्षते |
व्याधिराप्यायित इव तस्य मूलं छिनत्ति सः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not ignore the ascendancy of a rival. People are usually in denial when they hear about their rivals making good progress. If he does ignore, his enemies will finish him - much the same way as a long ignored disease would finish him.
quotes.transliteration:
śatrupakṣaṃ samṛdyaṃtaṃ yo mohāt samupekṣate |
vyādhirāpyāyita iva tasya mūlaṃ chinatti saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाग्देव्या सह सख्यमाचर वह ब्रह्माणमाविष्कुरु
प्राविण्यं गतिषु प्रदर्शय निजं विद्याबलं निर्मलम् |
मीनं मा स्पृश मौक्तिकं कवलय क्रीड द्रुतं द्यौस्तले
चेदव्रीड बकाधम प्रतिभया हंसत्वमाकांक्षसि ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Duck! you secretly wish to be a swan. I will tell you what you need to do to become one. You must be friends with Saraswathi (goddess of learning). You must climb the back of Lord Brahma (the creator). You must show elegance in your walking. You must study more and increase your knowledge. You must give up eating fish. Instead you must have pearls (pure crystal water) for food. You must learn to fly in the sky. When you can do all these, then you can wish to be a swan.
quotes.transliteration:
vāgdevyā saha sakhyamācara vaha brahmāṇamāviṣkuru
prāviṇyaṃ gatiṣu pradarśaya nijaṃ vidyābalaṃ nirmalam |
mīnaṃ mā spṛśa mauktikaṃ kavalaya krīḍa drutaṃ dyaustale
cedavrīḍa bakādhama pratibhayā haṃsatvamākāṃkṣasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नराधिपा नीचजनानुवर्तिनो
बुधोपदिष्टेन पथा न यान्ति ये |
विशन्त्यतो दुर्गममार्गनिर्गमं
समस्तसंभाधमनर्थपंजरम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If the king does not follow the path shown by wise noble men and follows the rogues then he will be walking on a road to a cage from where you can not return.
quotes.transliteration:
narādhipā nīcajanānuvartino
budhopadiṣṭena pathā na yānti ye |
viśantyato durgamamārganirgamaṃ
samastasaṃbhādhamanarthapaṃjaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनारंभो हि कार्याणां प्रथमं बुद्धिलक्षणम् ।
प्रारब्धस्यांतगमनं द्वितीयं बुद्धिलक्षणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is wise to deliberate a lot before taking up a task. Not doing it (for right reasons) is the first sign of wisdom. But finishing what has been started is the second sign of wisdom.
quotes.transliteration:
anāraṃbho hi kāryāṇāṃ prathamaṃ buddhilakṣaṇam |
prārabdhasyāṃtagamanaṃ dvitīyaṃ buddhilakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न चागमादृते धर्मस्तर्केण व्यवतिष्ठते ।
ऋषीणामपि यज्ज्ञानं तदप्यागमपूर्वकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Rituals are an integral way of following dharma. One can not establish dharma in a society based solely on logic. Even the ancient sages who started these rituals knew about this. All their knowledge (about the universe) is hence not opposed to the rituals that they began.
quotes.transliteration:
na cāgamādṛte dharmastarkeṇa vyavatiṣṭhate |
ṛṣīṇāmapi yajjñānaṃ tadapyāgamapūrvakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सहजान्धदृशः स्वदुर्नये परदोषेक्षणदिव्यचक्षुषः ।
स्वगुणोच्चगिरो मुनिव्रत्ताः परवर्णग्रहणेष्वसाधवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Blind to short-comings in self. Extremely good sight while finding fault in others. Loud voice when talking about self and a saint like silence when praising others. Such is the nature of bad people.
quotes.transliteration:
sahajāndhadṛśaḥ svadurnaye paradoṣekṣaṇadivyacakṣuṣaḥ |
svaguṇoccagiro munivrattāḥ paravarṇagrahaṇeṣvasādhavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मानमनुशोच त्वं किमन्यमनुशोचसि ।
आयुस्ते क्षीयते यस्य स्थितस्य च गतस्य च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You should think (and feel sad) for yourself. Why do you grieve for others? Your life that gets eroded everyday irrespective of who stays with you or leaves you.
quotes.transliteration:
ātmānamanuśoca tvaṃ kimanyamanuśocasi |
āyuste kṣīyate yasya sthitasya ca gatasya ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पततु नभः स्फुटतु मही चलतु गिरयो मिलन्तु वारिधयः।
अधरोत्तरमस्तु जगत् का हानिर्वीतरागस्य ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the skies fall, earth shatter, mountains move, oceans merge or let the world be turned upside down. How does it matter to a person who has no greed (or wishes).
quotes.transliteration:
patatu nabhaḥ sphuṭatu mahī calatu girayo milantu vāridhayaḥ|
adharottaramastu jagat kā hānirvītarāgasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
या दुस्त्यजा दुर्मतिभिर्या न जीर्यति जीर्यतः |
तां तृष्णां संत्यजेत् प्राज्ञः सुखेनैवाभिपूर्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Wicked people can not get rid of it. It does not get weaker in spite of you getting weaker due to old age. Such is the nature of greed. A wise man will realize that he has to distance himself from it. That is the only way he will be surrounded by happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
yā dustyajā durmatibhiryā na jīryati jīryataḥ |
tāṃ tṛṣṇāṃ saṃtyajet prājñaḥ sukhenaivābhipūryate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
याचना हि पुरुषस्य महत्वं
नाशयत्यखिलमेव तथा हि |
सद्य एव भगवानपि विष्णु-
र्वामनो भवति याचितुमिच्छन् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Beggary will destroy all the good qualities that a man has. Look at even Lord Vishnu. He had to become Vamana (midget) when he chose to beg alms from Raja Bali.
quotes.transliteration:
yācanā hi puruṣasya mahatvaṃ
nāśayatyakhilameva tathā hi |
sadya eva bhagavānapi viṣṇu-
rvāmano bhavati yācitumicchan ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शास्त्रार्थं तत्त्वतोज्ञात्वा चरिष्यामो वयं सदा |
न नो व्यसनिता काचित् विमतिप्रतिबोधने ||
quotes.englishTranslation: We are living in a way that is appropriate in terms of what is written in the scriptures. I think I have understood their essence. But I do not have the patience (or intent) to confront every one that says the opposite and make him believe in our scriptures.
quotes.transliteration:
śāstrārthaṃ tattvatojñātvā cariṣyāmo vayaṃ sadā |
na no vyasanitā kācit vimatipratibodhane ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाराजके जनपदे योगक्षेमः प्रवर्तते |
न चाप्यराजके सेना शत्रून् विषहते युधि ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When there is no king the welfare of the subjects is totally neglected. Without the chief, the army can not defend the country against the enemies.
quotes.transliteration:
nārājake janapade yogakṣemaḥ pravartate |
na cāpyarājake senā śatrūn viṣahate yudhi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धिग्बलं क्षत्रियबलं ब्रह्मतेजो बलं बलं ।
एकेन ब्रह्मदण्डेन सर्वास्त्राणि हतानि मे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Disgrace on all the strengths that is derived from muscle power. It is the brilliance of knowledge that is more powerful. By his one master stroke, all my weapons are destroyed. (This was uttered by Vishvamitra after losing to Vasishta in a fight)
quotes.transliteration:
dhigbalaṃ kṣatriyabalaṃ brahmatejo balaṃ balaṃ |
ekena brahmadaṇḍena sarvāstrāṇi hatāni me ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुरु गुरुवचो निपीतं
भूयो भूयो विचिन्तयाधीतं ।
विद्या गुरूपदिष्टा चिर
परिवृष्टा विभूषणं वपुषः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Whatever teacher teaches a student must imbibe. What is understood must be contemplated again and again. A well thought out education is a decoration to the body.
quotes.transliteration:
kuru guruvaco nipītaṃ
bhūyo bhūyo vicintayādhītaṃ |
vidyā gurūpadiṣṭā cira
parivṛṣṭā vibhūṣaṇaṃ vapuṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ते सभाग्या मनुष्येषु कृतार्था नृप निश्चितम् ।
स्मरंति ये स्मारयंति हरेर्नाम कलौयुगे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! King, In the Epoch of Kali, only those who remember and chant God's holy name will be fortunate. Also those people who encourage others to do so.
quotes.transliteration:
te sabhāgyā manuṣyeṣu kṛtārthā nṛpa niścitam |
smaraṃti ye smārayaṃti harernāma kalauyuge ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाधीतेऽत्र जनो यदि कश्चिधीते शते सहस्रे वा |
दुस्तर्केषु श्राम्यति दूरीकुरुते श्रुतिस्मृतिसुतर्कान् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In a place where people do not study (vedas and other scriptures), even if there exists one rogue among hundreds (at least thousands) then he will lead all of them away from dharma (the moral code of life) as depicted in the scriptures. He will use his contorted logic and convince everybody else that his way of adharma (against dharma) is right.
quotes.transliteration:
nādhīteऽtra jano yadi kaścidhīte śate sahasre vā |
dustarkeṣu śrāmyati dūrīkurute śrutismṛtisutarkān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नात्यर्थमर्थार्थनया धीमानुद्वेजयेज्जनम् ।
अब्धिर्दत्ताश्वरत्नश्रीर्मथ्यमानोऽसृजद्विषम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man will not press his employees too much in order to earn a lot of profits. He will do just enough to keep them motivated. When the Gods and Demons churned the ocean, the ocean first gave the divine horse (ucchaishravas), the lot of gems, then Goddess Lakshmi. Still they continued to churn. Hence the ocean gave them poison (halaahala). PS: The real order of what the ocean offered during the churning is not accurate in the verse above. But it is only indicative of how a servant's response will be if he is demanded to work beyond reasonable times.
quotes.transliteration:
nātyarthamarthārthanayā dhīmānudvejayejjanam |
abdhirdattāśvaratnaśrīrmathyamānoऽsṛjadviṣam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्यसनेष्वेव सर्वेषु यस्य बुद्धिर्न हीयते ।
स तेषां पारमभ्येति तत्प्रभावादसंशयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a man can hold his nerve and think rationally even when in trouble, then such a man will easily overcome the trouble using that very quality (rational thinking under pressure).
quotes.transliteration:
vyasaneṣveva sarveṣu yasya buddhirna hīyate |
sa teṣāṃ pāramabhyeti tatprabhāvādasaṃśayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्ञात्वाऽपराधं देशं च कालं बलमथापि वा ।
वयः कर्म च वित्तं च दण्डं दण्ड्येषु पातयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must consider all the following to give appropriate punishment for a crime - nature of the crime, place where it happened, time when it happened, strength of the perpetrator, his age, his occupation, his financial status. Only then will it be just.
quotes.transliteration:
jñātvāऽparādhaṃ deśaṃ ca kālaṃ balamathāpi vā |
vayaḥ karma ca vittaṃ ca daṇḍaṃ daṇḍyeṣu pātayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वेषामेव शौचानाम् अर्थशौचं परं स्मृतम् ।
योऽर्थे शुचिर्हि स शुचिर्न मृद्वारिशुचिः शुचिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Among all types of cleanliness, being clean in finance is the cleanest. The one who is clean in finance is the cleanest not the one who is clean with respect to all other 9 forms of cleanliness that include body, clothes and so on.
quotes.transliteration:
sarveṣāmeva śaucānām arthaśaucaṃ paraṃ smṛtam |
yoऽrthe śucirhi sa śucirna mṛdvāriśuciḥ śuciḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न लङ्घयेद्वत्सतन्त्रीं प्रधावेच्च वर्षति ।
न चोदके निरीक्षेत स्वं रूपमिति धारणा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You must not cross the rope used to tie a calf. (because the calf may be scared and try running away. This may hurt the calf. You may trip over the rope). You must not run when it is raining for obviuous danger of slippery landscapes. One must not see his own reflection in water (deep water. One of the common effects of doing so is 'vertigo').
quotes.transliteration:
na laṅghayedvatsatantrīṃ pradhāvecca varṣati |
na codake nirīkṣeta svaṃ rūpamiti dhāraṇā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नमो नेदिष्ठाय प्रियदव दविष्ठाय च नमो
नमः क्षोदिष्ठाय स्मरहर महिष्ठाय च नमः ।
नमो वर्षिष्ठाय त्रिनयन यविष्ठाय च नमो
नमः सर्वस्मै ते तदिदमतिसर्वाय च नमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I salute to the forest loving God who is both very near and very far. I salute to the greed killing God who is both very small and very big. I salute to God with the three eyes who very old and for ever young. I salute to Him who is all this and much more.
quotes.transliteration:
namo nediṣṭhāya priyadava daviṣṭhāya ca namo
namaḥ kṣodiṣṭhāya smarahara mahiṣṭhāya ca namaḥ |
namo varṣiṣṭhāya trinayana yaviṣṭhāya ca namo
namaḥ sarvasmai te tadidamatisarvāya ca namaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शतेषु जायते शूरः सहस्रेषु च पण्डितः ।
वक्ता दशसहस्रेषु दाता भवति वा न वा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One out of hundred men will be brave. One out of a thousand men would be a scholar. One out of ten thousand men will be a good orator. It is not yest sure if there is a person who can be called a real donor (selfless).
quotes.transliteration:
śateṣu jāyate śūraḥ sahasreṣu ca paṇḍitaḥ |
vaktā daśasahasreṣu dātā bhavati vā na vā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जीर्यन्ति जीर्यतः केशा दन्ता जीर्यन्ति जीर्यतः ।
चक्षुःश्रोत्रे च जीर्येते तृष्णैका तु न जीर्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The hair, the teeth, the eyes and the ears show signs of aging by getting weaker and weaker. It is only greed in a man that just does not get weaker (older).
quotes.transliteration:
jīryanti jīryataḥ keśā dantā jīryanti jīryataḥ |
cakṣuḥśrotre ca jīryete tṛṣṇaikā tu na jīryate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यजातां पाण्डवः स्वर्गमवत्विन्द्रस्तपत्विनः |
वयं हनाम द्विशतः सर्वः स्वार्थं समीहते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Let Yudhistira do penance, let Indra take care of heaven, let the sun give sunlight (and heat), let us kill the enemies. Every one in this world works for their own selfish reasons.
quotes.transliteration:
yajātāṃ pāṇḍavaḥ svargamavatvindrastapatvinaḥ |
vayaṃ hanāma dviśataḥ sarvaḥ svārthaṃ samīhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नानुतिष्ठति कार्याणि भयेषु च बिभेति च |
क्षिप्तं राज्याच्च्युतो दीनस्तृणैस्तुल्यो भवेदिह ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king does not undertake necessary project at the right time (when subjects need it the most) and does not fear from all the troubles facing him - he will soon lose his kingdom and respect. He will be as insignificant as a blade of grass.
quotes.transliteration:
nānutiṣṭhati kāryāṇi bhayeṣu ca bibheti ca |
kṣiptaṃ rājyāccyuto dīnastṛṇaistulyo bhavediha ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुष्टस्य दण्डः सुजनस्य पूजा
न्यायेन कोशस्य च संप्रवृद्धिः |
अपक्षपातोऽर्थिषु राष्ट्ररक्षा
पञ्चैव यज्ञाः कथिता नृपाणाम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the five pious duties of a king - punish the guilty, honor the rich, collect tax and enhance wealth without troubling the subjects, be impartial while giving judgment, defense of the state.
quotes.transliteration:
duṣṭasya daṇḍaḥ sujanasya pūjā
nyāyena kośasya ca saṃpravṛddhiḥ |
apakṣapātoऽrthiṣu rāṣṭrarakṣā
pañcaiva yajñāḥ kathitā nṛpāṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न त्यजेद्धर्ममर्यादामपि क्लेशदशां श्रितः ।
हरिश्चंद्र हि धर्मार्थी सेहे चाण्डालदासताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not breach Dharma even when in trouble. Raja Harishchandra never wavered from following Dharma even when was enslaved by a chandala (race which runs the crematoriums).
quotes.transliteration:
na tyajeddharmamaryādāmapi kleśadaśāṃ śritaḥ |
hariścaṃdra hi dharmārthī sehe cāṇḍāladāsatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परावाताः शिलाहाराः कदाचिदपि चातकाः |
न पिबन्ति महितोयं व्रतनस्ते भवन्ति किम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A pigeon eats (survives on ) just the stalks of corn. The chataka bird does never drink water from the ground. It only drinks water directly from the clouds when it rains. The above birds are only following their nature. Can we say they are penancing?
quotes.transliteration:
parāvātāḥ śilāhārāḥ kadācidapi cātakāḥ |
na pibanti mahitoyaṃ vratanaste bhavanti kim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निध्यायसि विषयसुखं न ध्यायसि विषममस्य परिपाकम् ।
बंधुं तमेव चिंतय बंधुं मोक्तुं च यः क्षमते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You are attaching yourself to worldly pleasures, not fully understanding the misery that follows. You should be thinking about God who alone can help you both attain attachment and detachment. He is the only attachment that one must have.
quotes.transliteration:
nidhyāyasi viṣayasukhaṃ na dhyāyasi viṣamamasya paripākam |
baṃdhuṃ tameva ciṃtaya baṃdhuṃ moktuṃ ca yaḥ kṣamate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देशभङ्गे प्रवासे वा व्याधिषु व्यसनेष्वपि ।
रक्षेदेव स्वदेहादि पश्चाद्धर्मं समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When the country is being plundered (by enemies), when on travel, when ill, when in trouble - one must strive to protect his own body. Only later must he worry about following Dharma (the moral code of life).
quotes.transliteration:
deśabhaṅge pravāse vā vyādhiṣu vyasaneṣvapi |
rakṣedeva svadehādi paścāddharmaṃ samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहो एषां वरं जन्म सर्वप्राण्युपजीवनम् ।
सुजनस्येव येषां वै विमुखा यान्ति नार्थिनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh!! these (trees) have a very noble life by supporting life of many beings (birds and animals). Like a virtuous person, they never disappoint any one who seeks their help.
quotes.transliteration:
aho eṣāṃ varaṃ janma sarvaprāṇyupajīvanam |
sujanasyeva yeṣāṃ vai vimukhā yānti nārthinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नियमयसि विमार्गप्रस्थितानात्तदंडः
प्रशमयसि विवादं कल्पसे रक्षणाय |
अतनषु विभवेषु ज्ञातयः सन्तु नाम
त्वयि तु परिसमाप्तं बन्धुकृत्यं प्रजानाम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: This verse describes how must a noble king behave. He must bring those who wander astray by punishing them. He must settle disputes among his subjects fairly. He must understand that many people will be willing to be his relatives/well-wishers when he has all the money. But he himself must behave as if all his subjects are his relatives. One must not favor any one subject more than the other.
quotes.transliteration:
niyamayasi vimārgaprasthitānāttadaṃḍaḥ
praśamayasi vivādaṃ kalpase rakṣaṇāya |
atanaṣu vibhaveṣu jñātayaḥ santu nāma
tvayi tu parisamāptaṃ bandhukṛtyaṃ prajānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राजसेवा मनुष्याणामसिधारावलेहनम् |
पञ्चाननपरिष्वङ्गो व्यालीवदनचुंबनम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Serving a king is a risky business. It is as risky as licking the sharp end of a blade, hugging a lion or kissing a snake.
quotes.transliteration:
rājasevā manuṣyāṇāmasidhārāvalehanam |
pañcānanapariṣvaṅgo vyālīvadanacuṃbanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्म एव हतो हन्ति धर्मो रक्षति रक्षितः ।
तस्माद्धर्मो न हन्तव्यः मानो धर्मो हतोवाधीत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any one who goes against dharma will be destroyed by dharma itself. He who protects dharma shall be protected by dharma. Hence it is not a good idea to destroy oneself by following dharma.
quotes.transliteration:
dharma eva hato hanti dharmo rakṣati rakṣitaḥ |
tasmāddharmo na hantavyaḥ māno dharmo hatovādhīt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राणानामनिलेन वृत्तिरुचिता सत्कल्पवृक्षे वने
तोये काञ्चानपद्मरेणुकपिशे धर्माभिषेकक्रिया ।
ध्यानं रत्नशिलातलेषु विबुधस्त्रीसंनिधौ संयमः
यत्कांक्षंति तपोभिरन्यमुनयः तस्मिंस्तपस्यंत्यमी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is a magical tree that can give all they desire. But beneath that, they are just doing rythmic breathing. There is a thin layer of pollen from lotus flowers over the water of the pond which is giving it a gold like glitter. In such a water they are only doing holy ablutions. They sit among gems and other precious stones and meditate. They have their senses under control even in presence of beautiful ladies of heaven. There are other people who do penances to be in such a situation. But, these saints are doing penances even after being among such luxuries.
quotes.transliteration:
prāṇānāmanilena vṛttirucitā satkalpavṛkṣe vane
toye kāñcānapadmareṇukapiśe dharmābhiṣekakriyā |
dhyānaṃ ratnaśilātaleṣu vibudhastrīsaṃnidhau saṃyamaḥ
yatkāṃkṣaṃti tapobhiranyamunayaḥ tasmiṃstapasyaṃtyamī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो न वेत्ति गुणान्यस्य न तं सेवेत पण्डितः ।
न हि तस्मात्फलं किंचित्सुकृष्णादूषरादिव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man must not serve a master who does not appreciate his qualities. It is just like ploughing hard a land with saline soil (desert). No result will come of it.
quotes.transliteration:
yo na vetti guṇānyasya na taṃ seveta paṇḍitaḥ |
na hi tasmātphalaṃ kiṃcitsukṛṣṇādūṣarādiva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षन्तव्यं प्रभुणा नित्यं कार्यिणां नृणाम् |
बालवृद्धातुराणां च कुर्वता हितमात्मनः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When a subject objects to a kings decision, the king must be inclined to forgive him. The cause of the objection would be despair that things are not going all to well. One must always endure criticism from children, seniors and sick people. Only good shall come out of it.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣantavyaṃ prabhuṇā nityaṃ kāryiṇāṃ nṛṇām |
bālavṛddhāturāṇāṃ ca kurvatā hitamātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनीषिणाः सन्ति न ते हितैषिणः
हितैषिणः सन्ति न ते मनीषिणाः ।
सहृच्च विद्वानपि दुर्लभो नृणां
यथौषधं स्वादु हितञ्च दुर्लभम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are intellectuals but they are not well-wishers. There are well wishers but they are not intellectual. It is hard to find a kind hearted person who is also intelligent - just like it is hard to find medicine that also tastes well.
quotes.transliteration:
manīṣiṇāḥ santi na te hitaiṣiṇaḥ
hitaiṣiṇaḥ santi na te manīṣiṇāḥ |
sahṛcca vidvānapi durlabho nṛṇāṃ
yathauṣadhaṃ svādu hitañca durlabham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सहैव मृत्युर्व्रजति सह मृत्युर्निषीदति ।
गत्वा सुदीर्घमध्वानं सह मृत्युर्निवर्तते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you go anywhere, death follows you. If you sit, it will sit next to you. If you go somewhere far, it will come with you. At last, it will take you along with it.
quotes.transliteration:
sahaiva mṛtyurvrajati saha mṛtyurniṣīdati |
gatvā sudīrghamadhvānaṃ saha mṛtyurnivartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अभिवादनशीलस्य नित्यं वृद्धोपसेविनः ।
चत्वारि संप्रवर्धन्ते कीर्तिरायुर्यशो बलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: For a person who respects and takes good care of elders will see his prospects grow - fame, longevity, success and strength
quotes.transliteration:
abhivādanaśīlasya nityaṃ vṛddhopasevinaḥ |
catvāri saṃpravardhante kīrtirāyuryaśo balam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा हि पथिकः कश्चिच्छायामाश्रित्य विश्रमेत् ।
विश्रम्य च पुनर्गच्छेत् तद्वद्भूतसमागमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Travelers seek shade to rest during the journey. They may meet others there. After taking rest, they proceed on their journey. Our associations in life are also temporary just like the travelers with other people at the resting place.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā hi pathikaḥ kaścicchāyāmāśritya viśramet |
viśramya ca punargacchet tadvadbhūtasamāgamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्र निर्मलभावेन सम्सारे वर्तते गृही ।
धर्मं चरति निष्कामं तत्रैव रमते हरिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sri Hari will reside there where family men will lead their life according to Dharma with pure thoughts and without any attachemnts and greed. Family, World
quotes.transliteration:
yatra nirmalabhāvena samsāre vartate gṛhī |
dharmaṃ carati niṣkāmaṃ tatraiva ramate hariḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सतीमपि ज्ञातिकुलैकसंश्रयां
जनोऽन्यथा भर्तृमतीं विशंकते ।
अतः समीपे परिणेतुरिष्यते
प्रियाप्रिया वा प्रमदा स्वबंदुभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a devoted wife is looked up by suspicion by other people (relatives) if she is not living with her husband (and living with her parents). So, a wife must live with her husband irrespective of whether he likes her or not.
quotes.transliteration:
satīmapi jñātikulaikasaṃśrayāṃ
janoऽnyathā bhartṛmatīṃ viśaṃkate |
ataḥ samīpe pariṇeturiṣyate
priyāpriyā vā pramadā svabaṃdubhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्ञात्वा बुद्बुदभंगुरं धनमिदं दीपप्रकंपं वपुः
तारुण्यं तरलेक्षणाक्षितरलं विद्युच्चलं दोर्बलं ।
रे रे जीव गुरुप्रसादवशतः किंचिद्विधेहि द्रुतं
दानध्यानतपोविधानविषयं पुण्यं पवित्रोचितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth is as fickle as a bubble on water surface. The body is as uncertain as flick of a flame. Youth is as reliable as the trembling look from the corner of the eye of a damsel. The strength in your arms are like lightning. Given all these uncertainties, one must accumulate good karma as early as possible. Start doing some charity, meditation, penance and other good deeds to earn some good karma.
quotes.transliteration:
jñātvā budbudabhaṃguraṃ dhanamidaṃ dīpaprakaṃpaṃ vapuḥ
tāruṇyaṃ taralekṣaṇākṣitaralaṃ vidyuccalaṃ dorbalaṃ |
re re jīva guruprasādavaśataḥ kiṃcidvidhehi drutaṃ
dānadhyānatapovidhānaviṣayaṃ puṇyaṃ pavitrocitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नारुंतुदः स्यादार्तोऽपि न परद्रोहकर्मधीः |
यथाऽस्योद्विजते वाचा नालोक्यां तामुदीरयेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not speak harshly to any one even when he is in misery himself. Even in one's mind he must not think about hurting others. One must not speak in a way that will be perceived by most as inappropriate (or against prevailing norms).
quotes.transliteration:
nāruṃtudaḥ syādārtoऽpi na paradrohakarmadhīḥ |
yathāऽsyodvijate vācā nālokyāṃ tāmudīrayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बुद्धश्चेच्चित्तमातंगः स्मृतिरज्ज्वा समंततः ।
भयमस्तंगतं सर्वं कृत्स्नं कल्याणमागतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one controls his mind (which runs in all directions) by meditating, just like an elephant is controlled by tying ropes, then there is no place for fear. All good things will follow you.
quotes.transliteration:
buddhaśceccittamātaṃgaḥ smṛtirajjvā samaṃtataḥ |
bhayamastaṃgataṃ sarvaṃ kṛtsnaṃ kalyāṇamāgatam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पदाहतोऽपि दृढदण्डसमाहतोऽपि
यं दंष्ट्रया स्पृशति तं किल हन्ति सर्पः |
कोप्येष एव पिशुनोग्रमनुष्यधर्मः
कर्णे परं स्पृशति हन्ति परं समूलम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A snake bites those who stamp over it, or beat it with a stick. Only the person who gets bitten by a snake dies. But the behavior of a rumor monger is strange. He bites one person's ears (with a rumor) and some one else gets destroyed all together.
quotes.transliteration:
padāhatoऽpi dṛḍhadaṇḍasamāhatoऽpi
yaṃ daṃṣṭrayā spṛśati taṃ kila hanti sarpaḥ |
kopyeṣa eva piśunogramanuṣyadharmaḥ
karṇe paraṃ spṛśati hanti paraṃ samūlam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रागं विजृंभयति संश्रयते प्रमादं
दोषान् न चिंतयति साहसमभ्युपैति |
स्वच्छंदतो व्रजति नेच्छति नीतिमार्गं
बुद्धिं शुभां सुविदुषामवशीकरोति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: It increases desires. It accompanies madness. It does not let you see your own mistakes. It makes you take up risky adventures. It makes you yearn for a care-free existence. It forces you to go away from the right path. It controls your wisdom even though are intelligent. Such is the effect of youth on a person.
quotes.transliteration:
rāgaṃ vijṛṃbhayati saṃśrayate pramādaṃ
doṣān na ciṃtayati sāhasamabhyupaiti |
svacchaṃdato vrajati necchati nītimārgaṃ
buddhiṃ śubhāṃ suviduṣāmavaśīkaroti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पृथिवी रत्न संपूर्णा हिरण्यं पशवः स्त्रियः ।
नालमेकस्य तत्सर्वमिति मत्वा शमं व्रजेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This earth gives lot of gems, gold, animals and women. Even if all of that is given to one person he will still not be satisfied. The moral of the story being that greed can not be satisfied by trying to satiate it.
quotes.transliteration:
pṛthivī ratna saṃpūrṇā hiraṇyaṃ paśavaḥ striyaḥ |
nālamekasya tatsarvamiti matvā śamaṃ vrajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रोक्तः प्रत्युत्तरं नाह विरुद्धं प्रभुणा च यः |
न समीपे हसत्युच्चैः स भवेद्राजवल्लभः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He will be liked by a king who will neither speak against him nor laugh loudly in his presence. [But that is not good always to be liked by such a king.]
quotes.transliteration:
proktaḥ pratyuttaraṃ nāha viruddhaṃ prabhuṇā ca yaḥ |
na samīpe hasatyuccaiḥ sa bhavedrājavallabhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वरं कार्यं मौनं न च वचनमुक्तं यदनृतं
वरं क्लैब्यं पुंसां न च परकलत्राभिगमनम् ।
वरं प्राणत्यागो न च पिशुनवादेष्वभिरतिः
वरं भिक्षाशित्वं न च परधनास्वादनबलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Better be silent than telling lies. Better be impotent than be promiscuous. Better death than be a rumor-monger. Better live on alms than be feel powerful based on money belonging to someone else.
quotes.transliteration:
varaṃ kāryaṃ maunaṃ na ca vacanamuktaṃ yadanṛtaṃ
varaṃ klaibyaṃ puṃsāṃ na ca parakalatrābhigamanam |
varaṃ prāṇatyāgo na ca piśunavādeṣvabhiratiḥ
varaṃ bhikṣāśitvaṃ na ca paradhanāsvādanabalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सेवेव मानमखिलं ज्योत्स्नेव तमो जरेव लावण्यम् |
हरिहरकथेव दुरितं गुणशतमप्यर्थिता हरति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Servitude will kill any self-respect you have. Moon light will kill all darkness. Old age will kill youthfulness. Listening to stories of Lord Vishnu and Shiva will kill all your worries and troubles. Likewise, greediness will kill any other good qualities a man may have.
quotes.transliteration:
seveva mānamakhilaṃ jyotsneva tamo jareva lāvaṇyam |
hariharakatheva duritaṃ guṇaśatamapyarthitā harati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकाण्डपातजातानामार्द्राणां मर्मभेदिनां ।
गाढशोकप्रहाराणामचिंतैव माहौषधिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Not thinking about them is the best medicine for grief like - babies that died while taking birth, fresh wounds causing great pain (body shattering pain), any other series of great grief.
quotes.transliteration:
akāṇḍapātajātānāmārdrāṇāṃ marmabhedināṃ |
gāḍhaśokaprahārāṇāmaciṃtaiva māhauṣadhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तदश्मसारं हृदयं बतेदं
यद्गृह्यमाणैर्हरिनामधेयैः ।
न विक्रियेताथ यदा विकारो
नेत्रे जलं गात्ररुहेषु हर्षः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: tadashmasAram hRudayam batedam He is a stone hearted fellow if his eyes don't become moist with tears when talking about the Lord.
quotes.transliteration:
tadaśmasāraṃ hṛdayaṃ batedaṃ
yadgṛhyamāṇairharināmadheyaiḥ |
na vikriyetātha yadā vikāro
netre jalaṃ gātraruheṣu harṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चाटतस्करदुर्वृत्तमहासाहसिकादिभिः ।
पीड्यमानाः प्रजा रक्षेत्कायस्थैश्च विशेषतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A King must protect his subjects from the deceivers, thieves, rogues, bandits and especially the clerical staff (of administration)
quotes.transliteration:
cāṭataskaradurvṛttamahāsāhasikādibhiḥ |
pīḍyamānāḥ prajā rakṣetkāyasthaiśca viśeṣataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रज्ञया मानसं दुःखं हन्याच्छारीरमौषधैः ।
एतद्विज्ञानसामर्थ्यं न बालैः समतामियात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like taking medicine eases pain in the body, knowledge eases the pain in the mind. Such is the power of knowledge. Without that, we are all like kids who can not tell what is troubling them.
quotes.transliteration:
prajñayā mānasaṃ duḥkhaṃ hanyācchārīramauṣadhaiḥ |
etadvijñānasāmarthyaṃ na bālaiḥ samatāmiyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो यत्र कुशलो कार्ये तं तत्र विनियोजयेत् ।
कर्मस्वधृष्टकर्मा यच्छास्त्रज्ञोपि विमुह्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must allocate work to people based on their skills. One must not try to do all work himself. Even a well educated man may get lost if he tries to do something that he knows nothing about.
quotes.transliteration:
yo yatra kuśalo kārye taṃ tatra viniyojayet |
karmasvadhṛṣṭakarmā yacchāstrajñopi vimuhyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आधिव्याधिपरितापादद्य श्वो वा विनाशिने ।
को हि नाम शरीराय धर्मापेतं समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This nameless body is destined to be destroyed by illness - of body or mind. It's only a matter of today or tomorrow. Why should one do any thing against dharma (moral code of life) for the sake of this body?
quotes.transliteration:
ādhivyādhiparitāpādadya śvo vā vināśine |
ko hi nāma śarīrāya dharmāpetaṃ samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विनयो रत्नमुकुटं सच्छास्त्रं मणिकुण्डले ।
त्यागश्च कङ्कणं येषां कि तेषां जडमण्डनैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What good are earthly ornaments for a person who wears the crown of humility, his knowledge of scriptures as ear pieces and sacrifice as his bangles?
quotes.transliteration:
vinayo ratnamukuṭaṃ sacchāstraṃ maṇikuṇḍale |
tyāgaśca kaṅkaṇaṃ yeṣāṃ ki teṣāṃ jaḍamaṇḍanaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शाठ्येन मित्रं कपटेन धर्मं
परोपतापेन समृद्धभावम् |
सुखेन विद्यां परुषेण नारीं
वांछन्ति ये नूनमपण्डितास्ते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He is a fool who thinks he can get friends by tricking people, follow dharma by deceit, can get richer (happily) by troubling others, get education by lazing around, win a woman's heart by force. A person with such beliefs is definitely fooling himself.
quotes.transliteration:
śāṭhyena mitraṃ kapaṭena dharmaṃ
paropatāpena samṛddhabhāvam |
sukhena vidyāṃ paruṣeṇa nārīṃ
vāṃchanti ye nūnamapaṇḍitāste ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वयो बुद्ध्यर्थवाग्वेष श्रुताभिजनकर्मणां ।
आचरेत्सदृशीं वृत्तिं अजिह्मामशठां तथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must keep himself busy with occupation that suits his age, intelligence, wealth, language (speaking ability), dressing sense, education, lineage (family born into) and skill. At any cost one must not cheat. One must not walk down the path of dishonesty.
quotes.transliteration:
vayo buddhyarthavāgveṣa śrutābhijanakarmaṇāṃ |
ācaretsadṛśīṃ vṛttiṃ ajihmāmaśaṭhāṃ tathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यशोदया समा कापि देवतनां तु भूतले ।
उलूखले यया बद्धो मुक्तिदो मुक्तिमिच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no one equal to Yashodha even in the group of Gods. While every one is trying to free themselves from the bondage the only one who can free them all (Krishna) is trying to free himself from Yashodha.
quotes.transliteration:
yaśodayā samā kāpi devatanāṃ tu bhūtale |
ulūkhale yayā baddho muktido muktimicchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्रजन्ति ते मूढधियः पराभवं
भवन्ति मायाविषु येन मायिनः |
प्रविश्य हि घ्नंति शठास्तथाविधान्
असंवृताङ्गान् निशिता इवेषवः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If person does not deal with a crook in crooked manner, he is bound to face defeat. They must be paid back in their own coin. Just like how an arrow most certainly hits (and hurts) that part of the body (how ever small) that is not covered by the armor, such deceitful people search and hurt only those people who are innocent and incapable of deceit themselves.
quotes.transliteration:
vrajanti te mūḍhadhiyaḥ parābhavaṃ
bhavanti māyāviṣu yena māyinaḥ |
praviśya hi ghnaṃti śaṭhāstathāvidhān
asaṃvṛtāṅgān niśitā iveṣavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हेतौ लिंगे प्रशमने रोगाणामपुनर्भवे |
ज्ञानं चतुर्विधं यस्य स राजार्हो भिषक्तमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A doctor is worthy to be called a Royal Doctor only if he is skilled in all four dimensions of medicine - recognizing the symptoms, diagnosing the root cause, curing the disease and preventing it from re-occurrence.
quotes.transliteration:
hetau liṃge praśamane rogāṇāmapunarbhave |
jñānaṃ caturvidhaṃ yasya sa rājārho bhiṣaktamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणवज्जनसंसर्गात् याति नीचोपि गौरवम् ।
पुष्पमालाप्रसङ्गेन सूत्रम् शिरसि धार्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When found among virtuous people, even the wicked get lot of respect. Just like a simple thread gets worn on the head when it is found with in the chain of flowers.
quotes.transliteration:
guṇavajjanasaṃsargāt yāti nīcopi gauravam |
puṣpamālāprasaṅgena sūtram śirasi dhāryate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षमा धनुः करे यस्य दुर्जनः किं करिष्यति ।
अतृणे पतितो वह्निः स्वयमेवोपशाम्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one is armed with patience, no wicked person can harm him. Fire that falls were there is no dry grass dies on its own.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣamā dhanuḥ kare yasya durjanaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati |
atṛṇe patito vahniḥ svayamevopaśāmyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न कन्यायाः पिता विद्वान् गृह्णीयात् शुल्कमण्वपि ।
गृह्णन् शुल्कं हि लोभेन स्यान्नरोऽपत्यविक्रयी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Long ago the dowry custom in India was reverse of what exists today. The groom paid the bride's father a fee before asking for his daughter's hand. But this shloka advises the father not to accept even a small fee. The father of the bride is warned that if he accepts even a small amount as fee, it would tantamount to selling his own child.
quotes.transliteration:
na kanyāyāḥ pitā vidvān gṛhṇīyāt śulkamaṇvapi |
gṛhṇan śulkaṃ hi lobhena syānnaroऽpatyavikrayī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रस्तावसदृशं वाक्यं सद्भावसदृशं प्रियम् ।
आत्मशक्तिसमं कोपं यो जानाति स पण्डितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Being able to talk according to context, liking some thing because of its good nature, to keep anger in one's limits; he is a wise man who can do the above.
quotes.transliteration:
prastāvasadṛśaṃ vākyaṃ sadbhāvasadṛśaṃ priyam |
ātmaśaktisamaṃ kopaṃ yo jānāti sa paṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पाण्डित्येन प्रचण्डेन येन माद्यन्ति दुर्जनाः ।
तेनैव सज्जना रूढां यान्ति शान्तिमनुत्तमाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a rogue acquires some knowledge he starts flaunting it. The same knowledge is used by wise men to seek the ultimate inner peace.
quotes.transliteration:
pāṇḍityena pracaṇḍena yena mādyanti durjanāḥ |
tenaiva sajjanā rūḍhāṃ yānti śāntimanuttamām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अभावावधयो भावा निधनावधि जीवितम् ।
अविवेकस्त्वनवधिर्येनायं जायते भ्रमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All materials come with a definite date by which it will get destroyed. Some early, some late. But foolishness has no such date. It lives on and on. That creates all these illusions.
quotes.transliteration:
abhāvāvadhayo bhāvā nidhanāvadhi jīvitam |
avivekastvanavadhiryenāyaṃ jāyate bhramaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकः प्रजायते जन्तुरेक एव प्रलीयते ।
एकोऽनुभुन्क्ते सुकृतं एक एव च दुष्कृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Beings come to this world alone. They die alone. Alone must they enjoy the fruits of the good deeds. Alone must they suffer the fruits of their mis-deeds.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaḥ prajāyate jantureka eva pralīyate |
ekoऽnubhunkte sukṛtaṃ eka eva ca duṣkṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यं ब्रूयात् प्रियं ब्रूयात्
न ब्रूयात् सत्यमप्रियम् ।
प्रियं च नानृतं ब्रूयात्
एष धर्मः सनातनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must speak truth. One must speak pleasant. One must not speak the truth that is harsh. One must not speak a lie just because it is pleasant. This is the essence of Dharma.
quotes.transliteration:
satyaṃ brūyāt priyaṃ brūyāt
na brūyāt satyamapriyam |
priyaṃ ca nānṛtaṃ brūyāt
eṣa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न किंचित् साधवो धीरा भक्ता ह्येकान्तिनो मम ।
वांछंत्यपि मया दत्तं कैवल्यमपुनर्भवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The courageous, noble devotees of mine who seek solitude do not wish for anything. Not even heaven or the release from eternal cycle of births - both of which I can bestow on them.
quotes.transliteration:
na kiṃcit sādhavo dhīrā bhaktā hyekāntino mama |
vāṃchaṃtyapi mayā dattaṃ kaivalyamapunarbhavam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न जीवनाशोऽस्ति हि देहभेदे
मिथ्यैतदाहुर्म्रियते किलेति ।
जीवस्तु देहांतरितः प्रयाति
दशार्धतैवास्य शरीरभेदः ॥
