quotes.englishTranslation: When the soul changes the body, there is no loss of life. One must not lie that there is death. When the soul changes form, the body unites with the five forces of nature.
quotes.transliteration:
na jīvanāśoऽsti hi dehabhede
mithyaitadāhurmriyate kileti |
jīvastu dehāṃtaritaḥ prayāti
daśārdhataivāsya śarīrabhedaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रीषु नर्मविवाहे च वृत्त्यर्थे प्राणसंकटे ।
गोब्राह्मणार्थे हिंसयां नानृतं स्याज्जुगुप्सितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not a sin to lie under these circumstances - when talking to a woman, during a wedding, in your occupation, facing danger to your life, to protect a brahmin who accepts donation of a cow, to escape from violence.
quotes.transliteration:
strīṣu narmavivāhe ca vṛttyarthe prāṇasaṃkaṭe |
gobrāhmaṇārthe hiṃsayāṃ nānṛtaṃ syājjugupsitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वंगाः कथमंगाः कथमित्यनुयुंक्ते वृथा देशान् ।
कीदृक् कृतान्तपुरमिति कोऽपि जिज्ञासते लोकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: How is Vanga kingdom? How is the Anga kingdom? People ask such questions about far of lands. No one in this world are contemplating on where they would be after death. How would that kingdom of death be?
quotes.transliteration:
vaṃgāḥ kathamaṃgāḥ kathamityanuyuṃkte vṛthā deśān |
kīdṛk kṛtāntapuramiti koऽpi jijñāsate lokaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विषं रुधिरमासाद्य प्रसर्पति यथा तनौ |
तथैव छिद्रमासाद्य दोषस्चित्ते प्रसर्पति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like how poison spreads through the entire body with the help of blood, one bad quality (weakness) will spread through the entire mind using a single opportunity (a moment of weakness).
quotes.transliteration:
viṣaṃ rudhiramāsādya prasarpati yathā tanau |
tathaiva chidramāsādya doṣascitte prasarpati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत् कॄत्वा न भवेद्धर्मो न कीर्तिर्न यशो भुवि ।
शरीरस्य भवेत् खेदः कस्तत् कर्म समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who would do any action that does not fall into these categories? - to uphold Dharma, to bring you glory or to spread your fame. All actions of man can be classified as above.
quotes.transliteration:
yat kṝtvā na bhaveddharmo na kīrtirna yaśo bhuvi |
śarīrasya bhavet khedaḥ kastat karma samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अक्षराणि परीक्ष्यंतामंबराडंबरेण किं ।
शंभुरंबरहीनोपि सर्वज्ञः किं न कथ्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let us examine the education (wisdom) of a person. What good are the clothes and ornaments? Isn't Lord Shiva known to be Sarvajna (Omniscient or know-all) in spite of not wearing any clothes?
quotes.transliteration:
akṣarāṇi parīkṣyaṃtāmaṃbarāḍaṃbareṇa kiṃ |
śaṃbhuraṃbarahīnopi sarvajñaḥ kiṃ na kathyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मंत्रे तीर्थे द्विजे दैवे दैवज्ञे भेषजे गुरौ ।
यादृशीं भावनां कुर्यात् सिद्धिर्भवति तादृशी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Hymns, holy places, a brahmin, God, one who has realized God, Doctor and teacher. If you treat them well, you will benefit. If you treat them badly, you will suffer. What happens to you depends on how you treat them.
quotes.transliteration:
maṃtre tīrthe dvije daive daivajñe bheṣaje gurau |
yādṛśīṃ bhāvanāṃ kuryāt siddhirbhavati tādṛśī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वर्णं यथा ग्रावसु हेमकारः
क्षेत्रेषु योगैस्तदभिज्ञ आप्नुयात् ।
क्षेत्रेषु देहेषु तथात्मयोगैः
अध्यात्मविद् ब्रह्मगतिं लभेत ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a gold hunter can get gold from barren earth with his knowledge and perseverance, one must make use of this body and persevere with spirituality and attain salvation.
quotes.transliteration:
svarṇaṃ yathā grāvasu hemakāraḥ
kṣetreṣu yogaistadabhijña āpnuyāt |
kṣetreṣu deheṣu tathātmayogaiḥ
adhyātmavid brahmagatiṃ labheta ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न स्वल्पस्य कृते भूरि नाशयेन्मतिमान् नरः ।
एतदेवात्रपाण्दित्यं यत्स्वल्पाद्भूरिरक्षणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man will never lose a fortune while running behind petty goals. He is never "Penny wise, Pound foolish". Not losing sight of long term goals is a hall mark of wise men.
quotes.transliteration:
na svalpasya kṛte bhūri nāśayenmatimān naraḥ |
etadevātrapāṇdityaṃ yatsvalpādbhūrirakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आदित्यस्योदयं तात तांबूलं भारतीकथा ।
इष्टा भार्या समित्रं च अपूर्वाणि दिनेदिने ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Father! the sunrise, beetle leaves, story from the Mahabharatha, loving wife and a good friend always have a freshness with them. They are always new.
quotes.transliteration:
ādityasyodayaṃ tāta tāṃbūlaṃ bhāratīkathā |
iṣṭā bhāryā samitraṃ ca apūrvāṇi dinedine ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नूनं जनेन पुरुषे महति प्रयुक्तं
अगः परं तदनुरूपफलं प्रसूते |
कृत्वा रघूद्वहगतेः क्षणमंतारायं
यद्भार्गवः परगतेर्विहतिं प्रपेदे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: When a common man's actions impede the great undertakings of a noble man, then the common man will meet with befitting fate (as punishment). Look at what happened to Parashurama (one of the incarnations of Lord Vishnu who will be in that form ever without salvation) for having impeded Rama's mission on earth although it was only for a second. Parashurama is destined to be in this world (earth) for ever without ever returning to his original self (as Vishnu).
quotes.transliteration:
nūnaṃ janena puruṣe mahati prayuktaṃ
agaḥ paraṃ tadanurūpaphalaṃ prasūte |
kṛtvā raghūdvahagateḥ kṣaṇamaṃtārāyaṃ
yadbhārgavaḥ paragatervihatiṃ prapede ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वृक्षांश्छित्वा पशून्हत्वा कृत्वा रुधिरकर्दमं ।
यद्येवं गम्यते स्वर्गे नरकः केन गम्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If people who cut trees, kill animals and spill blood go to heaven then who goes to hell?
quotes.transliteration:
vṛkṣāṃśchitvā paśūnhatvā kṛtvā rudhirakardamaṃ |
yadyevaṃ gamyate svarge narakaḥ kena gamyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुवर्णपुष्पितां पृथ्वीं विचिन्वन्ति नरास्त्रयः |
शूरश्च कृतविद्यश्च यश्च जानाति सेवितुम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Three kinds of people in this world will hit gold (happiness) - the brave, the wise and a person who knows the art of service.
quotes.transliteration:
suvarṇapuṣpitāṃ pṛthvīṃ vicinvanti narāstrayaḥ |
śūraśca kṛtavidyaśca yaśca jānāti sevitum ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतिमलिने कर्त्व्ये भवति
खलानामतीवनिपुणा धीः ।
तिमरे हि कौशिकानां रूपं
प्रतिपाद्यते दृष्टिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Rogues have the keenest intelligence while commiting henious deeds. Just like the owl can make out objects visually only at night.
quotes.transliteration:
atimaline kartvye bhavati
khalānāmatīvanipuṇā dhīḥ |
timare hi kauśikānāṃ rūpaṃ
pratipādyate dṛṣṭiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यवीयानात्मनः पुत्रः शिष्यश्चापि गुणोदितः ।
पुत्रवत्ते त्रयश्चिंत्या धर्मश्चेदत्र कारणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one wishes to follow Dharma, then he must treat his brother, his son and his disciple - all three of them equally as if they were his children.
quotes.transliteration:
yavīyānātmanaḥ putraḥ śiṣyaścāpi guṇoditaḥ |
putravatte trayaściṃtyā dharmaścedatra kāraṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अत्यंतमलिनः कायः नवच्छिद्रसमन्वितः ।
स्रवत्येष दिवारात्रं प्रातःस्नानेन शुद्ध्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This body, which has nine holes is very dirty. Dirt gets into the body through the nine holes all during the day or night. Hence every morning one must wash himself.
quotes.transliteration:
atyaṃtamalinaḥ kāyaḥ navacchidrasamanvitaḥ |
sravatyeṣa divārātraṃ prātaḥsnānena śuddhyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गर्भे व्याधौ श्मशाने च पुराणे या मतिर्भवेत् ।
सा यदि स्थिरतां याति को न मुच्येत बन्धनात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When in the womb, or ill, or attending a funeral, or listening to the scriptures a man gets some clarity on his thoughts. (In the womb, his mind is not corrupted by the world). If only wisdom found in such situations would stay with them all along; every one would be liberated from this world.
quotes.transliteration:
garbhe vyādhau śmaśāne ca purāṇe yā matirbhavet |
sā yadi sthiratāṃ yāti ko na mucyeta bandhanāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गात्रेषु वलयः प्राप्ताः श्वेताश्चैव शिरोरुहाः ।
जरया पुरुषो जीर्णः किं हि कृत्वा प्रभावयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wrinkles all over the body, grey hair on the head, weakness in the body - is there anything that can prevent this happening to anybody?
quotes.transliteration:
gātreṣu valayaḥ prāptāḥ śvetāścaiva śiroruhāḥ |
jarayā puruṣo jīrṇaḥ kiṃ hi kṛtvā prabhāvayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न जातु कामः कामानामुपभोगेन शम्यति ।
हविषा कृष्णवर्त्मेव भूय एवाभिवर्धते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Lust, wanton or greed will not be conquered by satisfying it. It is like trying to douse a fire by adding ghee. It will only grow stronger.
quotes.transliteration:
na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānāmupabhogena śamyati |
haviṣā kṛṣṇavartmeva bhūya evābhivardhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शतं दद्यान्न विवदेदिति सुज्ञस्य सम्मतम् |
विना हेतुमपि द्वंद्वं तत्स्यान्मूर्खस्य लक्षणम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man will avoid arguments if it can be avoided with some loss (about a hundred rupees). He thinks like that because he knows that the hundred he would save by an argument is not worth the fight. On the other hand, a fool would kick up an argument even if he has no reason to do so. He may be just hoping that the other person will just give up some money. Money so earned will never do any good.
quotes.transliteration:
śataṃ dadyānna vivadediti sujñasya sammatam |
vinā hetumapi dvaṃdvaṃ tatsyānmūrkhasya lakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वाधीनं समतिक्रम्य मातरं पितरं गुरुम् ।
अस्वाधीनं कथं दैवं प्रकारैरभिराध्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Mother, father and teacher are the Gods that we see. What kind of religion is that which tells us to forget them and go behind Gods that we do not see?
quotes.transliteration:
svādhīnaṃ samatikramya mātaraṃ pitaraṃ gurum |
asvādhīnaṃ kathaṃ daivaṃ prakārairabhirādhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनिष्टादिष्टलाभेपि नायतिर्जायते शुभा ।
यत्रास्ति विषसंसर्गोऽमृतम् तदपि मृत्यवे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A bad thing is always bad. Even if it gives you short term profit, the long term effect will always be bad. If a little bit of poison is mixed with elixir, the result is still death.
quotes.transliteration:
aniṣṭādiṣṭalābhepi nāyatirjāyate śubhā |
yatrāsti viṣasaṃsargoऽmṛtam tadapi mṛtyave ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अच्युतानंतगोविन्दनामोच्चारणभेषजात् ।
नश्यन्ति सकलाः रोगाः सत्यं सत्यं ब्रवीम्यहम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Achyuta, Ananta and Govinda - if one chants the name of god like this, it becomes a medicine that can cure all ailments. I swear on this truth.
quotes.transliteration:
acyutānaṃtagovindanāmoccāraṇabheṣajāt |
naśyanti sakalāḥ rogāḥ satyaṃ satyaṃ bravīmyaham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भवन्ति बहवो मूर्ख़ाः क्वचिदेको विशुद्धधीः |
त्रासितोऽपि सदा मूर्ख़ैरचलो यस्य बुद्धिमान् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There are many a fools in this world. But there are very few people who can be called wise. Only those who can keep their minds from wavering in spite of attacks from the foolish are the only real wise people.
quotes.transliteration:
bhavanti bahavo mūrख़āḥ kvacideko viśuddhadhīḥ |
trāsitoऽpi sadā mūrख़airacalo yasya buddhimān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्लिष्टा क्रिया कस्यचिदात्मसंस्था
संक्रान्तिरन्यस्य विशेषयुक्ता ।
यस्योभयं साधु स शिक्षकाणां
धुरि प्रतिष्ठापयितव्य एव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Some people can perform particular tasks very well. Some other people can teach very well but may not perform the task that well. If a person can do both, then he must be treated as the best of the teachers.
quotes.transliteration:
śliṣṭā kriyā kasyacidātmasaṃsthā
saṃkrāntiranyasya viśeṣayuktā |
yasyobhayaṃ sādhu sa śikṣakāṇāṃ
dhuri pratiṣṭhāpayitavya eva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं मित्रमंते सुकृतं न लोकाः
किं ध्येयमीशस्य पदं न शोकाः ।
किं काम्यमव्याजसुखं न भोगाः
किं जल्पनीयं हरिनाम नान्यत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kim mitramante sukRutam na lokAH Who is a friend at the end? Your own deeds, not the entire world. Who should be remembered? the foot of the lord, not the miseries. What should be desired? Pure happiness of salvation, not sensory pleasures. What should be spoken? Only the name of Hari (God), not anything else.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ mitramaṃte sukṛtaṃ na lokāḥ
kiṃ dhyeyamīśasya padaṃ na śokāḥ |
kiṃ kāmyamavyājasukhaṃ na bhogāḥ
kiṃ jalpanīyaṃ harināma nānyat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वेदाभ्यासस्तपो ज्ञानं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः |
धर्मक्रियात्मचिन्ता च सात्त्विकं गुणलक्षणं ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the signs of a virtuous person - studying vedas, penance, acquiring knowledge, cleanliness, control over senses, following dharma (moral code of life) and introspection.
quotes.transliteration:
vedābhyāsastapo jñānaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ |
dharmakriyātmacintā ca sāttvikaṃ guṇalakṣaṇaṃ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तृष्णे देवि नमस्तुभ्यं याया वित्तान्विता अपि ।
अकृत्येषु नियोज्यन्ते भ्राम्यन्ते दुर्गमेष्वपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Goddess of greed!! I salute with respect. The reason I do that is because I think you are very powerful. Under your influence even wealthy people (whose needs are satisfied) will wander in desolate areas - forts and hills (searching for money).
quotes.transliteration:
tṛṣṇe devi namastubhyaṃ yāyā vittānvitā api |
akṛtyeṣu niyojyante bhrāmyante durgameṣvapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्माथौ यत्र न स्यातां शुश्रूषावाऽपि तद्विधा ।
तत्र विद्या न वक्तव्या शुभं बीजमिवोषरे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not teach a person anything if he does not have any one of the following qualities: follows dharma, is wealthy, has a mindset for service. Without any of these qualities, teaching him is equivalent to sowing seeds in a barren land.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmāthau yatra na syātāṃ śuśrūṣāvāऽpi tadvidhā |
tatra vidyā na vaktavyā śubhaṃ bījamivoṣare ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाच्यावाच्यं प्रकुपितो न विजानाति कर्हिचित् ।
नाकार्यमस्ति क्रुद्धस्य नावाच्यं विद्यते क्वचित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A angry person can not understand what is to be said and what is to be left unsaid. He can not control himself. He can do anything (wrong) and speak anything (bad). Hence one must refrain from anger.
quotes.transliteration:
vācyāvācyaṃ prakupito na vijānāti karhicit |
nākāryamasti kruddhasya nāvācyaṃ vidyate kvacit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पृथिव्यां त्रीणि रत्नानि जलमन्नंसुभाषितम् ।
मूढैः पाषाणखण्डेषुरत्नसंज्ञा विधीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world there are three gems - water, food and wise sayings. Idiots are those who search for gems among stones.
quotes.transliteration:
pṛthivyāṃ trīṇi ratnāni jalamannaṃsubhāṣitam |
mūḍhaiḥ pāṣāṇakhaṇḍeṣuratnasaṃjñā vidhīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अवैष्णवो हतो देशो हतं श्राद्धमपात्रकम् ।
हतमश्रोत्रिये दानमनाचारं हतं कुलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A country is ruined when its people lose faith (in God). A ritual (for the dead) is ruined if there are no deserved people who can accept donations. Charity to given to some one who is not well versed with the scriptures is a waste. A whole family get ruined if it forgets the culture and traditions of the times.
quotes.transliteration:
avaiṣṇavo hato deśo hataṃ śrāddhamapātrakam |
hatamaśrotriye dānamanācāraṃ hataṃ kulam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य कृत्यं न विघ्नन्ति शीतमुष्णं भयं रतिः ।
समृद्धिरसमृद्धिर्वा स वै पण्डित उच्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only he whose work can not be hindered by cold, heat, fear, lust, affluence or poverty is called a wise man.
quotes.transliteration:
yasya kṛtyaṃ na vighnanti śītamuṣṇaṃ bhayaṃ ratiḥ |
samṛddhirasamṛddhirvā sa vai paṇḍita ucyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नान्नमद्यादेकवासाः न नग्नः स्नानमाचरेत् |
न मूत्रं पथि कुर्वीत न भस्मनि न गोव्रजे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must cover both his upper and lower body while eating. One must not take bath entirely naked. One must not urinate by the side of a path, or over ashes, or in a cattle shed. It is obvious why one must not urinate by the side of the path. Ashes and Cow-dung (cow-urine) are known to have other good uses. It should not be contaminated by human urine.
quotes.transliteration:
nānnamadyādekavāsāḥ na nagnaḥ snānamācaret |
na mūtraṃ pathi kurvīta na bhasmani na govraje ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विपदो नैव विपदः सम्पदो नैव सम्पदः ।
विपद्विस्मरणं विष्णोः सम्पन्नारायणस्मृतिः ॥
विपदो नैव विपदः
quotes.englishTranslation: Miseries are not real miseries. Wealth is not real wealth. Forgetting Vishnu is the real misery. Remembering him always is the real wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
vipado naiva vipadaḥ sampado naiva sampadaḥ |
vipadvismaraṇaṃ viṣṇoḥ sampannārāyaṇasmṛtiḥ ||
vipado naiva vipadaḥ

quotes.sanskritText:
दौर्भाग्यमिन्द्रियाणां कृष्णे विषये हि शाश्वतिके ।
क्षणिकेषु पापकरणेष्वपि सज्जन्ते यदन्यविषयेषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very unfortunate that the senses choose to indulge in all things that are temporary and which could lead a man to Sin. They should have indulged themselves in everything to do with Lord Krishna - see him, listen to him, chant his name and so on. This is the only kind of indulgence that is free from sin.
quotes.transliteration:
daurbhāgyamindriyāṇāṃ kṛṣṇe viṣaye hi śāśvatike |
kṣaṇikeṣu pāpakaraṇeṣvapi sajjante yadanyaviṣayeṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शास्त्रज्ञः कपटानुसारकुशलो वक्ता न च क्रोधनः
तुल्यो मित्रपरस्वकेषु चरितं दृष्ट्वैव दत्तोत्तरः |
क्लिबान् पालयिता शठान् व्यथयिता धर्म्यो न लोभान्वितः
द्विर्भावे परतत्त्वबद्धहृदयो राज्ञश्च कोपापहः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A judge has to be well educated. He is skilled in recognizing deceit. He speaks pleasantly. He speaks with friends and foe alike with respect and humility. He can see watch your actions and tell what you are up to. He will protect the weak. He will punish the culprits. He is not easily influenced by greed. When in doubt he will always do the right thing. His actions will relieve the king of any anger.
quotes.transliteration:
śāstrajñaḥ kapaṭānusārakuśalo vaktā na ca krodhanaḥ
tulyo mitraparasvakeṣu caritaṃ dṛṣṭvaiva dattottaraḥ |
klibān pālayitā śaṭhān vyathayitā dharmyo na lobhānvitaḥ
dvirbhāve paratattvabaddhahṛdayo rājñaśca kopāpahaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं किं न जीर्यति मुकुंदपदारविंद-
द्वंद्वाभिवंदनकृतामिह देहभाजाम् ।
अत्यद्भुतौषधविशेषनिषेवकाणा-
मापादयेत किमनर्थमपथ्यसेवा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is that sin that can does not wither way when one hurls himself (surrenders) at the lotus feet of Mukunda (Lord Vishnu) with palms closed and body prostrated? When one is given a potion of best medicine, then bad food can not harm him.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ kiṃ na jīryati mukuṃdapadāraviṃda-
dvaṃdvābhivaṃdanakṛtāmiha dehabhājām |
atyadbhutauṣadhaviśeṣaniṣevakāṇā-
māpādayeta kimanarthamapathyasevā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दृष्टः सप्रेमदेव्या, किमिदमति, भयात्संभ्रमाच्चासुरीभिः
शान्तान्तस्तत्त्वसारैः सकरुणमृषिभिर्विष्णुना सस्मितेन ।
आकृष्यास्त्रं सगर्वैरुपशमितवधूसंभ्रमैर्दैत्यवीरैः
सानन्दं देवताभिर्मयपुरदहने धूर्जटिः पातु युष्मान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When Lord Shiva got ready to burn the cities built by the demon Tripurasura, Goddess Parvati is looking at him with love. The demons are scared and watching him with fear. The saints who have the cosmic realization are looking at him with compassion. Lord Vishnu is watching him with a smile. The Rakshasas are consoling their wives and drawing weapons. The Gods are all happy watching him. Let that Lord Shiva save us all.
quotes.transliteration:
dṛṣṭaḥ sapremadevyā, kimidamati, bhayātsaṃbhramāccāsurībhiḥ
śāntāntastattvasāraiḥ sakaruṇamṛṣibhirviṣṇunā sasmitena |
ākṛṣyāstraṃ sagarvairupaśamitavadhūsaṃbhramairdaityavīraiḥ
sānandaṃ devatābhirmayapuradahane dhūrjaṭiḥ pātu yuṣmān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अशक्तस्तु भवेत्साधुः ब्रह्मचारी तु निर्धनः |
रोगार्तो दैवभक्तः स्यात् वृद्धा नारी पतिव्रता ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He who is weak will never hurt others. A penniless fellow will always be celibate. A person who is sick will seek God. An old woman is always chaste. All these people are virtuous not because they are good by nature - but only because they can not be otherwise.
quotes.transliteration:
aśaktastu bhavetsādhuḥ brahmacārī tu nirdhanaḥ |
rogārto daivabhaktaḥ syāt vṛddhā nārī pativratā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यौवनं धनसंपत्तिः प्रभुत्वमविवेकिता |
एकैकमप्यनर्थाय किमु यत्र चतुष्टयं ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Youth, wealth, power, stupidity - any one of them is a recipe for calamity. What can I say when all of them are present in one place? The poet is commenting on what could happen to a stupid prince.
quotes.transliteration:
yauvanaṃ dhanasaṃpattiḥ prabhutvamavivekitā |
ekaikamapyanarthāya kimu yatra catuṣṭayaṃ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जातिवुद्यावयःशक्तिरारोग्यं बहुपक्षता ।
अर्थित्वं वित्तसंपत्तिरष्टावेते वरे गुणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are the eight characteristics one must look for in a bridegroom - he must belong to a virtuous family, well educated, young (youthful), strength, healthy, liked by many (not hated by majority), willingness to start a family and wealth (at least a drive to earn money)
quotes.transliteration:
jātivudyāvayaḥśaktirārogyaṃ bahupakṣatā |
arthitvaṃ vittasaṃpattiraṣṭāvete vare guṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मूलच्छेदं रिपोः कुर्यादथवा न प्रकोपयेत् |
अन्यथासौ विनाशाय पादस्पृष्ट इवोरगः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must destroy the enemy by his roots. If not able to do so, he must not provoke the enemy. Otherwise it will be like stamping a snake and getting bitten in return.
quotes.transliteration:
mūlacchedaṃ ripoḥ kuryādathavā na prakopayet |
anyathāsau vināśāya pādaspṛṣṭa ivoragaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा यमः प्रियद्वेषौ प्राप्ते काले नियच्छति ।
तथा राज्ञा नियंतव्याः प्रजास्तद्धि यमव्रतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must punish the guilty even if they are his own relatives. Much similar to the way how Yama (Death) takes away a person as soon as his time is over. This penance of impartiality expected from a King is known as Yamavrata (penance of Yama).
quotes.transliteration:
yathā yamaḥ priyadveṣau prāpte kāle niyacchati |
tathā rājñā niyaṃtavyāḥ prajāstaddhi yamavratam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संक्षेपात्कथ्यते धर्मो जनाः किं विस्तरेण वः ।
परोपकारः पुण्याय पापाय परपीडनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I will tell you what constitutes Dharma concisely. There is no use giving long discourses on this aspect. Helping others will lead to good karma. Troubling others will lead to bad karma.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃkṣepātkathyate dharmo janāḥ kiṃ vistareṇa vaḥ |
paropakāraḥ puṇyāya pāpāya parapīḍanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनित्यो विजयो यस्मात् दृश्यते युध्यमानयोः ।
पराजयश्च संग्रामे तस्माद्युद्धं विवर्जयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Victory in a battle is a matter of chance. You may win or lose. So, one must not wage wars. Sooner or later, you will lose one of them.
quotes.transliteration:
anityo vijayo yasmāt dṛśyate yudhyamānayoḥ |
parājayaśca saṃgrāme tasmādyuddhaṃ vivarjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अधीत्य चतुरो वेदान् व्याकृत्याष्टादश स्मृतीः ।
अहो श्रमस्य वैफल्यं आत्मापि कलितो न चीत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All effort of understanding the four vedas and studying eighteen puranas are all in vain if one does not understand the self (Atma).
quotes.transliteration:
adhītya caturo vedān vyākṛtyāṣṭādaśa smṛtīḥ |
aho śramasya vaiphalyaṃ ātmāpi kalito na cīt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संप्राप्ताय त्वतिथये प्रदद्यादासनोदके ।
अन्नं चैव यथाशक्ति सत्कृत्य विधिपूर्वकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a guest comes to your home, you must comfort him by asking him to take a seat and offer water to quench his thirst. This is a must. Later, you can also offer him food and other pleasantries befitting on your capacity.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃprāptāya tvatithaye pradadyādāsanodake |
annaṃ caiva yathāśakti satkṛtya vidhipūrvakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तस्माद्धर्मं सहायार्थं नित्यं संचिनुयाच्छनैः ।
धर्मेण हि सहायेन तमस्तरति दुस्तरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must keep accumulating good karma every day so that it may come in handy some day. So accumulated good will help you overcome the toughest times.
quotes.transliteration:
tasmāddharmaṃ sahāyārthaṃ nityaṃ saṃcinuyācchanaiḥ |
dharmeṇa hi sahāyena tamastarati dustaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाधार्मिके वसेद्ग्रामे न व्याधिबहुले भृशं |
नैकः प्रपद्येताध्वानं न चिरं पर्वते वसेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not live in a village where people do not follow dharma. One must not live in a place where disease is wide spread. One must not undertake a long journey alone. One must not live for long on a mountain. Living on mountain can take a toll on one's health
quotes.transliteration:
nādhārmike vasedgrāme na vyādhibahule bhṛśaṃ |
naikaḥ prapadyetādhvānaṃ na ciraṃ parvate vaset ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न बान्धवा न सुहृदो न धनानि महान्त्यपि ।
अलं धारयितुं शक्ताः तमस्युग्रे निमज्जतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one is really deep trouble, nobody can save him - not relatives, not well wishers, not even loads of money. This is apparent when one is stung by a scorpion.
quotes.transliteration:
na bāndhavā na suhṛdo na dhanāni mahāntyapi |
alaṃ dhārayituṃ śaktāḥ tamasyugre nimajjataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा यथा हि पुरुषः शास्त्रं समधिगच्छति ।
तथा तथा विजानाति विज्ञानां चास्य रोचते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: As and when a person reads (and understands) scriptures, his knowledge expands; so does his quest for more.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā yathā hi puruṣaḥ śāstraṃ samadhigacchati |
tathā tathā vijānāti vijñānāṃ cāsya rocate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वंसहा ये ऋजवः प्रतिज्ञातार्थपालकाः ।
परोपकारिणः सेव्याः निर्धना अपि ते जनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a poor man is venerable if he is very tolerant, innocent, honest and helpful to others.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvaṃsahā ye ṛjavaḥ pratijñātārthapālakāḥ |
paropakāriṇaḥ sevyāḥ nirdhanā api te janāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जानाति मन्दरगिरिर्जलधेर्महत्वं
लङ्केश्वरोऽपि गुरुतां रजताचलस्य ।
विद्वज्जनस्य वचसो महिमानमुच्चै
र्विद्याविवेकचतुरा विबुधा (विदन्ति) ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The mandara mountain alone knows the power of the sea. So does only Ravana knows the weight of Kailasa. Similarly, when the learned speak, only a person with good education and intelligence can comprehend them entirely.
quotes.transliteration:
jānāti mandaragirirjaladhermahatvaṃ
laṅkeśvaroऽpi gurutāṃ rajatācalasya |
vidvajjanasya vacaso mahimānamuccai
rvidyāvivekacaturā vibudhā (vidanti) ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दानाय लक्ष्मिस्सुकृताय विद्या
चिन्ता परब्रह्मविनिश्चयाय ।
परोपकाराय वचांसि यस्य
वन्द्यस्त्रिलोकी तिलकस्य एव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He - whose wealth is for charity whose education is for doing good deeds whose thoughts are always about GOD. He is respected like the best in all three worlds.
quotes.transliteration:
dānāya lakṣmissukṛtāya vidyā
cintā parabrahmaviniścayāya |
paropakārāya vacāṃsi yasya
vandyastrilokī tilakasya eva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रारब्धव्ये निरुद्योगः जागर्तव्ये प्रसुप्तकः ।
विश्वस्तव्ये भयस्थाने हा नरः को न हन्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Man does not do what he is meant to do. He sleeps when he should be alert. He is scared of things which he must believe (and vice-versa). This being the story, is there anything that can stop him from being destroyed (killed).
quotes.transliteration:
prārabdhavye nirudyogaḥ jāgartavye prasuptakaḥ |
viśvastavye bhayasthāne hā naraḥ ko na hanyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वनानि दहतो वह्ने सखा भवति मारुतः ।
स एव दीप नाशय कृशे कस्यास्ति सहृदम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fire that burns down the forest finds a friend in the wind. The same wind kills a small lamp. There is no body who supports the weak.
quotes.transliteration:
vanāni dahato vahne sakhā bhavati mārutaḥ |
sa eva dīpa nāśaya kṛśe kasyāsti sahṛdam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नास्तिक्यं वेदनिंदां च देवतानां च कुत्सनम् |
द्वेषं दंभं च मानं च क्रोधं तैक्ष्ण्यं च वर्जयेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must stay away from atheism, slandering the Vedas, speaking ill of the Gods, enmity with any one, pride, displaying self-importance, anger and caustic nature.
quotes.transliteration:
nāstikyaṃ vedaniṃdāṃ ca devatānāṃ ca kutsanam |
dveṣaṃ daṃbhaṃ ca mānaṃ ca krodhaṃ taikṣṇyaṃ ca varjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लब्ध्वेह मानुषीं योनीं ज्ञानविज्ञानसंभवाम् ।
आत्मानं यो न बुद्ध्येत न क्वचिच्छममाप्नुयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: After getting a human birth and ability to think - generalize and dissect thoughts, if one can not know self, then he can not find happiness (peace) anywhere.
quotes.transliteration:
labdhveha mānuṣīṃ yonīṃ jñānavijñānasaṃbhavām |
ātmānaṃ yo na buddhyeta na kvacicchamamāpnuyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विवेकः सह संयत्या विनयो विद्यया सह ।
प्रभुत्वं प्रश्रयोपेतं चिह्नमेतन्महात्मनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Intelligence must be accompanied by control; Education by humility; power by simplicity - this is how you recognise a great man.
quotes.transliteration:
vivekaḥ saha saṃyatyā vinayo vidyayā saha |
prabhutvaṃ praśrayopetaṃ cihnametanmahātmanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वाशुचि निधानस्य कृतघ्नस्य विनाशिनः ।
शरीरकस्यापि कृते मूढाः पापानि कुर्वते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People do all sorts of dirty business, be disrespectful of those who help him, chase impermanent wealth - all these sins are committed by fools only for the sake of body.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvāśuci nidhānasya kṛtaghnasya vināśinaḥ |
śarīrakasyāpi kṛte mūḍhāḥ pāpāni kurvate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नास्ति वेदात् परं शास्रं नास्ति मातुः परो गुरुः |
नास्ति दानात् परं मित्रं इहलोके परत्र च ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no scripture better than the Vedas. One who studies them need not study anything else. There is no better teacher than one's own mother. There is no better friend than (the fruits of) charity. All these hold true in this world as well as in the other world (after life).
quotes.transliteration:
nāsti vedāt paraṃ śāsraṃ nāsti mātuḥ paro guruḥ |
nāsti dānāt paraṃ mitraṃ ihaloke paratra ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्या प्रवसतो मित्रं भार्या मित्रं गृहे सतः ।
आतुरस्य भिषक् मित्रं दानं मित्रं मरिष्यतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Education is the only savior of tourist. Wife and friends are the only savior of person who stays at home. A doctor is the only savior of the sick. Charity is the only savior of a dying man.
quotes.transliteration:
vidyā pravasato mitraṃ bhāryā mitraṃ gṛhe sataḥ |
āturasya bhiṣak mitraṃ dānaṃ mitraṃ mariṣyataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तपः शौचं दया सत्यं इति पादाः कृते कृताः ।
अधर्मांशैस्त्रयो भग्नाः स्मयसंगमदैस्तव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Penance, cleanliness, compassion and truth were the feet on which the Kruta Yuga world stood. By the influence of adharma (sin) three of them are destroyed in present time.
quotes.transliteration:
tapaḥ śaucaṃ dayā satyaṃ iti pādāḥ kṛte kṛtāḥ |
adharmāṃśaistrayo bhagnāḥ smayasaṃgamadaistava ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनेकदोषोदुष्टोऽपि कायः कस्य न वल्लभः ।
कुर्वन्नपि व्यलीकानि यः प्रियः प्रिय एव सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if it has many faults and does bad things, there is no one who does not love his own body. It even puts us into trouble many times. Still, we continue to love it.
quotes.transliteration:
anekadoṣoduṣṭoऽpi kāyaḥ kasya na vallabhaḥ |
kurvannapi vyalīkāni yaḥ priyaḥ priya eva saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वेषामप्यघवतामिदमेव सुनिष्कृतम् ।
नामव्याहरणं विष्णोर्यतस्तद्विषया मतिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Of all the ways of making peace, a sinner must resort to chanting Vishnu's name as much as possible. It is then that his mind gets clarified since he would be thinking about only Him.
quotes.transliteration:
sarveṣāmapyaghavatāmidameva suniṣkṛtam |
nāmavyāharaṇaṃ viṣṇoryatastadviṣayā matiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परोपदेशे पाण्डित्यं सर्वेषां सुकरं नृणाम् ।
धर्मे स्वीयमनुष्ठानं कस्यचित्तु महात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very easy to advise others what is the right thing to do. But there are very few noble men who put into action what they preach. It is very difficult to live by the code of Dharma all life.
quotes.transliteration:
paropadeśe pāṇḍityaṃ sarveṣāṃ sukaraṃ nṛṇām |
dharme svīyamanuṣṭhānaṃ kasyacittu mahātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परितप्यत एव नोत्तमः परितप्तोऽप्यपरः सुसंवृतिः |
परवृद्धिभिराहितव्यथः स्फुटनिर्भिन्नदुराशयोधमः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A noble man will never feel envious about other people's success. A common man will feel jealous, but may not show it. He will also not let it affect him. But a rogue can not stop thinking about it,. He will attract a lot of grief due to other people's success and can not even hide it
quotes.transliteration:
paritapyata eva nottamaḥ paritaptoऽpyaparaḥ susaṃvṛtiḥ |
paravṛddhibhirāhitavyathaḥ sphuṭanirbhinnadurāśayodhamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्वस्त्वया सुखसंवित्तिः स्मरणीयाधुनातनी |
इति स्वप्नोपमान् मत्वा कामान्मागास्तदंगताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: All the sensory pleasures you enjoy today will only be recalled as memories tomorrow. Tomorrow they are nothing more real than dream. Hence sensory pleasures must be treated like all other dreams - unreal.
quotes.transliteration:
śvastvayā sukhasaṃvittiḥ smaraṇīyādhunātanī |
iti svapnopamān matvā kāmānmāgāstadaṃgatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाभिनंदेत मरणं नाभिनंदेत जीवितं ।
कालमेव प्रतीक्षेत निदेशं भृतको यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not highlight death or birth. As a servant to time, one must simply obey orders.
quotes.transliteration:
nābhinaṃdeta maraṇaṃ nābhinaṃdeta jīvitaṃ |
kālameva pratīkṣeta nideśaṃ bhṛtako yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुत्रो भृत्यः सुहृन्मित्रं भार्या धर्मश्च सत्यता |
तस्यैतान्यपयास्यन्ति क्रोधशीलस्य निश्चितम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: All of these will leave a person who under the grasp of anger at all times - son, servant, well-wishers, friends, wife, dharma and truth. None of these can peacefully co-exist with anger.
quotes.transliteration:
putro bhṛtyaḥ suhṛnmitraṃ bhāryā dharmaśca satyatā |
tasyaitānyapayāsyanti krodhaśīlasya niścitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न विद्यया केवलया तपसा वाऽपि पात्रता ।
यत्र वृत्तमिमे चोभे तद्दि पात्रं प्रकीर्तितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One does not become eligible (for anything) just being educated or having done some penance. One must have good character to augment the other two.
quotes.transliteration:
na vidyayā kevalayā tapasā vāऽpi pātratā |
yatra vṛttamime cobhe taddi pātraṃ prakīrtitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुलीनः शीलसंपन्नो वाग्मी दक्षः प्रियंवदः |
यथोक्तवादी स्मृतिमान् दूतः स्यात्सप्तभिर्गुणैः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A messenger (mediator) needs to have the following seven qualities - be born in a virtuous family (and thus have like qualities), have righteous character, be a good orator, efficient, soft spoken, straight forward, have good memory. All these were found only in Krishna and hence was sent in to mediate between the Pandavas and the Kauravas.
quotes.transliteration:
kulīnaḥ śīlasaṃpanno vāgmī dakṣaḥ priyaṃvadaḥ |
yathoktavādī smṛtimān dūtaḥ syātsaptabhirguṇaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
योऽहिंसकानि भूतानि हिनस्त्यात्मसुखेच्छया ।
स जीवंश्च मृतश्चैव न क्वचित्सुखमेधते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who kills innocent animals like a deer for fun (or his happiness), will not be happy when he is alive or dead.
quotes.transliteration:
yoऽhiṃsakāni bhūtāni hinastyātmasukhecchayā |
sa jīvaṃśca mṛtaścaiva na kvacitsukhamedhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तथा रत्नमासाद्य सुजनः परितुष्यति ।
यथा तत् तद्गताकांक्षे पात्रे दत्वा प्रहृष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A virtuous person will be happy when he gets something valuable. But he will be more happy when he finds a right person who needs it and will be glad to part with it.
quotes.transliteration:
na tathā ratnamāsādya sujanaḥ parituṣyati |
yathā tat tadgatākāṃkṣe pātre datvā prahṛṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उदीरितोऽर्थः पशूनापि गृह्यते
हयश्च नागाश्च वहन्ति देशिताः ।
अनुक्तमप्यूहति पण्डीतो जनः
परेंगितज्ञानफला हि बुद्धयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any animal can understand instructions if they are explained elaborately. Elephants and horses can follow instructions very well. But only a wise man can understand what is not said. Perhaps, understanding other's mind is what is called as 'wisdom'
quotes.transliteration:
udīritoऽrthaḥ paśūnāpi gṛhyate
hayaśca nāgāśca vahanti deśitāḥ |
anuktamapyūhati paṇḍīto janaḥ
pareṃgitajñānaphalā hi buddhayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनोरथानां न समाप्तिरस्ति
वर्षायुतेनापि तथाब्दलक्षैः |
पूर्णेषु पूर्णेषु मनोरथानां
उत्पत्तयः सन्ति पुनर्नवानाम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no end to the desires. They never get satisfied even after a lakh years of prosperity. As and when some desires get fulfilled, some more are born.
quotes.transliteration:
manorathānāṃ na samāptirasti
varṣāyutenāpi tathābdalakṣaiḥ |
pūrṇeṣu pūrṇeṣu manorathānāṃ
utpattayaḥ santi punarnavānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यच्च कामसुखं लोके यच्च दिव्यं महत् सुखम् ।
तृष्णाक्षयसुखस्यैते नार्हतः षोडशीं कलाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The happiness got by lust or any transcendental experience is not even 16th part of the happiness that comes from lack of desire.
quotes.transliteration:
yacca kāmasukhaṃ loke yacca divyaṃ mahat sukham |
tṛṣṇākṣayasukhasyaite nārhataḥ ṣoḍaśīṃ kalām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दातव्यं भोक्तव्यं धनविषये संचयो न कर्तव्यः ।
पश्येह मधुकरीणां संचितमर्थं हरन्त्यन्ये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth must be handled in only 2 ways - spend it or donate it. One must never accumulate it. Look at what happens to bees that accumulate honey. Someone else is going to loot it.
quotes.transliteration:
dātavyaṃ bhoktavyaṃ dhanaviṣaye saṃcayo na kartavyaḥ |
paśyeha madhukarīṇāṃ saṃcitamarthaṃ harantyanye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्यूतमेतत्पुरा कल्पे दृष्टं वैरकरं महत् ।
तस्मात् द्यूतं न सेवेत हास्यार्थमपि बुद्धिमान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: From history we have learnt that gambling begets enmity of great proportions. Hence a wise man will not indulge in gambling - not even for fun.
quotes.transliteration:
dyūtametatpurā kalpe dṛṣṭaṃ vairakaraṃ mahat |
tasmāt dyūtaṃ na seveta hāsyārthamapi buddhimān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वशरीरशरीरिनावपि श्रुतसंयोगविपर्ययौ यदि ।
विरहः किमिवानुतापयेद्वद बाह्यैर्विषयैर्विपश्चिताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A scholar realizes that the life inside our own body is itself not permanent. Why should one be sad when one parts with material attachments of the outside world?
quotes.transliteration:
svaśarīraśarīrināvapi śrutasaṃyogaviparyayau yadi |
virahaḥ kimivānutāpayedvada bāhyairviṣayairvipaścitām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहो तम इवेदं स्यान्न प्रज्ञायेत किंचन |
राजा चेन्न भवेल्लोके विभजन् साध्वसाधुनी ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Alas! the whole world will be filled with darkness if there is not even one king who is capable of knowing the difference between right and wrong. Without him, others (subjects) will not be able to tell the difference.
quotes.transliteration:
aho tama ivedaṃ syānna prajñāyeta kiṃcana |
rājā cenna bhavelloke vibhajan sādhvasādhunī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शरीरपतनावधि प्रभुनिषेवणापादनात्
अबिंधनधनंजयप्रशमदं धनं दंधनम् ।
ध्नंजयविवर्धनं धनमुदूडहोवर्धनं
सुसाधनमबाधनं सुमनसां समाराधनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All the wealth accumulated to mitigate hunger by working for a king all life long is of no use. The only wealth that is permanent and worthwhile is the memory of Sri Krishna who protected Arjuna, who lifted Goverdhana mountain, who can make all troubles go away, who will satiate the hunger of knowledge of all wise men.
quotes.transliteration:
śarīrapatanāvadhi prabhuniṣevaṇāpādanāt
abiṃdhanadhanaṃjayapraśamadaṃ dhanaṃ daṃdhanam |
dhnaṃjayavivardhanaṃ dhanamudūḍahovardhanaṃ
susādhanamabādhanaṃ sumanasāṃ samārādhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बालादपि ग्रहीतव्यं युक्तमुक्तं मनीषिभिः ।
रवेरविषये किं न प्रदीपस्य प्रकाशनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men listen to even small kids if they are talking appropriately. Even a small lamp can illuminate darkness not touched by Sun.
quotes.transliteration:
bālādapi grahītavyaṃ yuktamuktaṃ manīṣibhiḥ |
raveraviṣaye kiṃ na pradīpasya prakāśanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रज्ञागुप्तशरीरस्य किं करिष्यन्ति संगताः ।
गृहीतच्छत्रहस्तस्य वारिधारा इवारयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Presence of mind is a great asset. With that one can defend himself from all his enemies, just as a person would use an umbrella to shield from pouring rain.
quotes.transliteration:
prajñāguptaśarīrasya kiṃ kariṣyanti saṃgatāḥ |
gṛhītacchatrahastasya vāridhārā ivārayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शोचन्ति चामयो यत्र विनश्यत्याशु तत्कुलम् |
न शोचन्ति तु यत्रैता वर्धते तद्धि सर्वदा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If the women of the house are in distress (due to oppression) then that family will surely be destroyed. If they are not oppressed (and hence happy) then such a family surely prosper.
quotes.transliteration:
śocanti cāmayo yatra vinaśyatyāśu tatkulam |
na śocanti tu yatraitā vardhate taddhi sarvadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विषयाननुकूलयितुं विषयिणि हृदये विधीयतां यत्नः ।
दृशि देयमौषधं को दृश्ये दत्त्वा सुखी भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one intends to make the external stimuli pleasant then he has to look inwards and condition the receptors in his heart. If the mind is clean and conditioned, it will not get anxious every time the eyes are attracted to something. Who can be happy if the medicine given to the eyes is applied to the objects the eye sees?
quotes.transliteration:
viṣayānanukūlayituṃ viṣayiṇi hṛdaye vidhīyatāṃ yatnaḥ |
dṛśi deyamauṣadhaṃ ko dṛśye dattvā sukhī bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोकः स्वयं श्रेयसि नष्टदृष्टि-
र्योऽर्थान् समीहेत निकामकामः |
अन्योन्यवैरः सुखलेशहेतो-
रनन्तदुःखं च न वेद मूढः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He who begs money for his own sake will never prosper. He will get on himself lot of grief that comes from hating others. But stupid as they are, will never realize this (and hence continue to do so).
quotes.transliteration:
lokaḥ svayaṃ śreyasi naṣṭadṛṣṭi-
ryoऽrthān samīheta nikāmakāmaḥ |
anyonyavairaḥ sukhaleśaheto-
ranantaduḥkhaṃ ca na veda mūḍhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निर्धनश्चापि कामार्थी दरिद्रः कलहप्रियः |
मंदशास्त्रो विवादार्थी त्रिविधं मूर्खलक्षणम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: There are three kinds of fools - one who has lot of desires in spite of being penniless, one who gets into fights (quarrels) in spite of being poor (and hence at a disadvantage), one who gets into a debate without having enough knowledge about the subject matter.
quotes.transliteration:
nirdhanaścāpi kāmārthī daridraḥ kalahapriyaḥ |
maṃdaśāstro vivādārthī trividhaṃ mūrkhalakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इन्द्रियाणां विचरतां विषयेष्वपहारिषु ।
संयमे यत्नमातिष्ठेद्विद्वान् यंतेव वाजिनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Our senses are like horses that pull the chariot to different directions. Just like a charioteer, a wise man must keep them under control to be able to proceed forward.
quotes.transliteration:
indriyāṇāṃ vicaratāṃ viṣayeṣvapahāriṣu |
saṃyame yatnamātiṣṭhedvidvān yaṃteva vājinām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रियं ब्रूयादकृपणः शूरः स्यादविकत्थनः ।
दाता नापात्रवर्षी स्यात्प्रगल्भः स्यादनिष्ठुरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A rich person must also be nice. A brave courageous person must never boast. A benevolent person must not help those who do not deserve. A scholar must carefully avoid contraversies so that people do not reject his idea becuase of him.
quotes.transliteration:
priyaṃ brūyādakṛpaṇaḥ śūraḥ syādavikatthanaḥ |
dātā nāpātravarṣī syātpragalbhaḥ syādaniṣṭhuraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दृष्टिपूतं न्यसेत्पादं पस्त्रपूतं जलं पिबेत् ।
सत्यपूतां वदेद्वाचं मनःपूतं समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must set foot only after seeing (cleared after an inspection by the eye). One must drink water only after passing through a cloth. One must utter words that pass through the truth filter. One's actions must pass the conscience test.
quotes.transliteration:
dṛṣṭipūtaṃ nyasetpādaṃ pastrapūtaṃ jalaṃ pibet |
satyapūtāṃ vadedvācaṃ manaḥpūtaṃ samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा खनन् खनित्रेण नरो वार्यधिगच्छति ।
तथा गुरुगतां विद्यां शुश्रूषुरधिगच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you take a pickax and dig earth for long, you get your reward - water. If you do similar hard work at serving at teacher, you get wisdom.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā khanan khanitreṇa naro vāryadhigacchati |
tathā gurugatāṃ vidyāṃ śuśrūṣuradhigacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नापत्यानि न वित्तानि न सौधानि भवन्त्यहो ।
मृत्युना नीयमानस्य पुण्यपापे परं पुरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sons, wealth or buildings will not come in handy when death drags you away from this world. In the after life, only the good-deeds and sins you committed come with you.
quotes.transliteration:
nāpatyāni na vittāni na saudhāni bhavantyaho |
mṛtyunā nīyamānasya puṇyapāpe paraṃ puraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मत्तं प्रमत्तमुन्मत्तं सुप्तं बालं स्त्रियं जडम् ।
प्रपन्नं विरथं भीतं न रिपुं हन्ति धर्मवित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A warrior who knows Dharma will never kill an enemy who is either insane, intoxicated, excited, sleeping, child, lady, incapacitated, surrendered, lost his chariot or scared.
quotes.transliteration:
mattaṃ pramattamunmattaṃ suptaṃ bālaṃ striyaṃ jaḍam |
prapannaṃ virathaṃ bhītaṃ na ripuṃ hanti dharmavit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पिता माता भ्राता प्रियसहचरी सूनुनिवहः
सुहृत् स्वामी माद्यत्करिभटरथाश्वः परिकरः ।
निमज्जंतं जंतुं नरककुहरे रक्षितुमलं
गुरोर्धर्माधर्मप्रकटनपरात् कोऽपि न परः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only a capable teacher can make one see the difference between right and wrong. All the others - father, mother, wife, children, friends, employer, army consisting of elephants, soldiers, chariots, horses and relatives - are useless in this regard since all of them make one prejudiced.
quotes.transliteration:
pitā mātā bhrātā priyasahacarī sūnunivahaḥ
suhṛt svāmī mādyatkaribhaṭarathāśvaḥ parikaraḥ |
nimajjaṃtaṃ jaṃtuṃ narakakuhare rakṣitumalaṃ
gurordharmādharmaprakaṭanaparāt koऽpi na paraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बंधुस्त्रीभृत्यवर्गस्य बुद्धेः सत्वस्य चात्मनः ।
आपन्निकषपाषाणे नरो जानाति सारताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is only during the times of trouble that peoples support to you is tested - like gold on touch stone. This applies to relative, wife, servants and one's own wisdom and courage.
quotes.transliteration:
baṃdhustrībhṛtyavargasya buddheḥ satvasya cātmanaḥ |
āpannikaṣapāṣāṇe naro jānāti sāratām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पश्यस्यद्रौ ज्वलमग्निं न पुनः पादयोरधः |
यत्परं शिक्षयस्येवं न स्वं शिक्षयसि स्वयम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: You can see the fire on top of a far off mountain, but can not see the fire right under your feet. You are eager in disciplining others. But you are not self disciplined. Such is the human nature - we only see faults in others.
quotes.transliteration:
paśyasyadrau jvalamagniṃ na punaḥ pādayoradhaḥ |
yatparaṃ śikṣayasyevaṃ na svaṃ śikṣayasi svayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ताडितस्य हि योधस्य श्लाघनीय कर्मणा ।
अकालांतरिता पूजा नाशयत्येव वेदनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a soldier comes home after being wounded in battle, all his pain will go away if you decorate him with praises (and medals) without delay.
quotes.transliteration:
tāḍitasya hi yodhasya ślāghanīya karmaṇā |
akālāṃtaritā pūjā nāśayatyeva vedanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकैश्वर्ये स्थितोऽपि प्रणतबहुफले यः स्वयं कृत्तिवासाः
कान्तासंमिश्रदेहोऽप्यविषयमनसां यःपरस्ताद्यतीनां ।
अष्टाभिर्यस्य कृत्स्नं जगदपि तनुभिर्भिभ्रतो नाभिमानः
सन्मार्गलोकनाय व्यपनयतु स वस्तामसीं वृत्तिमीशः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let Lord Shiva lead our mind towards the right path of salvation by destroying the darkness with in. He is capable of bestowing any amount of wealth on any one who comes begging. But he himself dresses in animal hide. He is sharing half his body with his wife. Yet he is not interested in worldly pleasures; thereby he is the best among all saints (mendicants). He is protecting the entire world with eight of his forces. Yet he is not proud of the same.
quotes.transliteration:
ekaiśvarye sthitoऽpi praṇatabahuphale yaḥ svayaṃ kṛttivāsāḥ
kāntāsaṃmiśradehoऽpyaviṣayamanasāṃ yaḥparastādyatīnāṃ |
aṣṭābhiryasya kṛtsnaṃ jagadapi tanubhirbhibhrato nābhimānaḥ
sanmārgalokanāya vyapanayatu sa vastāmasīṃ vṛttimīśaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाक्सायका वदनान्निष्पतन्ति
यैराहतः शोचति रात्र्यहानि ।
परस्परं मर्मसु ते पतन्ति
तान् पण्डितो नापसृजेत्परेषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: parasparam marmasu te patanti Words are like arrows falling (shot) off our mouth. One who is hit by that arrow will suffer night and day. Usually people hit other where they are most vulnerable. Wise men will not use such tactics on others since they do not wish to hurt others.
quotes.transliteration:
vāksāyakā vadanānniṣpatanti
yairāhataḥ śocati rātryahāni |
parasparaṃ marmasu te patanti
tān paṇḍito nāpasṛjetpareṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ये तीक्ष्णमंत्राः सचिवा भज्यन्ते सह तेन वै |
विषमेषु रथाः शीघ्रा मंदसारथयो यथा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a cart is destroyed along with all its travelers because of going over horrible roads by a unskilled carts man, a king and his kingdom will be destroyed if he listens to a minister who has extreme views.
quotes.transliteration:
ye tīkṣṇamaṃtrāḥ sacivā bhajyante saha tena vai |
viṣameṣu rathāḥ śīghrā maṃdasārathayo yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राणा यथात्मनोऽभीष्टा भूतानामपि ते तथा ।
आत्मौपम्येन भूतेषु दयां कुर्वन्ति साधवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just as one is scared about his own life, so are every body else too. Only virtuous people understand this and treat every one just the same way he treats himself.
quotes.transliteration:
prāṇā yathātmanoऽbhīṣṭā bhūtānāmapi te tathā |
ātmaupamyena bhūteṣu dayāṃ kurvanti sādhavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अत्यादरो भवेद्यत्र कार्यकारणवर्जितः ।
तत्र शंका प्रकर्तव्या परिणामे सुखावहा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you find that you are getting too much respect/praise when there is not much cause (or reason), you must start doubting the motives. This will always lead to happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
atyādaro bhavedyatra kāryakāraṇavarjitaḥ |
tatra śaṃkā prakartavyā pariṇāme sukhāvahā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अप्रकटीकृतशक्तिः शक्तोपि जनतिरस्क्रियां लभते ।
निवसन्नंतर्दारुणि लंघ्यो वह्निर्न तु ज्वलितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One may be strong. If he does not show is strength every now and then, he will be subject to public neglect. One may walk across fire when it hidden inside the wood. But the same can not be done when it is in the form of flames.
quotes.transliteration:
aprakaṭīkṛtaśaktiḥ śaktopi janatiraskriyāṃ labhate |
nivasannaṃtardāruṇi laṃghyo vahnirna tu jvalitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न गोप्रदानं न महीप्रदानं
न चान्नदानं न तथात्मदानम् ।
यथा वदन्तीह महाप्रदानं
सर्वप्रदानेष्वभयप्रदानम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no greater help than giving refuge to some one who is scared. It is greater than giving a cow, land, food or himself.
quotes.transliteration:
na gopradānaṃ na mahīpradānaṃ
na cānnadānaṃ na tathātmadānam |
yathā vadantīha mahāpradānaṃ
sarvapradāneṣvabhayapradānam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यशोऽधिगंतुं सुखलिप्सया वा
मनुष्यसंख्यामतिवर्तितुं वा |
निरुत्साकानामभियोगभाजां
समुत्सुकेवांकमुपैति सिद्धिः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Greatness like fame, happiness and ability to rise above human mediocrity comes naturally (as if coincidental) to people who have trained themselves not to react to joy and sadness and believe in action
quotes.transliteration:
yaśoऽdhigaṃtuṃ sukhalipsayā vā
manuṣyasaṃkhyāmativartituṃ vā |
nirutsākānāmabhiyogabhājāṃ
samutsukevāṃkamupaiti siddhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अरक्षितारो हर्तारश्युल्कव्याजेन पार्थिवाः ।
हारिणो जनवित्तानां संप्राप्ते तु कलौ युगे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the times of Kali (present time), kings will not protect the subjects. They will loot them in the pretext of collecting taxes.
quotes.transliteration:
arakṣitāro hartāraśyulkavyājena pārthivāḥ |
hāriṇo janavittānāṃ saṃprāpte tu kalau yuge ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रायो धनमतामेव धने तृष्णा गरीयसी |
पश्य कोटिद्वयासक्तं लक्षाय प्रवणं धनुः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Normally those who have a lot of wealth are the ones greedy for more. Look at the person who has two crores, he still wants a lakh more. This verse is a play on words. The two ends of a bow is also known as koti (a crore). The target is known as laksha (a lakh).
quotes.transliteration:
prāyo dhanamatāmeva dhane tṛṣṇā garīyasī |
paśya koṭidvayāsaktaṃ lakṣāya pravaṇaṃ dhanuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा काष्ठं च काष्ठं च समेयातां महार्णवे
समेत्य च व्यपेयातां कालमासाद्य कञ्चन ।
एवं भार्याश्च पुत्राश्च ज्ञातयश्च धनानि च
समेत्य व्यवधावन्ति ध्रुवो ह्येषां विना भवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like how sticks floating in the sea come together for some time and later drift away, so is man's associations with his kin - wife, son, relatives and wealth. They may seem like it all came running together for you. But you can be certain that you will have to part with them.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā kāṣṭhaṃ ca kāṣṭhaṃ ca sameyātāṃ mahārṇave
sametya ca vyapeyātāṃ kālamāsādya kañcana |
evaṃ bhāryāśca putrāśca jñātayaśca dhanāni ca
sametya vyavadhāvanti dhruvo hyeṣāṃ vinā bhavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दातृयाचकयोर्भेदः कराभ्यामेव दर्शितः ।
एकस्य गच्छताधस्तादुपर्यन्यस्य तिष्ठता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The difference between the donor and the beggar can be known by looking at the position of their hands. The donor's hand point downwards and that of the beggar extends upwards. This is sometimes used as an idiom to describe a person. (Ex: Charudatta was such a good person that his palms always point downwards)
quotes.transliteration:
dātṛyācakayorbhedaḥ karābhyāmeva darśitaḥ |
ekasya gacchatādhastāduparyanyasya tiṣṭhatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुश्रूषस्य गुरून् कुरु प्रियसखीवृत्तिं सपत्नीजने
भर्तुर्विप्रकृतापि रोषणतया मा स्म प्रतीपंगमः ।
भूयिष्ठं भव दक्षिणा परिजने भाग्येष्वनुत्सेकिनी
यांत्येवं गृहिणीपदं युवतयो वामाः कुलस्याधयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Take good care of the elders. Be friendly with the other wives (of the king). Do not succumb to (an show) anger if husband does something wrong. Treat the servants with dignity. Do not become headstrong when you have lot of wealth. If a young girl behaves thus, she can be called a good wife. If not, she will become a head ache to the entire family.
quotes.transliteration:
śuśrūṣasya gurūn kuru priyasakhīvṛttiṃ sapatnījane
bharturviprakṛtāpi roṣaṇatayā mā sma pratīpaṃgamaḥ |
bhūyiṣṭhaṃ bhava dakṣiṇā parijane bhāgyeṣvanutsekinī
yāṃtyevaṃ gṛhiṇīpadaṃ yuvatayo vāmāḥ kulasyādhayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तथा ह्यघवान् राजन् पूयेत तप आदिभिः ।
यथा कृष्णार्पितप्राणस्तत्पूरुषनिषेवया ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! King, a sinner can not redeem himself by doing penances. But he can redeem himself by serving devotees who have offered their lives to the service of God (and follow their path).
quotes.transliteration:
na tathā hyaghavān rājan pūyeta tapa ādibhiḥ |
yathā kṛṣṇārpitaprāṇastatpūruṣaniṣevayā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य भक्तिर्भगवति हरौ निःश्रेयसेश्वरे ।
विक्रीडतोऽमृतांभोधौ किं क्षुद्रैः खतकोदकैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a person is has complete devotion in God, then he is always happy as though he is floating in an ocean of elixir. What is the use of inferior small wells for him?
quotes.transliteration:
yasya bhaktirbhagavati harau niḥśreyaseśvare |
vikrīḍatoऽmṛtāṃbhodhau kiṃ kṣudraiḥ khatakodakaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत् पृथिव्यां व्रीहियवं हिरण्यं पशवः स्त्रियः |
नालमेकस्य तत्सर्वमिति मत्वा शमं व्रजेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: All the food grains, gold, animals (husbandry/poultry/cattle) and the women in the world, if given to one person will also never satisfy him. Hence, one must stop efforts towards satisfying greed and concentrate on eliminating it.
quotes.transliteration:
yat pṛthivyāṃ vrīhiyavaṃ hiraṇyaṃ paśavaḥ striyaḥ |
nālamekasya tatsarvamiti matvā śamaṃ vrajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अयं निजः परो वेति गणना लघुचेतसाम् ।
उदारचरितानां तु वसुधैव कुटुंबकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This person is one of us, that one is not... So think only thesmall minded people. For the liberals, the world itself is one big family.
quotes.transliteration:
ayaṃ nijaḥ paro veti gaṇanā laghucetasām |
udāracaritānāṃ tu vasudhaiva kuṭuṃbakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्खलितः स्खलितो वध्य इति चेन्निश्चितं भवेत् |
द्वित्रा यद्यवशिष्येरन् बहुदोषा हि मानवाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If you dictate that every person who makes a mistake be killed, then there will be at most two or three people left in this world. Here, almost all people have committed one or the other mistake in life.
quotes.transliteration:
skhalitaḥ skhalito vadhya iti cenniścitaṃ bhavet |
dvitrā yadyavaśiṣyeran bahudoṣā hi mānavāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परित्यजेदर्थकामौ यौ स्यातां धर्मवर्जितौ |
धर्मं चाप्यशुभोदर्कं लोकविक्रुष्टमेव च ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must avoid all means of wealth and pleasures that are against the moral code of life - Dharma. Even Dharma (of older times) has to be given up if it now becomes irrelevant, against the collective morality of all people or is found to yield bad results in future.
quotes.transliteration:
parityajedarthakāmau yau syātāṃ dharmavarjitau |
dharmaṃ cāpyaśubhodarkaṃ lokavikruṣṭameva ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्रुद्धः पापं न कुर्यात् कः क्रुद्धो हन्याद्गुरूनपि ।
क्रुद्धः पुरुषया वाचा नरः साधूनधिक्षिपेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who does not commit sins when he is angry? People are known to kill even their teachers even when they are angry. An angry person is even capable of hurting a noble man which could land him in trouble. So, one must not give himself into anger.
quotes.transliteration:
kruddhaḥ pāpaṃ na kuryāt kaḥ kruddho hanyādgurūnapi |
kruddhaḥ puruṣayā vācā naraḥ sādhūnadhikṣipet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न तथ्यवचनं सत्यं नातथ्यवचनं मृषा ।
यद्भूतहितमत्यन्तं तत्सत्यमितरन्मृषा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just stating a fact as is not the same as telling truth. Not choosing to tell a fact is not the same as telling a lie. That which brings real (permanent) happiness to beings is truth. What does not is false.
quotes.transliteration:
na tathyavacanaṃ satyaṃ nātathyavacanaṃ mṛṣā |
yadbhūtahitamatyantaṃ tatsatyamitaranmṛṣā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षणशः कणश्चैव विद्याम् अर्थ च साध्येत् ।
क्षणे नष्टे कुतो विद्या कणे नष्टे कुतो धनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must give a lot of importance to every second (with respect to time) and every grain of cereals. If you lose seconds how will one get educated. If you lose grains, how will you gain wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣaṇaśaḥ kaṇaścaiva vidyām artha ca sādhyet |
kṣaṇe naṣṭe kuto vidyā kaṇe naṣṭe kuto dhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ये कार्यिकेभ्योऽर्थमेव गृह्णीयुः पापचेतसः |
तेषां सर्वस्वमादाय राजा कुर्यात् प्रवासनम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must identify those wicked corrupt officials who take bribe from people who come for help. He must confiscate all their wealth and chase them out of the country.
quotes.transliteration:
ye kāryikebhyoऽrthameva gṛhṇīyuḥ pāpacetasaḥ |
teṣāṃ sarvasvamādāya rājā kuryāt pravāsanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकामस्य क्रिया काचिद्दृष्यते नेह कर्हिचित् ।
यद्यद्धि कुरुते किंचित् तत्तत् कामस्य चेष्टितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What can be achieved someone lacking desire? There is nothing that seems to be possible. Whatever has been done seems to have its roots in desire.
quotes.transliteration:
akāmasya kriyā kāciddṛṣyate neha karhicit |
yadyaddhi kurute kiṃcit tattat kāmasya ceṣṭitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा तरोर्मूलनिषेचनेन
तृप्यन्ति तत्स्कन्धभुजोपशाखाः ।
प्राणोपहाराच्च यथेन्द्रियाणां
तथैव सर्वार्हणमच्युतेज्या ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you pour water to the root of a tree, the branches and the leaves feel happy. If the body is fed with life energy, the senses are happy. Just like that, if we worship Lord Vishnu, all other Gods also feel happy.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā tarormūlaniṣecanena
tṛpyanti tatskandhabhujopaśākhāḥ |
prāṇopahārācca yathendriyāṇāṃ
tathaiva sarvārhaṇamacyutejyā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्नेनानुमितोऽप्यर्थः कुशलैरनुमातृभिः ।
अभियुक्ततरैरन्यैरन्यथैवोपपाद्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Many logicians may have used lots of points and proved a point. But that does not stop more intelligent people coming along and using more logic points and prove it wrong. The same is true for them also. So, matters of faith are never proved by logic.
quotes.transliteration:
yatnenānumitoऽpyarthaḥ kuśalairanumātṛbhiḥ |
abhiyuktatarairanyairanyathaivopapādyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनृतं चाटुवादश्च धनयोगो महानयं ।
सत्यं वैदुष्यमित्येष योगो दारिद्र्यकारकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the times of kali (present age) deceit and sycophancy will earn you money. Truth and uprightness will lead you to poverty.
quotes.transliteration:
anṛtaṃ cāṭuvādaśca dhanayogo mahānayaṃ |
satyaṃ vaiduṣyamityeṣa yogo dāridryakārakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देहेंद्रियप्राणमनोधियां यो
जन्माप्ययक्षुद्भयतर्षकृच्छ्रैः ।
संसारधर्मैरविमुह्यमानः
स्मृत्या हरेर्भागवत प्रधानः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is said to have understood Bhagavata only when he can detach himself from love towards his body, senses, life, mind, intellect and other worldly feelings like birth, death, hunger, fear and thirst. In other words, understanding Bhagavata completely will get you closer to the goal of detachment.
quotes.transliteration:
deheṃdriyaprāṇamanodhiyāṃ yo
janmāpyayakṣudbhayatarṣakṛcchraiḥ |
saṃsāradharmairavimuhyamānaḥ
smṛtyā harerbhāgavata pradhānaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न पाणिपादचपलो न नेत्रचपलोऽनृजुः ।
न स्याद्वाक्चपलश्चैव न परद्रोहकर्मधीः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must stay clear of the following sins both in action and thought - picking up useless things (stealing), loafing around aimlessly, ogling, lying, loose talk (slander), hurting others.
quotes.transliteration:
na pāṇipādacapalo na netracapaloऽnṛjuḥ |
na syādvākcapalaścaiva na paradrohakarmadhīḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुरुलाघवमर्थानामारंभे कर्माणां फलम् ।
दोषं वा यो न जानाति स बाल इति होच्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Before starting on an endeavor if one does not analyze the outcome of his actions in terms of profit-loss, good-bad then he can only be branded as foolish (or naive).
quotes.transliteration:
gurulāghavamarthānāmāraṃbhe karmāṇāṃ phalam |
doṣaṃ vā yo na jānāti sa bāla iti hocyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न हि धर्मार्थसिद्ध्यर्थं पानमेवं प्रशस्यते ।
पानादर्थश्च धर्मश्च कामश्च परिहीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Intoxication is not conducive to following Dharma or gaining wealth. If one continues to drink, he will not be able to follow Dharma, accumulate wealth or enjoy lust.
quotes.transliteration:
na hi dharmārthasiddhyarthaṃ pānamevaṃ praśasyate |
pānādarthaśca dharmaśca kāmaśca parihīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यं देवाः समासेन मनुष्यास्त्वनृतं स्मृताः |
इहैव तस्य देवत्वं यस्य सत्ये स्थिता मतिः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In sanskrit etymology, one can break the word "deva" (= Gods) as truth and "mnushya" (=humans) as lies. But if a human being on earth keeps his mind (and actions) truthful all his life, he is to be considered as God on earth.
quotes.transliteration:
satyaṃ devāḥ samāsena manuṣyāstvanṛtaṃ smṛtāḥ |
ihaiva tasya devatvaṃ yasya satye sthitā matiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दयया सर्वभूतेषु संतुष्ट्या येनकेन वा ।
सर्वेन्द्रियोपशान्त्या च तुष्यत्याशु जनार्दनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are three ways to please God - showing indiscriminate compassion towards every body, getting satisfied with what one has by what ever means (having no more wants) or by curbing all senses.
quotes.transliteration:
dayayā sarvabhūteṣu saṃtuṣṭyā yenakena vā |
sarvendriyopaśāntyā ca tuṣyatyāśu janārdanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्मर्तव्यः सततं कृष्णो विस्मर्तव्यो न जातुचित् ।
सर्वे विधिनिषेधाः स्युरेतयोरेव किंकराः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All rituals are corollaries of the these two basic rules - "Always remember Sri Krishna. Never forget him".
quotes.transliteration:
smartavyaḥ satataṃ kṛṣṇo vismartavyo na jātucit |
sarve vidhiniṣedhāḥ syuretayoreva kiṃkarāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राणाघातान्निवृत्तिः परधनहरणे संयमः सत्यवाक्यम्
काले शक्त्या प्रदेयं युवतिजनकथामूकभावः परेषाम् ।
तृष्णास्रोतोविबंधो गुरुषु च विनतिः सर्वभूतानुकंपा
सामान्यं सर्वशास्त्रेष्वनुपहतविधिः श्रेयसामेषमार्गः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following are generally accepted in all scriptures as the way to lead a successful life: stay away from hurting animals, do not steal from others, be honest, keep donating now-and-then, keep mum when people talk about a young girl (do not defame her by heresay), keep a check on your desires, be humble with your teachers, compassion towards all beings.
quotes.transliteration:
prāṇāghātānnivṛttiḥ paradhanaharaṇe saṃyamaḥ satyavākyam
kāle śaktyā pradeyaṃ yuvatijanakathāmūkabhāvaḥ pareṣām |
tṛṣṇāsrotovibaṃdho guruṣu ca vinatiḥ sarvabhūtānukaṃpā
sāmānyaṃ sarvaśāstreṣvanupahatavidhiḥ śreyasāmeṣamārgaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अविवेकघनांधगिज्ञ्मुखे बहुधा संततदुःखवर्षिणि ।
भगवन् भवदुर्दिने पथस्खलितं मामवलोकयाच्युत ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I am in the thick darkness without any knowledge. There is a incessant rain of miseries. In such a day, I have strayed from the righteous ways. Oh! Achyuta, please look after me.
quotes.transliteration:
avivekaghanāṃdhagijñmukhe bahudhā saṃtataduḥkhavarṣiṇi |
bhagavan bhavadurdine pathaskhalitaṃ māmavalokayācyuta ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अप्राज्ञेन च कातरेन च गुणः स्याद्भक्तियुक्तेन कः
प्रज्ञविक्रमशालिनोऽपि हि भवेत् किं भक्तिहीनात्फलम् ।
प्रज्ञाविक्रमभक्तयः समुदिताः येषां गुणा भूतये
ते भृत्या नृपतेः कलत्रमितरे संपत्सु चापत्सु च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If servant is very loyal, but neither intelligent nor brave then he is of o use. If he is both intelligent and brave but lacks loyalty, then again he is of no use. Only those servants who are loyal, courageous and intelligent are the real reasons of a kings prosperity. The others are only playing supporting roles at all times.
quotes.transliteration:
aprājñena ca kātarena ca guṇaḥ syādbhaktiyuktena kaḥ
prajñavikramaśālinoऽpi hi bhavet kiṃ bhaktihīnātphalam |
prajñāvikramabhaktayaḥ samuditāḥ yeṣāṃ guṇā bhūtaye
te bhṛtyā nṛpateḥ kalatramitare saṃpatsu cāpatsu ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनुष्ये कदलीस्तंभे निःसारे सारमार्गणम् |
यः करोति स संमूढो जलबुद्बुदसन्निभे ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not search for a purpose/meaning in human life which is as dry (devoid of strength/life/sap) like a stem of banana plant and as ephemeral as a water bubble.
quotes.transliteration:
manuṣye kadalīstaṃbhe niḥsāre sāramārgaṇam |
yaḥ karoti sa saṃmūḍho jalabudbudasannibhe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तावद्रागादयःस्तेनास्तावत्कारागृहं गृहम् ।
तावन्मोहो अंघ्रिनिगडो यावत्कृष्ण न ते जनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Krishna, when you are not with people thieves like sensory pleasures will steal your life. House will be like a prison. Attachment will be like heel chains. Krishna
quotes.transliteration:
tāvadrāgādayaḥstenāstāvatkārāgṛhaṃ gṛham |
tāvanmoho aṃghrinigaḍo yāvatkṛṣṇa na te janāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पादोऽधर्मस्य कर्तारं पादः साक्षिणमृच्छति ।
पादः सभासदः सर्वान् पादो राजानमृच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a sin is committed in an assembly (king's court), then one quarter of the sin is attributed to the perpetrator. Another quarter to the by-standers who did not appose it. Another quarter to the members of the assembly who may have the powers to stop it but did not. The last quarter is attributed to the king.
quotes.transliteration:
pādoऽdharmasya kartāraṃ pādaḥ sākṣiṇamṛcchati |
pādaḥ sabhāsadaḥ sarvān pādo rājānamṛcchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विदुरेष्यदपायमात्मना
परतः श्रद्दधतेऽथवा बुधाः|
न परोपहितं न च स्वतः
प्रमिमीतेऽनुभवादृतेऽल्पधीः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man can foretell the problems that may come in his way. If he misses it, then he will at least listen when others tell him about the troubles he is going to face. But an idiot will neither see it himself, not listen to people who warn him about it. He comes to know about it only when he will experiences it (and unprepared).
quotes.transliteration:
vidureṣyadapāyamātmanā
parataḥ śraddadhateऽthavā budhāḥ|
na paropahitaṃ na ca svataḥ
pramimīteऽnubhavādṛteऽlpadhīḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुष्केणैकेन वॄक्षेण वनं पुष्पितपादपं |
कुलं चारित्रहीनेन पुरुषेणेव दह्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A single dry tree is enough to cause a forest fire and harm the entire forest. Just like that, one bad person in the family can lead to the destruction of the entire family.
quotes.transliteration:
śuṣkeṇaikena vṝkṣeṇa vanaṃ puṣpitapādapaṃ |
kulaṃ cāritrahīnena puruṣeṇeva dahyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मुकुंद मूर्ध्ना प्रणिपत्य याचे
भवंतमेकांतमियंतमर्थम् ।
अविस्मृतिस्त्वच्चरणारविंदे
भवे भवे मेSस्तु भवत्प्रसादात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Mukunda! I am prostrating before you humbly and asking for just one important wish. Bless me so that I will never forget the lotus feet of yours in all my future births.
quotes.transliteration:
mukuṃda mūrdhnā praṇipatya yāce
bhavaṃtamekāṃtamiyaṃtamartham |
avismṛtistvaccaraṇāraviṃde
bhave bhave meSstu bhavatprasādāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चातकस्त्रिचतुरान् पयःकणान्
याचते जलधरं पिपासया ।
सोऽपि पूरयति विश्वमंभसा
हन्त हन्त महतामुदारता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The thirsty chataka bird begs the clouds to give it a couple of drops of water. The Clouds douse the entire earth in return. Really, the generosity of the virtuous people is worth appreciating.
quotes.transliteration:
cātakastricaturān payaḥkaṇān
yācate jaladharaṃ pipāsayā |
soऽpi pūrayati viśvamaṃbhasā
hanta hanta mahatāmudāratā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
षड्दोषाः पुरुषेणेह हातव्या भूतिमिच्छता |
निद्रा तन्द्रा भयं क्रोधः आलस्यं दीर्घसूत्रता ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Any person who intends to prosper should get rid of these six qualities in him: excessive sleep, easily fatigued, fear, anger, laziness, procrastination.
quotes.transliteration:
ṣaḍdoṣāḥ puruṣeṇeha hātavyā bhūtimicchatā |
nidrā tandrā bhayaṃ krodhaḥ ālasyaṃ dīrghasūtratā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अप्राप्तकालं वचनं बृहस्पतिरपि ब्रुवन् ।
लभते बह्ववज्ञानमपमानं च पुष्कलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one utter wrong words at an inappropriate time, he is bound to attract wrath and humiliation - even when he is Bruhaspati (teacher of the Gods) himself.
quotes.transliteration:
aprāptakālaṃ vacanaṃ bṛhaspatirapi bruvan |
labhate bahvavajñānamapamānaṃ ca puṣkalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कोऽतिभारः समर्थानां किं दूरं व्यवसायिनम् ।
को विदेशः सविद्यानां कः परः प्रियवादिनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no weight that a able guy can not handle. There is no distance too long for a businessman. For a well educated man, no country will seem foreign. A person who talks sweetly will feel every one as close friend.
quotes.transliteration:
koऽtibhāraḥ samarthānāṃ kiṃ dūraṃ vyavasāyinam |
ko videśaḥ savidyānāṃ kaḥ paraḥ priyavādinām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शक्तः परजने दाता स्वजने दुःखजीविनि |
मध्वापातो विषास्वादः स धर्मप्रतिरूपकः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If one's family members and relatives are in trouble and still he helps others in search of fame, then it is not Dharma (moral code of life). It only looks like Dharma. It may seem like honey in the beginning, but will turn into poison. In fact, it is exactly opposite to Dharma.
quotes.transliteration:
śaktaḥ parajane dātā svajane duḥkhajīvini |
madhvāpāto viṣāsvādaḥ sa dharmapratirūpakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नन्दंत्युदित आदित्ये नन्दंत्यस्तमिते रवौ ।
आत्मनो नावबुध्यन्ते मनुष्या जीवितक्ष्ययम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People rejoice at sunrise and sunset. They are not aware of the reduction in their lifetime.
quotes.transliteration:
nandaṃtyudita āditye nandaṃtyastamite ravau |
ātmano nāvabudhyante manuṣyā jīvitakṣyayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदि न प्रणयेद्राजा दण्डं दण्डेष्व तंद्रितः |
शूले मत्स्यानि वापक्ष्यन् दुर्बलान् बलवत्तराः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king is not vigilant enough to punish the guilty then the mighty will destroy the weak as if they are fish to be eaten.
quotes.transliteration:
yadi na praṇayedrājā daṇḍaṃ daṇḍeṣva taṃdritaḥ |
śūle matsyāni vāpakṣyan durbalān balavattarāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पात्रविशेषे न्यस्तं गुणांतरं व्रजति शिल्पमाधातुः |
जलमिव समुद्रशुक्तौ मुक्ताफलतां पयोदस्य ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Under the able guidance of a teacher (who understands your strengths) even a stone can attain greatness. Just like a drop of pure water from the rain into a sea will be transformed into a pearl if it is nutured in an oyster.
quotes.transliteration:
pātraviśeṣe nyastaṃ guṇāṃtaraṃ vrajati śilpamādhātuḥ |
jalamiva samudraśuktau muktāphalatāṃ payodasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पथि पतितमस्थि दृष्ट्वा स्पर्शभयादन्यमार्गतो याति ।
नो पश्यति निजदेहं चास्थि सहस्रावृतं परितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you see a bone along the path, you are worried about touching it and becoming dirty (unholy) and change your path. But you are unaware of thousands of bones (and other such impurities) in his own body.
quotes.transliteration:
pathi patitamasthi dṛṣṭvā sparśabhayādanyamārgato yāti |
no paśyati nijadehaṃ cāsthi sahasrāvṛtaṃ paritaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
साम्ना दानेन भेदेन समस्तैरथवा पृथक् |
विजेतुं प्रयतेतारीन् न युद्धेन कदाचन ||
quotes.englishTranslation: First one must exercise reasonable patience. Then We must try to concede some ground - compromise. Then we must try to penetrate the adversary's thinking and try to change it. All these are the methods are to be employed separately or together to win over an enemy. Waging a war is never a solution.
quotes.transliteration:
sāmnā dānena bhedena samastairathavā pṛthak |
vijetuṃ prayatetārīn na yuddhena kadācana ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गोपहीन यथा गावः विलयं यान्त्यपालिताः ।
एवं नृपतिहीना हि विलयं यान्ति वै प्रजाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Without the shepherd, the cows all get dispersed. Their life is in danger because lone cows are eaten up by predators. Just like that, citizens' lives will be put in danger if there is no king.
quotes.transliteration:
gopahīna yathā gāvaḥ vilayaṃ yāntyapālitāḥ |
evaṃ nṛpatihīnā hi vilayaṃ yānti vai prajāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनेकानि च शास्त्राणि स्वल्पायुर्विघ्नकोतयः ।
तस्मात्सारं विजानीयात् क्षीरं हंस इवांभसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is so many scriptures to study. Life time is so little. On top of that, there are crores of obstacles. Hence the jist of these scriptures have to be studied just like a swan can drink milk and leave out the water.
quotes.transliteration:
anekāni ca śāstrāṇi svalpāyurvighnakotayaḥ |
tasmātsāraṃ vijānīyāt kṣīraṃ haṃsa ivāṃbhasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अदेशकालज्ञमनायतिक्षमं
यदप्रियं लाघवकारि चात्मनः ।
योऽत्राब्रवीत्कारणवर्जितं वचो
न तद्वचः स्याद्विषमेव तद्वचः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one speaks without knowledge of space (where he is), or time, or without fore thought, or discomforting others, or diminishing one's own weight, or without any reason - such speech will be as dangerous as poison.
quotes.transliteration:
adeśakālajñamanāyatikṣamaṃ
yadapriyaṃ lāghavakāri cātmanaḥ |
yoऽtrābravītkāraṇavarjitaṃ vaco
na tadvacaḥ syādviṣameva tadvacaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मार्जालभक्षिते दुःखं यादृशं गृहकुक्कुटे ।
न तादृञ्ममताशून्ये कलविंकेऽथ मूषके ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When the cat eats the hen of the house, we feel very very sad. The same sadness is not there when it kills a sparrow or a mouse. This shows that the source of our sadness is attachment and not the real loss of life.
quotes.transliteration:
mārjālabhakṣite duḥkhaṃ yādṛśaṃ gṛhakukkuṭe |
na tādṛñmamatāśūnye kalaviṃkeऽtha mūṣake ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्या शस्त्रस्य शास्त्रस्य द्वे विद्ये प्रतिपत्तये ।
आद्या हास्याय वृद्धत्वे द्वितीयाद्रियते सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The two kinds of education - scriptures and weapons; both are well respected. But as one gets old, his mastery on weapons becomes a joke and is laughed about. But his mastery on scriptures only becomes sharper and is respected more.
quotes.transliteration:
vidyā śastrasya śāstrasya dve vidye pratipattaye |
ādyā hāsyāya vṛddhatve dvitīyādriyate sadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थानर्थौ विनिश्चित्य व्यवसायं भजेत ह |
गुणतः संग्रहं कुर्याद्दोषतस्तु विसर्जयेत् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Before taking up a project, one must do a thorough analysis of pros and cons. If pros over weigh cons, then it must done. If not, the it must be dropped.
quotes.transliteration:
arthānarthau viniścitya vyavasāyaṃ bhajeta ha |
guṇataḥ saṃgrahaṃ kuryāddoṣatastu visarjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अंधा विद्वज्जनैर्हीना मूका कविभिरुज्झिता |
बधिरा गायनैर्हीना सभा भवति भूभृताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A King's court is blind if it does not have wise men in it. It is dumb if it does not have poets. It is deaf if it does not have singes. A balanced court is always a collections of poets, singers and wise ministers.
quotes.transliteration:
aṃdhā vidvajjanairhīnā mūkā kavibhirujjhitā |
badhirā gāyanairhīnā sabhā bhavati bhūbhṛtām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
योऽन्यथासंतमात्मानं अन्यथा सत्सु भाषते |
स पापकृत्तवो लोके स्तेन आत्मापहारकः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: He whose actions are solely directed towards getting praised by others or giving an impression of nobility is a sinner, a con man (thief) and is destroying himself.
quotes.transliteration:
yoऽnyathāsaṃtamātmānaṃ anyathā satsu bhāṣate |
sa pāpakṛttavo loke stena ātmāpahārakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृषावादं परिहरेत् कुर्यात् प्रियमयाचितः ।
न च कामान्न संरंभान्न द्वेषाद्धर्ममुत्सृजेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not engage in dry arguments. One must always do good to others without any expectation. Sensory pleasures, haste and hatred must not make one leave the path of Dharma.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛṣāvādaṃ pariharet kuryāt priyamayācitaḥ |
na ca kāmānna saṃraṃbhānna dveṣāddharmamutsṛjet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सहजांधदृशः स्वदुर्नये
परदोषेक्षणदिव्यचक्षुषः |
स्वगुणोच्चगिरो मुनिव्रताः
परवर्णग्रहणेष्वसाधवः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Wicked people are blind to their own mistakes. But they have microscopic eyes when it comes to finding faults with others. They shout on top of their voices when they are talking about their qualities. However, they go mute when asked to describe other people's virtues
quotes.transliteration:
sahajāṃdhadṛśaḥ svadurnaye
paradoṣekṣaṇadivyacakṣuṣaḥ |
svaguṇoccagiro munivratāḥ
paravarṇagrahaṇeṣvasādhavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बृहत्सहायः कार्यांतं क्षोदीयानपि गच्छति |
संभूयांभोधिमभ्येति महानद्या नगापगा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: With help of the mighty and powerful, even the weak (and insignificant) can attain greatness. A mountain stream can also join the ocean if it can get some help from a river (by joining it as a tributary).
quotes.transliteration:
bṛhatsahāyaḥ kāryāṃtaṃ kṣodīyānapi gacchati |
saṃbhūyāṃbhodhimabhyeti mahānadyā nagāpagā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उभयोर्नास्ति भोगेच्छा परार्थं धनसंचयः ।
कृपणोदारयोः पश्य तथापि महदंतरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Both the miser and the generous do not accumulate wealth with the idea of enjoying it themselves. They always leave it for others. But what a difference between them.
quotes.transliteration:
ubhayornāsti bhogecchā parārthaṃ dhanasaṃcayaḥ |
kṛpaṇodārayoḥ paśya tathāpi mahadaṃtaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मनाशे धर्मनाशे व्यसने सुमहत्यपि ।
दोषाय नानृतवचो भवती ह न संशयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When faced with death, harm to dharma (morality), grave danger it is acceptable to lie. There is no sin attached to such a lie.
quotes.transliteration:
ātmanāśe dharmanāśe vyasane sumahatyapi |
doṣāya nānṛtavaco bhavatī ha na saṃśayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मो हि यत्नैः पुरुषेण साध्यः
भुजङ्गजिह्वचपला नृपुश्रियः ।
तस्मात्प्रजापालनमात्रबुद्ध्या
हतेषु देहेषु गुणा धरन्ते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must follow dharma even when it is difficult to do so. The wealth and kingdom are as fickle as the tongue of a snake. Hence a wise king will rule fairly and strive to keep the subjects happy. In this way his fame will live even after his body is dead.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmo hi yatnaiḥ puruṣeṇa sādhyaḥ
bhujaṅgajihvacapalā nṛpuśriyaḥ |
tasmātprajāpālanamātrabuddhyā
hateṣu deheṣu guṇā dharante ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जीयंतां दुर्जया देहे रिपवश्चक्षुरादयः ।
जितेषु ननु लोकोऽयं तेषुकृत्स्नस्त्वया जितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must control the enemies with in his own body - eyes and other senses. If one has control over them, then it is equivalent to have won over the whole world.
quotes.transliteration:
jīyaṃtāṃ durjayā dehe ripavaścakṣurādayaḥ |
jiteṣu nanu lokoऽyaṃ teṣukṛtsnastvayā jitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अज्ञानादथवा ज्ञानादुत्तमश्लोकनाम यत् ।
