संकीर्तितमघं पुंसो देहेदेधो यथानलः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Intentionally or not, if one utters the names of the God then his sin will burn down just like wood put in fire.
quotes.transliteration:
ajñānādathavā jñānāduttamaślokanāma yat |
saṃkīrtitamaghaṃ puṃso dehededho yathānalaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मात्रा स्वस्रा दुहित्रा वा नाविविक्तासनो भवेत् ।
बलवानिंद्रियग्रामो विद्वांसमपि कर्षति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not sit alone with mother, sister or daughter. The sense organs are very strong and can derail the thoughts of a wise man. (some Freudian theory??)
quotes.transliteration:
mātrā svasrā duhitrā vā nāviviktāsano bhavet |
balavāniṃdriyagrāmo vidvāṃsamapi karṣati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परस्परामिषतया जगतो भिन्नवर्त्मनः |
दंडाभावे परिध्वंसी मात्स्यो न्यायः प्रवर्तते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: In the absence of punishment people who hate each other (think differently) will destroy each other. When such a social order exists (like in fish - big fish eat small fish) then the weak will suffer.
quotes.transliteration:
parasparāmiṣatayā jagato bhinnavartmanaḥ |
daṃḍābhāve paridhvaṃsī mātsyo nyāyaḥ pravartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहोरात्राणि गच्छन्ति सर्वेषां प्राणिनामिह ।
आयूंषि क्षपयन्त्याशु ग्रीष्मे जलमिवांशवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Every being goes through the experience of day and night every day. Just like the sun rays sucks in water during the Greeshma month, the cycle of day and night erode our longevity.
quotes.transliteration:
ahorātrāṇi gacchanti sarveṣāṃ prāṇināmiha |
āyūṃṣi kṣapayantyāśu grīṣme jalamivāṃśavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दाने तपसि शौर्ये वा यस्य न प्रथितं यशः ।
विद्यायामर्थलाभे वा मातुरुच्चार एव सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must attain fame in any one of the following - charity, penance, bravery, education, wealth. If not, he is nothing but a discharge from his mother's uterus.
quotes.transliteration:
dāne tapasi śaurye vā yasya na prathitaṃ yaśaḥ |
vidyāyāmarthalābhe vā māturuccāra eva saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न जातु कामान्न भयान्न लोभात्
धर्मं जह्याज्जीवितस्यापि हेतोः ।
नित्यो धर्मः सुखदुःखे त्वनित्ये
जीवो नित्यो हेतुरस्य त्वनित्यः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not give up Dharma succumbing to lust, or fear, or greed, or simply to the desire to live. Dharma is eternal. Happiness and Sorrow are temporary and will pass. Life (as in life on this world) is eternal. Each living being (us) and the reason for it to be in this world is temporary.
quotes.transliteration:
na jātu kāmānna bhayānna lobhāt
dharmaṃ jahyājjīvitasyāpi hetoḥ |
nityo dharmaḥ sukhaduḥkhe tvanitye
jīvo nityo heturasya tvanityaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनुगंतुं सतां वर्त्म यदि कृत्स्नं न शक्यते ।
स्वल्पमप्यनुगंतव्यं मार्गस्थो नावसीदति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one can not copy all the good habits of a noble man, he must at least emulate a few. If one is on a right track, then he will not get wasted.
quotes.transliteration:
anugaṃtuṃ satāṃ vartma yadi kṛtsnaṃ na śakyate |
svalpamapyanugaṃtavyaṃ mārgastho nāvasīdati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न ह्यतोऽन्यः शिवः पंथा विशतः संसृताविह ।
वासुदेवे भगवति भक्तियोगो यतो भवेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If there is a path available for people that leads them to devotion towards God, then there is no better path than this.
quotes.transliteration:
na hyatoऽnyaḥ śivaḥ paṃthā viśataḥ saṃsṛtāviha |
vāsudeve bhagavati bhaktiyogo yato bhavet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रजा संरक्षणं धर्मो धार्मिकस्य महीभृतः ।
तस्मादसाधून् धर्माय निघ्नन् दोषैर्न लिप्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Protection of his subjects is the utmost duty of a King. In the process he has to punish/kill the rogues who trouble the subjects. There is no sin attached to such acts.
quotes.transliteration:
prajā saṃrakṣaṇaṃ dharmo dhārmikasya mahībhṛtaḥ |
tasmādasādhūn dharmāya nighnan doṣairna lipyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जन्मेदं वंध्यतां नीतं भवभोगोपलिप्सया ।
काचमूल्येन विक्रीतो हन्त चिंतामणिर्मया ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I have wasted this human life running behind worldly pleasures. It is akin to selling a precious gem for the price of a glass piece.
quotes.transliteration:
janmedaṃ vaṃdhyatāṃ nītaṃ bhavabhogopalipsayā |
kācamūlyena vikrīto hanta ciṃtāmaṇirmayā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वर्गापवर्गयोः पुंसां रसायां भुवि संपादं ।
सर्वसामपि सिद्धीनां मूलं तच्चरणार्चनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Heaven or hell, rebirth or emancipation, wealth in this world - all of these have the same source; your (God's) feet. You are the cause of everything.
quotes.transliteration:
svargāpavargayoḥ puṃsāṃ rasāyāṃ bhuvi saṃpādaṃ |
sarvasāmapi siddhīnāṃ mūlaṃ taccaraṇārcanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जटाभाराजिनैर्युक्ता दांभिका वेषधारिणः ।
भ्रमन्ति ज्ञानिवल्लोके भ्रामयन्ति जनानपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: jataabhaaraajinairyuktaa daambhikaa veshadhaarinah | bhramanti jnaanivalloke bhraamayanti janaanapi || Zottelhaar tragend, in Gazellenhaut steckend irren verkleidete Lügner durchs Land als hätten sie Ahnung, doch wollen sie nur das Volk verwirren.
quotes.transliteration:
jaṭābhārājinairyuktā dāṃbhikā veṣadhāriṇaḥ |
bhramanti jñānivalloke bhrāmayanti janānapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मातृपितृसुतभ्रातृबंधुदारादिसंगमः ।
प्रपायामिव जंतूनां नद्यां काष्ठौघवच्चलः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Mother, father, son, brother, friends, relatives, wife and other acquaintances - none of them are permanent. Our associations with them are as temporary as those between animals at a water hole, or logs of wood floating in the river.
quotes.transliteration:
mātṛpitṛsutabhrātṛbaṃdhudārādisaṃgamaḥ |
prapāyāmiva jaṃtūnāṃ nadyāṃ kāṣṭhaughavaccalaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महतां योऽपराध्येते दूरस्थोऽस्मीति नाश्वसेत् ।
दीर्घौ बुद्धिमतो बाहू ताभ्यां हिंसति हिंसकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not hurt mighty people, run away and live peacefully. The arms of wise men are long (great reach) and they will hurt you back.
quotes.transliteration:
mahatāṃ yoऽparādhyete dūrasthoऽsmīti nāśvaset |
dīrghau buddhimato bāhū tābhyāṃ hiṃsati hiṃsakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चक्रिणो दशमीस्थस्य रोगिणो भारिणः स्त्रियाः ।
स्नातकस्य च राज्ञश्च पंथा देयो वरस्य च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A pedestrian has to get off the road to leave way for the following: a chariot, old people, the sick, people carrying load, women, a person who just finished his religious studies on his way to get married, a king.
quotes.transliteration:
cakriṇo daśamīsthasya rogiṇo bhāriṇaḥ striyāḥ |
snātakasya ca rājñaśca paṃthā deyo varasya ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भार्येयं मधुराकृतिर्मम मम प्रीत्यान्वितोऽयं सुतः
स्वर्णस्यैष महानिधिर्मम ममासौ बंधुरो बांधवः ।
रम्यं हर्म्यमिदं ममेत्थमनया व्यामोहितो मया
मृत्युं पश्यति नैव दैवहतकः क्रुद्धं पुरश्चारिणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This is my beautiful wife. This is my son whom I love so much. This treasure of gold is mine. These are my people, my friends and relatives. This house is mine. A man who thinks like this is under the grasp of illusion and does not see the truth - which is right in front of him as 'death'.
quotes.transliteration:
bhāryeyaṃ madhurākṛtirmama mama prītyānvitoऽyaṃ sutaḥ
svarṇasyaiṣa mahānidhirmama mamāsau baṃdhuro bāṃdhavaḥ |
ramyaṃ harmyamidaṃ mametthamanayā vyāmohito mayā
mṛtyuṃ paśyati naiva daivahatakaḥ kruddhaṃ puraścāriṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्यौतिषं व्यवहारं च प्रायश्चित्तं चिकित्सितं ।
अजानन् यो नरो ब्रूयात् साहसं किमतः परम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no felony greater than pretending to know astrology, be a judge, suggesting ways of redemption and practicing medicine without having enough knowledge in the matter.
quotes.transliteration:
jyautiṣaṃ vyavahāraṃ ca prāyaścittaṃ cikitsitaṃ |
ajānan yo naro brūyāt sāhasaṃ kimataḥ param ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सेवाध्वजोऽजलिमूर्ध्व्नि हृदि दैन्यं मुखे स्तुतिः |
आशाग्रहगृहीतानां कियतीयं विडंबना ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Isn't it funny? A person who is blinded by greed makes servitude his flagship, on his head he keeps an open palm (a sign of beggary). He is weak at heart and always has praise (of others) in his mouth (anticipating some money/help in return)
quotes.transliteration:
sevādhvajoऽjalimūrdhvni hṛdi dainyaṃ mukhe stutiḥ |
āśāgrahagṛhītānāṃ kiyatīyaṃ viḍaṃbanā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतिक्रान्तसुखाः कालाः प्रत्युपस्थितदारुणाः ।
श्वः श्वः पापीयदिवसा पृथिवी गतयौवना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The good days are over. Days filled with misery are ahead. Every following day is more sinful than the previous. The earth is past its youth.
quotes.transliteration:
atikrāntasukhāḥ kālāḥ pratyupasthitadāruṇāḥ |
śvaḥ śvaḥ pāpīyadivasā pṛthivī gatayauvanā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भव्यमभव्यं वा नः प्रलिखतु वेधाः सुदुर्मेधाः ।
सव्यमसव्यं वा नः शरणं चरणं महेशस्य ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the creator write prosperity or misery in my fate. I would not bother.Either way I will bow down to the left or the right foot of the Lord. It does not matter whether it is misery or prosperity when the destination is the Lord himself.
quotes.transliteration:
bhavyamabhavyaṃ vā naḥ pralikhatu vedhāḥ sudurmedhāḥ |
savyamasavyaṃ vā naḥ śaraṇaṃ caraṇaṃ maheśasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अद्भिर्गात्राणि शुध्यन्ति मनः सत्येन शुध्यति ।
विद्यातपोभ्यां भूतात्मा बुद्धिर्ज्ञानेन शुध्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Water cleanses the body parts. Mind is cleansed by truth. Education and penance will cleanse the human soul. Intellect is cleansed by knowledge.
quotes.transliteration:
adbhirgātrāṇi śudhyanti manaḥ satyena śudhyati |
vidyātapobhyāṃ bhūtātmā buddhirjñānena śudhyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रियां तु रोचमानायां सर्वं तद्रोचते कुलम् |
तस्यां त्वरोचमानायां सर्वमेव न रोचते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If the women in the family are happy, then the whole family will be happy. If they are sad (or dissatisfied) then the entire clan will be made unhappy.
quotes.transliteration:
striyāṃ tu rocamānāyāṃ sarvaṃ tadrocate kulam |
tasyāṃ tvarocamānāyāṃ sarvameva na rocate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अब्रुवन् कस्यचिन्निंदामात्मपूजामवर्णयन् ।
न कश्चिद् गुणसंपन्नः प्रकाशो भुवि दृश्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Can not find a single person who categorically never praises himself and never criticises others. So rare is that quality.
quotes.transliteration:
abruvan kasyacinniṃdāmātmapūjāmavarṇayan |
na kaścid guṇasaṃpannaḥ prakāśo bhuvi dṛśyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्व शरीरमशेषाणां श्लेषादीनां महाचयः ।
क्व कान्तिशोभासौन्दर्यरमणीयादयो गुणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: On one side we have this body that has lot of impurities and imperfections. On the other side we have adjectives like - brilliance, distinguish merit, beauty, enchanting and so on. The two are worlds apart.
quotes.transliteration:
kva śarīramaśeṣāṇāṃ śleṣādīnāṃ mahācayaḥ |
kva kāntiśobhāsaundaryaramaṇīyādayo guṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्के चेन्मधु विंदेत किमर्थं पर्वतं व्रजेत् ।
इष्टस्यार्थस्य संसिद्धौ को विद्वान् यत्नमाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one gets honey in the plant in the backyard, who will climb the mountain to get honey. They will not. Just like that, any scholar will not put any effort after (he thinks) he has learned all there is to learn.
quotes.transliteration:
arke cenmadhu viṃdeta kimarthaṃ parvataṃ vrajet |
iṣṭasyārthasya saṃsiddhau ko vidvān yatnamācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थनाशं मनस्तापं गृहे दुश्चरितानि च ।
वंचनं चापमानं च मतिमान्न प्रकाशयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man keep these incidents private - having lost money, having enmity, having a rogue in the household, being cheated and being humiliated.
quotes.transliteration:
arthanāśaṃ manastāpaṃ gṛhe duścaritāni ca |
vaṃcanaṃ cāpamānaṃ ca matimānna prakāśayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोकनन्दन चन्दनद्रुम सखे नास्मिन् वने स्थीययतां
दुर्वंशैः पुरुषैरसारहृदयैराक्रान्तमेतद्वनम् ।
तेऽप्यन्योन्यनिघर्षनातदहनज्वालावलीव्याकुलाः
न स्वान्येव कुलानि केवकममी सर्वं दहेयुर्वनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A sandalwood tree can not exist peacefully in a forest full of hard useless trees. Similarly a virtous people can not co-exist with hard hearted rough rogues. The fire started by their mutual hatred will not only burn them, but also burn the good trees (virtuous people).
quotes.transliteration:
lokanandana candanadruma sakhe nāsmin vane sthīyayatāṃ
durvaṃśaiḥ puruṣairasārahṛdayairākrāntametadvanam |
teऽpyanyonyanigharṣanātadahanajvālāvalīvyākulāḥ
na svānyeva kulāni kevakamamī sarvaṃ daheyurvanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं तीर्थं? हरिपादपद्मभजनं किं रत्नं? अच्छा मतिः
किं शास्त्रं? श्रवणेन यस्य गलति द्वैतांधकारोदयः ।
किं मित्रं? सततोपकाररसिकं तत्त्वावभोधः सखे
कः शत्रुर्वद खेददानकुशलो दुर्वासनानां चयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Which is a pilgrim place? Wherever people are worshiping the lotus like foot of the Lord. What is a gem? A pure mind. What are the scriptures? Anything, by listening which we get a unified view of the world and get rid of the darkness created by our differentiating mind. Who is a friend? he who is always ready to help and keeps us moral by giving out useful advice. Who is an enemy? He who specializes in giving out worries and bad habits.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ tīrthaṃ? haripādapadmabhajanaṃ kiṃ ratnaṃ? acchā matiḥ
kiṃ śāstraṃ? śravaṇena yasya galati dvaitāṃdhakārodayaḥ |
kiṃ mitraṃ? satatopakārarasikaṃ tattvāvabhodhaḥ sakhe
kaḥ śatrurvada khedadānakuśalo durvāsanānāṃ cayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः समुत्पति क्रोधं निगृह्णाति हयं यथा ।
स यंतेत्युच्यते सद्बिर्न यो रश्मिषु सज्जते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: 'Sarathi' - the real meaning of the word is 'one who controls anger' that arises in the mind just like a horse. It does not just mean a 'charioteer'
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ samutpati krodhaṃ nigṛhṇāti hayaṃ yathā |
sa yaṃtetyucyate sadbirna yo raśmiṣu sajjate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कः कं शक्तो रक्षितुं मृत्यु काले
रज्जुच्छेदे के घटं धारयन्ति ।
एवं लोकस्तुल्यधर्मो वनानां
काले काले छिद्यते रुह्यते च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who can help any one at the time of death? Can any one pull a pot from the well when the rope is broken? There is the same rule in the world of people as well as in the forest. Old trees fall down, young ones grow tall.
quotes.transliteration:
kaḥ kaṃ śakto rakṣituṃ mṛtyu kāle
rajjucchede ke ghaṭaṃ dhārayanti |
evaṃ lokastulyadharmo vanānāṃ
kāle kāle chidyate ruhyate ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुचिर्बालतृणाहारो विदूरक्रमणक्षमः ।
लुब्धकाद्गीतलोभेन मृगो मृगयते वधम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A deer almost leads a saintly life; clean, eating only young grass, capable of running long distance. But it gets drawn (almost intoxicated) towards the song of a hunter and get killed as a result.
quotes.transliteration:
śucirbālatṛṇāhāro vidūrakramaṇakṣamaḥ |
lubdhakādgītalobhena mṛgo mṛgayate vadham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नयनाभ्यां प्रसुप्तोऽपि जागर्ति नयचक्षुषा |
व्यक्तक्रोधप्रसादश्च स राजा पूज्यते जनैः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if the king has his eyes shut he must have his eyes of wisdom (logic, politics) open. Using that eye he must make his anger or pleasure decisively known to his subjects. People will always like such Kings.
quotes.transliteration:
nayanābhyāṃ prasuptoऽpi jāgarti nayacakṣuṣā |
vyaktakrodhaprasādaśca sa rājā pūjyate janaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तज्ज्ञानं प्रशमकरं यदिंद्रियाणां
तज्ज्ञेयं यदुपनिषत्सु निश्चितार्थम् ।
ते धन्या भुवि परमार्थनिश्चितेहाः
शेषास्तु भ्रमनिलये परिभमन्तः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That is knowledge that can arrest the speed at which our senses drive our mind. The subject of our studies should be to know more about the cosmic spirit mentioned in the Upanishats. There are some lucky people in this world who know all about the spirit. The rest of them are wandering in the illusion of knowing it.
quotes.transliteration:
tajjñānaṃ praśamakaraṃ yadiṃdriyāṇāṃ
tajjñeyaṃ yadupaniṣatsu niścitārtham |
te dhanyā bhuvi paramārthaniścitehāḥ
śeṣāstu bhramanilaye paribhamantaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न पूतो न तपस्वि च न यज्वा न च धर्मभाक् ।
क्रोधस्य यो वशं गच्छेत्तस्य लोकद्वयं न हि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person who gets angry easily is neither pure, not a saint, nor a virtuous person. He shall not find happiness in either of the words (present and after life).
quotes.transliteration:
na pūto na tapasvi ca na yajvā na ca dharmabhāk |
krodhasya yo vaśaṃ gacchettasya lokadvayaṃ na hi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अधोऽधः पश्यतः कस्य महिमा नोपचीयते ।
उपर्युपरि पश्यन्तः सर्व एव दरिद्रति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you always look downwards (people less privileged than you) then anyone can feel great about himself. If one only looks above, then he will always feel miserable. It is all about perspectives.
quotes.transliteration:
adhoऽdhaḥ paśyataḥ kasya mahimā nopacīyate |
uparyupari paśyantaḥ sarva eva daridrati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनःप्रीतिकरः स्वर्गः नरकस्तिद्विपर्यायः ।
नरकस्वर्गसंज्ञे वै पापपुण्ये द्विजोत्तम ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What gives you happiness is heaven. The opposite is hell. When you do good deeds, your conscience makes you happy. When you hurt some one, it makes you sad. Hence the Heaven and Hell are also associated with doing good deeds and bad deeds respectively.
quotes.transliteration:
manaḥprītikaraḥ svargaḥ narakastidviparyāyaḥ |
narakasvargasaṃjñe vai pāpapuṇye dvijottama ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रद्धया धार्यते धर्मो बहुभिर्नार्थराशिभिः ।
निष्किंचना हि मुनयः श्रद्धावन्तो दिवंगताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dedication and perseverance is the key to following Dharma - the moral code of life. Dharma can not be followed by having heaps of money. Mendicants and saints have attained salvation by strictly following Dharma and not by earning lot of wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
śraddhayā dhāryate dharmo bahubhirnārtharāśibhiḥ |
niṣkiṃcanā hi munayaḥ śraddhāvanto divaṃgatāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दोषेभ्यो नैव भेतव्यं दृढा चेद्भक्तिरुच्यते ।
तिमिरेभ्यो न हि भयं दीपश्चेल्लभ्यते महान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One need not be afraid of lapses (in his methods of rituals) id the devotion towards God is strong and true. No one will be afraid of darkness when he has the greatest lamp in his hand.
quotes.transliteration:
doṣebhyo naiva bhetavyaṃ dṛḍhā cedbhaktirucyate |
timirebhyo na hi bhayaṃ dīpaścellabhyate mahān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दानेन तुल्यो निधिरस्ति नान्यो
लोभाश्च नान्योऽस्ति रिपुः पृथिव्याम् ।
विभूषणं शीलसमं न चान्यत्
संतोषतुल्यं धनमस्ति नान्यत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no treasure (that comes in handy) better than charity. No worse enemy than greed. No better decoration than good character. No better wealth than happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
dānena tulyo nidhirasti nānyo
lobhāśca nānyoऽsti ripuḥ pṛthivyām |
vibhūṣaṇaṃ śīlasamaṃ na cānyat
saṃtoṣatulyaṃ dhanamasti nānyat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देहेऽस्थिमांसरुधिरेऽभिमतिं त्यज त्वं
जायासुतादिषु सदा ममतां विमुंच ।
पश्यानिशं जगदिदं क्षणभंगनिष्ठं
वैराग्यरागरसिको भव भक्तिनिष्ठः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: dehe asthi mAmsa rudhire abhimati tyaja tvaM Give up your excessive longing for this body that is made up of bones, muscles and blood. Stop your yearning for your wife and kids. When you look at this world, always be aware that it is ephemeral. Lead the life with detached austerity and faith in God.
quotes.transliteration:
deheऽsthimāṃsarudhireऽbhimatiṃ tyaja tvaṃ
jāyāsutādiṣu sadā mamatāṃ vimuṃca |
paśyāniśaṃ jagadidaṃ kṣaṇabhaṃganiṣṭhaṃ
vairāgyarāgarasiko bhava bhaktiniṣṭhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आयुःक्षणलवमात्रं न लभ्यते हेमकोटिभिः क्वापि ।
तच्चेद्गच्छति सर्वं मृषा ततः काऽधिका हानिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if you are ready to give crores and crores of gold coins, you can not extend your lifetime even by a fraction of a second. Life is so precious. If one is wasting such a precious life, then there is no bigger loss than that.
quotes.transliteration:
āyuḥkṣaṇalavamātraṃ na labhyate hemakoṭibhiḥ kvāpi |
taccedgacchati sarvaṃ mṛṣā tataḥ kāऽdhikā hāniḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न चात्रातीव कर्तव्यं दोषदृष्टिपरं मनः ।
दोषो ह्यविद्यमानोऽपि तच्चित्तानां प्रकाशते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not pay too much attention to negative feelings surrounding one self. If one concentrates too much on them, he will see negativity even when none such exist.
quotes.transliteration:
na cātrātīva kartavyaṃ doṣadṛṣṭiparaṃ manaḥ |
doṣo hyavidyamānoऽpi taccittānāṃ prakāśate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असंसक्तः शरीरेऽस्मिन्नमुनोत्पादितो गृहे ।
अपत्ये द्रविणे वापि कः कुर्यान्ममतां बुधः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men do not have any attachment towards their own body. How can we expect them to have any attachment towards household, children, wealth which come into existence only through the body.
quotes.transliteration:
asaṃsaktaḥ śarīreऽsminnamunotpādito gṛhe |
apatye draviṇe vāpi kaḥ kuryānmamatāṃ budhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मा कंचन शुचो राजन् यदीश्वरवशं जगत् ।
लोकाः सपाला यस्येमे वहंति बलिमीशितुः ।
स संयुनक्ति भूतानि स एव वियुनक्ति च ।
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh King! you are not the owner of this world. It is God who owns all world. So you don't have to feel sad for any loss. It is God who brings beings together and separates them. People may worship many other Gods or leaders. But all those salutations finally end up with the Creator himself.
quotes.transliteration:
mā kaṃcana śuco rājan yadīśvaravaśaṃ jagat |
lokāḥ sapālā yasyeme vahaṃti balimīśituḥ |
sa saṃyunakti bhūtāni sa eva viyunakti ca |

quotes.sanskritText:
यदध्रुवस्य देहस्य सानुबंधस्य दुर्मतिः ।
ध्रुवाणि मन्यते मोहाद् गृहक्षेत्रवसूनि च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only an idiot would treat destroyable like house, site or sons with great attachment. He does not know all these are attached to him only through the body which itself is very destructible.
quotes.transliteration:
yadadhruvasya dehasya sānubaṃdhasya durmatiḥ |
dhruvāṇi manyate mohād gṛhakṣetravasūni ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कर्मणा मनसा वाचा परपीडां करोति यः ।
तद्बीजं जन्म फलति प्रभूतं तस्य समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person hurts others through his action, words or thought then its consequences will make him suffer in the subsequent lives.
quotes.transliteration:
karmaṇā manasā vācā parapīḍāṃ karoti yaḥ |
tadbījaṃ janma phalati prabhūtaṃ tasya samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राज्ञोऽपि जल्पतां पुंसां श्रुत्वा वाचः शुभाशुभाः ।
गुणवद्ववाक्यमादत्ते हंसः क्षीरमिवांभसः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a swan has the ability to separate milk from water, a wise man must pay attention to only those words that matter (truth). There will be gregarious people who will speak a lot about others - some true, but mostly baseless.
quotes.transliteration:
prājñoऽpi jalpatāṃ puṃsāṃ śrutvā vācaḥ śubhāśubhāḥ |
guṇavadvavākyamādatte haṃsaḥ kṣīramivāṃbhasaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
साकारो निःस्पृहो वाग्मी नानाशास्त्रविचक्षणः |
परचित्तावगंता च राज्ञो दूतः स इष्यते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king needs a spy who has the following characters - agreeable looks, selfless, good oratory skills, learned in various disciplines, ability to read another's mind.
quotes.transliteration:
sākāro niḥspṛho vāgmī nānāśāstravicakṣaṇaḥ |
paracittāvagaṃtā ca rājño dūtaḥ sa iṣyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पृथुशास्त्रकथाकंथारोमन्थेन वृथैव किम् ।
अन्वेष्टव्यं प्रयत्नेन तत्त्वज्ञैर्ज्योतिरांतरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is little use in reading and studying scriptures, stories and other topics related to mythology. Real people who are realized souls will always search for light inwards.
quotes.transliteration:
pṛthuśāstrakathākaṃthāromanthena vṛthaiva kim |
anveṣṭavyaṃ prayatnena tattvajñairjyotirāṃtaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गीर्भिर्गुरूणां परुषाक्षराभिः
तिरस्कृता यान्ति नरा महत्त्वम् ।
अलब्धशाणोत्कषणा नराणां
न जातु मौलौ मणयो वसन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one is berated by his teacher with harsh words, he only attains greatness. A gem that has not gone through rough times on the grind stone never gets to be worn on the head by men.
quotes.transliteration:
gīrbhirgurūṇāṃ paruṣākṣarābhiḥ
tiraskṛtā yānti narā mahattvam |
alabdhaśāṇotkaṣaṇā narāṇāṃ
na jātu maulau maṇayo vasanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बलीयसा हीनबलो विरोधं
न भूतिकामो मनसापि वान्छेत् ।
न वध्यतेत्यन्तबलो हि यस्मा-
द्व्यक्तं प्रणशोस्ति पतंगवृत्तेः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you are weak, you should not even think of attacking a strong opponent. The strong and mighty will never fail. Destruction is assured to a person who is on the same path as the moth being attracted to flames.
quotes.transliteration:
balīyasā hīnabalo virodhaṃ
na bhūtikāmo manasāpi vānchet |
na vadhyatetyantabalo hi yasmā-
dvyaktaṃ praṇaśosti pataṃgavṛtteḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अध्रुवे हि शरीरे यो न करोति तपोऽर्जनम् ।
स पश्चात्ताप्यते मूढो मृतो दृष्ट्वात्मनो गतिम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This is a destructible body that we live in. So, we must do our best to attain good karma by doing a lot of penance. If not, you will look upon yourself as a fool in your afterlife.
quotes.transliteration:
adhruve hi śarīre yo na karoti tapoऽrjanam |
sa paścāttāpyate mūḍho mṛto dṛṣṭvātmano gatim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वासुदेवे भगवति भक्तिमुद्वहतां नृणाम् ।
ज्ञानवैराग्यवीर्याणां नेह कश्चिद्व्यपाश्रयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who have strong devotion in God, will have knowledge, austerity and courage. To get these, they do not need any help.
quotes.transliteration:
vāsudeve bhagavati bhaktimudvahatāṃ nṛṇām |
jñānavairāgyavīryāṇāṃ neha kaścidvyapāśrayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धुनोति निबिडं तमः परिलुनाति पापाटवीं
तनोति च मनोरथानुपचिनोति नानोत्सवान् ।
अनक्ति च पुनः पुनर्ननु मनः सुधानिर्झरैः
व्यनक्ति परिओपक्त्रिमं वृषगिरीशभक्तिः शुभम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one has strong devotion towards God, it will illuminate thick darkness, destroy the forest of sins, expands thoughts about God, gives various kinds of happiness. It will flood the mind again an again with elixir so that it can remain happy for ever.
quotes.transliteration:
dhunoti nibiḍaṃ tamaḥ parilunāti pāpāṭavīṃ
tanoti ca manorathānupacinoti nānotsavān |
anakti ca punaḥ punarnanu manaḥ sudhānirjharaiḥ
vyanakti pariopaktrimaṃ vṛṣagirīśabhaktiḥ śubham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संहतिः श्रेयसी पुंसां स्वकुलैरल्पकैरपि |
तुषेणापि परित्यक्तः तण्डुलो न प्ररोहति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Success comes to only those people who stick by their fellow beings even if they are weak. A seed of rice can not grow into a plant if it abandons the husk.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃhatiḥ śreyasī puṃsāṃ svakulairalpakairapi |
tuṣeṇāpi parityaktaḥ taṇḍulo na prarohati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रीणामशिक्षितपटुत्वममानुशीषु
संदृश्यते किमुत याः प्रतिभोधवत्यः |
प्रागंतरिक्षगमनात्स्वमपत्यजातं
अन्यैर्द्विजैः परभृताः खलु पोषयन्ति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Women are smart even if they do not go to school. This trait can be seen even in birds (and animals). A wise man will understand this immediately. A nightingale will lays its eggs in the crow's nest and bring up its young ones very carefully. It is so smart and cunning.
quotes.transliteration:
strīṇāmaśikṣitapaṭutvamamānuśīṣu
saṃdṛśyate kimuta yāḥ pratibhodhavatyaḥ |
prāgaṃtarikṣagamanātsvamapatyajātaṃ
anyairdvijaiḥ parabhṛtāḥ khalu poṣayanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राज्यं हि सुमहत्तन्त्रं धार्यते नाकृतात्मभिः |
न शक्यं मृदुना वोढुमायासस्थानमुत्तमम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Governing a state is a huge responsibility. A person who has not conquered his senses can not carry out the responsibility. Also a person who is too soft and gets tired easily can not be a king. ,
quotes.transliteration:
rājyaṃ hi sumahattantraṃ dhāryate nākṛtātmabhiḥ |
na śakyaṃ mṛdunā voḍhumāyāsasthānamuttamam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अग्नि शेषं ऋणः शेषं शत्रुः शेषं तथैव च ।
पुनः पुनः प्रवर्धेत तस्मात् शेषं न कारयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fire, loan, enemy - should never be left remaining. Even small trace will grow back into a monster size.
quotes.transliteration:
agni śeṣaṃ ṛṇaḥ śeṣaṃ śatruḥ śeṣaṃ tathaiva ca |
punaḥ punaḥ pravardheta tasmāt śeṣaṃ na kārayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बालांश्च यौवनस्थांश्च वृद्धान् गर्भगतानपि ।
सर्वानाविशते मृत्युः एवं भूतमिदं जगत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It does not matter if you are child, or youth, or old, or even hiding in the womb of your mother - death overpowers every being in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
bālāṃśca yauvanasthāṃśca vṛddhān garbhagatānapi |
sarvānāviśate mṛtyuḥ evaṃ bhūtamidaṃ jagat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तृणपर्णोदकाहाराः सततं वनवासिनः ।
जंबूकाखुमृगाद्यश्च तापसास्ते भवन्ति किम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If everybody who live in the forest and eat only grass and leaves were to attain sainthood - are all the foxes, mice and deer saints?
quotes.transliteration:
tṛṇaparṇodakāhārāḥ satataṃ vanavāsinaḥ |
jaṃbūkākhumṛgādyaśca tāpasāste bhavanti kim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अस्ति पुत्रो वशी यस्य बृत्यो भार्या तथैव च ।
अभावे सति संतोषः स्वर्गस्थोऽसौ महीतले ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a man has a obedient son, willing wife, loyal servant and is happy even when poor - he is said to have found heaven on earth.
quotes.transliteration:
asti putro vaśī yasya bṛtyo bhāryā tathaiva ca |
abhāve sati saṃtoṣaḥ svargasthoऽsau mahītale ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा पुमान्न स्वांगेषु शिरः पाण्यादिषु क्वचित् ।
पारक्यबुद्धिं कुरुते एवं भूतेषु मत्परः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a man considers his own body parts - head, arms - as his very own, a person who understands and has complete devotion considers all beings of the world as his own.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā pumānna svāṃgeṣu śiraḥ pāṇyādiṣu kvacit |
pārakyabuddhiṃ kurute evaṃ bhūteṣu matparaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चलन्ति गिरियः कामं युगान्तपवनाहताः ।
कृच्छ्रेऽपि न चलत्येव धीरणां निश्चलं मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The the mighty mountains also move under pressure from the winds that blow on the doom's day. Even in extreme trouble, the mind of a courageous person does not waver one bit.
quotes.transliteration:
calanti giriyaḥ kāmaṃ yugāntapavanāhatāḥ |
kṛcchreऽpi na calatyeva dhīraṇāṃ niścalaṃ manaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अतिपरिचयदवज्ञा संततगमनादनादरो भवति ।
मलये भिल्लपुरंध्री चन्दनतरुकाष्ठमिंधनं कुरुते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Familiarity gives way to contempt. Frequently visiting some one will lead to reduced respect. In the Malaya mountains, the wife of the hunter uses sandalwood for firewood.
quotes.transliteration:
atiparicayadavajñā saṃtatagamanādanādaro bhavati |
malaye bhillapuraṃdhrī candanatarukāṣṭhamiṃdhanaṃ kurute ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राज्ञो हि रक्षाधिकृताः परस्वादायिनः शठाः |
भृत्या भवन्ति प्रायेण तेभ्यो रक्षेदिमाः प्रजाः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Those officials appointed by the king himself can turn rogue and loot money from the subjects. Hence a King has to be aware of such people and protect the subjects from such rogues.
quotes.transliteration:
rājño hi rakṣādhikṛtāḥ parasvādāyinaḥ śaṭhāḥ |
bhṛtyā bhavanti prāyeṇa tebhyo rakṣedimāḥ prajāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रियः सपुरुषाः मार्गं सर्वालंकारसंयुताः |
निर्भयः प्रतिपद्यन्ते यदा रक्षति भूमिपः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If the king performs his duty of protecting his subjects very well, only then will the women of the state roam freely (and fearlessly) with their men on the streets of the city.
quotes.transliteration:
striyaḥ sapuruṣāḥ mārgaṃ sarvālaṃkārasaṃyutāḥ |
nirbhayaḥ pratipadyante yadā rakṣati bhūmipaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुजीर्णमन्नं सुविचक्षणः सुतः
सुशासिता स्त्री नृपतिः सुसेवितः ।
सुचिंत्य चोक्तं सुविचार्य यत्कृतं
सुदीर्घकाले न हि याति विक्रियाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: suchintya choktam suvichArya yatkRutam The following are of great value and their value does not erode over time: well digested food, well brought up son, well cultured woman, well served king, well thought speech, well deliberated action.
quotes.transliteration:
sujīrṇamannaṃ suvicakṣaṇaḥ sutaḥ
suśāsitā strī nṛpatiḥ susevitaḥ |
suciṃtya coktaṃ suvicārya yatkṛtaṃ
sudīrghakāle na hi yāti vikriyām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मानुष्यं नावमासाद्य तर दुःखमहानदीम् ।
मूढ! कालो न निद्राय इयं नौर्दुर्लभा पुनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A human birth is very rare and one must find himself fortunate. Once born as a human, we should try to cross the river of misery (endless cycle of births). You fool! this is not the time to waste by sleeping. We may not get this boat again.
quotes.transliteration:
mānuṣyaṃ nāvamāsādya tara duḥkhamahānadīm |
mūḍha! kālo na nidrāya iyaṃ naurdurlabhā punaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनुगंतुं सतां धर्म कृत्स्नं यदि न शक्यते ।
स्वल्पमप्यनुगन्तव्यं मार्गस्थो नावसीदति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must try to follow the practices of noble men. It is not possible to follow them completely, at least a little effort must be done. By going down their path, one will definitely not be destroyed.
quotes.transliteration:
anugaṃtuṃ satāṃ dharma kṛtsnaṃ yadi na śakyate |
svalpamapyanugantavyaṃ mārgastho nāvasīdati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदसत्यं वदेन्मॄत्यः यद्वाऽसेव्यं च सेवते ।
यद्गच्छति विदेशं च तत्सर्वमुदरार्थतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A man may tell lies (cheat), eat the un-eatables (non-veg), leave his home country and go travel abroad. He will do all those only to satisfy the hunger in his stomach.
quotes.transliteration:
yadasatyaṃ vadenmṝtyaḥ yadvāऽsevyaṃ ca sevate |
yadgacchati videśaṃ ca tatsarvamudarārthataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं वाससैवं न विचारणीयं
वासः प्रधानं खलु योग्यतायाः ।
पीतांबरं वीक्ष्य ददौ तनूजां
दिगंबरं वीक्ष्य विषं समुद्रः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not think that appearances do not matter at all. People judge the ability of a person by what he wears. During the great churning of the ocean, the king of the ocean gave his daughter Lakshmi to Vishnu seeing that he was wearing gold. He gave poison to Shiva since he was not wearing anything (smeared his body with ash).
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ vāsasaivaṃ na vicāraṇīyaṃ
vāsaḥ pradhānaṃ khalu yogyatāyāḥ |
pītāṃbaraṃ vīkṣya dadau tanūjāṃ
digaṃbaraṃ vīkṣya viṣaṃ samudraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कर्मणा जायते सर्वं कर्मैव गतिसाधनम् ।
तस्मात्सर्वप्रयत्नेन साधु कर्म समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Action leads to everything. Without action there would be nothing. Good action begets good results. Bad action begets misery. Hence one must always strive to do good deeds.
quotes.transliteration:
karmaṇā jāyate sarvaṃ karmaiva gatisādhanam |
tasmātsarvaprayatnena sādhu karma samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति ।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you offer me anything with pure earnest devotion, I shall accept. It may be leaves, flowers, fruits or water. (as told by Lord Krishna)
quotes.transliteration:
patraṃ puṣpaṃ phalaṃ toyaṃ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati |
tadahaṃ bhaktyupahṛtamaśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्मिन् देशे य आचारो व्यवहारः कुलस्थितिः ।
तथैव परिपाल्योऽसौ यदा वशमुपागतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a king conquers another state, he must rule that state as per the laws and customs prevailing in that state. He must uphold the dignity of the families in that state. He must ensure that business runs as usual.
quotes.transliteration:
yasmin deśe ya ācāro vyavahāraḥ kulasthitiḥ |
tathaiva paripālyoऽsau yadā vaśamupāgataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निरानन्दा दारा व्यसनविमुखो बांधवजनो
जडीभूतो मित्रं धनविरहशीर्णः परिजनः |
असन्तुष्टं चेतः कुलिशकठिनं जीवितमिदं
विधिर्वामारंभस्तदपि च मनो वाञ्छति सुखम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: This verse describes the state of a utterly hopeless situation of a man. Wife is not happy. Relatives have opted to look the other way (to escape the awkward situation of being forced to help him). Friends are not responding. Those well-wishers around him are all penniless. Mind is unsatisfied. Life is as hard as it gets. Fate is against him. Still the mind is still wanting all the pleasures of life. Even when faced with such great misery, a man does not understand that the root cause of all misery is greed and desire.
quotes.transliteration:
nirānandā dārā vyasanavimukho bāṃdhavajano
jaḍībhūto mitraṃ dhanavirahaśīrṇaḥ parijanaḥ |
asantuṣṭaṃ cetaḥ kuliśakaṭhinaṃ jīvitamidaṃ
vidhirvāmāraṃbhastadapi ca mano vāñchati sukham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनो न वैराग्यतरंगितं चेत्
वृथा तदा दानतपः प्रयासः ।
लावण्यमंगे यदि नांगनानां
मुधा तदा विभ्रमवल्गितानि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: mano na vairAgyatarangitam chet If austerity is not imbibed into the mindset, then there is no use of doing charity, penance and other efforts (towards salvation). All moves and tricks used by beautiful ladies to please you are a waste if they don't have charm.
quotes.transliteration:
mano na vairāgyataraṃgitaṃ cet
vṛthā tadā dānatapaḥ prayāsaḥ |
lāvaṇyamaṃge yadi nāṃganānāṃ
mudhā tadā vibhramavalgitāni ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लुब्धस्य नश्यति यशः पिशुनस्य मैत्री
नष्टक्रियस्य कुलमर्थपरस्य धर्मः |
विद्याफलं व्यसनिनः कृपणस्य सौख्यं
राज्यं प्रमत्तसचिवस्य नराधिपस्य ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A greedy person soon loses any shat at fame. A rumormonger loses his friends. A perpetual wrong-doer loses his family. A person whose only aim is to earn money strays from the path of dharma - the moral code of life. An addict loses any chance at getting good education. A miser loses piece of mind. A king with a bad (mad) minister loses his kingdom.
quotes.transliteration:
lubdhasya naśyati yaśaḥ piśunasya maitrī
naṣṭakriyasya kulamarthaparasya dharmaḥ |
vidyāphalaṃ vyasaninaḥ kṛpaṇasya saukhyaṃ
rājyaṃ pramattasacivasya narādhipasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अशोचयन् प्रतिकुर्वीत यदि पश्येत् पराक्रमम् ।
भैषज्यमेतद्दुःखस्य यदेतन्नानुचिंतयेत्॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you are courageous, you must attack the problems head on. Not thinking too much about it is the medicine for unhappiness.
quotes.transliteration:
aśocayan pratikurvīta yadi paśyet parākramam |
bhaiṣajyametadduḥkhasya yadetannānuciṃtayet||

quotes.sanskritText:
तिरस्कुर्वन्ति कृतिनस्तापयन्ति तपस्व्नः ।
आत्मानमपि हिंसन्ति क्रोधान्धाः किमतःपरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: They will dismiss (ridicule) the people who accomplish goals. They will hurt the people who lead a life as if it were a penance. They some times evern hurt themselves. These are the people who are blinded by anger - what else can it be?
quotes.transliteration:
tiraskurvanti kṛtinastāpayanti tapasvnaḥ |
ātmānamapi hiṃsanti krodhāndhāḥ kimataḥparam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्याविनयोपेता हरति न चेतांसि कस्य मनुजस्य ।
मणिकाञ्चनसंयोगो जनयति लोकस्य लोचनानंदम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who can restrain themselves from liking a well educated man who is also humble? A gem embedded in gold is very pleasing for the eyes world over.
quotes.transliteration:
vidyāvinayopetā harati na cetāṃsi kasya manujasya |
maṇikāñcanasaṃyogo janayati lokasya locanānaṃdam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अपराधं सहेताल्पं तुष्येदल्पेऽपि चोदये ।
महोपकारांश्चाध्यक्षान् प्रग्रहेणाभिपूजयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Minor offences must be overlooked. One must rejoice at even small successes. When one helps you explicitly, he must be respected.
quotes.transliteration:
aparādhaṃ sahetālpaṃ tuṣyedalpeऽpi codaye |
mahopakārāṃścādhyakṣān pragraheṇābhipūjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जनिता चोपनेता च यश्च विद्यां प्रयच्छति ।
अन्नदाता भयत्राता पंचैते पितरः स्मृताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following people must be treated with respect as if they were our fathers - one who gives birth, one who initiates us to the sanatana dharma, one who teaches us, one who gives us food and one who protects us and rescues us from fear.
quotes.transliteration:
janitā copanetā ca yaśca vidyāṃ prayacchati |
annadātā bhayatrātā paṃcaite pitaraḥ smṛtāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दाता लघुरपि सेव्यो भवति न कृपणो महानपि समृद्ध्या ।
कूपोऽतः स्वादुजलः प्रीत्यै लोकस्य न समुद्रः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if a generous person is poor, he is to be treated with respect. A miser deserves no respect even if he is rich and prosperous. People like water from a small well but not from the mighty ocean.
quotes.transliteration:
dātā laghurapi sevyo bhavati na kṛpaṇo mahānapi samṛddhyā |
kūpoऽtaḥ svādujalaḥ prītyai lokasya na samudraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुरौ यत्र परीवादो निंदा वापि प्रवर्तते ।
कर्णौ तत्र पिधातव्यौ गंतव्यं वा ततोऽन्यतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one finds irresponsible people slandering his teacher, he must immediately close his ears. It is better if he moves away from such a place. This is important because listening to such slander will cause doubt in one's mind regarding his master. He will not be able to follow the teacher completely if such doubt exists.
quotes.transliteration:
gurau yatra parīvādo niṃdā vāpi pravartate |
karṇau tatra pidhātavyau gaṃtavyaṃ vā tatoऽnyataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शास्त्रेषु हीनाः कवयो भवन्ति
कवित्वहीनस्य पुराणभट्टाः |
पुराणहीनाः कृषका भवन्ति
भग्नाः कृषेर्भगवता भवन्ति ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who are not well versed with the scriptures will become poets. Those who are not poetic will at least be able to recite the puranas (mythological stories). Those who can not do that will become agriculturists. Those who can not do agriculture, will roam villages asking for alms.
quotes.transliteration:
śāstreṣu hīnāḥ kavayo bhavanti
kavitvahīnasya purāṇabhaṭṭāḥ |
purāṇahīnāḥ kṛṣakā bhavanti
bhagnāḥ kṛṣerbhagavatā bhavanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नातिदूरे न चासन्ने अत्याढ्ये चातिदुर्बले ।
वृत्तिहीने च मूर्खे च षट्सु कन्या न दीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not give his daughter in marriage to a person who live in far off land, or very near you, or very strong (cause he could become oppressive soon), or very weak, or does not want to work or is an idiot.
quotes.transliteration:
nātidūre na cāsanne atyāḍhye cātidurbale |
vṛttihīne ca mūrkhe ca ṣaṭsu kanyā na dīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः ।
न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one leaves the path shown by the scriptures and follows his desires, then he will not attain self realization. He will not be happy. He will not attain salvation also.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ śāstravidhimutsṛjya vartate kāmakārataḥ |
na sa siddhimavāpnoti na sukhaṃ na parāṃ gatim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विप्रास्मिन्नगरे महान् कथय कस्तालद्रुमाणां गणः
को दाता, रजको ददाति वसनं प्रातर्गृहीत्वा निशि ।
को दक्षः, परदारवित्तहरणे सर्वोऽपि दक्षो जनः
कस्माज्जीवसि हे सखे, विषकृमिन्यायेन जीवाम्यहम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Brahmin! tell me who is the tallest (in stature) in this city? Only the palm trees here can be called tall. Who is the greatest giver? The washer man is the only person who gives; that too at night he gives the clothes he took in the morning. Who is efficient? Everyone is efficient when it comes to stealing money from other people's wives. Then how do you live here? I follow the principles of a poisonous bacteria to survive here; do everything to live and not care about anything else.
quotes.transliteration:
viprāsminnagare mahān kathaya kastāladrumāṇāṃ gaṇaḥ
ko dātā, rajako dadāti vasanaṃ prātargṛhītvā niśi |
ko dakṣaḥ, paradāravittaharaṇe sarvoऽpi dakṣo janaḥ
kasmājjīvasi he sakhe, viṣakṛminyāyena jīvāmyaham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पठन्ति वेदशास्त्राणि बोधयन्ति परस्परम् |
न जानन्ति परं तत्त्वं दर्वी पाकरसं यथा ||
quotes.englishTranslation: They study the Vedas. Exchange notes and teach each other. Still they do not know the ultimate truth that is being discussed in the Vedas. Just like the serving spoon that never knows the taste of the dish.
quotes.transliteration:
paṭhanti vedaśāstrāṇi bodhayanti parasparam |
na jānanti paraṃ tattvaṃ darvī pākarasaṃ yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रासादिकत्वमारोग्यं प्रामोद्यं चिरजीवितम् |
चक्रवर्तिसुखं स्फीतं शान्तः प्राप्नोति संसरन् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One who can find peace within himself will enjoy the following even while living in this world (of misery) - satisfaction, good health, happiness, longevity, great pleasures worthy of an emperor.
quotes.transliteration:
prāsādikatvamārogyaṃ prāmodyaṃ cirajīvitam |
cakravartisukhaṃ sphītaṃ śāntaḥ prāpnoti saṃsaran ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अप्रियस्यापि पथ्यस्य परिणामः सुखावहः ।
वक्ता श्रोता च यत्रास्ति रमन्ते तत्र संपदः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A matured (well thought out) speech will bring out good results even when it is harsh (or unpleasant). Any place where there are people with clear mind to talk and open mind to listen will always prosper.
quotes.transliteration:
apriyasyāpi pathyasya pariṇāmaḥ sukhāvahaḥ |
vaktā śrotā ca yatrāsti ramante tatra saṃpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शत्रुमुन्मूलयेत्प्राज्ञः तीक्ष्णं तीक्ष्णेन शत्रुणा |
व्यथाकरं सुखार्थाय कंटकेनेव कंटकम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A strong enemy must be vanquished by taking help of another enemy. To relieve the pain, a thorn must be taken out (of the foot) by using another thorn only.
quotes.transliteration:
śatrumunmūlayetprājñaḥ tīkṣṇaṃ tīkṣṇena śatruṇā |
vyathākaraṃ sukhārthāya kaṃṭakeneva kaṃṭakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनल्पकं धरायां हि सर्वज्ञस्यापि दारुणाः ।
आरोपयन्ति मालिन्यं काकोला इव खलाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world, rogues will not let virtuous people live in peace. They smear them with false allegations. They did not even let Lord Shiva alone. They made him drink the Kalakuta poison.
quotes.transliteration:
analpakaṃ dharāyāṃ hi sarvajñasyāpi dāruṇāḥ |
āropayanti mālinyaṃ kākolā iva khalāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुलभशरणमाश्रयो भयानां
वनगहनं तिमिरं च तुल्यमेव |
उभयमपि हि रक्षतेऽन्धकारो
जनयति यश्च भयानि यश्च भीतः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Darkness and a deep forest are alike. They create fear and they also protect those who seek refuge. Look at the theif who steals at night and the person who runs away from him. The forest protects the thief and the darkness protects the other (from the thief)
quotes.transliteration:
sulabhaśaraṇamāśrayo bhayānāṃ
vanagahanaṃ timiraṃ ca tulyameva |
ubhayamapi hi rakṣateऽndhakāro
janayati yaśca bhayāni yaśca bhītaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नित्यं क्रोधात्तपो रक्षेत् धर्मं रक्षेच्च मत्सरात् |
विद्यां मानापमानाभ्यामात्मानं तु प्रमादतः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: At all times, one must protect his penance from anger, morality from jealousy, knowledge from emotions (reactions to praise or ridicule), one self from madness.
quotes.transliteration:
nityaṃ krodhāttapo rakṣet dharmaṃ rakṣecca matsarāt |
vidyāṃ mānāpamānābhyāmātmānaṃ tu pramādataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संचितस्यापि महता वत्स क्लेशेन मानवैः |
यशसस्तपसश्चैव क्रोधो नाशकरः परः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: People collect good qualities like fame and righteousness with all the hard work and care. But if they succumb to anger, then all of that goes away in an instant.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃcitasyāpi mahatā vatsa kleśena mānavaiḥ |
yaśasastapasaścaiva krodho nāśakaraḥ paraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
समुत्पत्तिं च मांसस्य वधबंधौ च देहिनां ।
प्रसमीक्ष्य निवर्तेत सर्वमांसस्य भक्षणात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must spare a thought to the source of all the meat. Where does it come from? How are animals killed or held captive for the same? After having thought about all these, one must (will) give up all meat eating. This verse stresses the point that one must not distance himself from the horrors of sourcing of the meat while eating it.
quotes.transliteration:
samutpattiṃ ca māṃsasya vadhabaṃdhau ca dehināṃ |
prasamīkṣya nivarteta sarvamāṃsasya bhakṣaṇāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शब्दजालं महारण्यं चित्तभ्रमणकारणम् ।
अतः प्रयत्नाज्ज्ञातव्यं तत्त्वज्ञात्तत्त्वमात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Words are like a forest. Our mind gets lost wandering about it. Therefore one must guard against such possibility. For that, one has to study about inner conscience from a wise master.
quotes.transliteration:
śabdajālaṃ mahāraṇyaṃ cittabhramaṇakāraṇam |
ataḥ prayatnājjñātavyaṃ tattvajñāttattvamātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ब्राह्मे मुहूर्ते चोत्थाय चिन्तयेदात्मनो हितं ।
धर्मार्थकामान् स्वे काले यथाशक्ति न हापयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must get up early in the morning and think about his own wellness. Be positive. Whenever the world presents opportunities to earn some Dharma (by doing good deeds), wealth and pleasures - he must grab it. He must not let them go (in the guise of austerity).
quotes.transliteration:
brāhme muhūrte cotthāya cintayedātmano hitaṃ |
dharmārthakāmān sve kāle yathāśakti na hāpayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मश्च लोकदंभार्थं पाषाण्डार्थं तपस्विनः ।
विविधा वाग्वंचनार्थं कलौ सत्यानुसारिणी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the time of kali (present age) the Rishis will claim to follow dharma only to show off. Their words will be very convinsing like truth. But they are only meant to cheat you. All these are so unrighteous in nature.
quotes.attribution: Bruahat Parashara Smriti, Epoch of Kali
quotes.transliteration:
dharmaśca lokadaṃbhārthaṃ pāṣāṇḍārthaṃ tapasvinaḥ |
vividhā vāgvaṃcanārthaṃ kalau satyānusāriṇī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः सततं परिपृच्छति शृणोति संधारयत्यनिशं ।
तस्य दिवाकरकिरणैर्नलिनीव विवर्धते बुद्धिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He will become a wise man who will keep asking questions, listens to answers and contemplates on each of them. His knowledge will bloom like a lotus when sun rays fall on it.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ satataṃ paripṛcchati śṛṇoti saṃdhārayatyaniśaṃ |
tasya divākarakiraṇairnalinīva vivardhate buddhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कायः सन्निहितापायः संपदः क्षणभंगुराः ।
समागमाः सापगमाः सर्वेषामेव देहिनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The body will be destroyed in time by death. Wealth lasts for a second. All the things attached to this body are bound to be destroyed in time.
quotes.transliteration:
kāyaḥ sannihitāpāyaḥ saṃpadaḥ kṣaṇabhaṃgurāḥ |
samāgamāḥ sāpagamāḥ sarveṣāmeva dehinām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जलबिंदुनिपातेन क्रमशः पूर्यते घटः ।
स हेतुः सर्वविद्यानां धर्मस्य च धनस्य च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A pot gets filled by water falling into it one drop at a time. It may take a lot of time, but it does get filled. Such is the story of accumulation of knowledge (via education), good karma (by doing noble deeds), and wealth. All of these are very slow processes.
quotes.transliteration:
jalabiṃdunipātena kramaśaḥ pūryate ghaṭaḥ |
sa hetuḥ sarvavidyānāṃ dharmasya ca dhanasya ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाचा निर्मलया सुधामधुरया यां नाथ शिक्षामदाः
तां स्वप्नेऽपि न संस्मराम्यहमहंभावावृतो निस्त्रिपः ।
इत्यागःशतशालिनं पुनरपि स्वीयेषु मां बिभ्रतः
तत्त्वो नास्ति दयानिधिर्यदुपते मत्तो न मत्तः परः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Your teachings were wonderful, pure and sweet. But I was too proud to learn any of them. I have made hundreds of mistakes. Yet to accept me. There is not greater truth than the fact that you are the no one more compassionate than you and no bigger idiot than me.
quotes.transliteration:
vācā nirmalayā sudhāmadhurayā yāṃ nātha śikṣāmadāḥ
tāṃ svapneऽpi na saṃsmarāmyahamahaṃbhāvāvṛto nistripaḥ |
ityāgaḥśataśālinaṃ punarapi svīyeṣu māṃ bibhrataḥ
tattvo nāsti dayānidhiryadupate matto na mattaḥ paraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कवीनां प्रतिभा चक्षुः शास्त्रं चक्षुर्विपश्चिताम् |
ज्ञानं चक्षुर्महर्षीणां चराश्चक्षुर्महीक्षिताम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: For poets - their talent is their eyes. Scriptures are the eyes of a wise man. Knowledge (about the universe) is the eye of a sage. A king has his spies as his eyes.
quotes.transliteration:
kavīnāṃ pratibhā cakṣuḥ śāstraṃ cakṣurvipaścitām |
jñānaṃ cakṣurmaharṣīṇāṃ carāścakṣurmahīkṣitām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अपूज्या यत्र पूज्यन्ते पूज्यानां तु विमानना ।
त्रीणि तत्र प्रवर्तन्ते दुर्भिक्षं मरणं भयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In a place where respectable people are not respected and where rogues are respected more - three things are bound to be abundant. Poverty (misery), death and fear.
quotes.transliteration:
apūjyā yatra pūjyante pūjyānāṃ tu vimānanā |
trīṇi tatra pravartante durbhikṣaṃ maraṇaṃ bhayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संरक्षणार्थं जन्तूनां रात्रावहनि वा सदा ।
शरीरस्यात्यये चैवे समीक्ष्य वसुधां चरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when it is tough, one must look out for safe grounds for his animals (cattle and pets) - not considering whether it is day or night.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃrakṣaṇārthaṃ jantūnāṃ rātrāvahani vā sadā |
śarīrasyātyaye caive samīkṣya vasudhāṃ caret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राजा धर्मश्च कामश्च द्रव्याणां चोत्तवो निधिः |
धर्मः शुभं वा पापं वा राजमूलं प्रवर्तते ||
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must be the source of guidance for the subjects in matters of morality and pleasures. He must also be the source of wealth. All actions in the state - moral, good or bad are all attributed to the king.
quotes.transliteration:
rājā dharmaśca kāmaśca dravyāṇāṃ cottavo nidhiḥ |
dharmaḥ śubhaṃ vā pāpaṃ vā rājamūlaṃ pravartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भेषजस्नेहलवणगन्धधान्यगुडादिषु |
पण्येषु प्रक्षिपन् हीनं पणान् दाप्यस्तु षोडश ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a miser shopkeeper is adulterating medicine, oil, salt, sandal wood paste (perfume), cereals, jaggery and other such house hold items then he must be fined sixteen times the value of what he sold.
quotes.transliteration:
bheṣajasnehalavaṇagandhadhānyaguḍādiṣu |
paṇyeṣu prakṣipan hīnaṃ paṇān dāpyastu ṣoḍaśa ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अयशः प्राप्यते येन येन चापगतिर्भवेत् ।
स्वर्गाश्च भ्रश्यते येन तत्कर्म न समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not do any action that will harm his fame, lead him to destruction or lose a chance at heaven.
quotes.transliteration:
ayaśaḥ prāpyate yena yena cāpagatirbhavet |
svargāśca bhraśyate yena tatkarma na samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकुर्वन् विहितं कर्म निन्दितं च समाचरन् |
प्रसक्तश्चेंद्रियार्थेषु प्रायश्चित्तीयते नरः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: If a man does not do his duties and follows a condemned path being a slave to sensory pleasures, then he is bound to spend the last day of his life in regret. He will long for atonement of wrong doings.
quotes.transliteration:
akurvan vihitaṃ karma ninditaṃ ca samācaran |
prasaktaśceṃdriyārtheṣu prāyaścittīyate naraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्यूतादनर्थः संरम्भो द्यूतात् स्नेहक्षयो महान् ।
पक्षाणां चापि महतां द्यूताद्भेदः प्रवर्तते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Gambling is the start of many bad habits leading to miserable times. It strains good friendships. It is known to cause rift even in otherwise strong cohesive groups (of friends).
quotes.transliteration:
dyūtādanarthaḥ saṃrambho dyūtāt snehakṣayo mahān |
pakṣāṇāṃ cāpi mahatāṃ dyūtādbhedaḥ pravartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
त्रिभिर्वर्षैस्त्रिभिर्मासैस्त्रिभिः पक्षैस्त्रिभिर्दिनैः ।
अत्युत्कटैः पुण्यपापैरिहैव फलमश्नुते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a deed you do has extraordinary consequences (either good or bad), then you will face its consequences in this life (and in this world) only. That could be with in 3 years, or 3 months, or 3 fortnights or just 3 days.
quotes.transliteration:
tribhirvarṣaistribhirmāsaistribhiḥ pakṣaistribhirdinaiḥ |
atyutkaṭaiḥ puṇyapāpairihaiva phalamaśnute ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लुब्ध्वा सुदुर्लभतरं नरजन्म जंतुः
तत्रापि पौरुषमतः सदसद्विवेकम् ।
संप्राप्य चैहिकसुखाभिरतो यदि स्यात्
धिक् तस्य जन्म कुमतेः पुरुषाधमस्य ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: lubdhvA sudurlabhataram nara janma jantu After having got the rare (and most sought after) human birth, and been rewarded with courage and wisdom to judge right and wrong if you still indulge in all the material pleasures then such a bad person with bad mind will be damned.
quotes.transliteration:
lubdhvā sudurlabhataraṃ narajanma jaṃtuḥ
tatrāpi pauruṣamataḥ sadasadvivekam |
saṃprāpya caihikasukhābhirato yadi syāt
dhik tasya janma kumateḥ puruṣādhamasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्रोडीकरोति प्रथमं जातं नित्यमनित्यता ।
धात्रीव जननी पश्चात्तदा शोकस्य कः क्रमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: As soon as one is born, impermanence embraces him and stays with him all along; even before his mother gets hold of him. That being so, why should anyone feel grief when one finally dies?
quotes.transliteration:
kroḍīkaroti prathamaṃ jātaṃ nityamanityatā |
dhātrīva jananī paścāttadā śokasya kaḥ kramaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सहसा विदधीत न क्रियां
अविवेकः परमापदां पदम् |
वृणते हि विमृश्यकारिणं
गुणलुब्धाः स्वयमेव संपदः ||
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not hurry while starting something important. If you hurry, it is a recipie for disaster. Success automatically comes to those who patiently plan before starting something. Success is attracted towards such qualities.
quotes.transliteration:
sahasā vidadhīta na kriyāṃ
avivekaḥ paramāpadāṃ padam |
vṛṇate hi vimṛśyakāriṇaṃ
guṇalubdhāḥ svayameva saṃpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ततोऽनृतं मदं कामं रजो वैरं च पंचमम् ।
अमूनि पंचस्थानानि ह्यधर्मप्रभवः कलिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Kali's influence is usually wicked. His wickedness is usually expressed in five ways - dishonesty, intoxication, lust, impurity (of thought and action), enemity.
quotes.transliteration:
tatoऽnṛtaṃ madaṃ kāmaṃ rajo vairaṃ ca paṃcamam |
amūni paṃcasthānāni hyadharmaprabhavaḥ kaliḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भगवानेक एवैष सर्व क्षेत्रेष्ववस्थितः ।
अमुष्य दुर्भगत्वं वा क्लेशो वा कर्मभिः कुतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is only one God. He is present every where and inside each being - living or non-living. That being so, how can he ever have misery, misfortune o any form of trouble. We perceive these feelings because we look at ourselves in isolation and miss the big picture.
quotes.transliteration:
bhagavāneka evaiṣa sarva kṣetreṣvavasthitaḥ |
amuṣya durbhagatvaṃ vā kleśo vā karmabhiḥ kutaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पारुष्यमनृतं चैव पैशुन्यं चापि सर्वशः |
असंबद्धप्रलापश्च वाज्ञ्मयं स्याच्चतुर्विधम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: Harsh words (hurting some one with words), dishonesty, calumny, banter - these are the four sins that can be committed by speech.
quotes.transliteration:
pāruṣyamanṛtaṃ caiva paiśunyaṃ cāpi sarvaśaḥ |
asaṃbaddhapralāpaśca vājñmayaṃ syāccaturvidham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य धर्मविहीनानि दिनान्यायान्ति यान्ति च ।
स लोहकारभस्त्रेव श्वसन्नपि न जीवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A man who does not lead his life following Dharma is simply breathing - not living. He is similar to the bag used by blacksmiths to blow air into the fire.
quotes.transliteration:
yasya dharmavihīnāni dinānyāyānti yānti ca |
sa lohakārabhastreva śvasannapi na jīvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ज्येष्ठो भ्राता पिता चैव यश्च विद्यां प्रयच्छति ।
त्रयस्ते पितरो ज्ञेया धर्मे वर्त्मनि वर्तिनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must treat eldest brother, real father and teacher with the same reverence that a father deserves.
quotes.transliteration:
jyeṣṭho bhrātā pitā caiva yaśca vidyāṃ prayacchati |
trayaste pitaro jñeyā dharme vartmani vartinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असारे खलु संसारे सुखभ्रांतिः शरीरिणाम् ।
लालापानमिवांगुष्ठे बालानां स्थन्यविभ्रमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Every being in this world who thinks is happy with his worldly possessions is disillusioned. A baby happily sucks the thumb and licks its own saliva thinking it is mother's breast milk.
quotes.transliteration:
asāre khalu saṃsāre sukhabhrāṃtiḥ śarīriṇām |
lālāpānamivāṃguṣṭhe bālānāṃ sthanyavibhramaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दातृत्वमेव सर्वेभ्यो गुणेभ्यो भासतेतराम् ।
ज्ञातृत्वसहितं तेच्चेत्सुवर्णस्येव सौरभम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Generosity is the quality that shines the brightest among all other qualities. But if knowledge and generosity get together, then it is like adding perfume to gold.
quotes.transliteration:
dātṛtvameva sarvebhyo guṇebhyo bhāsatetarām |
jñātṛtvasahitaṃ teccetsuvarṇasyeva saurabham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शोकस्थानसहस्राणि दुःखस्थानशतानि च ।
दिवसे दिवसे मूढमाविशन्ति न पण्डितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Everyday there are thousand reasons to feel sad, hundred reasons to worry. Such things only bother fools; not wise men.
quotes.transliteration:
śokasthānasahasrāṇi duḥkhasthānaśatāni ca |
divase divase mūḍhamāviśanti na paṇḍitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्कर्म कुर्वतोऽस्य स्यात्परितोषोंऽतरात्मनः ।
तत्प्रयत्नेन कुर्वीत विपरीतं तु वर्जयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must strive hard to get those work done which give satisfaction to our conscience. Those activities that trouble conscience should be avoided.
quotes.transliteration:
yatkarma kurvatoऽsya syātparitoṣoṃऽtarātmanaḥ |
tatprayatnena kurvīta viparītaṃ tu varjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुण्यस्य फलमिच्छन्ति पुणयं नॆच्छन्ति मानवाः ।
पापस्य तु न हीच्छन्ति पापं कुर्वन्ति यत्नतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People want to enjoy the fruits of good deeds, but don't want to perform good deeds. They don't want to be punished due to their sins, but continue committing those sins even after knowing that it is a sin.
quotes.transliteration:
puṇyasya phalamicchanti puṇayaṃ naॆcchanti mānavāḥ |
pāpasya tu na hīcchanti pāpaṃ kurvanti yatnataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकेनापि सुपुत्रेण सिंही स्वपिति निर्भयम् ।
सहैव दशभिः पुत्रैर्भारं वहति गर्दभी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A lioness has only one son - a good son. So, she can sleep fearlessly. A donkey on the other hand has ten children. But she has to still slog it out by lifting weights.
quotes.transliteration:
ekenāpi suputreṇa siṃhī svapiti nirbhayam |
sahaiva daśabhiḥ putrairbhāraṃ vahati gardabhī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोहं स्पर्शात्स्वर्णं स्पर्शमणेर्यत्रकुत्रचित्क्षिप्तम् ।
तद्वच्चरणस्पर्शाद् भक्तः कृष्णस्य विमलः स्यात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A touchstone can turn any metal to gold any where by simply touching it. Just like that, getting a chance to touch the feet of Lord Krishna will purify you completely.
quotes.transliteration:
lohaṃ sparśātsvarṇaṃ sparśamaṇeryatrakutracitkṣiptam |
tadvaccaraṇasparśād bhaktaḥ kṛṣṇasya vimalaḥ syāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुखमध्ये स्थितं दुःखं दुःखमध्ये स्थितं सुखम् ।
द्वयमन्योन्यसंयुक्तं प्रोच्यते जलपंकवत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is grief in the middle of happiness. There is happiness in the middle of grief. Both of them are entangled and cannot exist independently - inseparable just like mud and water in slush.
quotes.transliteration:
sukhamadhye sthitaṃ duḥkhaṃ duḥkhamadhye sthitaṃ sukham |
dvayamanyonyasaṃyuktaṃ procyate jalapaṃkavat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स बन्धुर्यो विपन्नानां आपदुद्धरणक्षमः ।
न तु दुर्विहितातीतवस्तूपालंभपण्डितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is a real friend (or relative) who can get people out of trouble. A person can not be a friend who can not manage other people's troubles or keep reminding them about past failures.
quotes.transliteration:
sa bandhuryo vipannānāṃ āpaduddharaṇakṣamaḥ |
na tu durvihitātītavastūpālaṃbhapaṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुलं च शीलं च सनाथतां च
विद्यां च वित्तं च वपुर्वयश्च ।
एतान्गुणान्सप्त विचिन्त्य देया
कन्या बुधैः शेषमचिंतनीयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only the following seven attributes of a groom are important - family, character, ownership of property, education, wealth, physique and age. Rest all are immaterial. A wise man will only look at these qualities in a groom before marrying his daughter to him.
quotes.transliteration:
kulaṃ ca śīlaṃ ca sanāthatāṃ ca
vidyāṃ ca vittaṃ ca vapurvayaśca |
etānguṇānsapta vicintya deyā
kanyā budhaiḥ śeṣamaciṃtanīyam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उज्वलगुणमभ्युदितं
क्षुद्रो द्रष्टुं न कथमपि क्षमते ।
दग्ध्वा तनुमपि शलभः
दीप्तं दीपार्चिषं हरति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: ujvalaguNam abhyuditam dagdhvA tanumapi shalabhaH A wicked person can never be at peace if he sees a noble man prosper. He will do anything to harm the noble man. A moth is ready to get burnt in the process of putting out the flame.
quotes.transliteration:
ujvalaguṇamabhyuditaṃ
kṣudro draṣṭuṃ na kathamapi kṣamate |
dagdhvā tanumapi śalabhaḥ
dīptaṃ dīpārciṣaṃ harati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तप्ते सर्पिषि पूरिका क्षिप्ताह्यामा करोति बहुशब्दान्
पक्वा यथा यथा सोनयति हि शब्दं ततस्तूष्णीम् ।
तद्वत्पुंसो ज्ञानं यावत्स्वल्पं करोति बहुवादान्
पूर्णज्ञानप्राप्तौ शान्तो विजितेन्द्रियस्तथा भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When poori (a snack) is dropped into hot ghee, it makes a lot of noise. But when it bakes properly, it stops making noise and rises up. Just like that a person with half-knowledge will make a lot of noise and arguments. One he gets full knowledge he will have more control over his senses and hence will find peace within himself.
quotes.transliteration:
tapte sarpiṣi pūrikā kṣiptāhyāmā karoti bahuśabdān
pakvā yathā yathā sonayati hi śabdaṃ tatastūṣṇīm |
tadvatpuṃso jñānaṃ yāvatsvalpaṃ karoti bahuvādān
pūrṇajñānaprāptau śānto vijitendriyastathā bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धवलान्यातपत्राणि वाजिनश्च मनोरमाः ।
सदा मत्ताश्च मातंगाः प्रसन्ने सति भूपतौ ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If the King is kept happy you can enjoy everything. There will be white umbrellas, horses, damsels and raging elephants. It is in our best interests to keep him happy.
quotes.transliteration:
dhavalānyātapatrāṇi vājinaśca manoramāḥ |
sadā mattāśca mātaṃgāḥ prasanne sati bhūpatau ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुरुं प्रयोजनोद्देशादर्चयन्ति न भक्तितः ।
दुग्धदात्रीति गौर्गेहे पोष्यते न तु धर्मतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People worship the teacher (or the planet Guru) becuase there is benefit in doing so - not out of devotion. People rear cows at home because they give milk - not with dharma in mind. A quick reality check.
quotes.transliteration:
guruṃ prayojanoddeśādarcayanti na bhaktitaḥ |
dugdhadātrīti gaurgehe poṣyate na tu dharmataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मात्रा समं नास्ति शरीरपोषणं
विद्यासमं नास्ति शरीर भूषणम् ।
भार्यासमं नास्ति शरीरतोषणं
चिंतासमं नास्ति शरीरशोषणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Nobody/nothing nourishes the body like mother. Nothing decorates the body better than education/knowledge. Nothing satiates the body like the wife. Nothing troubles the body more than worry.
quotes.transliteration:
mātrā samaṃ nāsti śarīrapoṣaṇaṃ
vidyāsamaṃ nāsti śarīra bhūṣaṇam |
bhāryāsamaṃ nāsti śarīratoṣaṇaṃ
ciṃtāsamaṃ nāsti śarīraśoṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ग्रस्तो ज्वरेण रोगी शीताम्भश्चाम्ललवणशाकमपि
सान्निध्ये यदि त्स्यादविवेकात्क्षुत्तृडाकुलो भोक्ता ।
पाता चैवं विद्वान् स्त्रीधनभोगाद्यभावदाहेन
तप्तः सविधे स्त्रीधनदर्शनतः स्यात् स्वमार्ग विभ्रष्टः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A patient having high fever is lying helpless. Beside him are cold water, sour fruits, salty food and pickles. When he is thirsty or hungry, he will consume them against common knowledge that they are harmful - him being an idiot. Similarly, even scholars who are deprived of wealth and women know that desire of women and wealth is harmful. Yet, when they see these distractions around, they succumb to it and stray from the path of virtue.
quotes.transliteration:
grasto jvareṇa rogī śītāmbhaścāmlalavaṇaśākamapi
sānnidhye yadi tsyādavivekātkṣuttṛḍākulo bhoktā |
pātā caivaṃ vidvān strīdhanabhogādyabhāvadāhena
taptaḥ savidhe strīdhanadarśanataḥ syāt svamārga vibhraṣṭaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न धैर्येण विना लक्ष्मीर्न शौर्येण विना जयः ।
न ज्ञानेन विना मोक्षो न दानेन विना यशः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There can not be wealth without courage, victory without bravery, salvation without knowledge and fame without charity.
quotes.transliteration:
na dhairyeṇa vinā lakṣmīrna śauryeṇa vinā jayaḥ |
na jñānena vinā mokṣo na dānena vinā yaśaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इदं लब्धमिदं नष्टमिदं लप्स्ये पुनर्धिया ।
इति चिंतयतामेव जीर्णमायुः शरीरिणाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Got this, lost that, will think about how to regain that later - people spend their entire lives in such thoughts.
quotes.transliteration:
idaṃ labdhamidaṃ naṣṭamidaṃ lapsye punardhiyā |
iti ciṃtayatāmeva jīrṇamāyuḥ śarīriṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धनिकः श्रोत्रियो राजा नदी वैद्यस्तु पञ्चमः ।
पञ्च यत्र न विद्यन्ते न कुर्यात्तत्र संस्थितिम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not live in a town that does not have these five - rich people (to provide employment), wise men (to consult), king (to maintain order), a river (to provide life) and a doctor.
quotes.transliteration:
dhanikaḥ śrotriyo rājā nadī vaidyastu pañcamaḥ |
pañca yatra na vidyante na kuryāttatra saṃsthitim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्नेहाद्वा यदि वा लोभात् भयाद्ज्ञानतोऽपि वा ।
कुर्वन्यनुग्रहं ये तु तत्पापं तेषु गच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one give a donation (helps someone) out of friendship, fear, greed or ignorance - then the sins of the taker will be attributed to the giver. This is perhaps due to the encouragement given in the form of donation to commit the sin.
quotes.transliteration:
snehādvā yadi vā lobhāt bhayādjñānatoऽpi vā |
kurvanyanugrahaṃ ye tu tatpāpaṃ teṣu gacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रीमद्गृहेतु धात्री तत्पुत्रं पाति लालयते
स्वीयात्स्नेहाधिक्येनत्वपि नैजं न साहि तं मनुते ।
एवंदृढप्रबोधस्तद्वन्मनुजः स्वपुत्र पौत्रादीन्
सस्नेहं पालयतु ज्ञात्वा विश्वस्य तं परं पितरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A nanny in a rich household will look after her master's son with more care than her own children. But the feeling is not real. Just like that, wise men of the world will treat their children and grandchildren with lot of care. But they know that the father of all beings in this world is none other than God himself. Hence it is our duty to take care of them just like the nanny. We are not supposed to develop any attachment towards them.
quotes.transliteration:
śrīmadgṛhetu dhātrī tatputraṃ pāti lālayate
svīyātsnehādhikyenatvapi naijaṃ na sāhi taṃ manute |
evaṃdṛḍhaprabodhastadvanmanujaḥ svaputra pautrādīn
sasnehaṃ pālayatu jñātvā viśvasya taṃ paraṃ pitaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा हि रश्मयोऽश्वस्य द्विरदस्यांकुशो यथा ।
नरेंद्रधर्मो लोकस्य तथा प्रग्रहणं स्मृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One needs ropes and blinds to control a horse. One needs hooks to control an elephant. The king needs to follow his duties (Rajadharma) to control the dealings of the world.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā hi raśmayoऽśvasya dviradasyāṃkuśo yathā |
nareṃdradharmo lokasya tathā pragrahaṇaṃ smṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षमया जय विप्रेंद्रान् दयया जय संश्रितान् ।
तत्त्वबुद्ध्या जयात्मानं तेजसा जय पार्थिवान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Brahmins must be won over by patience, refugees by compassion, self by wisdom and philosophy, other kings by valour and brilliance.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣamayā jaya vipreṃdrān dayayā jaya saṃśritān |
tattvabuddhyā jayātmānaṃ tejasā jaya pārthivān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विरूपो यावदादर्शे नात्मनः पश्यते मुखम् ।
मन्यते तावदात्मानमन्येभ्यो रूपवत्तरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even an ugly looking person believes he is more beautiful than everyone else until he looks at the mirror.
quotes.transliteration:
virūpo yāvadādarśe nātmanaḥ paśyate mukham |
manyate tāvadātmānamanyebhyo rūpavattaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तस्मात्सर्वेषु भूतेषु दयां कुरुत सौहृदं ।
आसुरं भावमुन्मुच्य यया तुष्यत्यधोक्षजः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You should show compassion and love towards all beings. You must shun any demonic qualities. God will be happy then.
quotes.transliteration:
tasmātsarveṣu bhūteṣu dayāṃ kuruta sauhṛdaṃ |
āsuraṃ bhāvamunmucya yayā tuṣyatyadhokṣajaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुर्लभो मानुषो देहो देहिनां क्षणभंगुरः ।
तत्रापि दुर्लभं मन्ये वैकुंठप्रियदर्शनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Getting a human birth is very rare. But the life is also very short. In this short life it is very rare to meet someone who has pure devotion towards God.
quotes.transliteration:
durlabho mānuṣo deho dehināṃ kṣaṇabhaṃguraḥ |
tatrāpi durlabhaṃ manye vaikuṃṭhapriyadarśanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नापृष्टः कस्यचिद्ब्रूयात् न चान्यायेन पृच्छतः ।
जानन्नपि हि मेधावी जडवल्लोक आचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never answer without being asked a question. One must never answer when the question is not posed with correct intentions (illegal, arrogance, etc..). Even after knowing everything, a wise man must act stupid in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
nāpṛṣṭaḥ kasyacidbrūyāt na cānyāyena pṛcchataḥ |
jānannapi hi medhāvī jaḍavalloka ācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परोपकारशून्यस्य धिङ्मनुष्यस्य जीवितम् ।
जीवन्तु पशवो येषां चर्माप्युपक्रिष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Damned let be the person who has not helped anyone in his whole life. Let the animals live instead. They are useful even after their death because of their hide.
quotes.transliteration:
paropakāraśūnyasya dhiṅmanuṣyasya jīvitam |
jīvantu paśavo yeṣāṃ carmāpyupakriṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सकृत् कंदुकपातेन पतत्यार्यः पतन्नपि ।
तथा पतति मूर्खास्तु मृत्पिण्डपतनं यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If and when a virtuous person falls down, he will bounce back like a ball. When a fool falls down, it will be like a ball made of mud.
quotes.transliteration:
sakṛt kaṃdukapātena patatyāryaḥ patannapi |
tathā patati mūrkhāstu mṛtpiṇḍapatanaṃ yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उत्तमोऽप्यधमस्य स्याद्याज्ञानम्रकरः क्वचित् ।
कौस्तुभादीनि रत्नानि ययाचे हरिरंभुधिम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sometimes even the virtuous people will have spread their hands asking for favours from lowly persons. Didn't Lord Vishnu have to ask the Lord of Seas for gems like Kaustubha?
quotes.transliteration:
uttamoऽpyadhamasya syādyājñānamrakaraḥ kvacit |
kaustubhādīni ratnāni yayāce hariraṃbhudhim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गतार्थान्नानुशोचन्ति नार्थयन्ते मनोरथान् ।
वर्तमानेन वर्तन्ते तेन मे पाण्डवाः प्रियाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: They do not long for what they have lost. They do not desire for what they don't have. They are happy with whatever they have at the moment. So, I like the Pandavas (as told by Krishna)
quotes.transliteration:
gatārthānnānuśocanti nārthayante manorathān |
vartamānena vartante tena me pāṇḍavāḥ priyāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नानृषिः कुरुते काव्यं नारुद्रः क्रमपाठकः ।
नादेवांशो ददात्यन्नं नाविष्णुः पृथिवीपतिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only people who are sages (in parts) can be poets. Only people with qualities of Rudra can chant vedas. Only people who have godly qualities will be able to give food to others. Only people with qualities of Vishnu can rule and be good kings.
quotes.transliteration:
nānṛṣiḥ kurute kāvyaṃ nārudraḥ kramapāṭhakaḥ |
nādevāṃśo dadātyannaṃ nāviṣṇuḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदीच्छसि वशीकर्तुं जगदेकेन कर्मणा ।
परापवादसस्येभ्यो गां चरन्तीं निवारय ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you intend to conquer the entire world with one action, that action should be to control the cow that is tempted to eat the grass of slander. Control your mind for giving in to the temptation to tall ill about others.
quotes.transliteration:
yadīcchasi vaśīkartuṃ jagadekena karmaṇā |
parāpavādasasyebhyo gāṃ carantīṃ nivāraya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
काकमांसं शुनोच्छिष्टं स्वल्पं तदपि दुर्लभम् ।
भक्षितेनापि किं तेन तृप्तिर्येन न जायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is meat from a crow. Left over after a dog has feasted on it. It is very little. Even that is not easy to get. If I eat that, it will not even satisfy the slightest hunger. Why should I even eat it if it is not able to quench any hunger? At a higher level of consciouses, all worldly pleasures seem too insignificant as this.
quotes.transliteration:
kākamāṃsaṃ śunocchiṣṭaṃ svalpaṃ tadapi durlabham |
bhakṣitenāpi kiṃ tena tṛptiryena na jāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चत्वारो धनदायादाः धर्माग्निनृपतस्कराः ।
तेषां ज्येष्ठावमानेन त्रयः कुप्यंति सोदराः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharma, fire, king and thieves are brothers. They are the cousins of wealth. If the eldest of the brothers is humiliated (by wealth) then the other three will destroy wealth to take revenge. Ergo: wealth must be spent by never viloating Dharma, the moral code of life.
quotes.transliteration:
catvāro dhanadāyādāḥ dharmāgninṛpataskarāḥ |
teṣāṃ jyeṣṭhāvamānena trayaḥ kupyaṃti sodarāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वैराग्यमिव वृद्धानामौचित्यं महतामिव |
शुचिशीलमिवार्याणां सत्यं राज्ञां विभूषणम् ||
quotes.englishTranslation: To be free from worldly desires is an old man's duty. To be humble is the duty of the virtuous. To be clean and chaste is the duty of any person who expects respect. To be truthful to his responsibilities is the fore most duty of a king.
quotes.transliteration:
vairāgyamiva vṛddhānāmaucityaṃ mahatāmiva |
śuciśīlamivāryāṇāṃ satyaṃ rājñāṃ vibhūṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनस्वी म्रियते कामं कार्पण्यं न तु गच्छति ।
अपि निर्वाणमायाति नानलो याति शीतताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Any person with self respect would prefer death to humiliation (or living thus). Fire will cease to exist, but never get cold.
quotes.transliteration:
manasvī mriyate kāmaṃ kārpaṇyaṃ na tu gacchati |
api nirvāṇamāyāti nānalo yāti śītatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तावद्भयं द्रविणगेहसुहृन्निमित्तं
शोकः स्पृहा परिभवो विपुलश्च लोभः ।
तावन्ममेत्यसदवग्रह आर्तिमूलं
यावन्न तेंऽघ्रिमभयं प्रवृणीत लोकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: tAvat mameti asat avagraha ArtimUlam Until the day people don't believe fearlessly in God and worship him, they will suffer in the clutches of 'attachment' towards wealth, house, relatives and friends which is the root cause of all misery like fear, grief, greed, humiliation, greed and desire.
quotes.transliteration:
tāvadbhayaṃ draviṇagehasuhṛnnimittaṃ
śokaḥ spṛhā paribhavo vipulaśca lobhaḥ |
tāvanmametyasadavagraha ārtimūlaṃ
yāvanna teṃऽghrimabhayaṃ pravṛṇīta lokaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जिता सभा वस्त्रवता मिष्टाशा गोमता जिता ।
अध्वा जितो यनवता सर्वं शीलवता जितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A well dressed man has the audience in his control. A man with a cow is in control of when he can have sweets. If you have a vehicle, you have the path under your control. A man of good character can control everything.
quotes.transliteration:
jitā sabhā vastravatā miṣṭāśā gomatā jitā |
adhvā jito yanavatā sarvaṃ śīlavatā jitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गृहारंभो हि दुःखाय न सुखाय कदाचन ।
सर्पः परकृतं वेश्म प्रविश्य सुखमेधते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Starting a home (and family) always leads to pain and suffering. It never leads to (permanent) happiness. Ants that build the anthill are always under tension (due to attachment). A snake that takes over their home spends its time happily.
quotes.transliteration:
gṛhāraṃbho hi duḥkhāya na sukhāya kadācana |
sarpaḥ parakṛtaṃ veśma praviśya sukhamedhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृत्युर्जन्मवतां वीर देहेन सह जायते ।
अद्यवाब्दशतांते वा मृत्युर्वै प्राणिनां ध्रुवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Be brave. Death is certain for every being that takes birth. It is born along with the being. It may happen right now or after a hundred years. But it is certain.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtyurjanmavatāṃ vīra dehena saha jāyate |
adyavābdaśatāṃte vā mṛtyurvai prāṇināṃ dhruvaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निमित्तमुद्दिश्य हि यः प्रकुप्यति
ध्रुवं स तस्यापगमे प्रशाम्यति ।
अकारणद्वेषपरोहि यो भवेत्
कथं नरस्तं परितोषयिष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: nimittam uddeshya hi yaH prakupyati When one's anger has a specific source (reason), then the anger subsides naturally when the reason no longer exists. But if one is indulged in meaningless hatred then how can we find ways to placate him?
quotes.transliteration:
nimittamuddiśya hi yaḥ prakupyati
dhruvaṃ sa tasyāpagame praśāmyati |
akāraṇadveṣaparohi yo bhavet
kathaṃ narastaṃ paritoṣayiṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्याधेरनिष्टसंस्पर्शात् श्रमादिष्टविवर्जनात् ।
दुःख चतुर्भिः शारीरं कारणैः संप्रवर्तते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Physical pain is caused by four stimuli - disease, bad luck, exhaustion (over working) or by separation from something you love.
quotes.transliteration:
vyādheraniṣṭasaṃsparśāt śramādiṣṭavivarjanāt |
duḥkha caturbhiḥ śārīraṃ kāraṇaiḥ saṃpravartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देहे पातिनि का रक्षा यशो रक्ष्यमपातवत् ।
नरः पातितकायोऽपि यशःकायेन जीवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What can one achieve by protecting the body that is bound to get destroyed? If you want to protect anything, it must be your fame. A person lives long due to his fame - long after the body is dead.
quotes.transliteration:
dehe pātini kā rakṣā yaśo rakṣyamapātavat |
naraḥ pātitakāyoऽpi yaśaḥkāyena jīvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुग्रामवासः कुजनस्य सेवा
कुभोजनं क्रोधमुखी च भार्या ।
मूर्खश्च पुत्रो विधवा च कन्या
दहन्ति चैतानि जनं विनाग्निम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These things will burn a man alive without any fire - living in a bad village, serving a bad master, a bad meal, an angry wife, idiot son, widow daughter.
quotes.transliteration:
kugrāmavāsaḥ kujanasya sevā
kubhojanaṃ krodhamukhī ca bhāryā |
mūrkhaśca putro vidhavā ca kanyā
dahanti caitāni janaṃ vināgnim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महाजनस्य संपर्कः कस्य नॊन्नतिकारकः ।
पद्मपत्रस्थितं तॊयं धत्ते मुक्ताफलश्रियम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who will not benefit by the company of the virtuous? A drop of water on a lotus leaves will attain the shine of a pearl.
