quotes.transliteration:
mahājanasya saṃparkaḥ kasya naॊnnatikārakaḥ |
padmapatrasthitaṃ taॊyaṃ dhatte muktāphalaśriyam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकोऽहमस्मीति च मन्यसे त्वं
न हृच्छयं वेत्सि मुनिं पुराणम् ।
यो वेदिता कर्मणः पापकस्य
तस्यांतिके त्वं वृजिनं करोषि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: eko aham asmi iti cha manyase tvam You are thinking that you are acting alone and there are no witnesses for your action. But you are forgetting about the greatest ancient saint who resides in your heart (God). He keeps track of all your actions and sins associated with them. How can you commit such sins in his presence?
quotes.transliteration:
ekoऽhamasmīti ca manyase tvaṃ
na hṛcchayaṃ vetsi muniṃ purāṇam |
yo veditā karmaṇaḥ pāpakasya
tasyāṃtike tvaṃ vṛjinaṃ karoṣi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पदस्था विषमस्थानां मित्राणां न भवन्ति ये ।
आयासिता तैर्जननी गर्भभारेण केवलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who do not help their friends in distress in spite of occupying high positions of power as useless alive. They only troubled their mothers by burdening her during pregnancy. There is no other achievement during their lives.
quotes.transliteration:
padasthā viṣamasthānāṃ mitrāṇāṃ na bhavanti ye |
āyāsitā tairjananī garbhabhāreṇa kevalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्याकुलोऽपि विपत्पाशैः स्मरेद्विष्णुं सदा हृदि ।
शरतल्पगतो भीष्मः सस्मार गरुडध्वजम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one is anxious, or entangled in lot of troubles - he should continue to think of Vishnu in his heart. That will help him get through it without any problems. Even when Bheeshma was laid to rest on a bed of arrows, he was still thinking of Garudadhvaja (one with eagle flag = Vishnu).
quotes.transliteration:
vyākuloऽpi vipatpāśaiḥ smaredviṣṇuṃ sadā hṛdi |
śaratalpagato bhīṣmaḥ sasmāra garuḍadhvajam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शौर्ये तपसि दाने च यस्य न प्रथितं यशः ।
विद्यायामर्थलाभे वा मातुरुच्चार एव सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must have attained fame in any of the below - courage, penance, charity, education or accumulation of wealth. If one is not able to do so, then his life is a waste - as if his mother gave birth to just a lump of flesh.
quotes.transliteration:
śaurye tapasi dāne ca yasya na prathitaṃ yaśaḥ |
vidyāyāmarthalābhe vā māturuccāra eva saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आरोग्यं विद्वत्ता सज्जनमैत्री महाकुले जन्म ।
स्वाधीनता च पुंसां महदैश्वर्यं विनाप्यर्थैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Health, wisdom, friendship of virtuous people, birth in a noble family, personal freedom - these are the wealth that one can get even without any money.
quotes.transliteration:
ārogyaṃ vidvattā sajjanamaitrī mahākule janma |
svādhīnatā ca puṃsāṃ mahadaiśvaryaṃ vināpyarthaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न यस्य चेष्टितं विद्यान्न कुलं न पराक्रमम् ।
न तस्य विश्वसेत्प्राज्ञः यदीच्छेच्छ्रियमात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man who is interested in protecting his wealth will not believe a person unless he knows something about the person - education, family or past valour.
quotes.transliteration:
na yasya ceṣṭitaṃ vidyānna kulaṃ na parākramam |
na tasya viśvasetprājñaḥ yadīcchecchriyamātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नवे वयसि यः शान्तः स शान्त इति मे मतिः ।
धातुषु क्षीयमाणेषु शान्तिः कस्य न जायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: I consider a person as calm only when he is calm at a young age. When one is old and capabilities diminished, who is not calm?
quotes.transliteration:
nave vayasi yaḥ śāntaḥ sa śānta iti me matiḥ |
dhātuṣu kṣīyamāṇeṣu śāntiḥ kasya na jāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विभूतिस्त्यागहीनेव सत्यहीनेव भारति ।
विद्या प्रशमहीनेव न भाति स्त्री पतिं विना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth is incomplete if one does not have a charitable mind. Speech is incomplete and useless if it is not truthful. Education is incomplete unless it helps you subdue your emotion. A lady without her husband is similarly incomplete.
quotes.transliteration:
vibhūtistyāgahīneva satyahīneva bhārati |
vidyā praśamahīneva na bhāti strī patiṃ vinā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दूरादतिथयो यस्य गृहमायांति निर्वृताः ।
गृहस्थः स तु विज्ञेयः शेषास्तु गृहरक्षिणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If guests come from far off places and spend time happily with you, only then can you be called as a good household man. The rest of the men are just guarding the house.
quotes.transliteration:
dūrādatithayo yasya gṛhamāyāṃti nirvṛtāḥ |
gṛhasthaḥ sa tu vijñeyaḥ śeṣāstu gṛharakṣiṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न कुलं वृत्तहीनश्च प्रमाणमिति मे मतिः ।
अंतेष्वपि हि जातानां वृत्तमेव विशिष्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The family of a person with lose character can not be trusted. Even a person of low birth can be trusted if he is of good character.
quotes.transliteration:
na kulaṃ vṛttahīnaśca pramāṇamiti me matiḥ |
aṃteṣvapi hi jātānāṃ vṛttameva viśiṣyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अहो साहजिकं प्रेम दूरादपि विराजते ।
चकोरनयनद्वन्द्वमाह्लादयति चन्द्रमाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When love is natural, distance does not matter. The moon and the chakora bird enjoy just watching each other.
quotes.transliteration:
aho sāhajikaṃ prema dūrādapi virājate |
cakoranayanadvandvamāhlādayati candramāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
महानप्यल्पतां याति निर्गुणे गुणविस्तरः ।
आधाराधेयभावेन गजेंद्र इव दर्पणे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a thing with great qualities will look common if viewed from a narrow/lowly point of view. The object inherits the property of the medium. When seen in a mirror, even an elephant looks small.
quotes.transliteration:
mahānapyalpatāṃ yāti nirguṇe guṇavistaraḥ |
ādhārādheyabhāvena gajeṃdra iva darpaṇe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वो दण्डजितो लोके दुर्लभो हि शुचिर्नरः ।
दण्डस्य हि भयात्सर्वं जगत्भोगाय कल्पते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Order is preserved in this world by punishment. It is very rare to find people who are clean by themselves. Punishment preserves the order so that we can enjoy fearlessly in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvo daṇḍajito loke durlabho hi śucirnaraḥ |
daṇḍasya hi bhayātsarvaṃ jagatbhogāya kalpate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्वौ सम्मताविह मृत्यू दुरापौ
यद् ब्रह्मसंधारणया जितासुः ।
कलेवरं योगरतो विजह्याद्
यद्ग्रणीर्वीरशयेऽनिवृत्तः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kalevaram yogarato vijahyAd There are two difficult ways of dying honourably. Both are very difficult. One is to be able to reconcile with the supreme being and leave this body as is and die by performing Yoga (that enables you to control your breath). The other is to be in the fore-front of an army and never return from battlefield.
quotes.transliteration:
dvau sammatāviha mṛtyū durāpau
yad brahmasaṃdhāraṇayā jitāsuḥ |
kalevaraṃ yogarato vijahyād
yadgraṇīrvīraśayeऽnivṛttaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुभं वाप्यशुभं कर्म फलकालमपेक्षते ।
शरदैव फलत्याशु शालिर्न सुरभौ क्वचित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Be it auspicious or not - results will come only when the time is right. Paddy grows well in sharat season(when there is no rain) and not when it is vasanta season(spring, when everything else blooms).
quotes.transliteration:
śubhaṃ vāpyaśubhaṃ karma phalakālamapekṣate |
śaradaiva phalatyāśu śālirna surabhau kvacit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आरंभ गुर्वी क्षयिणी क्रमेण
लुब्ध्वि पुर्व वृद्धिमति च पश्चात् ।
दिनस्य पुर्वार्ध परार्ध बिन्न
छायैव मैत्रि खलु सज्जनानम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wicked person's friendship is too huge at start and slowly shrinks. But a good person's friendship is small to start and grows with time. Their difference is just like shadows at morning and evening times.
quotes.transliteration:
āraṃbha gurvī kṣayiṇī krameṇa
lubdhvi purva vṛddhimati ca paścāt |
dinasya purvārdha parārdha binna
chāyaiva maitri khalu sajjanānam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वर्णप्रकर्षे सति कर्णिकारं
दुनोति निर्गंधतया स्म चेतः ।
प्रायेण सामग्र्यविधौ गुणानां
पराङ्मुखी विश्व सृजः प्रवृत्तिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The flowers of golden shower tree are bright in color, but disappoint due to the lack of fragrance. It is the nature of this Creator that he does not put in effort to make one thing good in all respects. He intentionally leaves out a few qualities in anything he makes.
quotes.transliteration:
varṇaprakarṣe sati karṇikāraṃ
dunoti nirgaṃdhatayā sma cetaḥ |
prāyeṇa sāmagryavidhau guṇānāṃ
parāṅmukhī viśva sṛjaḥ pravṛttiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृदुमप्यवमन्यंते तीक्ष्णा दुद्विजते जनः ।
मा तीक्ष्णो मा मृदुर्भूस्त्वं तीक्ष्णो भव मृदुर्भव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not be too soft. People will ridicule them. One must not be too aggressive. People will be anxious around them. One must not be too soft or too aggressive always. One must be soft and aggressive as required.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛdumapyavamanyaṃte tīkṣṇā dudvijate janaḥ |
mā tīkṣṇo mā mṛdurbhūstvaṃ tīkṣṇo bhava mṛdurbhava ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गृहकार्ये संलग्नाः स्वान्ते सञ्चिन्तयन्ति परपुरुषम् ।
जारिण्य इव कुरुध्वे हृदिपरमेशं बहिः कार्यम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Prostitutes have a very efficient way of multitasking. While they do all the housework, in their mind they are thinking about other men. Be like them. Always think about God at all times. Outwardly, take part in all the work of this world.
quotes.transliteration:
gṛhakārye saṃlagnāḥ svānte sañcintayanti parapuruṣam |
jāriṇya iva kurudhve hṛdiparameśaṃ bahiḥ kāryam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दरिद्रो यस्त्वसंतुष्टः कृपणो योऽजितेन्द्रियः ।
गुणेष्वसक्तधीरीशो गुणसङ्गो विपर्ययः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who is poor - he who is not satisfied. Who is pitiable - who has not overcome his senses. Who is the king - he whose mind does not run behind material pleasures. Running behind pleasures is a sure path to destruction.
quotes.transliteration:
daridro yastvasaṃtuṣṭaḥ kṛpaṇo yoऽjitendriyaḥ |
guṇeṣvasaktadhīrīśo guṇasaṅgo viparyayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नीतिज्ञा नियतिज्ञा वेदज्ञा अपि भवन्ति शास्त्रज्ञाः ।
ब्रह्मज्ञा अपि लभ्याः स्वाज्ञानज्ञानिनो विरलाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You can find people who know it all about morality, future, the vedas. You can even find people who know about Brahman - the creator. But it is very rare to find people who will know about their own ignorance.
quotes.transliteration:
nītijñā niyatijñā vedajñā api bhavanti śāstrajñāḥ |
brahmajñā api labhyāḥ svājñānajñānino viralāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुर्मंत्रिणं कमुपयान्ति न नीतिदोषाः
संतापयन्ति कमपथ्यभुजं न रोगाः ।
कं श्रीर्न दर्पयति कं न निहन्ति मृत्युः
कं स्त्रीकृता न विषयाः परितापयन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Which bad minister is immune to the after effects of his ill advice? Who does not fall sick after eating bad food? Who does not become arrogant when he becomes rich? Who does death spare? Who does not get agitated (excited) by the pleasure a woman can give.
quotes.transliteration:
durmaṃtriṇaṃ kamupayānti na nītidoṣāḥ
saṃtāpayanti kamapathyabhujaṃ na rogāḥ |
kaṃ śrīrna darpayati kaṃ na nihanti mṛtyuḥ
kaṃ strīkṛtā na viṣayāḥ paritāpayanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्यवस्थितः प्रशांतात्मा कुपितोऽप्यभयंकरः ।
अव्यवस्थितचित्तस्य प्रसादोऽपि भयंकरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a usually calm and collected person gets angry - it is still not that fearsome. One can expect him to be rational and control his anger. But if one sees inconsistent fellow bestow favors, he must get worried. Because such people could turn around any time and hurt you.
quotes.transliteration:
vyavasthitaḥ praśāṃtātmā kupitoऽpyabhayaṃkaraḥ |
avyavasthitacittasya prasādoऽpi bhayaṃkaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनीषिणः संति न ते हितैषिणो
हितैषिणः संति न ते मनीषिणः ।
सुहृच्च विद्वानपि दुर्लभो नृणां
यथौषधं स्वादु हितं च दुर्लभम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are wise men. But they are not my well wishers. There are a lot of well wishers. But they are not wise. It is difficult in this world to find a person who is both wise and a well wisher - just like it is hard to find a medicine that also tastes good.
quotes.transliteration:
manīṣiṇaḥ saṃti na te hitaiṣiṇo
hitaiṣiṇaḥ saṃti na te manīṣiṇaḥ |
suhṛcca vidvānapi durlabho nṛṇāṃ
yathauṣadhaṃ svādu hitaṃ ca durlabham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कस्यैकांतं सुखमुपनतं दुःखमेकांततो वा ।
नीचैर्गच्छत्युपरि च दशा चक्रनेमिक्रमेण ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who in this world can live always in good fortune or always in misfortune? Fortune of a person is like a band on a wheel - it goes up and comes down alternately in time.
quotes.transliteration:
kasyaikāṃtaṃ sukhamupanataṃ duḥkhamekāṃtato vā |
nīcairgacchatyupari ca daśā cakranemikrameṇa ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्यसनानंतरं सौख्यं स्वल्पमप्यधिकं भवेत् ।
काषायरसमास्वाद्य स्वाद्वतीवांबु विंदते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That sweetness that follows misery will look like a lot even when it is little. After drinking a bitter medicine, even plain water seems sweet.
quotes.transliteration:
vyasanānaṃtaraṃ saukhyaṃ svalpamapyadhikaṃ bhavet |
kāṣāyarasamāsvādya svādvatīvāṃbu viṃdate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जटिलो मुण्डी लुंचितकेशः
काषायांबरबहुकृतवेषः ।
पश्यन्नपि च न पश्यति मूढः
उदरनिमित्तं बहुकृतवेषः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: pashyannapi cha na pashyati mUDhaH You see mendicants invarious forms - with matted hair, shaven head, hair removed by plucking, wearing saffron robes and so on. These are all people who would not know even if they saw the truth right in front of their eyes. They are having various forms only to fill their stomach.
quotes.transliteration:
jaṭilo muṇḍī luṃcitakeśaḥ
kāṣāyāṃbarabahukṛtaveṣaḥ |
paśyannapi ca na paśyati mūḍhaḥ
udaranimittaṃ bahukṛtaveṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कस्मादिंदुरसौ धिनोति जगतीं पीयूषगर्भैः करैः
कस्माद्वा जलधारयैष धरणीं धाराधरः सिंचति ।
भ्रामं भ्राममयं च नंदयति वा कस्मात्त्रिलोकीं रविः
साधूनां हि परोपकारकरणे नोपाध्यपेक्षं मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Why does the moon grace the earth with his pleasant rays? Why do the coulds shower plesant rain on the ground to help us? Why does the Sun go round and round every day to light up all three worlds? All virtuous people never expect anything in return while helping others.
quotes.transliteration:
kasmādiṃdurasau dhinoti jagatīṃ pīyūṣagarbhaiḥ karaiḥ
kasmādvā jaladhārayaiṣa dharaṇīṃ dhārādharaḥ siṃcati |
bhrāmaṃ bhrāmamayaṃ ca naṃdayati vā kasmāttrilokīṃ raviḥ
sādhūnāṃ hi paropakārakaraṇe nopādhyapekṣaṃ manaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वभावसुंदरं वस्तु न संस्कारमपेक्षते ।
मुक्तारत्नस्य शाणाश्मघर्षणं नोपयुज्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That which is inherently beautiful does not expect to be polished by customs and propriety. A pearl does not need scrubbing against a stone to make it shine.
quotes.transliteration:
svabhāvasuṃdaraṃ vastu na saṃskāramapekṣate |
muktāratnasya śāṇāśmagharṣaṇaṃ nopayujyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रकाशयति दीपको भुवि पदार्थजातंतथाप्यधस्थ-
तिमिरं महन्नहि विनाशमागच्छति ।
महात्मनिकटस्थिताज्ञसुतदारकाणां महात्मनो-
महिमवेदनं न खलु हृद्यबन्धापहम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A lamp is capable of throwing light on any object and drive away the darkness. But it can not make the darkness right below it go away at all. Similarly, a scholar may teach the entire world to renounce and let go of attachments. But it is usually seen that people who are close him - wife, sons (children) and servants will not be able to let go of their attachments towards him.
quotes.transliteration:
prakāśayati dīpako bhuvi padārthajātaṃtathāpyadhastha-
timiraṃ mahannahi vināśamāgacchati |
mahātmanikaṭasthitājñasutadārakāṇāṃ mahātmano-
mahimavedanaṃ na khalu hṛdyabandhāpaham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वयमाहृत्य भुञ्जानाः बलिनोऽपि स्वभावतः ।
गजेन्द्रश्च नरेन्द्रश्च प्रायः सीदन्ति दुःखिताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Elephants and Kings have some qualities in common. They are very strong and self made. They both lose weight (become weak) when they are unhappy (in trouble).
quotes.transliteration:
svayamāhṛtya bhuñjānāḥ balinoऽpi svabhāvataḥ |
gajendraśca narendraśca prāyaḥ sīdanti duḥkhitāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निकटस्थं गरीयांसमपि लोको न मन्यते ।
पवित्रमपि यन्मर्त्या न नमस्यन्ति जाह्नवीं ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When something great is right beside and readily available, people will not pay attention. People on the banks of Ganga do not worship her every day.
quotes.transliteration:
nikaṭasthaṃ garīyāṃsamapi loko na manyate |
pavitramapi yanmartyā na namasyanti jāhnavīṃ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ननु दीपः स्वमात्मानं स्वात्मनैवावभासयन् ।
दृष्टो दीपप्रकाशार्थं न हि दीपांतराहृतिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A lamp glows by itself. One does not need some other light to look for a lamp. Virtues of noble men are similar. They stand for themselves without any need for endorsements.
quotes.transliteration:
nanu dīpaḥ svamātmānaṃ svātmanaivāvabhāsayan |
dṛṣṭo dīpaprakāśārthaṃ na hi dīpāṃtarāhṛtiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निस्सर्पे बद्धसर्पे वा वस्तुं हि भवने सुखं ।
दृष्टनष्टभुजंगे तु निद्रा दुःखेन गम्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is easy to live in a house where there is no snake or the snake has been captured. But is is very difficult to sleep peacefully when you have seen a snake and it is has disappeared later.
quotes.transliteration:
nissarpe baddhasarpe vā vastuṃ hi bhavane sukhaṃ |
dṛṣṭanaṣṭabhujaṃge tu nidrā duḥkhena gamyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विष्णुपदेऽतिपवित्रे द्रष्टुं गृध्रः सदोर्ध्वमाभ्रमति
क्षोण्यामेव जुगुप्सितमांसं कुत्रास्ति तद्वदिह ।
वाक्पाटवेन चकितान् श्रुतॄन्कुर्वन्नगाध-बोध इव
दृष्टिं कनकयुतायां कान्तायां पण्डितो विषयतृष्णक् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: An eagle resides at the extremely pure feet Lord Vishnu. He always flies high in the sky. Yet, even at that distance his eyes are set on the rotting flesh way down on the ground. Similarly, a scholar may be giving out discourses imparting the best knowledge he has to general public. If he is still not let go of his greed, his eyes will be set on the beautiful women in the audience who wear a lot of golden ornaments.
quotes.transliteration:
viṣṇupadeऽtipavitre draṣṭuṃ gṛdhraḥ sadordhvamābhramati
kṣoṇyāmeva jugupsitamāṃsaṃ kutrāsti tadvadiha |
vākpāṭavena cakitān śrutṝnkurvannagādha-bodha iva
dṛṣṭiṃ kanakayutāyāṃ kāntāyāṃ paṇḍito viṣayatṛṣṇak ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तीक्ष्णधारेण खड्गेन वरं जिह्वा द्विधा कृता ।
न तु मानं परित्यज्य देहि देहीति भाषणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is better to rip your tongue with a sharp knife than losing self respect and uttering "Give me! Give me!". Never plead for alms.
quotes.transliteration:
tīkṣṇadhāreṇa khaḍgena varaṃ jihvā dvidhā kṛtā |
na tu mānaṃ parityajya dehi dehīti bhāṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यं सत्यं मुनेर्वाक्यं नादत्तमुपतिष्ठते ।
अंबुभिः पूरिता पृथ्वी चातकस्य मरुस्थली ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very true what the ancient sages have spoken - What is not be yours, you will never get it. Even when the earth is so abundant with water, for the chataka bird it is as good as a desert. Chataka (Cucculus melanoleucus) is said to subsist only on rain water freshly poured from the clouds drinking it before it reaches the ground.
quotes.transliteration:
satyaṃ satyaṃ munervākyaṃ nādattamupatiṣṭhate |
aṃbubhiḥ pūritā pṛthvī cātakasya marusthalī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धन्या बधिरा अंधास्त एव जीवन्ति मानुषे लोके ।
न शृण्वन्ति पिशुनजनं खलानां ॠद्धिं न प्रेक्षन्ते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Blessed are the deaf and the blind who live in this world. They don't have to listen to the rumormongers and see the prosperity of the wicked.
quotes.transliteration:
dhanyā badhirā aṃdhāsta eva jīvanti mānuṣe loke |
na śṛṇvanti piśunajanaṃ khalānāṃ ṝddhiṃ na prekṣante ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षंतव्यो मंदबुद्धीनामपराधो मनीषिणा ।
न हि सर्वत्र पांडित्यं सुलभं पुरुषे क्वचित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man must forgive mistakes done by lesser intelligent people. It is not possible to find wisdom every where. It can only be found in some places.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣaṃtavyo maṃdabuddhīnāmaparādho manīṣiṇā |
na hi sarvatra pāṃḍityaṃ sulabhaṃ puruṣe kvacit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदि स्याद् गृहे माता पत्नी वा पतिदेवता ।
व्यंगे रथ इव प्राज्ञः को नामासीत दीनवत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A household without a mother or a chaste wife is crippled like a broken chariot. It makes one truly miserable. Who in their right mind would continue to live in that?
quotes.transliteration:
yadi syād gṛhe mātā patnī vā patidevatā |
vyaṃge ratha iva prājñaḥ ko nāmāsīta dīnavat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पंथा देयो ब्राह्मणाय गवे राज्ञे ह्यचक्षुषे ।
वृद्धाय भारतप्ताय गर्भिण्ये दुर्बलाय च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must stand aside and leave way for a brahmin, cows, king, blind, old people, people carrying weight, pregnant women and weak.
quotes.transliteration:
paṃthā deyo brāhmaṇāya gave rājñe hyacakṣuṣe |
vṛddhāya bhārataptāya garbhiṇye durbalāya ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ऐश्वर्यादनपेतमीश्वरमयं लोकोऽर्थतः सेवते
तं गच्छंत्यनु ये विपत्तिषु पुनस्ते तत्प्रतिष्ठाशया ।
भर्तुये प्रलयेऽपि पूर्वसुकृतासंगेन निःसंगया
भक्त्या कार्यधुरं वहन्ति भवस्ते दुर्लभास्त्वादृशाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are many who will serve the king when he is prosperous looking to gain some wealth in the process. There are a few who will serve him when he is dethroned in hope that when he regains power, he will be able to help them. But very rare (non-existent) are those people who will still work for their kings (his fame) long after the king is dead. They do it only because they remember the good deeds their king did.
quotes.transliteration:
aiśvaryādanapetamīśvaramayaṃ lokoऽrthataḥ sevate
taṃ gacchaṃtyanu ye vipattiṣu punaste tatpratiṣṭhāśayā |
bhartuye pralayeऽpi pūrvasukṛtāsaṃgena niḥsaṃgayā
bhaktyā kāryadhuraṃ vahanti bhavaste durlabhāstvādṛśāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नातंत्री वाद्यते वीणा नाचक्रो वर्तते रथः ।
नापतिः सुखमेधेत या स्यादपि शतात्मजा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A veena can not play melodiously without the strings. A chariot can not move without the wheels. A wife can not live happily without her man (after his death) even if she has hundred children.
quotes.transliteration:
nātaṃtrī vādyate vīṇā nācakro vartate rathaḥ |
nāpatiḥ sukhamedheta yā syādapi śatātmajā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वर्धत्यधर्मेण नरस्ततो भद्राणि पश्यति ।
ततः सपत्नान् जयति समूलस्तु विनश्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: By leaving the path of morality, one can prosper, find himself safe and secured, triumph over all his enemies. But one day, he shall be destroyed completely - from root to tip.
quotes.transliteration:
vardhatyadharmeṇa narastato bhadrāṇi paśyati |
tataḥ sapatnān jayati samūlastu vinaśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न हि पापानि कर्माणि शुद्ध्यंत्यनशनादिभिः ।
सीदत्यनशनादेव मांसशोणितलेपनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One's sins will not be washed away by rituals like fasting. Only thing that is achieved by fasting is wearing of the body made of flesh and blood.
quotes.transliteration:
na hi pāpāni karmāṇi śuddhyaṃtyanaśanādibhiḥ |
sīdatyanaśanādeva māṃsaśoṇitalepanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कान्तापुत्राभावे प्रसह्य मोहेन कस्यचित्पुत्रम्
श्वानं शुकं हरिं वा सम्पाल्याप्यात्मबन्धनं कुरुते ।
कुरुते न चात्मबन्धोः कथारतिं श्री हरेर्विषय दृष्टिः
दुग्धतृषं तक्रेण हि मायावशतः सुनिपुणोऽपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When people are free from wife and children, they are free from all bondages. But such people will create more bondages with somebody else's children, or a dog, or a parrot, or a monkey. They will not create bondage with God by listening to the stories of Vishnu and Lakshmi. This is like trying to quench the thirst for milk by drinking curd. It is surprising that even learned and knowledgeable people do this.
quotes.transliteration:
kāntāputrābhāve prasahya mohena kasyacitputram
śvānaṃ śukaṃ hariṃ vā sampālyāpyātmabandhanaṃ kurute |
kurute na cātmabandhoḥ kathāratiṃ śrī harerviṣaya dṛṣṭiḥ
dugdhatṛṣaṃ takreṇa hi māyāvaśataḥ sunipuṇoऽpi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कृपणेन समो दाता न भूतो न भविष्यति ।
अस्पृशन्नेव वित्तानि यः परेभ्यः प्रयच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There has never been any one more charitable than a miser. There will never be one. Who else can donate his entire wealth to others without ever touching it?
quotes.transliteration:
kṛpaṇena samo dātā na bhūto na bhaviṣyati |
aspṛśanneva vittāni yaḥ parebhyaḥ prayacchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणगणनारंभे पतति न कठिनी सुसंभ्रमा यस्य ।
तेनांबा यदि सुतिनी वद वंध्या कीदृशी भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one starts counting good qualities, if out finger does not point enthusiastically at someone then what can we say about his mother. If she is a mother indeed, then who can be called as 'childless'? Ergo, having a son without good qualities is equivalent to not having a son (or worse).
quotes.transliteration:
guṇagaṇanāraṃbhe patati na kaṭhinī susaṃbhramā yasya |
tenāṃbā yadi sutinī vada vaṃdhyā kīdṛśī bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कवितस्य गांभीर्यमौदार्यमृद्धेः
प्रभुत्वस्य शौर्यं गुरुत्वस्य विद्यां ।
महावंश्यतायाः सदाचारपूर्तिं
महत्त्वस्य सौलभ्यमाकल्पमाहुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A poem is well decorated only when it is pregnant with deep meaning. A charitable mind decorates a wealthy person. Valour decorates a person in power. Knowledge decorates a teacher. A good character decorates a person born in a good family. A great person is decorated by being accessible to everyone.
quotes.transliteration:
kavitasya gāṃbhīryamaudāryamṛddheḥ
prabhutvasya śauryaṃ gurutvasya vidyāṃ |
mahāvaṃśyatāyāḥ sadācārapūrtiṃ
mahattvasya saulabhyamākalpamāhuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रीणामार्यस्वभावानां परमं दैवतं पतिः ।
नातो विशिष्टं पश्यामि बांधवं विमृशंत्यहम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wife with good culture will treat her husband no less than God. I have analyzed in many ways. There is no one more intimate to a wife than her husband - not even God.
quotes.transliteration:
strīṇāmāryasvabhāvānāṃ paramaṃ daivataṃ patiḥ |
nāto viśiṣṭaṃ paśyāmi bāṃdhavaṃ vimṛśaṃtyaham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ईहंते सर्वभूतानि तदिदं कर्मसंज्ञितम् ।
सिद्धिक्षेत्रमिदं पुण्यमयमेवाश्रमो महान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All the beings of the world depend on this state of man - running a household. In this state, a lot of importance is given to action - which sustains the cycle of actions in the whole world. This is a state reachable by people who have done good deeds before.
quotes.transliteration:
īhaṃte sarvabhūtāni tadidaṃ karmasaṃjñitam |
siddhikṣetramidaṃ puṇyamayamevāśramo mahān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उत्तमैरुत्तमैर्नित्यं संबंधानाचरेत्सह ।
निनीषुः कुलमुत्कर्षमधमानधमांस्त्यजेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must always forge relations with better and better families. This way, your own clan will keep improving. One must break ties with rogues.
quotes.transliteration:
uttamairuttamairnityaṃ saṃbaṃdhānācaretsaha |
ninīṣuḥ kulamutkarṣamadhamānadhamāṃstyajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुमंत्रिते सुविक्रांते सुकृतौ सुविचारिते ।
प्रारंभे कृतबुद्धीनां सिद्धिरव्यभिचारिणी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Success is certain for wise men who embark on a mission only after planning well, execute boldly, consult people (experts), and think ahead.
quotes.transliteration:
sumaṃtrite suvikrāṃte sukṛtau suvicārite |
prāraṃbhe kṛtabuddhīnāṃ siddhiravyabhicāriṇī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुभं वा यदि वा पापं यन्नृणां हृदि संस्थितम् ।
सुगूढमपि तज्ञेयं स्वप्नवाक्यात्तथा मदात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It does not matter whether it is good, bad, sinful or how deep it is buried. Everything will come out in dream talk or when intoxicated.
quotes.transliteration:
śubhaṃ vā yadi vā pāpaṃ yannṛṇāṃ hṛdi saṃsthitam |
sugūḍhamapi tajñeyaṃ svapnavākyāttathā madāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्वाविमौ कंटकौ तीक्ष्णौ शरीरपरिशोषिणौ ।
यश्चधनः कामयते यश्च कुप्यत्यनीश्वरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These two are sharp thorns that hurt the body (and mind) very much - a poor man's desires and weak man's anger.
quotes.transliteration:
dvāvimau kaṃṭakau tīkṣṇau śarīrapariśoṣiṇau |
yaścadhanaḥ kāmayate yaśca kupyatyanīśvaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न माता शपते पुत्रं न दोषं लभते मही ।
न हिंसां कुरुते साधुर्न देवः सृष्टिनाशकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A mother will never speak ill of her son. Nothing bad will ever stick to Earth. A virtuous person will never hurt any one. The Gods will never destroy this creation.
quotes.transliteration:
na mātā śapate putraṃ na doṣaṃ labhate mahī |
na hiṃsāṃ kurute sādhurna devaḥ sṛṣṭināśakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आत्मा जेयः सदा राज्ञा ततो जेयाश्च शत्रवः ।
अजितात्मा नरपतिर्विजयेत कथं रिपून् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must defeat himself (senses) first. Then he can think about defeating his enemies. If one can not defeat his own senses how can he defeat external enemies?
quotes.transliteration:
ātmā jeyaḥ sadā rājñā tato jeyāśca śatravaḥ |
ajitātmā narapatirvijayeta kathaṃ ripūn ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हालाहलो नैव विषं विषं रमा
जनाः परं व्यत्ययमत्र मन्वते ।
निपीय जागर्ति सुखेन तं शिवः
स्मृशन्निमां मुह्यति निद्रया हरिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not halahala that is the poison, it is Lakshmi that is poison. People have understood it the other way around. Shiva is still able to live peacefully after having consumed halahala. But after touching Lakshmi, Hari was mesmerized into a sleep.
quotes.transliteration:
hālāhalo naiva viṣaṃ viṣaṃ ramā
janāḥ paraṃ vyatyayamatra manvate |
nipīya jāgarti sukhena taṃ śivaḥ
smṛśannimāṃ muhyati nidrayā hariḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पठतो नास्ति मूर्खत्वं जपतो नास्ति पातकम् ।
मौनिनः कलहो नास्ति न भयं चास्ति जाग्रतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you read well, you will never appear foolish. You will not commit heinous crimes if you meditate regularly. If you stay silent, you will not get into fights. If you are alert, you will not have any fear.
quotes.transliteration:
paṭhato nāsti mūrkhatvaṃ japato nāsti pātakam |
mauninaḥ kalaho nāsti na bhayaṃ cāsti jāgrataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं नु मे स्यादिदं कृत्वा किं नु मे स्यादकुर्वतः ।
इति संचित्य मनसा प्राज्ञः कुर्वीत वा नवा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What would happen if I did so? What would happen if I did not do so? A wise man should always think on these lines and finally "do" or "not do". A perfect answer to the eternal question "To do or not to do?"
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ nu me syādidaṃ kṛtvā kiṃ nu me syādakurvataḥ |
iti saṃcitya manasā prājñaḥ kurvīta vā navā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भोगाय पामराणां योगाय विवेकिनां शरीरमिदम् ।
भोगाय च योगाय च न कल्पते दुर्विदग्धानाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Commoners believe that body is a means for enjoyment (getting their wishes satisfied). Wise men believe that the body is a means to attain salvation. For people who have lost their mind, the body is of no use.
quotes.transliteration:
bhogāya pāmarāṇāṃ yogāya vivekināṃ śarīramidam |
bhogāya ca yogāya ca na kalpate durvidagdhānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वकर्मधर्मार्जितजीवितानां
शास्त्रेषु दारेषु सदा रतानाम् ।
जितेंद्रियाणामतिथिप्रियाणां
गृहेऽपि मोक्षः पुरुषोत्तमानाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: jitendriyANAm atithi priyANAm It is salvation right in the middle of running household for those who - earn their living by doing moral acts only, who are always in the company of scriptures or their wives, who have their senses under their control and who loves to serve guests.
quotes.transliteration:
svakarmadharmārjitajīvitānāṃ
śāstreṣu dāreṣu sadā ratānām |
jiteṃdriyāṇāmatithipriyāṇāṃ
gṛheऽpi mokṣaḥ puruṣottamānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मर्कटस्य सुरपानं मध्ये वृश्चिकदंशनं ।
तन्मध्ये भूतसंचारो यद्वा तद्वा भविष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The antics of mankind seems to be like that of a monkey that has consumed alcohol, bitten by a scorpion and possessed by a demon.
quotes.transliteration:
markaṭasya surapānaṃ madhye vṛścikadaṃśanaṃ |
tanmadhye bhūtasaṃcāro yadvā tadvā bhaviṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उद्योगेन विना नैव कार्यं किमपि सिध्यति ।
न हि सुप्तस्य सिंहस्य प्रविशन्ति मुखे मृगाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Without effort no work will ever get done. Even for a ferocious lion, the deer will not go into its mount when it is sleeping.
quotes.transliteration:
udyogena vinā naiva kāryaṃ kimapi sidhyati |
na hi suptasya siṃhasya praviśanti mukhe mṛgāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परोऽप्यपत्यं हितकृद् यथौषधं
स्वदेहजोऽप्यामयवत् सुतोऽहितः ।
छिंद्यात् तदंगं यदुतात्मनोऽहितं
शेषं सुखं जीवति यद्विसर्जनात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Atmano ahitam Even if he is someone else's son, if he helps you (in time) like a medicine then he is to be considered one's own. On the other hand, if your own son is causing troubles then you should cut ties with him. Just like cutting a problematic body part will relieve the rest of the body from pain - rest of the family will be relieved if you cut the ties with such a bad child.
quotes.transliteration:
paroऽpyapatyaṃ hitakṛd yathauṣadhaṃ
svadehajoऽpyāmayavat sutoऽhitaḥ |
chiṃdyāt tadaṃgaṃ yadutātmanoऽhitaṃ
śeṣaṃ sukhaṃ jīvati yadvisarjanāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वरमेको गुणी पुत्रः न च मूर्खशतान्यपि ।
एकश्चन्द्रस्तमो हन्ति न च तारागणोऽपि च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One son with good character and wisdom is better than having a hundred of them who turn out to be fools. Only one Moon is able to illuminate the night, but not all the countless stars put together
quotes.transliteration:
varameko guṇī putraḥ na ca mūrkhaśatānyapi |
ekaścandrastamo hanti na ca tārāgaṇoऽpi ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कोऽर्थान् प्राप्य न गर्वितो विषयिणः कस्यापदोऽस्तं गताः
स्त्रीभिः कस्य न खण्डितं भुवि मनः को नाम राजप्रियः ।
कः कालस्य न गोचरत्वमगमत् कोऽर्थी गतो गौरवं
को वा दुर्जयदुर्गुणेषु पतितः क्षेमेण जातः पथि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who can keep arrogance out even after becoming wealthy? Who can claim that all their troubles are over? Whose mind has not been broken by a lady? Who does a king like? Who can cheat death for ever? Which beggar has ever gotten any respect? Who has come back to right path after having strayed to the path of sin?
quotes.transliteration:
koऽrthān prāpya na garvito viṣayiṇaḥ kasyāpadoऽstaṃ gatāḥ
strībhiḥ kasya na khaṇḍitaṃ bhuvi manaḥ ko nāma rājapriyaḥ |
kaḥ kālasya na gocaratvamagamat koऽrthī gato gauravaṃ
ko vā durjayadurguṇeṣu patitaḥ kṣemeṇa jātaḥ pathi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ना यावत्पुत्रं सूते तावद्गृहकर्म चेतसा कुरुते
जाते पुत्रे न तथा पुत्रानन्दाप्लुता भवति ।
अज्ञाह्येवं सततं नानाव्यवहारकृत्तदा भवति
वेत्तिनयावत् कृष्णं सेवानन्दाप्लुतस्ततो भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Until a woman does not have a child, she will take part in all household activities with lot of interest. Once she has some children, her energy is completely spent in keeping the children happy and she will lose interest in the household work. Similarly, until one finds the devotion in Krishna, they will indulge themselves with lot of interest in the worldly activities. After finding that devotion, they will care about nothing and derive all their happiness from serving the Lord.
quotes.transliteration:
nā yāvatputraṃ sūte tāvadgṛhakarma cetasā kurute
jāte putre na tathā putrānandāplutā bhavati |
ajñāhyevaṃ satataṃ nānāvyavahārakṛttadā bhavati
vettinayāvat kṛṣṇaṃ sevānandāplutastato bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परान्नं च परस्वं च परशय्याः परस्त्रियः ।
परवेश्मनि वासश्च शक्रस्यापि श्रियं हरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These things have the potential to erode the wealth of Indra (King of Gods) also - eating someone else's food, being controlled by someone else, sleeping on others bed, indulgence in other's wives, living in other people's house. One must avoid all of them.
quotes.transliteration:
parānnaṃ ca parasvaṃ ca paraśayyāḥ parastriyaḥ |
paraveśmani vāsaśca śakrasyāpi śriyaṃ haret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उदयति यदि भानुः पश्चिमे दिग्विभागे
प्रचलति यदि मेरुः शीततां याति वह्निः।
विकसति यदि पद्मं पर्वताग्रे शिलायां
न भवति पुनरुक्तं भाषितं सज्जनानाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if the Sun were to rise in the western sky, or the Mt Meru were to move, or fire would go cold, or lotus starts blooming on top of mountains among the rocks - words spoken by a virtuous person will never need to be repeated (corrected).
quotes.transliteration:
udayati yadi bhānuḥ paścime digvibhāge
pracalati yadi meruḥ śītatāṃ yāti vahniḥ|
vikasati yadi padmaṃ parvatāgre śilāyāṃ
na bhavati punaruktaṃ bhāṣitaṃ sajjanānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देहवाग्बुद्धिजं धीरा धर्मज्ञाः श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
क्षिपंत्यघं महदपि वेणुगुल्ममिवानलः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is natural that man commits sins by his action - of body, speech and intellect. But there are a few people who are courageous, know Dharma and have faith who can live their life so well that they destroy all those sins - as easily as fire destroys a cluster of bamboo shoots.
quotes.transliteration:
dehavāgbuddhijaṃ dhīrā dharmajñāḥ śraddhayānvitāḥ |
kṣipaṃtyaghaṃ mahadapi veṇugulmamivānalaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदशक्यं न तच्छक्यं यच्छक्यं शक्यमेव तत् ।
नोदके शकटं याति न नौका गच्छति स्थले ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That which is impossible will never be possible. That which is possible will always remain possible. A cart can not go over water and a ship can not move over land.
quotes.transliteration:
yadaśakyaṃ na tacchakyaṃ yacchakyaṃ śakyameva tat |
nodake śakaṭaṃ yāti na naukā gacchati sthale ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुर्जनस्य च सर्पस्य वरं सर्पो न दुर्जनः ।
सर्पो दशति काले तु दुर्जनस्तु पदे पदे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Between a wicked person and a snake, the snake is better. A snake bites occasionally only when provoked. But a wicked person does so often without any provocation.
quotes.transliteration:
durjanasya ca sarpasya varaṃ sarpo na durjanaḥ |
sarpo daśati kāle tu durjanastu pade pade ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथास्त्ररहिते पुंसि वृथा शौर्यपरिग्रहः ।
तथोपन्यासहीनस्य वृथा शास्त्रपरिग्रहः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A soldier may be very brave and courageous. His courage is useless if he does not have any weapons. Just like that, having lot of knowledge is useless if you can not articulate it well so that other people can understand.
quotes.transliteration:
yathāstrarahite puṃsi vṛthā śauryaparigrahaḥ |
tathopanyāsahīnasya vṛthā śāstraparigrahaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रयत्ने समके केचिदेव स्युः फलभागिनः ।
क्षीरोदमथनाद्देवैरमृतं प्रापि नासुरैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when many people put in the same efforts only a few actually attain the fruits. Such is the role of luck in all work. In the mighty churning of the ocean, the Gods got the elixir and the demons did not get it.
quotes.transliteration:
prayatne samake kecideva syuḥ phalabhāginaḥ |
kṣīrodamathanāddevairamṛtaṃ prāpi nāsuraiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सिन्धोस्तलगतमुक्तालाभो नाभून्निमज्जनादेकात्
सन्ति न सिन्धौ भूयो नह्यमेयं निमज्जनाल्लाभः ।
एवं श्रीकृष्णाप्तेरुपाय एको विनिष्फलो यातः
न श्रान्तव्यं पुनरपि श्रवणादिभिरीशसेवनाल्लाभः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you dive in the ocean only once, you may not get any pearls. But you must not conclude that there are no pearls in the ocean or no pearls at all. You must repeat the effort again and again. You may get it once. Just like that is is not easy to know about the true form of God. One has to keep trying hard to find it by trying again and again by showing devotion in the form of prayers, listening to God's stories and thinking about Him always.
quotes.transliteration:
sindhostalagatamuktālābho nābhūnnimajjanādekāt
santi na sindhau bhūyo nahyameyaṃ nimajjanāllābhaḥ |
evaṃ śrīkṛṣṇāpterupāya eko viniṣphalo yātaḥ
na śrāntavyaṃ punarapi śravaṇādibhirīśasevanāllābhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आन्द्रेन्धनमप्यनले शुष्कं पूर्वं ततो ज्वलति
एवं सज्जन सविधे पूर्वं लोभार्द्रता विशुष्य ततः ।
विषयाभिग्रस्तानां अन्तस्था कामरोष सहिता या
ज्वलति च विवेकवह्नौ साऽपी ज्वलति प्रकृष्ट सुखमन्ते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: aandra indhanam api anale shuShkam purvam tato jvalati Even when the fuel is wet, it will dry as soon as you put in fire and get burnt as it is meant to. Kinship with virtuous people is also like that. Even when people are drenched(wet) with greed, they will lose their lust and anger in the fire of knowledge. Along with that they will lose their greed also and find happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
āndrendhanamapyanale śuṣkaṃ pūrvaṃ tato jvalati
evaṃ sajjana savidhe pūrvaṃ lobhārdratā viśuṣya tataḥ |
viṣayābhigrastānāṃ antasthā kāmaroṣa sahitā yā
jvalati ca vivekavahnau sāऽpī jvalati prakṛṣṭa sukhamante ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रियवाक्यप्रदानेन सर्वे तुष्यंति जंतवः ।
तस्मात्तदेव वक्तव्यं वचने का दरिद्रता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Everyone becomes happier when spoken to pleasantly. So, we should always speak like that. Why be miserly when it comes to pleasant conversation?
quotes.transliteration:
priyavākyapradānena sarve tuṣyaṃti jaṃtavaḥ |
tasmāttadeva vaktavyaṃ vacane kā daridratā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विषं विषेण व्यथते वज्रं वज्रेण भिद्यते ।
गजेन्द्रो दृष्टसारेण गजेन्द्रेणैव बध्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Poison can be neutralised by another kind of poison. Diamond can be cut only by diamond. Only a stronger elephant can catch an elephant. Such is the nature of innate character.
quotes.transliteration:
viṣaṃ viṣeṇa vyathate vajraṃ vajreṇa bhidyate |
gajendro dṛṣṭasāreṇa gajendreṇaiva badhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मधुमक्षिका सशब्दं समन्ततोऽटति सुमरसास्वादम्
लभते न तावदेषा पश्चात्पुष्पस्थिता तूष्णीम् ।
भुङ्क्ते तद्वन्मनुजः ससंभ्रमः सर्वदाऽस्थिरप्रज्ञः
वादङ्करोति भक्तेः स्वादं नाऽप्नोति तद्गतौ शान्तः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: madhu makShikaa sa shabdam samantatotati suma rasa asvaadanam A bee will make a lot of noise till it sees a flower. Once it see it, it will quietly sit over it and suck the nectar. A man who is not knowledgeable about god is also thus - will make a lot of noise and arguments. Once he seeks God, he will be quiet and enjoy the solitude with Him.
quotes.transliteration:
madhumakṣikā saśabdaṃ samantatoऽṭati sumarasāsvādam
labhate na tāvadeṣā paścātpuṣpasthitā tūṣṇīm |
bhuṅkte tadvanmanujaḥ sasaṃbhramaḥ sarvadāऽsthiraprajñaḥ
vādaṅkaroti bhakteḥ svādaṃ nāऽpnoti tadgatau śāntaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्तिमितोन्नतसंचारा जनसंतापहारिणः ।
जायन्ते विरला लोके जलदा इव सज्जनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Virtuous people are like clouds. They live very high up (morally), help others and very rare. A cloud floats in the the sky and showers water on earth for the needy.
quotes.transliteration:
stimitonnatasaṃcārā janasaṃtāpahāriṇaḥ |
jāyante viralā loke jaladā iva sajjanāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शोको नाशयते धैर्यं शोको नाशयते श्रुतम् ।
शोको नाशयते सर्वं नास्तिं शोकसमो रिपुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Grief makes you lose courage. It makes you forget what you have learnt. It destroys everything you have. There is no greater enemy than grief.
quotes.transliteration:
śoko nāśayate dhairyaṃ śoko nāśayate śrutam |
śoko nāśayate sarvaṃ nāstiṃ śokasamo ripuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मातेव रक्षति पितेव हिते नियुक्ते
कांतेव चाभिरम्यत्यपनीय खेदम् ।
कीर्तिं च दिक्षु विमलां वितनोति लक्ष्मीं
किं किं न साधयति कल्पलतेव विद्या ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: mAteva rakShati piteva hite niyukte It protects you like a mother. It will show you the right path like a father. It will clear your fatigue and makes you happy like a wife. It will spread your fame in all directions. It will give you a lot of wealth. Education is like a magical creeper that will give you anything you wish.
quotes.transliteration:
māteva rakṣati piteva hite niyukte
kāṃteva cābhiramyatyapanīya khedam |
kīrtiṃ ca dikṣu vimalāṃ vitanoti lakṣmīṃ
kiṃ kiṃ na sādhayati kalpalateva vidyā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बहिर्जातविरागाय, शान्तचित्ताय दीयताम् ।
निर्मत्सराय शुचये यस्याहं प्रेयसां प्रियः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Give it (knowledge) to a person who is not interested in the outside world, whose mind is still and clear, who is not jealous and who is clean (in his mind) and one who likes me (God) a lot.
quotes.transliteration:
bahirjātavirāgāya, śāntacittāya dīyatām |
nirmatsarāya śucaye yasyāhaṃ preyasāṃ priyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षंतवो मन्दबुद्धीनामपराधो मनीषिणा ।
न हि सर्वत्रपाण्डित्यं सुलभं पुरुषे क्वचित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men should forgive mistakes by people less intelligent than them. They must understand that it is not possible to find common sense everywhere.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣaṃtavo mandabuddhīnāmaparādho manīṣiṇā |
na hi sarvatrapāṇḍityaṃ sulabhaṃ puruṣe kvacit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकं हि चक्षुरमलं सहजो विवेकः
विद्वद्भिरेव सह संवसनं द्वितीयम् ।
यस्यास्ति न द्वयमिदम् स्फुटमेव सोऽधः
तस्यापमार्गचलने वद कोऽपराधः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Having intelligence is like having one clean (good) eye. Being around wise men is like having the other eye. If one has not got either of them, he is as good as blind. Tell me, is there anything wrong (or surprising) that such a person would stray into bad path?
quotes.transliteration:
ekaṃ hi cakṣuramalaṃ sahajo vivekaḥ
vidvadbhireva saha saṃvasanaṃ dvitīyam |
yasyāsti na dvayamidam sphuṭameva soऽdhaḥ
tasyāpamārgacalane vada koऽparādhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रिये नातिभृशं हृष्येत् अप्रिये न च संज्वरेत् ।
न मुह्येदर्थच्छिद्रेषु न च धर्मं परित्यजेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When things happen as you like, you should not rejoice too much. You should not feel too sad when they go the other way also. When your wealth is destroyed, you should not lose your mind. One must not stray from the path of dharma ever.
quotes.transliteration:
priye nātibhṛśaṃ hṛṣyet apriye na ca saṃjvaret |
na muhyedarthacchidreṣu na ca dharmaṃ parityajet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रद्धधानाय भक्ताया विनीतायाननूयवे ।
भूतेषु कृतमैत्राय शुश्रूषाभिरताया च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The elixir of knowledge has to be bestowed on someone who shows dedication, devotion, humility, free from jealousy, has compassion towards all beings, willing to serve and willing to listen.
quotes.transliteration:
śraddhadhānāya bhaktāyā vinītāyānanūyave |
bhūteṣu kṛtamaitrāya śuśrūṣābhiratāyā ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किमत्र चित्रं यत्संतः परानुग्रहतत्पराः ।
न हि स्वदेहसौख्याय जायन्ते चंदनद्रुमाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is strange in noble men always being busy in helping others? Sandal wood trees do not grow for their own good. They dedicate their whole lives for others.
quotes.transliteration:
kimatra citraṃ yatsaṃtaḥ parānugrahatatparāḥ |
na hi svadehasaukhyāya jāyante caṃdanadrumāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असतां सङ्गदोषेण साधवो यान्ति विक्रियाम् ।
दुर्योधनप्रसंगेन भीष्मो गोहरणे गतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one is associated with rogues, they get corrupted even if they were virtuous by themselves. In the matter of Duryodhana, even Bheeshma participated in stealing cows from Virata's kingdom.
quotes.transliteration:
asatāṃ saṅgadoṣeṇa sādhavo yānti vikriyām |
duryodhanaprasaṃgena bhīṣmo goharaṇe gataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ईशप्रेम कथं स्यात्? स्वपतौ सत्याः सुनिर्मलं यथा प्रेम ।
कृपणस्य व धने स्यात् तद्वत् विश्वेश्वरे दृढप्रेम ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: How strong is the devotion of real devotees of God? As pure as the love of a chaste wife towards her husband and has lasting as a misers love towards his wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
īśaprema kathaṃ syāt? svapatau satyāḥ sunirmalaṃ yathā prema |
kṛpaṇasya va dhane syāt tadvat viśveśvare dṛḍhaprema ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मूर्खेण सह संयोगो विषादपि सुदुर्जरः ।
विज्ञेन सह संयोगः सुधारससमः स्मृतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: To befriend and deal with a fool will do greater damage than poison. The same with a wise man is sweeter than nectar.
quotes.transliteration:
mūrkheṇa saha saṃyogo viṣādapi sudurjaraḥ |
vijñena saha saṃyogaḥ sudhārasasamaḥ smṛtaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नैतत् समाचरेज्जातु मनसापि ह्यनीश्वरः ।
विनश्यत्याचरन् मौढ्याद् यथा रुद्रोऽब्धिजं विषम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is foolishness if someone tries to imitate Lord Shiva and drink poison. One must not even imagine doing feats that are beyond one's abilities (and out of the world).
quotes.transliteration:
naitat samācarejjātu manasāpi hyanīśvaraḥ |
vinaśyatyācaran mauḍhyād yathā rudroऽbdhijaṃ viṣam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जातस्य ममृत्युर्ध्रुव एष सर्वतः
प्रतिक्रिया यस्य न चेह क्षुप्ता ।
लोको यशश्चाथ ततो यदि ह्यमुं
को नाम मृत्युं न वृणीत युक्तम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: loko yashaH cha atha tato yadi hyamum Death is certain for every being that is born. It is the same truth every where. There is no way to get around this truth. But if one is guaranteed a good re-birth and fame, then who would not like death?
quotes.transliteration:
jātasya mamṛtyurdhruva eṣa sarvataḥ
pratikriyā yasya na ceha kṣuptā |
loko yaśaścātha tato yadi hyamuṃ
ko nāma mṛtyuṃ na vṛṇīta yuktam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दानोपभोगरहिता दिवसा यस्य यान्ति वै ।
स लोहकारभस्त्रेव श्वसन्नपि न जीवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person neither gives his wealth away, nor enjoys it then his living is a big waste. He is only breathing just like the air sac of a blacksmith and not 'living'.
quotes.transliteration:
dānopabhogarahitā divasā yasya yānti vai |
sa lohakārabhastreva śvasannapi na jīvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चरणामन्नमचराः दंष्ट्रिणामप्यदंष्ट्रिणः ।
अहस्ताश्च सहस्तानां शूराणां चैव भीरवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Immovables (plants) become food to the movables (herbivores). Those without teeth (canines) to those with teeth (carnivores). Those without hands(fish, birds) to those with hands (humans). Cowards become food for the brave.
quotes.transliteration:
caraṇāmannamacarāḥ daṃṣṭriṇāmapyadaṃṣṭriṇaḥ |
ahastāśca sahastānāṃ śūrāṇāṃ caiva bhīravaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सूर्यकरा स्सर्वगता अप्यादर्शे जले तथा क्षीरे ।
प्रतिबिम्बन्ति तथेश्वरतेजाम्स्यप्यत्रहृत्सु भक्तानाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sun rays can reach anywhere. But they are specially present (reflect from) mirror, water or milk. Just like that God is present everywhere. But he makes his presence explicit in the hearts of his true devotees.
quotes.transliteration:
sūryakarā ssarvagatā apyādarśe jale tathā kṣīre |
pratibimbanti tatheśvaratejāmsyapyatrahṛtsu bhaktānām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कालक्रमेण जगतः परिवर्तमाना ।
चक्रारपंक्तिरिव गच्छति भाग्यपंक्तिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Change is constant in this world over time. Luck follows a point on a wheel - going up and down as time progresses.
quotes.transliteration:
kālakrameṇa jagataḥ parivartamānā |
cakrārapaṃktiriva gacchati bhāgyapaṃktiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं भूषणं रुचिरमत्र यशो न रत्नं
किं कार्यमार्यचरितं सुकृतं न दोषः ।
किं चक्षुरप्रतिहतं धिषणा न नेत्रं
किं तापहारि मधुरं वचनं न पाथः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kim bhUShaNam ruchiram atra yasho na ratnam What decorate you? Fame. Not gems. What is one's duty? To live the Aryan way. Not sin. What gives you unlimited sight? Mind. Not eyes. What gives relief? Sweet words. Not Water.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ bhūṣaṇaṃ ruciramatra yaśo na ratnaṃ
kiṃ kāryamāryacaritaṃ sukṛtaṃ na doṣaḥ |
kiṃ cakṣurapratihataṃ dhiṣaṇā na netraṃ
kiṃ tāpahāri madhuraṃ vacanaṃ na pāthaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकस्यामृत्स्नायाविकारचित्राण्यनेकानि ।
ईशोऽप्येको नाना सेव्यो नानाप्रकारतो लोकैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The matter is the same - mud. But it can take so many different forms. God is also like that. He is alone. But we the people of this world see him differently and serve him differently.
quotes.transliteration:
ekasyāmṛtsnāyāvikāracitrāṇyanekāni |
īśoऽpyeko nānā sevyo nānāprakārato lokaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संगतिः श्रेयसि पुंसां स्वपक्षे च विशेषतः ।
तुषैरपि परिभ्रष्टा न प्ररोहंति तंडुलाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must enjoy the company of others, especially of people are like you and willing to help. If the rice is separate from the chaff, then it can not germinate. Even if the chaff is useless, rice will benefit from the association.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃgatiḥ śreyasi puṃsāṃ svapakṣe ca viśeṣataḥ |
tuṣairapi paribhraṣṭā na prarohaṃti taṃḍulāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शौर्यं स्वभावविजय इति हि मतं तत्कथं तदीय जयः
यद्वत्पुष्पात्सुफले जाते पुष्पच्छदा विशुष्य निपतन्ति ।
तद्वत्परमेशांशो यथा यथाऽन्तस्समुल्लसति
लय एव जयः स्यात् तथा तथा स्वस्वभावस्य ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: shaurayam svabhaava vijayaiti hi matam tat katham tadIya jayaH tadvat paramesha amsho yathaa yathaa antaH samullasati The real victory is only when one is able to conquer his habits (innate tendencies). What kind of victory is that? It is like what happens to the flower that wilts in order to give way to the fruit. Its own destruction is its victory. Similarly, when the thought of God comes into our mind - it takes over our conscience and destroys any pride that we may have. This is the real victory.
quotes.transliteration:
śauryaṃ svabhāvavijaya iti hi mataṃ tatkathaṃ tadīya jayaḥ
yadvatpuṣpātsuphale jāte puṣpacchadā viśuṣya nipatanti |
tadvatparameśāṃśo yathā yathāऽntassamullasati
laya eva jayaḥ syāt tathā tathā svasvabhāvasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विष्ठायामुपविष्टा क्षणात्तु नैवेद्यमधिवसति ।
तद्वच्चित्तं धर्मे कनके कान्तासु मक्षिकेव सति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A fly sits on waste one moment and sits on God's offering the next moment. Our mind is also fickle like that - it thinks about Dharma one moment, gold the next and wife the next moment.
quotes.transliteration:
viṣṭhāyāmupaviṣṭā kṣaṇāttu naivedyamadhivasati |
tadvaccittaṃ dharme kanake kāntāsu makṣikeva sati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रायः सरलचित्तानां जायते विपदागमः ।
ॠजुर्याति यथा छेदं न वक्रः पादपस्तथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is usually people who are righteous that get attract troubles. In a forest it is always the tree with a straight trunk that gets cut first (or falls first in case of wind).
quotes.transliteration:
prāyaḥ saralacittānāṃ jāyate vipadāgamaḥ |
ṝjuryāti yathā chedaṃ na vakraḥ pādapastathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दान्तस्य किमरण्येन तथाऽदान्तस्य भारत ।
यत्रैव निवसेद्दान्तस्तदरण्यं स चाश्रमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: For a person with restraint, an abundant forest is nothing because he does not wish to own anything more than what he needs. For a person with out restraints, the forest again is not enough because the entire forest can not satiate him. Where ever the ascetic lives, that is a forest – that is also a hermitage.
quotes.transliteration:
dāntasya kimaraṇyena tathāऽdāntasya bhārata |
yatraiva nivaseddāntastadaraṇyaṃ sa cāśramaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आसन्नमेव नृपतिर्भजते मनुष्यं
वुद्याविहीनमकुलीनमसंस्कृतं वा ।
प्रायेण भूमिपतयः प्रमदा लताश्च
यत्पार्श्वतो भवति तत्परिवेष्टयन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king will confide with a person who is always close to him even if he is not well educated, not of noble family and neither has good conduct. Be it a weak king, women or a creeper - they all seek support in whatever is physically close to them.
quotes.transliteration:
āsannameva nṛpatirbhajate manuṣyaṃ
vudyāvihīnamakulīnamasaṃskṛtaṃ vā |
prāyeṇa bhūmipatayaḥ pramadā latāśca
yatpārśvato bhavati tatpariveṣṭayanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्र भार्यागिरो वेद यत्र धर्मोऽर्थसाधनम् ।
यत्र स्वप्रतिभा मानं तस्मै श्रीकलये नमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You know that it is the time of Kali when people start considering words of their wives more seriously than the vedas, when living and preaching dharma becomes a means to earn livelihood, when everything is measured against self. We are definitely in that age.
quotes.transliteration:
yatra bhāryāgiro veda yatra dharmoऽrthasādhanam |
yatra svapratibhā mānaṃ tasmai śrīkalaye namaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विषादयमृतं ग्राह्यं बालादपि सुभाषितम् ।
अमित्रादपि सद्वृत्तं अमेध्यादपि कांचनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Elixir should be extracted even from poison. A good saying must be learnt even from a small child. Good character must be emulated even from enemies. Gold must be extracted even from garbage.
quotes.transliteration:
viṣādayamṛtaṃ grāhyaṃ bālādapi subhāṣitam |
amitrādapi sadvṛttaṃ amedhyādapi kāṃcanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दूरस्थोपि समीपस्थो यो यस्य हृदि वर्तते ।
यो यस्य हृदये नास्ति समीपस्थोऽपि दूरगः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if one is physically very far away from you, they are very close to you as long as you have them in your heart. On the other hand, if a person is not in your heart he can be considered to be distant even if he physically close.
quotes.transliteration:
dūrasthopi samīpastho yo yasya hṛdi vartate |
yo yasya hṛdaye nāsti samīpasthoऽpi dūragaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वचस्तत्र प्रयोक्तव्यं यत्रोक्तं लभते फलम् ।
स्थायीभवति चात्यंतं रागः शुक्लपटे यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must talk only when there will be some good use out of it. He must speak only when his words will stick and some action will follow. Just like color sticks better only on white cloth.
quotes.transliteration:
vacastatra prayoktavyaṃ yatroktaṃ labhate phalam |
sthāyībhavati cātyaṃtaṃ rāgaḥ śuklapaṭe yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पठकाः पाठकाश्चैव ये चान्ये शास्त्रचिंतकाः ।
सर्वे व्यसनिनो मूर्खाः यः क्रियवान् स पण्डितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Students, teachers and thinkers are all addicted to logic, discussion and theory. All are fools. Only the guys who act are the real scholars.
quotes.transliteration:
paṭhakāḥ pāṭhakāścaiva ye cānye śāstraciṃtakāḥ |
sarve vyasanino mūrkhāḥ yaḥ kriyavān sa paṇḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विकारहेतौ सति विक्रियंते ।
येषां न चेतांसि त एव दीराः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Only the mendicants who have conquered their senses can prevent their mind from being drawn towards various worldly attractions.
quotes.transliteration:
vikārahetau sati vikriyaṃte |
yeṣāṃ na cetāṃsi ta eva dīrāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संतोषस्त्रिषु कर्तव्यः कलत्रे भोजने धने ।
त्रिषु चैव न कर्तव्योऽध्ययने जपदानयोः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must observe strict limits when dealing with wife, food and wealth. No limits must be there in study, penance and charity.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃtoṣastriṣu kartavyaḥ kalatre bhojane dhane |
triṣu caiva na kartavyoऽdhyayane japadānayoḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुलभाः पुरुषा राजन् सततं प्रियवादिनः ।
अप्रियस्य तु पथ्यस्य वक्ता श्रोता च दुर्लभः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh King! it is very easy to find people who will talk sweetly always. But It is very hard to find people who can talk hard and right at the same time. It is also hard to find people who can listen to such talk.
quotes.transliteration:
sulabhāḥ puruṣā rājan satataṃ priyavādinaḥ |
apriyasya tu pathyasya vaktā śrotā ca durlabhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनेके फणिनः संति भेकभक्षणतत्पराः ।
एक एव हि शेषोऽयं धरणीधरणक्षमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are many snakes who are immersed in their usual activities of swallowing frogs. But there is only one - Shesha who is capable of holding the weight of the entire earth.
quotes.transliteration:
aneke phaṇinaḥ saṃti bhekabhakṣaṇatatparāḥ |
eka eva hi śeṣoऽyaṃ dharaṇīdharaṇakṣamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कायेन वाच मनसेंद्रियैर्वा
बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात् ।
करोति यद्यत्सकलं परस्मै
नारायणायेति समर्पयेत्तत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: karoti yadyat sakalam parasmai What ever you do with your body, speech, mind, senses, intellect, soul, by innate nature - should be offered in full to Him (Narayana).
quotes.transliteration:
kāyena vāca manaseṃdriyairvā
buddhyātmanā vā prakṛteḥ svabhāvāt |
karoti yadyatsakalaṃ parasmai
nārāyaṇāyeti samarpayettat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एतावज्जन्मसाफल्यं यदनायत्तवृत्तिता ।
ये पराधीनतां यतास्ते वै जीवन्ति के मृताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Life is only satisfying if your occupation does not let you be controlled by others. If it is 'living' even when controlled by others, then what is being 'dead'?
quotes.transliteration:
etāvajjanmasāphalyaṃ yadanāyattavṛttitā |
ye parādhīnatāṃ yatāste vai jīvanti ke mṛtāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकेन राजहंसेन या शोभा सरसोऽभवत् ।
न सा बकसहस्रेण परितस्तीरवासिना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The beauty that one royal swan imparts on to the lake can not be matched by the thousands of ducks that live on the banks of the lake.
quotes.transliteration:
ekena rājahaṃsena yā śobhā sarasoऽbhavat |
na sā bakasahasreṇa paritastīravāsinā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कश्चित् कस्यचिदेव स्यात्सुहृद्विसंभभाजनम् ।
पद्मं विकासयत्यर्कः संकोचयति कैरवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One can only be friends and help only a few. It is not possible to be friends with everyone and be good to all. Every morning, the Sun makes the pink lotus bloom while the white lotus wilts.
quotes.transliteration:
kaścit kasyacideva syātsuhṛdvisaṃbhabhājanam |
padmaṃ vikāsayatyarkaḥ saṃkocayati kairavam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विवेकालंकृतं चेतो रूपं गुणविवर्धितम् ।
सुहृद्बंधुवृता लक्ष्मीर्लक्षणं पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These are qualities found in a fortunate person - mind is bestowed with wisdom, beauty is augmented with good character and wealth is to be spent when well wishing relatives and friends.
quotes.transliteration:
vivekālaṃkṛtaṃ ceto rūpaṃ guṇavivardhitam |
suhṛdbaṃdhuvṛtā lakṣmīrlakṣaṇaṃ puṇyakarmaṇām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं धनेन कुबेरस्य सुभाषितगुणेन किं ।
वाचस्पतेश्च दीपेन रवेः सिंधोश्च बिंदुना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What good is wealth for Kubera? What good are subhashitas for Bruhaspati (teacher of Gods)? What good is a torch for Sun? What good is a drop of water for the sea?
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ dhanena kuberasya subhāṣitaguṇena kiṃ |
vācaspateśca dīpena raveḥ siṃdhośca biṃdunā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बहुधाप्यागमैर्भिन्नाः पंथानः सिद्धिहेतवः ।
त्वय्येव निपतत्योघा जाह्नवीया इवार्णवे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are many sects in this world who differ just in their way of worship and path taken to reach God. But just like all tributaries of Ganga reach the same ocean, all the sects reach the same God in spite of differences in the path taken.
quotes.transliteration:
bahudhāpyāgamairbhinnāḥ paṃthānaḥ siddhihetavaḥ |
tvayyeva nipatatyoghā jāhnavīyā ivārṇave ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वैकल्यं धरणीपातमयथोचितजल्पनम् ।
संनिपातस्य चिह्नानि मद्यं सर्वाणि दर्शयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Extreme anxiety, falling on the ground often, talking meaninglessly are all the signs of one losing his mind. Consuming alcohol also displays same symptoms.
quotes.transliteration:
vaikalyaṃ dharaṇīpātamayathocitajalpanam |
saṃnipātasya cihnāni madyaṃ sarvāṇi darśayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्यास्ति सर्वत्र गतिः स कस्मात्
स्वदेशरागेण हि याति नाशम् ।
तातस्य कूपोऽयमिति ब्रुवाणाः
क्षारं जलं कापुरुषाः पिबंति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: yasya asti sarvatra gatiH sa kasmAt If a person has talents that are valued at all places then why would he stick to his own country out attachment? It would certain failure. Only those who do not have talent will drink hard water from the well jest because their father dug it for them.
quotes.transliteration:
yasyāsti sarvatra gatiḥ sa kasmāt
svadeśarāgeṇa hi yāti nāśam |
tātasya kūpoऽyamiti bruvāṇāḥ
kṣāraṃ jalaṃ kāpuruṣāḥ pibaṃti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
येन केन प्रकारेण यस्य कस्यापिदेहिनः ।
संतोषं जनयेत् प्राज्ञस्तदेवेश्वरपूजनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: By what ever means one should make someone happy. Wise men believe that making other people happy is worship of God.
quotes.transliteration:
yena kena prakāreṇa yasya kasyāpidehinaḥ |
saṃtoṣaṃ janayet prājñastadeveśvarapūjanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकारणाविष्कृतवैरदारुणात्
असज्जनात्कस्य भयं न जायते ।
विषं महाहेरिव यस्य दुर्वचः
सुदुःसहं सन्निहितं सदा मुखे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who will not scared of a wicked person who is capable of hatred without any reason? They have a ready stock of harsh words in their mouth just like the venom in the mouth of snake.
quotes.transliteration:
akāraṇāviṣkṛtavairadāruṇāt
asajjanātkasya bhayaṃ na jāyate |
viṣaṃ mahāheriva yasya durvacaḥ
suduḥsahaṃ sannihitaṃ sadā mukhe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुले लज्जायां च स्वयशसि च माने च विमुखः
शरीरं विक्रीय क्षणिकमपि लोभाद्धनवति ।
तदाज्ञां कुर्वाणो हितमहितमित्येतदधुना
विचारातिक्रांतः किमिति परतंत्रो विमृशति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A man who does not care for the fame of his family, not bothered about his own long term future, his respect - may end up selling his loyalty to a wealthy person for the sake of the body (transient). He will follow only his instructions. He will lose the ability to think for himself and decide what is good for him and what is not. He is not in control of himself.
quotes.transliteration:
kule lajjāyāṃ ca svayaśasi ca māne ca vimukhaḥ
śarīraṃ vikrīya kṣaṇikamapi lobhāddhanavati |
tadājñāṃ kurvāṇo hitamahitamityetadadhunā
vicārātikrāṃtaḥ kimiti parataṃtro vimṛśati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुरोरप्यवलिप्तस्य कार्याकार्यमजानतः ।
उत्पथं प्रतिपन्नस्य कार्यं भवति शासनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a person strays from the right path and loses the distinction between right and wrong, we have the duty to correct him even when he is our teacher.
quotes.transliteration:
gurorapyavaliptasya kāryākāryamajānataḥ |
utpathaṃ pratipannasya kāryaṃ bhavati śāsanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मुनेरपि वनस्थस्य स्वानि कर्माणि कुर्वतः ।
उत्पद्यंते त्रयः पक्षा मित्रोदासीनशत्रवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a sage living all by himself in a forest will not be able to love/hate everyone/everything alike. Even he will have three kinds of relations - friends, ignored and foes.
quotes.transliteration:
munerapi vanasthasya svāni karmāṇi kurvataḥ |
utpadyaṃte trayaḥ pakṣā mitrodāsīnaśatravaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुत्रीति जाता महतीह चिंता
कस्मै प्रदेयेति महान्वितर्कः ।
दत्वा सुखं प्राप्स्यति वा न वेति
कन्यापितृत्वं खलु नाम कष्टम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a daughter is born it is a cause of great worry. First you have to worry about whom she should be married to. Its a great debate. After getting married, you get worried if she is happy or not. It is really difficult being a father of a daughter.
quotes.transliteration:
putrīti jātā mahatīha ciṃtā
kasmai pradeyeti mahānvitarkaḥ |
datvā sukhaṃ prāpsyati vā na veti
kanyāpitṛtvaṃ khalu nāma kaṣṭam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कीटोऽपि सुमनःसंगादारोहति सतां शिरः ।
अश्मापि याति देवत्वं महद्भिः सुप्रतिष्ठितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Along with the flower an insect also gets its place on top of the head of a virtuous men. Just like that, even a stone attains divinity if it is installed by a virtuous noble man.
quotes.transliteration:
kīṭoऽpi sumanaḥsaṃgādārohati satāṃ śiraḥ |
aśmāpi yāti devatvaṃ mahadbhiḥ supratiṣṭhitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कीर्तिर्हि पुरुषं लोके संजीवयति मातृवत् ।
अकीर्तिर्जीवितं हंति जीवतोऽपि शरीरिणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fame is what keeps a man filled with life – like his mother. Without any fame people are as good as dead.
quotes.transliteration:
kīrtirhi puruṣaṃ loke saṃjīvayati mātṛvat |
akīrtirjīvitaṃ haṃti jīvatoऽpi śarīriṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अभ्रच्छाया तृणाग्निश्च खले प्रीतिः स्थले जलम् ।
वेश्यासक्तिः कुमित्रं च षडेते बुद्बुदोपमाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following six things as ephemeral as a water bubble - shade from a cloud, fire from grass, love shown by a rogue, water that is spilled over land, attention span of a prostitute and a bad friend.
quotes.transliteration:
abhracchāyā tṛṇāgniśca khale prītiḥ sthale jalam |
veśyāsaktiḥ kumitraṃ ca ṣaḍete budbudopamāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्वत्वं च नृपत्वं च नैव तुल्यं कदाचन ।
स्वदेशे पूज्यते राजा विद्वान् सर्वत्र पूज्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never compare intellectual wealth and royalty. A king is respected only in his kingdom while a scholar is respected all over the world.
quotes.transliteration:
vidvatvaṃ ca nṛpatvaṃ ca naiva tulyaṃ kadācana |
svadeśe pūjyate rājā vidvān sarvatra pūjyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अत्युच्चपदाध्यासः पतानायेत्यर्थशालिनां शंसत् ।
आपांडुपततिपत्रं तरोरिदं बंधनग्रंथेः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is the same fate that awaits people reach the top using wealth. When a leaf turns white (pale), it loses the connection to the tree and falls off.
quotes.transliteration:
atyuccapadādhyāsaḥ patānāyetyarthaśālināṃ śaṃsat |
āpāṃḍupatatipatraṃ taroridaṃ baṃdhanagraṃtheḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पित्रोर्नित्यं प्रियं कुर्यादाचार्यस्य च सर्वदा ।
तेषु हि त्रिषु तुष्टेषु तपः सर्वं समाप्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must try to please the parents and the teacher every day. If you can make these three are happy, you can consider all penances as complete.
quotes.transliteration:
pitrornityaṃ priyaṃ kuryādācāryasya ca sarvadā |
teṣu hi triṣu tuṣṭeṣu tapaḥ sarvaṃ samāpyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथान्नं मधुसंयुक्तं मधुवान्येन संयुतम् ।
एवं तपश्च विद्या च संयुक्तं भेषजं महत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When food is mixed with honey or when honey is mixed with anything else, it makes a good medicine. Just like that a combination of penance and knowledge is a good medicine capable of curing anything.
quotes.transliteration:
yathānnaṃ madhusaṃyuktaṃ madhuvānyena saṃyutam |
evaṃ tapaśca vidyā ca saṃyuktaṃ bheṣajaṃ mahat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुताननेकान्विविधैर्पायैः क्रीडासु
संसज्य यथा हि माता ।
कार्येषु सक्ताऽपि तदार्तवाणीं
संश्रुत्य चायाति तथाहि चेशः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A mother may have left her kids to play by themselves after tricking them in various ways so that she can get some work done. But when one of them cries, she can hear it and will rush to console. Just like that God will rush to help his devotees when they are in trouble.
quotes.transliteration:
sutānanekānvividhairpāyaiḥ krīḍāsu
saṃsajya yathā hi mātā |
kāryeṣu saktāऽpi tadārtavāṇīṃ
saṃśrutya cāyāti tathāhi ceśaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गाधजलपिपास स्याच्चेच्छनकैस्तु पातव्यं
नोचेदधस्थपङ्कः पेयानुपयोगिजलमलं कुरुते ।
तद्वत्पावित्र्यार्थं दृढतरविश्वासधृक् भूत्वा
बुद्धिभ्रंशकवादान् विहाय कृष्णं भजेच्छनैर्मनुजः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you are thirsty and want to drink water from a lake, you know the one must drink the clear water without disturbing the bottom. If you disturb, then dirt from bottom will contaminate the drinkable water. Similarly, if you want to think about God, you must keep all other argumentative thoughts aside, think about Him only and sing about Krishna in a low melodious tone.
quotes.transliteration:
gādhajalapipāsa syāccecchanakaistu pātavyaṃ
nocedadhasthapaṅkaḥ peyānupayogijalamalaṃ kurute |
tadvatpāvitryārthaṃ dṛḍhataraviśvāsadhṛk bhūtvā
buddhibhraṃśakavādān vihāya kṛṣṇaṃ bhajecchanairmanujaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जिह्वयातिप्रमाथिन्या जनो रसविमोहितः ।
मृत्युमृच्छत्यसद् बुद्धिर्मीनस्तु बडिशैर्यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The tongue is the reason for many troubles. People get destroyed for the mere reason that it gets attracted towards tasty stuff - just like the fish that take the bait.
quotes.transliteration:
jihvayātipramāthinyā jano rasavimohitaḥ |
mṛtyumṛcchatyasad buddhirmīnastu baḍiśairyathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बलवानप्यशक्तोऽसौ धनवानपि निर्धनः ।
श्रुतवानपि मूर्खश्च यो धर्मविमुखो जनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you go against Dharma, you will remain weak in spite of being powerful, penniless in spite of being wealthy and a fool in spite of having read all the scriptures.
quotes.transliteration:
balavānapyaśaktoऽsau dhanavānapi nirdhanaḥ |
śrutavānapi mūrkhaśca yo dharmavimukho janaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पातंजले विष्णुपदापगायाः
पातंजले चापि नयेऽवगाहम् ।
आचक्षते शुद्धिदमाप्रसूते -
राऽचक्षते रागमधोक्षजे च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Bathing in ganga, reciting the works of Patanjali, having strong faith in God - these three things will cleanse ones speecgh (words), body and the mind - irrespective of the age (from birth to death)
quotes.transliteration:
pātaṃjale viṣṇupadāpagāyāḥ
pātaṃjale cāpi nayeऽvagāham |
ācakṣate śuddhidamāprasūte -
rāऽcakṣate rāgamadhokṣaje ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदृच्छयोपलब्धेन संतुष्टो मितभुञ् मुनिः ।
विविक्तशरणः शांतो मैत्रः करुण आत्मवान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is considered to have known his self if he can - be satisfied by windfall that happens once in a while (not greedy), eats less, lives a life of a hermit, likes to live by himself, peaceful, greets everyone friendly and shows compassion towards everyone.
quotes.transliteration:
yadṛcchayopalabdhena saṃtuṣṭo mitabhuñ muniḥ |
viviktaśaraṇaḥ śāṃto maitraḥ karuṇa ātmavān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुंदरोऽपि सुशीलोऽपि कुलीनोऽपि महाधनः ।
शोभते न विना विद्यां विद्या सर्वस्य भूषणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One may be handsome, with good character, born to a good family and have lot of wealth - yet he will not be accepted if he is not well educated. Education is the ornament that every one is looking for.
quotes.transliteration:
suṃdaroऽpi suśīloऽpi kulīnoऽpi mahādhanaḥ |
śobhate na vinā vidyāṃ vidyā sarvasya bhūṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृगा मृगैः संगमनुव्रजन्ति
गावश्चगोभिस्तुरगास्तुरंगैः ।
मूर्खाश्च मूर्खैः सुधियः सुधीभिः
समानशीलव्यसनेषु सख्यम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Deer will have good time around deer, cows with other cows, horses with other horses, fools with other fools and the virtuous with other virtuous people. People of similar qualities will always go along well.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛgā mṛgaiḥ saṃgamanuvrajanti
gāvaścagobhisturagāsturaṃgaiḥ |
mūrkhāśca mūrkhaiḥ sudhiyaḥ sudhībhiḥ
samānaśīlavyasaneṣu sakhyam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्थानस्थितानि पूज्यंते पूज्यंते च पदे स्थिताः ।
स्थानभ्रष्टा न पूज्यंते कुशा दंता नखा नराः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Things that are in their useful positions and in helpful roles are respected. They will no longer be respected if they lose the position or cease to be helpful. Just like the holy grass, teeth, finer and toe nails. This applies to people as well.
quotes.transliteration:
sthānasthitāni pūjyaṃte pūjyaṃte ca pade sthitāḥ |
sthānabhraṣṭā na pūjyaṃte kuśā daṃtā nakhā narāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मणिर्लुठति पादेषु काचः शिरसि धार्यते ।
यथैवास्तु तथैवास्तु काचः काचो मणिर्मणिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A gem may be worn on the foot where it get beaten often. A glass piece may be worn over the head. Let them be where they are. Wherever they are, a gem is a gem and glass piece is a glass piece.
quotes.transliteration:
maṇirluṭhati pādeṣu kācaḥ śirasi dhāryate |
yathaivāstu tathaivāstu kācaḥ kāco maṇirmaṇiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नरपतिहितकर्ता द्वेष्यतां याति लोके
जनपदहितकर्ता त्यज्यते पार्थिवेंद्रैः ।
इति महति विरोधे वर्तमाने समाने
नृपति जनपदानां दुर्लभः कार्यकर्ता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: iti mahati virodhe vartamAne samAne If one helps the king, people at large will not like him. If one works mostly in people's interest, then the kings will abandon him. With both sides equally important, it is very difficult to find a person who can balance the interests of both the king and the subjects.
quotes.transliteration:
narapatihitakartā dveṣyatāṃ yāti loke
janapadahitakartā tyajyate pārthiveṃdraiḥ |
iti mahati virodhe vartamāne samāne
nṛpati janapadānāṃ durlabhaḥ kāryakartā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रामाण्यबुद्धिः स्तोत्रेषु देवताबुद्धिरात्मनि ।
कीटबुद्धिर्मनुष्येषु नूतनायाः श्रियः फलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Approving of praises, thinking oneself as God (in control of everything) and treating everyone else as insects - these are the effects of newly acquired wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
prāmāṇyabuddhiḥ stotreṣu devatābuddhirātmani |
kīṭabuddhirmanuṣyeṣu nūtanāyāḥ śriyaḥ phalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कर्पूर इव दग्धोऽपि शक्तिमान् यो दिने दिने ।
नमोऽस्त्ववार्यवीर्याय तस्मै कुसुमधन्वने ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Salutations to the one who is holding up the flower-arrows - Manmatha, the god of Love. His power increases day by day even after being completely destroyed like the burning of camphor.
quotes.transliteration:
karpūra iva dagdhoऽpi śaktimān yo dine dine |
namoऽstvavāryavīryāya tasmai kusumadhanvane ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धत्ते भरं कुसुमपत्रफलावलीनां
घर्मव्यथां वहति शीतभवां रुजं च ।
यो देहमर्पयति चान्यसुखस्य हेतोः
तस्मैवदान्यगुरवे तरवे नमोऽस्तु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: yo dehamarpayati chAnya sukhasya hetoH A tree is the best example of charity. It bears the weight of the leaves, flowers, fruits and climbers. It protects you from the heat (by providing shade) and cold (by shielding you from cold winds), It doe all these for other people's happiness. I bow to you the the foremost among charity doers.
quotes.transliteration:
dhatte bharaṃ kusumapatraphalāvalīnāṃ
gharmavyathāṃ vahati śītabhavāṃ rujaṃ ca |
yo dehamarpayati cānyasukhasya hetoḥ
tasmaivadānyagurave tarave namoऽstu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुक्षेत्रं तव जन्मभूरिति पुनः पर्वानुबंधादियं
मेध्या मूर्तिरिति प्रभूतगरिमग्रामा न के के गुणाः ।
माधुर्यं परपीडितोऽपि धरसि क्रांतो गुरुग्रंथिभिः
सांगं सज्जनचेष्टितं क्व भवता संप्राप्तमिक्षो वद ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Your home land is the ground. From there you grow upwards as if there are chapters and chapters. Your presence is auspicious. You have so many great qualities. You give out sweetness even when crushed. You body is stiff and yet you possess all the virtuous qualities. Tell me Sugar cane, how did you come to be like this?
quotes.transliteration:
sukṣetraṃ tava janmabhūriti punaḥ parvānubaṃdhādiyaṃ
medhyā mūrtiriti prabhūtagarimagrāmā na ke ke guṇāḥ |
mādhuryaṃ parapīḍitoऽpi dharasi krāṃto gurugraṃthibhiḥ
sāṃgaṃ sajjanaceṣṭitaṃ kva bhavatā saṃprāptamikṣo vada ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोके प्रकृतिभिन्नेऽस्मिन्न कश्चित् कस्यचित् प्रियः ।
कार्यकारणसंबद्धं वालुकामुष्टिवज्जगत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world, different people will have different tastes. Nobody actually likes any other. They are all together just because they depend on each other for survival. They are all together just like grains of sand in a fist. If the fist loosens, they all go their own way.
quotes.transliteration:
loke prakṛtibhinneऽsminna kaścit kasyacit priyaḥ |
kāryakāraṇasaṃbaddhaṃ vālukāmuṣṭivajjagat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सध्रीचीनो ह्ययं लोके पंथाः क्षेमोऽकुतोभयः ।
सुशीलाः साधवो यत्र नारायणपरायणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is safe to follow the path that noble, virtuous devotees of Narayana follow. That path will lead us to God and is causes no fear.
quotes.transliteration:
sadhrīcīno hyayaṃ loke paṃthāḥ kṣemoऽkutobhayaḥ |
suśīlāḥ sādhavo yatra nārāyaṇaparāyaṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
चत्वारि यस्य द्वाराणि सुगुप्तान्यमरोत्तमाः ।
उपस्थमुदरं हस्तौ वाक्चतुर्थी स धर्मवित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are four doors through which adharma can enter us – private parts, stomach, hands and speech. If one guards these doors carefully at all the time, he will be known as Dharmajna and avoid all sins. All sins are related to man losing control of these doors.
quotes.transliteration:
catvāri yasya dvārāṇi suguptānyamarottamāḥ |
upasthamudaraṃ hastau vākcaturthī sa dharmavit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तस्मान्न कस्यचिद् द्रोहमाचरेत् स तथाविधः ।
आत्म्नः क्षेममन्विच्छन् द्रोग्धुर्वै परतो भयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not offend/cheat anyone. This is in his own interest. He who offends others is always in fear of the same treatment done to him.
quotes.transliteration:
tasmānna kasyacid drohamācaret sa tathāvidhaḥ |
ātmnaḥ kṣemamanvicchan drogdhurvai parato bhayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रत्यक्षकविकाव्यं च रूपं च कुलयोषितः ।
गृहवैद्यस्य विद्या च कस्यैचिद्यदि रोचते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following things are rarely liked, usually only by a single person - Works of a contemporary poet, beauty of chaste lady and medicine of a house doctor.
quotes.transliteration:
pratyakṣakavikāvyaṃ ca rūpaṃ ca kulayoṣitaḥ |
gṛhavaidyasya vidyā ca kasyaicidyadi rocate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्वत्सु विमलज्ञाना विरक्ता यतिषु स्थिताः ।
स्वीयेषु च गरोद्गारा नानाकाराः क्षितौ खलाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wicked people in this world come in various forms. When they are in company of scholars, they are in the form of a realised soul (with clear mind). When in company of saints they look like ascetics. When they are among others of their own kind, they become very vicious and spit poison.
quotes.transliteration:
vidvatsu vimalajñānā viraktā yatiṣu sthitāḥ |
svīyeṣu ca garodgārā nānākārāḥ kṣitau khalāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
छन्ना भवन्ति भुवि सत्पुरुषाः कथंचित्
स्वैः कारणैः गुरुजनैः नियम्यमानाः ।
भूयः परव्यसनमेत्य विमोक्तुकामाः
विस्मृत्य पूर्वनियमं विमृता भवन्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Great men lay in hiding in this world for various reason - their own reasons, or instructed by a teacher, following a code. But when they see someone in distress, they blow their cover and rush to help ignoring all previously followed rules.
quotes.transliteration:
channā bhavanti bhuvi satpuruṣāḥ kathaṃcit
svaiḥ kāraṇaiḥ gurujanaiḥ niyamyamānāḥ |
bhūyaḥ paravyasanametya vimoktukāmāḥ
vismṛtya pūrvaniyamaṃ vimṛtā bhavanti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निमित्तेभ्यः प्रवर्तन्ते सर्व एव स्वभूतये ।
अभिप्रायानुरोधोऽपि स्वार्थस्यैव प्रसिद्धये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Every action one takes is always intended to benefit him. Even when he follows someone else's opinions. No one will follow others unless they see that there is self interest in it.
quotes.transliteration:
nimittebhyaḥ pravartante sarva eva svabhūtaye |
