abhiprāyānurodhoऽpi svārthasyaiva prasiddhaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बालानां त्वानन्दः क्रीडनकैरेव रत्नधनबहुलः ।
तद्वद्भक्तस्यापि प्रभुण्याह्यधिको धनप्रतिष्टातः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Children like their toys much more than money or gems. Just like that, a true devotee will like proximity to God much more than fame or wealth.
quotes.transliteration:
bālānāṃ tvānandaḥ krīḍanakaireva ratnadhanabahulaḥ |
tadvadbhaktasyāpi prabhuṇyāhyadhiko dhanapratiṣṭātaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भक्तं शक्तं कुलीनं च न भृत्यमपमानयेत् ।
पुत्रवल्लालयेन्नित्यं य इच्छेच्छ्रियमात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you have a loyal, efficient servant from a good family (good culture) - you should never insult him. You must treat him as if he is your own son if you wish for prosperity.
quotes.transliteration:
bhaktaṃ śaktaṃ kulīnaṃ ca na bhṛtyamapamānayet |
putravallālayennityaṃ ya icchecchriyamātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विवादो धनसंबंधो याचनं स्त्रीषु संगतिः ।
आदानामग्रतः स्थानं मैत्रीभङ्गस्य हेतवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These are known to cause harm to friendship - getting involved in public controversy, money matters, begging, women, borrowing or changing social status.
quotes.transliteration:
vivādo dhanasaṃbaṃdho yācanaṃ strīṣu saṃgatiḥ |
ādānāmagrataḥ sthānaṃ maitrībhaṅgasya hetavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य वाङ्मनसी शुद्धे सम्यग्गुप्ते च सर्वदा ।
स वै सर्वमवाप्नोति वेदान्तोपगतं फलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you have your mind and speech clean and under control, then you will get all the fruits that are possible by the knowledge of vedantas (extracts of Vedas).
quotes.transliteration:
yasya vāṅmanasī śuddhe samyaggupte ca sarvadā |
sa vai sarvamavāpnoti vedāntopagataṃ phalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बह्वपि स्वेच्छया कामं प्रकीर्णमभिधीयते ।
अनुज्झितार्थसंबंधः प्रबंधो दुरुदाहरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is easy to talk freely about irrelevant things. Anybody can do it. It is very hard to choose a topic and create a well composed prose on it.
quotes.transliteration:
bahvapi svecchayā kāmaṃ prakīrṇamabhidhīyate |
anujjhitārthasaṃbaṃdhaḥ prabaṃdho durudāharaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रायो दुरंतपर्यंताः संपदोऽपि दुरात्मनाम् ।
भवन्ति हि शुभोदर्का विपदोऽपि महात्मनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Bad people will meet with bad ending even if they have wealth now. Good things will happen to good people. It may not be immediate, but surely in future.
quotes.transliteration:
prāyo duraṃtaparyaṃtāḥ saṃpadoऽpi durātmanām |
bhavanti hi śubhodarkā vipadoऽpi mahātmanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षणशः कणश्चैव विद्यामर्थं च साधयेत् ।
क्षणत्यागे कुतो विद्या कणत्यागे कुतो धनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wisdom must be accumulated second by second spent in reading. Wealth must be accumulated grain by grain. If you don't pay attention and lose seconds, you can not get good wisdom. If you don't pay attention to grains, you cannot get wealthy.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣaṇaśaḥ kaṇaścaiva vidyāmarthaṃ ca sādhayet |
kṣaṇatyāge kuto vidyā kaṇatyāge kuto dhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुण्यस्य फलमिच्छन्ति पुण्यं नेच्छन्ति मानवाः ।
पापस्य तु नहीच्छन्ति पापं कुर्वन्ति यत्नतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All humans want to enjoy the rewards for good action. But no one wants to do good action. No one looks forward to the punishment for sins. But continue committing sins every day.
quotes.transliteration:
puṇyasya phalamicchanti puṇyaṃ necchanti mānavāḥ |
pāpasya tu nahīcchanti pāpaṃ kurvanti yatnataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स किंसखा साधु न शास्ति योऽधिपं
हितान्न यः संशृणुते स किंप्रभुः ।
सदानुकूलेषु हि कुर्वते रतिं
नृपेष्वमात्येषु च सर्वसंपदः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: His is a bad friend who does not give good advice to the king. He is a bad king when he does not listen to the good advice. Only when the king and the minister have a very good relationship, does the kingdom prosper.
quotes.transliteration:
sa kiṃsakhā sādhu na śāsti yoऽdhipaṃ
hitānna yaḥ saṃśṛṇute sa kiṃprabhuḥ |
sadānukūleṣu hi kurvate ratiṃ
nṛpeṣvamātyeṣu ca sarvasaṃpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भवत्येकसले जन्म गंधस्तेषां पृथक् पृथक् ।
उत्पलस्य मृणालस्य मत्स्यस्य कुमुदस्य च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even though all of these are born at the same place (the pond) they smell different - the blue lotus, the red lotus, water lily and the fish. Innate qualities shape the outcome much more than the environment.
quotes.transliteration:
bhavatyekasale janma gaṃdhasteṣāṃ pṛthak pṛthak |
utpalasya mṛṇālasya matsyasya kumudasya ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निःसारस्य पदार्थस्य प्रायेणाडंबरो महान् ।
न हि स्वर्णे ध्वनिस्तादृक्कांस्ये यादृक् प्रजायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Usually an item that does not have good qualities will make up for it by having a lot of decorations. Bronze makes lot of sound while gold does not.
quotes.transliteration:
niḥsārasya padārthasya prāyeṇāḍaṃbaro mahān |
na hi svarṇe dhvanistādṛkkāṃsye yādṛk prajāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राच्यामुदये भाविनि सवितुः पूर्वं यथाऽनुरागः स्यात्
पश्चाद्गतिस्त्वनूरोः प्राकट्यं हर्षकृत् हिरण्यगर्भस्य ।
मनसीशप्राकट्ये भाविनि पूर्वं स्वधर्मं शीलत्वम्
पश्चाद्वैजिज्ञासा प्रभवति तस्माच्च भक्तिविज्ञानम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: At dawn before the sunrise, you will see beautiful red rays of Sun from the east. Then you will see the pleasant golden glow of the Sun and in time you will see the Sun in full brightness. The progression is similar to devotion towards God. First one leads his life morally. Then he starts thinking about God and finally realizes his existence through pure devotion.
quotes.transliteration:
prācyāmudaye bhāvini savituḥ pūrvaṃ yathāऽnurāgaḥ syāt
paścādgatistvanūroḥ prākaṭyaṃ harṣakṛt hiraṇyagarbhasya |
manasīśaprākaṭye bhāvini pūrvaṃ svadharmaṃ śīlatvam
paścādvaijijñāsā prabhavati tasmācca bhaktivijñānam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षमी दाता गुणग्राही स्वामी दुःखेन लभ्यते ।
शुचिर्दक्षोऽनुरक्तश्च जाने भृत्योऽपि दुर्लभः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Rare is a master who is forgiving, charitable and who can recognize good qualities in servants. At the same time, it is very rare to find a servant who is clean, efficient and loyal.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣamī dātā guṇagrāhī svāmī duḥkhena labhyate |
śucirdakṣoऽnuraktaśca jāne bhṛtyoऽpi durlabhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
येनास्य पितरो याता येन याता पितामहाः ।
तेन यायात्सतां मार्गं तेन गच्छन् न रिष्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one follows the footsteps of their fathers and forefathers then there will be no fear of straying from the path of Dharma - moral code of conduct.
quotes.transliteration:
yenāsya pitaro yātā yena yātā pitāmahāḥ |
tena yāyātsatāṃ mārgaṃ tena gacchan na riṣyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शुकवद् भाषणम् कुर्याद् बकवद्ध्यानमाचरेत् ।
अजवच्चवर्णं कुर्याद् गजवत् स्नानमाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise must emulate good qualities from everywhere - speech of a parrot, concentration of a crane, chewing (deliberation) of a goat and relaxed bathing of an elephant.
quotes.transliteration:
śukavad bhāṣaṇam kuryād bakavaddhyānamācaret |
ajavaccavarṇaṃ kuryād gajavat snānamācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्लिष्टो जनः किल जनैरनुरंजनीयः
तन्नो यदुक्तमशुभं च न तत् क्षमं ते ।
नैसर्गिकी सुरभिणः कुसुमस्य सिद्धा
मूर्ध्नि स्थितिर्न चरणैरवताडनानि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People should always say good things to others who are in trouble. As much as possible they must reduce their trouble. So, there is no forgiveness for the harsh words that I have uttered and you don't deserve it. A naturally fragrant flower has to placed on the head and not beaten up on by foot. (As told by Rama to Sita)
quotes.transliteration:
kliṣṭo janaḥ kila janairanuraṃjanīyaḥ
tanno yaduktamaśubhaṃ ca na tat kṣamaṃ te |
naisargikī surabhiṇaḥ kusumasya siddhā
mūrdhni sthitirna caraṇairavatāḍanāni ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जामातृसंपत्तिमचिंतयित्वा
पित्रा तु दत्ता स्वमनोभिलाषात् ।
कुलद्वयं हन्ति मदेन नारी
कूलद्वयं क्षुब्धजला नदीव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kuladvayam hanti madena nArI If the father does not weigh the qualities of the groom well and give her daughter away, then the bride will act arrogantly with her in-laws. This will ruin both the families. Just like a flooding rampant river will destroys both the banks.
quotes.transliteration:
jāmātṛsaṃpattimaciṃtayitvā
pitrā tu dattā svamanobhilāṣāt |
kuladvayaṃ hanti madena nārī
kūladvayaṃ kṣubdhajalā nadīva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जनो वै लोके एतस्मिन्न विद्याकामकर्मभिः ।
उच्चावचासु गतिषु न वेद स्वांगतिं भ्रमन् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People are walking around in this world without knowing what kind of life they are going to get next. It could be a better one or worse. They are ridden with ignorance, desire and consequences of prior actions.
quotes.transliteration:
jano vai loke etasminna vidyākāmakarmabhiḥ |
uccāvacāsu gatiṣu na veda svāṃgatiṃ bhraman ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कः संबंधो यदभिरमते वीक्ष्य मेघं मयूरी
कैवासत्तिर्यदमृतरुचौ सानुबंधा चकोरी ।
को वा हेतुः पतति परितो यत्कृशानौ पतंगः
प्रायेणैवं विधिपरवशा प्राणिनां चित्तवृत्तिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is the bonding between clouds and a peacock that makes it dance every time a dark could passes by? What is the source of love between the moon and chakora bird? Why does the moth circle around flames and finally die with it? All these beings are strung together by providence to things that they do.
quotes.transliteration:
kaḥ saṃbaṃdho yadabhiramate vīkṣya meghaṃ mayūrī
kaivāsattiryadamṛtarucau sānubaṃdhā cakorī |
ko vā hetuḥ patati parito yatkṛśānau pataṃgaḥ
prāyeṇaivaṃ vidhiparavaśā prāṇināṃ cittavṛttiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उद्वेजनीयो भूतानां नृशंसः पापकर्मकृत् ।
त्रयाणामपि लोकानामीश्वरोऽपि न तिष्ठति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a king behaves in such a way to make his subjects more anxious (and afraid) by his sinful acts, then it does not matter even if he is the lord of three worlds. He will still perish.
quotes.transliteration:
udvejanīyo bhūtānāṃ nṛśaṃsaḥ pāpakarmakṛt |
trayāṇāmapi lokānāmīśvaroऽpi na tiṣṭhati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः पठति लिखति पश्यति परिपृच्छति पण्डितानुपाश्रयति ।
तस्य दिवाकरकिरणैर्नलिनीदलमिव विकास्यते बुद्धिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one reads, writes, observes, questions and serves the scholars then his wisdom will grow like the petals of a lotus flower when touched by sun rays.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ paṭhati likhati paśyati paripṛcchati paṇḍitānupāśrayati |
tasya divākarakiraṇairnalinīdalamiva vikāsyate buddhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यधर्मविहीनेन न संदध्यात्कथंचन ।
सुसंधितोऽप्यसाधुत्वादचिराद्याति विक्रियाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not enter into a treaty with a rogue - who does not believe in truth and dharma. You may think you have designed the treaty very well, but him being a rogue will rescind it very soon and become an enemy again.
quotes.transliteration:
satyadharmavihīnena na saṃdadhyātkathaṃcana |
susaṃdhitoऽpyasādhutvādacirādyāti vikriyām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं लघुताया मूलं
प्राकृतपुरुषेषु या याञ्छा ।
रामादपि कः शूरः
स्मरशरनिहतो न यश्चलति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What reduces one's weight (perception of self respect)? Having to beg from a lowly person. Who is more stronger than Rama? He who can remain unmoved after being attacked by the arrows of Manmatha (Angel of love).
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ laghutāyā mūlaṃ
prākṛtapuruṣeṣu yā yāñchā |
rāmādapi kaḥ śūraḥ
smaraśaranihato na yaścalati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विना शीलेन वनिता वाग्मिता विद्यया विना ।
विनियोगैर्विना वित्तं मास्तु कस्यापि जीवनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let nobody ever have these in their lives - a lady without character, an orator without education (wisdom) and wealth that is not used properly.
quotes.transliteration:
vinā śīlena vanitā vāgmitā vidyayā vinā |
viniyogairvinā vittaṃ māstu kasyāpi jīvanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदा देवेषु वेदेषु गोषु विप्रेषु साधुषु ।
धर्मे मयि च विद्वेषः स वा आशु विनश्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Destruction comes fast at you when you go against the Gods, the vedas, cows, scholars, virtuous people, dharma and the supreme being.
quotes.transliteration:
yadā deveṣu vedeṣu goṣu vipreṣu sādhuṣu |
dharme mayi ca vidveṣaḥ sa vā āśu vinaśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुभिक्षं कृषके नित्यं नित्यं सुखमरोगिणि ।
भार्या भर्तुः प्रिया यस्य तस्य नित्योत्सवं गृहम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A farmer's house always has abundance. To be healthy is to be happy. If the husband and wife are loving, then there are festivities every day at home.
quotes.transliteration:
subhikṣaṃ kṛṣake nityaṃ nityaṃ sukhamarogiṇi |
bhāryā bhartuḥ priyā yasya tasya nityotsavaṃ gṛham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृत्कुम्भो जलपूर्णोह्यनास्थयोद्घटितमुखो यद्वत्
रिक्तः स्यादल्पदिनैर्जलप्रपूर्णो जलाप्लुतस्तद्वत् ।
प्रभुभक्त्या हृत् कुम्भः प्रपूरितो यत्रकुत्रचित् क्षिप्तः
रहितो भक्त्याऽपि भवेत् न तथास्यात्कृष्ण चरितेऽस्तः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you leave a mud pot filled with water unattended with the mouth open, in short period you will see that it will lose all water (to evaporation). On the other hand, if you take care of it by closing the lid, or refilling often, even little water will remain for long and never get empty. Just like that one must fill the heart with devotion towards God. Having done so, if one indulges in worldly pleasures, the devotion will vanish very soon. But if you continue to be keep Krishna in mind always, then the pot of devotion will never get empty.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtkumbho jalapūrṇohyanāsthayodghaṭitamukho yadvat
riktaḥ syādalpadinairjalaprapūrṇo jalāplutastadvat |
prabhubhaktyā hṛt kumbhaḥ prapūrito yatrakutracit kṣiptaḥ
rahito bhaktyāऽpi bhavet na tathāsyātkṛṣṇa cariteऽstaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न सत्यं केवलं सत्यमनृतं न तथानृतम् ।
हितं यत् सर्वलोकस्य तत्सत्यं शेषमन्यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just the facts will not make the complete truth. Anything that is not true is not false by default. There are always shades of grey. One thing is clear – what is good for the entire world is the truth, rest everything is not-truth.
quotes.transliteration:
na satyaṃ kevalaṃ satyamanṛtaṃ na tathānṛtam |
hitaṃ yat sarvalokasya tatsatyaṃ śeṣamanyathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाकाले म्रियते जंतुः विद्धः शरशतैरपि ।
कुशकंटकविद्धोऽपि प्राप्तकालो न जीवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When your time is not yet up, even if one throws a hundred arrows at you – nothing happens to you. However, when it is up, even a blade of grass can kill you.
quotes.transliteration:
nākāle mriyate jaṃtuḥ viddhaḥ śaraśatairapi |
kuśakaṃṭakaviddhoऽpi prāptakālo na jīvati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न गृहाणि न वस्त्राणि न प्राकारास्तिरस्क्रियाः ।
नेदृशा राजसत्काराः वृत्तमावरणं स्त्रियाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Houses, clothes, surrounding forts, veils and other royal protection can not protect a woman efficiently. It is the character of the woman that will protect her well.
quotes.transliteration:
na gṛhāṇi na vastrāṇi na prākārāstiraskriyāḥ |
nedṛśā rājasatkārāḥ vṛttamāvaraṇaṃ striyāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अपत्यदर्शनस्यार्थे प्राणानपि च या त्यजेत् ।
त्यजन्ति तामपि क्रूरा मातरं दारहेतवे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Parents risk their own lives to bring an offspring to this world. But sons so brought to this world abandon them for the sake of their wives.
quotes.transliteration:
apatyadarśanasyārthe prāṇānapi ca yā tyajet |
tyajanti tāmapi krūrā mātaraṃ dārahetave ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अपूर्वः कोऽपि कोशोऽयं विद्यते तव भारति ।
व्ययतो वृद्धिमायाति क्षयमायाति संचयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh Saraswathi, your treasure (wisdom) is great. It grows as you spend and depletes if you try to horde.
quotes.transliteration:
apūrvaḥ koऽpi kośoऽyaṃ vidyate tava bhārati |
vyayato vṛddhimāyāti kṣayamāyāti saṃcayāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न ह्यम्मयानि तीर्थानि न देवा मृच्छिलामयाः ।
ते पुनंत्युरुकालेन दर्शनादेव साधवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The holy water or the idols made of mud and stone will take a long time to cleanse one's sins. But virtuous people will cleanse sins by just by showing up (letting us see them).
quotes.transliteration:
na hyammayāni tīrthāni na devā mṛcchilāmayāḥ |
te punaṃtyurukālena darśanādeva sādhavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स जातो येन जातेन याति वंशः समुन्नतिं ।
परिवर्तनि संसारे मृतः को वा न जायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is remembered to be born if by his birth the family benefits and prospers. In this eternal world, what is so great is being re-born? Everyone does.
quotes.transliteration:
sa jāto yena jātena yāti vaṃśaḥ samunnatiṃ |
parivartani saṃsāre mṛtaḥ ko vā na jāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रभूतवयसः पुंसो धियः पाकः प्रवर्तते ।
जीर्णस्य चंदनतरोरामोद उपजायते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a man ages, his wisdom gets ripened. An old sandalwood tree is capable of producing the good smell only when it is old.
quotes.transliteration:
prabhūtavayasaḥ puṃso dhiyaḥ pākaḥ pravartate |
jīrṇasya caṃdanatarorāmoda upajāyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यद्वांछति दिवा मर्त्यो वीक्षते वा करोति वा ।
तत्स्वप्नेऽपि तदभ्यासाद्ब्रूते वाथ करोति वा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What one sees or does during the day is repeated by him at night during sleep in his dreams.
quotes.transliteration:
yadvāṃchati divā martyo vīkṣate vā karoti vā |
tatsvapneऽpi tadabhyāsādbrūte vātha karoti vā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असद्भिः शपथेनोक्तं जले लिखितमक्षरम् ।
सद्भिस्तु लीलया प्रोक्तं शिलालिखितमक्षरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When rogues proclaim their vows, it is as if written in water - good for nothing. But when virtuous people even make a passing remark, it is as if written on stone - they will live up to it.
quotes.transliteration:
asadbhiḥ śapathenoktaṃ jale likhitamakṣaram |
sadbhistu līlayā proktaṃ śilālikhitamakṣaram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्विविधो जायते व्याधिः शारीरो मानसस्तथा ।
परस्परं तयोर्जन्म निर्द्वंद्वं नोपलभ्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are two kinds of origins for disease – one is from he body and the other is mental. They are interdependent one each other. Without one, the other does not exist. All diseases are part physical and part mental. Solve one and the other gets solved by itself.
quotes.transliteration:
dvividho jāyate vyādhiḥ śārīro mānasastathā |
parasparaṃ tayorjanma nirdvaṃdvaṃ nopalabhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्रिमद्भवने दासः सेवां भक्त्यायुतः परां कुर्वन्
आदिवासं संतुद्यहि विश्रान्तौ सः परं सुखं मनुते ।
तद्वद्विहितं सकलं कार्यं संपाद्य कृतकृत्यः
परिलभ्य हि परितोषं परमेशेऽसौ परं सुखं लभते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In a wealthy man's house servants work all week and are very happy on the day of weekly holiday. Just like that we should work hard in this world and rest at the feet of the God happily after we have served all our duties earnestly.
quotes.transliteration:
śrimadbhavane dāsaḥ sevāṃ bhaktyāyutaḥ parāṃ kurvan
ādivāsaṃ saṃtudyahi viśrāntau saḥ paraṃ sukhaṃ manute |
tadvadvihitaṃ sakalaṃ kāryaṃ saṃpādya kṛtakṛtyaḥ
parilabhya hi paritoṣaṃ parameśeऽsau paraṃ sukhaṃ labhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भ्रमन् वनान्ते सुममंजरीषु
नेंदिंदिरो गंधफलीं चुचुंब ।
सा किं न रम्या स च किं न रंता
बलीयसी केवलमीश्वरेच्छा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: bhraman vanAnte suma manjarIShu A bee is going from flower to flower all over the forest. But it missed smelling the champaka flower. Isn't champaka fragrant enough? It is. Does the bee not know it? It does. Yet, the bee missed it. All of this happens only because God wants it that way.
quotes.transliteration:
bhraman vanānte sumamaṃjarīṣu
neṃdiṃdiro gaṃdhaphalīṃ cucuṃba |
sā kiṃ na ramyā sa ca kiṃ na raṃtā
balīyasī kevalamīśvarecchā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाद्रव्ये निहिता काचित्क्रिया फलवती भवेत् ।
न व्यापारशतेनापि शुकवत्पाठ्यते बकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not expend effort in trying to improve something that does not deserve the efforts (or simply can not change). No matter how hard you try, you can not teach a duck the same tricks you teach a parrot.
quotes.transliteration:
nādravye nihitā kācitkriyā phalavatī bhavet |
na vyāpāraśatenāpi śukavatpāṭhyate bakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्रोग्धव्यं न च मित्रेषु न विश्वस्तेषु कर्हिचित् ।
येषां चान्नानि भुंजीत यत्र स्यात् प्रतिश्रयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never cheat a friend, any one who believes in you, who have given you food or whose refuge you have taken.
quotes.transliteration:
drogdhavyaṃ na ca mitreṣu na viśvasteṣu karhicit |
yeṣāṃ cānnāni bhuṃjīta yatra syāt pratiśrayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दरिद्रता धीरतया विराजते
कुरूपता श्रीलतया विराजते ।
कुभोजनं चोष्णतया विराजते
कुवस्त्रता शुभ्रतया विराजते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Downside of poverty can to compensated by courage. If someone is not beautiful, then they can wear some ornaments. If food is not tasty, it can be remedied to some extent by heating. Clothes of bad quality can be kept clean to impress.
quotes.transliteration:
daridratā dhīratayā virājate
kurūpatā śrīlatayā virājate |
kubhojanaṃ coṣṇatayā virājate
kuvastratā śubhratayā virājate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदि न क्वापि विद्यायां सर्वथा क्रमते मतिः ।
मांत्रिकास्तु भविष्यामो योगिनो यतयोपि वा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: We could not learn any of the education streams since we could not understand them. But we will show off as the most knowledgeable people - magicians, saints or religious heads. That is when you know that you are living in the epoch of Kali (present times).
quotes.transliteration:
yadi na kvāpi vidyāyāṃ sarvathā kramate matiḥ |
māṃtrikāstu bhaviṣyāmo yogino yatayopi vā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बुधाग्रे न गुणान् ब्रूयात् साधु वेत्ति यतः स्वयं ।
मूर्खाग्रेऽपि च न ब्रूयात् बुधप्रोक्तं न वेत्ति सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not explain good qualities to a wise man - because he knows about them already. One must also not explain the same to a fool - because he will never understand the words of a wise man.
quotes.transliteration:
budhāgre na guṇān brūyāt sādhu vetti yataḥ svayaṃ |
mūrkhāgreऽpi ca na brūyāt budhaproktaṃ na vetti saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एकः संपन्नमश्नाति वस्ते वासश्च शोभनम् ।
योऽसंविभज्य भृत्येभ्यः को नृशंसतरस्ततः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Is there a bigger sinner than one who does not share his food (that too gourmet) and good clothes?
quotes.transliteration:
ekaḥ saṃpannamaśnāti vaste vāsaśca śobhanam |
yoऽsaṃvibhajya bhṛtyebhyaḥ ko nṛśaṃsatarastataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शत्रोरपि गुणा वाच्या दोषा वाच्या गुरोरपि ।
सर्वदा सर्वयत्नेन पुत्रे शिष्यवदाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A good quality has to be highlighted even if it is found in an enemy. If a vice is found in your teacher, that should be called out also. At all times, every effort must be made to treat a son like a student.
quotes.transliteration:
śatrorapi guṇā vācyā doṣā vācyā gurorapi |
sarvadā sarvayatnena putre śiṣyavadācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोहासिः स्पर्शमणेः स्पर्शात्स्वर्णासितां लभते ।
नपुनर्निहन्ति जन्तुं तद्वत्कृष्णाश्रितः साधुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: An iron sword will turn into a gold sword when it comes in contact with the touchstone. After that it can kill any being. Similarly anyone who comes takes refuge in Lord Krishna will become virtuous and never hurt anyone.
quotes.transliteration:
lohāsiḥ sparśamaṇeḥ sparśātsvarṇāsitāṃ labhate |
napunarnihanti jantuṃ tadvatkṛṣṇāśritaḥ sādhuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मस्तकन्यस्तभारादेर्दुःखमन्यैर्निवार्यते ।
क्षुधादिकृतदुःख तु विना स्वेन न केनचित् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you are carrying a burden on your head, someone can help you by taking part of it. But there are some troubles - of hunger and mind that no one else can help you with. You have get through it by yourself.
quotes.transliteration:
mastakanyastabhārāderduḥkhamanyairnivāryate |
kṣudhādikṛtaduḥkha tu vinā svena na kenacit ||

quotes.sanskritText:
य उद्यतमनादृत्य कीनाशमभियाचते ।
क्षीयते तद्यशः स्फीतं मानश्चावज्ञया हतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a person asks for help, it must be from someone who is an expert in the field. If you ask for help from a layman on a specialized subject, you will definitely fail. Your fame will be destroyed and pride hurt by ridicule.
quotes.transliteration:
ya udyatamanādṛtya kīnāśamabhiyācate |
kṣīyate tadyaśaḥ sphītaṃ mānaścāvajñayā hataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पषाणोऽम्भसि पतितो नहि बहुवर्षैरापि मृदुर्भवति
मृत्स्नास्यत्तु तदात्वे जलमिश्रा सुबहुमृद्वि ।
तद्वत् दृढहरिभक्तः छलैः प्रसङ्गैस्सुदुस्सहैर्व्यथितः
नस्यदमोभक्तः स्वल्पैरापि हेतुभिर्द्रुतं चलितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a stone is put in water, it will not melt for years. If you put a lump of mud, it will dirty the water almost immediately. This is the difference between a real devotee and a fake one. A real devotee will hold his belief in God through all miseries, troubles and illusions. A fake devotee will abandon his beliefs at the slightest discomfort.
quotes.transliteration:
paṣāṇoऽmbhasi patito nahi bahuvarṣairāpi mṛdurbhavati
mṛtsnāsyattu tadātve jalamiśrā subahumṛdvi |
tadvat dṛḍhaharibhaktaḥ chalaiḥ prasaṅgaissudussahairvyathitaḥ
nasyadamobhaktaḥ svalpairāpi hetubhirdrutaṃ calitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अविदितपरमानन्दो वदति जनो विषयमेव रमणीयम् ।
तिलतैलमेव मृष्टं येन न दृष्टं घृतं क्वापि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who have not experienced the unlimited supreme happiness will always be of the opinion that worldly pleasures give them more happiness. It is like the case of people who have never tasted ghee and hence consider sesame seed oil to be tasty.
quotes.transliteration:
aviditaparamānando vadati jano viṣayameva ramaṇīyam |
tilatailameva mṛṣṭaṃ yena na dṛṣṭaṃ ghṛtaṃ kvāpi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राप्य चलनधिकारान् शत्रुषु मित्रेषु बंधुवर्गेषु ।
नापकृतं नोपकृतं न सत्कृतं किं कृतं तेन ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Power is temporary. If one gets it and still does not use it to hurt his enemies, help his friends or favor his relatives - then what has he done?
quotes.transliteration:
prāpya calanadhikārān śatruṣu mitreṣu baṃdhuvargeṣu |
nāpakṛtaṃ nopakṛtaṃ na satkṛtaṃ kiṃ kṛtaṃ tena ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पक्षीणां बलमाकाशं मत्स्यानामुदकम् बलम् ।
बलं मूर्खस्य मौनित्वं तस्करस्यानृतं बलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Birds get their strength from wind, fish from water, fool from silence and a thief from lies.
quotes.transliteration:
pakṣīṇāṃ balamākāśaṃ matsyānāmudakam balam |
balaṃ mūrkhasya maunitvaṃ taskarasyānṛtaṃ balam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुभाषितं हारि विशत्यधो गलात्
न दुर्जनस्यार्करिपोरिवामृतम् ।
तदेव धत्ते हृदयेन सज्जनो
हरिर्महारत्नमिवातिनिर्मलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: tadeva dhatte hRudayena sajjano During the mythical churning of the ocean out came elixir. But it so happened that it would not go down the throat of Rahu (the enemy of Arka - Sun). Just like that anything good like a subhashita will not go down the intellect of wicked men. On the other hand virtuous people will take it to their heart. Just like what Vishnu did with the perfect koustubha gem.
quotes.transliteration:
subhāṣitaṃ hāri viśatyadho galāt
na durjanasyārkariporivāmṛtam |
tadeva dhatte hṛdayena sajjano
harirmahāratnamivātinirmalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दूरादुच्छ्रितपाणिराद्रनयनः प्रोच्छासितार्धासनो
गाढालिंगनतत्परः प्रियकथाप्रश्नेषु दत्तादरः।
अंतर्गूढविषो बहिर्मधुमयश्चातीव मायापटुः
को नामायमपूर्वनाटकविधिर्यः शिक्षितो दुर्जनैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: They raise both their hands from far off to welcome you. Their eyes are moist with tears when they see you. They happily give half their seat for you to sit. They are restless until they hug you tight. They show a lot of interest by esquiring about wellness of mutual friends. Inside they hide poison while their exterior is too sweet like honey. Such are the acts of villains. Wonder what is that stream of acting called which these rogues have so well mastered?
quotes.transliteration:
dūrāducchritapāṇirādranayanaḥ procchāsitārdhāsano
gāḍhāliṃganatatparaḥ priyakathāpraśneṣu dattādaraḥ|
aṃtargūḍhaviṣo bahirmadhumayaścātīva māyāpaṭuḥ
ko nāmāyamapūrvanāṭakavidhiryaḥ śikṣito durjanaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दूरस्थो ज्ञायते सर्वः पर्वते ज्वलनादिवत् ।
चूडामणिः शिरस्थोऽपि दृष्यते न स्वचक्षुषा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When things are far away, its a different perspective. Hence we can see things clearly - like a fire lit on top of a hill. But sometimes things very close to us can not be seen - like the gem on our crown can never be seen by our own eyes (without a mirror).
quotes.transliteration:
dūrastho jñāyate sarvaḥ parvate jvalanādivat |
cūḍāmaṇiḥ śirasthoऽpi dṛṣyate na svacakṣuṣā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नारिकेलसमाना हि दृश्यन्ते खलु सज्जनाः ।
अन्ये बदरिकातुल्या बहिरेव मनोहराः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is true that the virtuous can be compared to a coconut. They are tough and rough from the outside, but are sweet and tender inside. Other people are like the badarika fruit (berry of the jujube) which looks good only from outside.
quotes.transliteration:
nārikelasamānā hi dṛśyante khalu sajjanāḥ |
anye badarikātulyā bahireva manoharāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षुण्णोऽपि रोहति तरुः
क्षीणोऽप्युपचीयते पुनश्चन्द्रः ।
इति विमृशन्तः सन्तः
संतप्यन्ते न विधुरेषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A tree grows back after beign cut. The moon always grows back after the reducing cycle. Wise men know this and understand that life is similar. So, they do not grieve the departure of their loved ones.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣuṇṇoऽpi rohati taruḥ
kṣīṇoऽpyupacīyate punaścandraḥ |
iti vimṛśantaḥ santaḥ
saṃtapyante na vidhureṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षांत्या शुद्ध्यंति विद्वांसो दानेनकार्यकारिणः ।
प्रच्छन्नपापा जप्येन तपसा वेदवित्तमाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A scholar cleanses himself by forgiving. A sinner by resorting to charities. A secret sin is cleansed by meditation. Those who understand the vedas cleanse themselves by penances.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣāṃtyā śuddhyaṃti vidvāṃso dānenakāryakāriṇaḥ |
pracchannapāpā japyena tapasā vedavittamāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो ध्रुवाणि परित्यज्य अध्रुवाणि निषेवते।
ध्रुवाणि तस्य नश्यन्ति अध्रुवं नष्टमेव च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If one leaves the 'permanent' and runs behind the 'temporary' - he will end up losing both. The permanent is lost and the temporary would never have lasted anyway.
quotes.transliteration:
yo dhruvāṇi parityajya adhruvāṇi niṣevate|
dhruvāṇi tasya naśyanti adhruvaṃ naṣṭameva ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्माख्याने श्मशाने च रोगिणां या मतिर्भवेत् ।
सा सर्वदैव तिष्ठेच्छेत् को न मुच्येत बंधनात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: That wisdom that is realized during a discourse, at a funeral or when one is sick – if it stayed with a person through out his life, then who will not be liberated from all bondages?
quotes.transliteration:
dharmākhyāne śmaśāne ca rogiṇāṃ yā matirbhavet |
sā sarvadaiva tiṣṭhecchet ko na mucyeta baṃdhanāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देहदण्डनमात्रेण का मुक्तिरविवेकिनां ।
वल्मीकताडनादेव मृतः कुत्र महोरगः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: How can one get salvation only by hurting his body? One must be a fool to believe so. Will beating the ant hill kill the big snake? Body is the anthill. The attributes of the soul interested in the worldly pleasure is the snake. Until those attributes are squashed, the soul can not be liberated.
quotes.transliteration:
dehadaṇḍanamātreṇa kā muktiravivekināṃ |
valmīkatāḍanādeva mṛtaḥ kutra mahoragaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अबलस्य बलं राजा बालस्य रुदितं बलम् ।
बलं मूर्खस्य मौनित्वं तस्करस्यानृतं बलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king must be the power of the weak. A child gets its power from crying. A fool must use silence as his poer and ability tell lies is the power of thieves.
quotes.transliteration:
abalasya balaṃ rājā bālasya ruditaṃ balam |
balaṃ mūrkhasya maunitvaṃ taskarasyānṛtaṃ balam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थार्थी जीवलोकोऽयं श्मशानमपि सेवते ।
त्यक्त्वा जनयितारं स्वं निःस्वं गच्छति दूरतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are a few greedy sons (or daughters) in this world who choose to live (and earn) in a graveyard for the sake of money even if it means to abandon their poor and old parents.
quotes.transliteration:
arthārthī jīvalokoऽyaṃ śmaśānamapi sevate |
tyaktvā janayitāraṃ svaṃ niḥsvaṃ gacchati dūrataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गतेऽपि वयसि ग्राह्या विद्या सर्वात्मना बुधैः ।
यद्यपि स्यान्न फलदा सुलभा सान्यजन्मनि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men continue to learn from every one and get wiser even at a very old age. It is not a problem if their new wisdom does not give any results immediately. It will at least make it easier in the next birth.
quotes.transliteration:
gateऽpi vayasi grāhyā vidyā sarvātmanā budhaiḥ |
yadyapi syānna phaladā sulabhā sānyajanmani ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यवंतो जितक्रोधाः बलवंतो रणप्रियाः ।
क्षांतिशीलगुणोपेताः संधेयाः पुरुषोत्तमाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must befriend the virtuous whose qualities are: always truthful, never angry, strong, courageous at war, have patience, righteous.
quotes.transliteration:
satyavaṃto jitakrodhāḥ balavaṃto raṇapriyāḥ |
kṣāṃtiśīlaguṇopetāḥ saṃdheyāḥ puruṣottamāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न देवो विद्यते काष्ठे न पाषाणे न मृण्मये ।
भावे हि विद्यते देवस्तस्माद्भावो हि कारणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: God can not be found in a wood piece, stone or a mud idol. It is the belief that makes us feel the presence of God. Hence, only the feeling matters – not the material.
quotes.transliteration:
na devo vidyate kāṣṭhe na pāṣāṇe na mṛṇmaye |
bhāve hi vidyate devastasmādbhāvo hi kāraṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जिह्वा न वक्ति भगवद्गुणनामधेयं
चेतश्च स्मरति तच्चरणारविंदम् ।
कृष्णाय नो नमति यच्छिर एकादपि
तानानयध्वमसतोऽकृतविष्णुकृत्यान् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Bring them to me those rogues who have never done any of God's works - who have not used their tongue to utter the Lord's name, whose mind had never thought about the lotus feet of the Lord with reverence, who have never bowed their head in respect. (as told by Yama, the God of Death)
quotes.transliteration:
jihvā na vakti bhagavadguṇanāmadheyaṃ
cetaśca smarati taccaraṇāraviṃdam |
kṛṣṇāya no namati yacchira ekādapi
tānānayadhvamasatoऽkṛtaviṣṇukṛtyān ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निकेतनान्तर्ध्वान्तन्तु स्वल्प दीपेन नश्यति ।
ईशकृपाप्रसादेनानन्तपापानि सर्वदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A small wick light is enough to eliminate darkness from the insides of a house. Just like that even a small meeting (favor) from God is enough to eliminate the ill-effects of all the sins committed in all the previous lives.
quotes.transliteration:
niketanāntardhvāntantu svalpa dīpena naśyati |
īśakṛpāprasādenānantapāpāni sarvadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उच्छैर्गतिर्जगति सिध्यति धर्मतश्चेत्
तस्य प्रमा च वचनैः कृतकेतरैश्चेत् ।
तेषां प्रकाशनदशा च महीसुरैश्चेत्
तानंतरेण निपतेत् क्व नु मत्प्रणामः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is well understood that a good death is obtained only by following Dharma through out the life. That is possible only by reciting and understanding the scriptures (including the Vedas). That is achieved only the help of the Gods on earth who illuminate the directions here with they jist of the scriptures (perhaps teachers). So, who else should I pay my salutations to if not for them?
quotes.transliteration:
ucchairgatirjagati sidhyati dharmataścet
tasya pramā ca vacanaiḥ kṛtaketaraiścet |
teṣāṃ prakāśanadaśā ca mahīsuraiścet
tānaṃtareṇa nipatet kva nu matpraṇāmaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मिथ्याप्रशंसा खलु नाम कष्टा ।
येषां तु मिथ्यावचनेषु भक्तिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very hard for people to bear when others praise you falsely. But still, people love to hear the non-truth.
quotes.transliteration:
mithyāpraśaṃsā khalu nāma kaṣṭā |
yeṣāṃ tu mithyāvacaneṣu bhaktiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रायेण मनुजा लोके लोकतत्त्व विचक्षणाः ।
समुद्धरंति ह्यात्मानमात्मानैवाशुभाशयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: At some point, wise people of the world will understand the nature (and rules) of the world and save themselves from the misery they are in.
quotes.transliteration:
prāyeṇa manujā loke lokatattva vicakṣaṇāḥ |
samuddharaṃti hyātmānamātmānaivāśubhāśayāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ॠतामृताभ्यां जीवेत्तु मृतेन प्रमृतेन वा ।
सत्यानृताभ्यां अपि वा न श्ववृत्त्या कदाचन ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A household man can lead his life by the following ways. He can get food by gathering grains off a field. Someone may give him food without asking. He may beg for food. He can cultivate crops. He can do trade. But he must not do any servitude (like a dog).
quotes.transliteration:
ṝtāmṛtābhyāṃ jīvettu mṛtena pramṛtena vā |
satyānṛtābhyāṃ api vā na śvavṛttyā kadācana ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुकृतेन कुले जन्म सुकृतेन सुभाषितम् ।
सुकृतेन सती भार्या सुकृतेन कृती सुतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is only by good fortune (as a result of good karma in previous lives) that one gets to be born in a good family, collect good subhashitas, get married to a lady of good character and get a son who will achieve something substantial.
quotes.transliteration:
sukṛtena kule janma sukṛtena subhāṣitam |
sukṛtena satī bhāryā sukṛtena kṛtī sutaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ददाति प्रतिगृह्णाति गुह्यमाख्याति पृच्छति ।
भुंक्ते भोजयते चैव षड्विधं प्रीतिलक्षणम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following six are the tell-tale signs of love - sharing (giving and taking), telling and keeping secrets, sharing food (feeding and being fed).
quotes.transliteration:
dadāti pratigṛhṇāti guhyamākhyāti pṛcchati |
bhuṃkte bhojayate caiva ṣaḍvidhaṃ prītilakṣaṇam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पाषाणाः सर्व एवैते पद्मरागेषु को गुणः ।
प्रकाशः कश्चिदत्रस्तेऽपरत्र सन विद्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Ultimately all gems are stones. What is so great about rubies (padmaraaga)? Yes, these have a glitter in them that other stones don't have. It is very usual for people to generalize and lose sight of a gem among the ordinary - people, qualities etc...
quotes.transliteration:
pāṣāṇāḥ sarva evaite padmarāgeṣu ko guṇaḥ |
prakāśaḥ kaścidatrasteऽparatra sana vidyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कालकर्मगुणाधीनो देहोऽयं पांचभौतिकः ।
कथामन्यांस्तु गोपयेत्सर्पग्रस्तो यथा परम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Every being in this world is not free. It is always controlled by time, actions and qualities. This body is made of five elements. How can such a being ever help another being escape from this plight? Can an animal (mice) caught in the mouth of a snake save anything else?
quotes.transliteration:
kālakarmaguṇādhīno dehoऽyaṃ pāṃcabhautikaḥ |
kathāmanyāṃstu gopayetsarpagrasto yathā param ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भेतव्यं नृपतेस्ततः सचिवतो राज्ञस्ततो वल्लभात्
अन्येभ्यश्च वसंति येऽस्य भवने लब्ध प्रसादा विटाः ।
दैन्यादुन्मुखदर्शनापलपनैः पिंडार्थमायस्यतः
सेवां लाघवकारिणीं कृतधियः स्थाने श्ववृत्तिं विदुः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you are servant, you have to be afraid of the king, of the ministers, of people close to the king, of all people who live in the palace. One would have look at others with pitiful eyes. All these for just a handful of food. What wise men say about servitude is true - that it is similar to the life of a dog.
quotes.transliteration:
bhetavyaṃ nṛpatestataḥ sacivato rājñastato vallabhāt
anyebhyaśca vasaṃti yeऽsya bhavane labdha prasādā viṭāḥ |
dainyādunmukhadarśanāpalapanaiḥ piṃḍārthamāyasyataḥ
sevāṃ lāghavakāriṇīṃ kṛtadhiyaḥ sthāne śvavṛttiṃ viduḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नरकस्थोऽपि देहं वै न पुमांस्त्यक्तुमिच्छति ।
नारक्यां निर्वृतौ सत्यां देवमायाविमोहितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person who has parted ways from belief in God (and his creation) will not even give up (the love towards) his body even if he is living in hell. He will find something to live for even there.
quotes.transliteration:
narakasthoऽpi dehaṃ vai na pumāṃstyaktumicchati |
nārakyāṃ nirvṛtau satyāṃ devamāyāvimohitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गणेशः स्तौति मार्जारं स्ववाहस्याभिरक्षणे ।
महानपि प्रसंगेन नीचं सेवितुमिच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Lord Ganesha also appeases (prays to) a cat in order to protect his vehicle - the mouse. Even powerful people at appropriate times will have to serve lowly beings.
quotes.transliteration:
gaṇeśaḥ stauti mārjāraṃ svavāhasyābhirakṣaṇe |
mahānapi prasaṃgena nīcaṃ sevitumicchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तरुमूले संस्थायाशोभनपतगान् सनामतालिकया
दूरीकरोति मनुजो यथा तथा हीशचिन्तने मग्नः ।
परमेशस्यहि नाम्नां सतालिकेनेह मधुरगानेन
उत्सारयति निरोधैस्स्वान्तस्य च वासनाः कुपरिणामाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One who wants to sleep under a tree will call names and clap to drive out all the inauspicious birds (cacophony - crows, owls, etc..). Similarly, if one is interested in deep thinking about God, then he must sing songs about the Lord himself to drive out all the evil influences from his surroundings as well as with in himself.
quotes.transliteration:
tarumūle saṃsthāyāśobhanapatagān sanāmatālikayā
dūrīkaroti manujo yathā tathā hīśacintane magnaḥ |
parameśasyahi nāmnāṃ satālikeneha madhuragānena
utsārayati nirodhaissvāntasya ca vāsanāḥ kupariṇāmāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जाग्रतामपि निद्रा यः पश्यतामपि सोंऽधता ।
श्रुते सत्यपि जाड्यं यः प्रकाशेऽपि च यस्तमः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When under the influence of desire and attachment a man is sleeping even when awake, blind even when he can see, inanimate even when he can hear the truth. He sees darkness when there is so much light.
quotes.transliteration:
jāgratāmapi nidrā yaḥ paśyatāmapi soṃऽdhatā |
śrute satyapi jāḍyaṃ yaḥ prakāśeऽpi ca yastamaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तीर्थानि दीर्घाध्वपरिश्रमाणि
बहुव्ययानि क्रतुडंबराणि ।
तपांसि मुक्त्वा तनुशॊषणानि
हिंसाविरामे रमतां मतिर्वः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is the way to attain salvation? To do a pilgrimage, one has to tread a very difficult and dangerous path which is very tiresome. To do rituals, one has to spend a lot of money. To do penance, one has to punish his body a lot. So wise men resort to the best way - shun all kinds of violence.
quotes.transliteration:
tīrthāni dīrghādhvapariśramāṇi
bahuvyayāni kratuḍaṃbarāṇi |
tapāṃsi muktvā tanuśaॊṣaṇāni
hiṃsāvirāme ramatāṃ matirvaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इह विरचयन् साध्वीं शिष्यः क्रियां न निवार्यते
त्यजति तु यदा मार्गं मोहात्तदा गुरुरंकुशः ।
विनयरुचयस्तस्मात्संतः सदैव निरंकुशाः
परतरमतः स्वातंत्रेभ्यो वयं हि पराञ्मुखाः॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a student is going down the correct path, no one will stop him. If he strays, a teacher will punish him with love. In that respect every one is free as long as they are going down the right path (of Dharma). What more freedom does one want? We are happy in this type of freedom which is in our teacher's hands.
quotes.transliteration:
iha viracayan sādhvīṃ śiṣyaḥ kriyāṃ na nivāryate
tyajati tu yadā mārgaṃ mohāttadā gururaṃkuśaḥ |
vinayarucayastasmātsaṃtaḥ sadaiva niraṃkuśāḥ
parataramataḥ svātaṃtrebhyo vayaṃ hi parāñmukhāḥ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रजोभिः समसंख्याकाः पार्थिवैरिह जंतवः ।
तेषां ये केचनेहंते श्रेयो वै मनुजादयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world there are as many beings as particles of dust. Among them only a handful of them are capable of thinking about their own welfare. Mankind is one among them.
quotes.transliteration:
rajobhiḥ samasaṃkhyākāḥ pārthivairiha jaṃtavaḥ |
teṣāṃ ye kecanehaṃte śreyo vai manujādayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
युक्ति युक्तं प्रगृह्णीयात् बालादपि विचक्षणः ।
रवेरविषयं वस्तु किं न दीपः प्रकाशयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if a small boy says something appropriate - one must listen and take note. Isn't a small wick capable of lighting up places that are not lit up by the Sun?
quotes.transliteration:
yukti yuktaṃ pragṛhṇīyāt bālādapi vicakṣaṇaḥ |
raveraviṣayaṃ vastu kiṃ na dīpaḥ prakāśayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संतोषक्षतये पुंसामाकस्मिकधनागमः ।
सरसां सेतुभेदाय वर्षौघः स च न स्थिरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If people are stuck in misery, they will feel very happy when they get an unexpected windfall. But they do not realize that the source of money is not steady. An unexpected torrential rain will break the leeway of the tank and cause more harm than good.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃtoṣakṣataye puṃsāmākasmikadhanāgamaḥ |
sarasāṃ setubhedāya varṣaughaḥ sa ca na sthiraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गौरवं प्राप्यते दानान्न तु वित्तस्य संचयात् ।
स्थितिरुच्छैः पयोदानां पयोधीनामधः स्थितिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One gets respect by giving away wealth in charity, not by accumulating wealth. Clouds that give water has its place high in the sky. The seas that collect water has its place below.
quotes.transliteration:
gauravaṃ prāpyate dānānna tu vittasya saṃcayāt |
sthitirucchaiḥ payodānāṃ payodhīnāmadhaḥ sthitiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कृष्णार्पणं विदधतो नास्मादन्यः सुखावहो मार्गः ।
सर्वस्वं कृष्णगतं नह्यवशिष्टं ममत्वाय ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All that we have is offered to Krishna. There is nothing left beyond it. If we live life with this in mind, we will be very happy. When everything we see is His, there is nothing left to fight for for you and me.
quotes.transliteration:
kṛṣṇārpaṇaṃ vidadhato nāsmādanyaḥ sukhāvaho mārgaḥ |
sarvasvaṃ kṛṣṇagataṃ nahyavaśiṣṭaṃ mamatvāya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न देवाय न विप्राय न बंधुभ्यो न चात्मने ।
कृपणस्य धनं याति वह्नितस्करपार्थिवैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The wealth that a miser accumulates is not used for Gods, not brahmins, not his relatives – not even for himself. It will most likely be consumed by fire, or a thief or confiscated by the king.
quotes.transliteration:
na devāya na viprāya na baṃdhubhyo na cātmane |
kṛpaṇasya dhanaṃ yāti vahnitaskarapārthivaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रत्यहं प्रत्यवेक्षेत नरश्चरितमात्मनः ।
किं नु मे पशुभिस्तुल्यं किं नु सत्पुरुषैरिति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Everyday one must do introspection about his actions - were they like an animal or were they like a virtuous person?
quotes.transliteration:
pratyahaṃ pratyavekṣeta naraścaritamātmanaḥ |
kiṃ nu me paśubhistulyaṃ kiṃ nu satpuruṣairiti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्य न विपदि विषादः संपदि हर्षो रणे न भीरुत्वम् ।
तं भुवनत्रयतिलकं जनयति जननी सुतं विरलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is very rare that a mother gives birth to a son who is not sad when faced with trouble, not overly joyous when prosperous and not a coward in battlefield.
quotes.transliteration:
yasya na vipadi viṣādaḥ saṃpadi harṣo raṇe na bhīrutvam |
taṃ bhuvanatrayatilakaṃ janayati jananī sutaṃ viralam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यावत्स्वस्थमिदं देहं यावन्मृत्युश्च दूरतः ।
तावदात्महितं कुर्यात् प्राणाते किं करिष्यसि॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When you are in good health and death is still far away, you must ensure that you act in your best interest. What can one do at the end of his life?
quotes.transliteration:
yāvatsvasthamidaṃ dehaṃ yāvanmṛtyuśca dūrataḥ |
tāvadātmahitaṃ kuryāt prāṇāte kiṃ kariṣyasi||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः स्वभावो हि यस्यास्ति स नित्यं दुरतिक्रमः ।
श्वा यदि क्रियते राजा स किं नाश्नात्युपानहम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People will follow their innate qualities everyday no matter what environment they are put into. If you make a dog the king - will it stop eating footwear?
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ svabhāvo hi yasyāsti sa nityaṃ duratikramaḥ |
śvā yadi kriyate rājā sa kiṃ nāśnātyupānaham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कायः सन्निहितापायः संपदः पदमापदाम् ।
समागमाः सापगमाः सर्वमुत्पादि भंगुरम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: We have a wonderful body. But there is always danger to it right round the corner. Misery follows closes on the heels of prosperity. All unions are followed by separation. Anything that is made will be destroyed.
quotes.transliteration:
kāyaḥ sannihitāpāyaḥ saṃpadaḥ padamāpadām |
samāgamāḥ sāpagamāḥ sarvamutpādi bhaṃguram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणैः शर्वकल्पोऽपि सीदत्येव निराश्रयः ।
अनर्घमपि माणिक्यं हेमाश्रयमपेक्षते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if a man has great qualities to be almost equal to Lord Shiva, he will wilt (decline) if he is not properly supported (by family, state, etc..). Even a extremely rare gem needs the support of gold to shine as an ornament.
quotes.transliteration:
guṇaiḥ śarvakalpoऽpi sīdatyeva nirāśrayaḥ |
anarghamapi māṇikyaṃ hemāśrayamapekṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जिता धनवता दारा जिता वस्त्रवता सभा ।
जितो ह्यभ्यागतो गोभिर्दारिद्र्यं कृषिभिर्जितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One can get a good wife by being wealthy. One can control a gathering if he wears good clothes. Guests can be pleased if one has cows in his house. One can overcome poverty if they take up agriculture.
quotes.transliteration:
jitā dhanavatā dārā jitā vastravatā sabhā |
jito hyabhyāgato gobhirdāridryaṃ kṛṣibhirjitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वहिंसानिवृत्ता ये नराः सर्वंसहाश्च ये ।
सर्वस्याश्रयभूताश्च ते नराः स्वर्गगामिनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who renounce every form of violence, face all miseries with courage and patience and give shelter to all who seek refuge - will go to heaven.
quotes.transliteration:
sarvahiṃsānivṛttā ye narāḥ sarvaṃsahāśca ye |
sarvasyāśrayabhūtāśca te narāḥ svargagāminaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
फेत्कुर्वंत्यपि फेरवोऽतिपरुषं चेत् पार्श्वयोराश्रिताः
काकाश्चेदुपरि स्थिताः प्रतिदिशं कुर्वंति कोलाहलम् ।
मंदं मंदमुपांतमीलितदृशो मार्गे पदं न्यस्यतः
किं भेदं समुपैति कुंजरपतेर्मानोन्नता भ्रूलता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Let the jackals taking shelter on either sides of the elephant do their harshest noises. Let the crows on top create havoc. The elephant sees the path it wants to go with its half closed eyes and puts one step on another being least bothered about the distractions. It will not even break its eyebrows. Such is the concentration and steadiness of virtuous men.
quotes.transliteration:
phetkurvaṃtyapi pheravoऽtiparuṣaṃ cet pārśvayorāśritāḥ
kākāścedupari sthitāḥ pratidiśaṃ kurvaṃti kolāhalam |
maṃdaṃ maṃdamupāṃtamīlitadṛśo mārge padaṃ nyasyataḥ
kiṃ bhedaṃ samupaiti kuṃjarapatermānonnatā bhrūlatā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एवं बलिभ्यो बलिनः संति राघवनंदन ।
नावज्ञा हि परे कार्या य इच्छेत् प्रियमात्मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Like this, there will always be someone stronger than you no matter how strong you are. So, if you care about your well being, you must be aware of capabilities of others
quotes.transliteration:
evaṃ balibhyo balinaḥ saṃti rāghavanaṃdana |
nāvajñā hi pare kāryā ya icchet priyamātmanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाणां यस्यैकोऽपि न विद्यते ।
अजागलस्तनस्येव तस्य जन्म निरर्थकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharma, wealth, pleasures and salvation are the four goals of mankind. If one is not able to achieve any one of them in his life, then his whole life is a waste – just like the flesh at the neck of a goat. It looks like an udder, but does not have milk.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmārthakāmamokṣāṇāṃ yasyaikoऽpi na vidyate |
ajāgalastanasyeva tasya janma nirarthakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
साक्षरा विपरीताश्चेद्राक्षसा एव केवलम् ।
सरसो विपरीतश्चेत् सरसत्वं न मुंचति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person may be educated. But under stress if he is not able to control himself, then he will only be a 'rakshasa' - evil. But on the other hand, if a mild person is able to retain his composure he will be called 'sarasatva' even if he is not educated. But one with mild manners (sarasa) will retain his mildness (sarasatva, close to goddess of wisdom.)]
quotes.transliteration:
sākṣarā viparītāścedrākṣasā eva kevalam |
saraso viparītaścet sarasatvaṃ na muṃcati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
औत्सुक्यमात्रमवसाययति प्रतिष्ठा
क्लिश्नाति लब्धपरिपालनवृत्तिरेवं ।
नातिश्रमापनयनाय न च श्रमाय
राज्यं स्वहस्तधृतदंडमिवातपत्रम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Before becoming a king, a prince is restless looking forward to the power. Once he gets the power, with it comes great responsibility. Also he will be anxious to retain the power. He would have just traded one anxiety for other. Holding the umbrella will give you respite from sun but will make you tired because of effort involved in holding it up. Running a state is similar to that.
quotes.transliteration:
autsukyamātramavasāyayati pratiṣṭhā
kliśnāti labdhaparipālanavṛttirevaṃ |
nātiśramāpanayanāya na ca śramāya
rājyaṃ svahastadhṛtadaṃḍamivātapatram ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तावद्धर्मकथा मनोभवरुचिर्मोक्षस्पृहा जायते
यावत्तृप्तिसुखोदयेन न जनः क्षुत् क्षामकुक्षिः क्षणम् ।
प्राप्ते भोजनचिंतनस्य समये वित्तं निमित्तं विना
धर्मे कस्य धियः स्मरं स्मरति कः केनेक्ष्यते मोक्षभूः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: prApte bhojanachintanasya samaye vittam nimittam vinA Kama(luxuries), stories about dharma and salvation make sense only when one the carnal necessities (hunger) are satisfied. Who can think about dharma when you are hungry and it is lunch (or dinner) time? Who can even think of other luxuries of life? Who can even think about salvation?
quotes.transliteration:
tāvaddharmakathā manobhavarucirmokṣaspṛhā jāyate
yāvattṛptisukhodayena na janaḥ kṣut kṣāmakukṣiḥ kṣaṇam |
prāpte bhojanaciṃtanasya samaye vittaṃ nimittaṃ vinā
dharme kasya dhiyaḥ smaraṃ smarati kaḥ kenekṣyate mokṣabhūḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्वैत्यं कल्पय कज्जले कपिकुलेष्वारोपयाचापलं
कोदण्डे जनयार्जवं विरचय ग्राव्णां गणे मार्दवम् ।
निंबे साधय माधुरीं सुरभितामादौ रसोने कुरु
प्रेमाणं गणिकाजनेऽपि चतुरः पश्चात् सखे द्रक्ष्यसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: shvaityam kalpaya kajjale kapi kuleShu aaropaya achApalam Dear friend, if you are very smart then try changing these - make kajal white, bring order and calmness to a group of monkeys, make the bow straight, make a hard rock soft, bring sweetness to neem, bring fragrance to garlic. At this point you will realize that all these may be possible, but it is not possible to find love in the heart of a prostitute.
quotes.transliteration:
śvaityaṃ kalpaya kajjale kapikuleṣvāropayācāpalaṃ
kodaṇḍe janayārjavaṃ viracaya grāvṇāṃ gaṇe mārdavam |
niṃbe sādhaya mādhurīṃ surabhitāmādau rasone kuru
premāṇaṃ gaṇikājaneऽpi caturaḥ paścāt sakhe drakṣyasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बलिनो बलिनः स्निह्यंत्यबलं तु न गृह्णते ।
दावं दीपयते चण्डो दीपं व्याहन्ति मारुतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The strong and powerful always make friendship with equals. They will not care about the weak. Wind will give more power to a equally powerful forest fire, but will kill a weak lamp light.
quotes.transliteration:
balino balinaḥ snihyaṃtyabalaṃ tu na gṛhṇate |
dāvaṃ dīpayate caṇḍo dīpaṃ vyāhanti mārutaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
घृतकुंभसमा नारी तप्तांगारसमः पुमान् ।
तस्माद्घृतं च वह्निं च नैकत्र स्थापयेद् बुधः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A woman is like a pot of ghee and man is like a ball of fire. If they come in contact, it will create havoc. Hence wise people will not keep them together in the same place.
quotes.transliteration:
ghṛtakuṃbhasamā nārī taptāṃgārasamaḥ pumān |
tasmādghṛtaṃ ca vahniṃ ca naikatra sthāpayed budhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
औषधार्थसुमंत्राणां बुद्धेश्चैव महात्मनाम् ।
असाध्यं नास्ति लोकेऽत्र यद्ब्रह्मांडस्य मध्यगम्॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is nothing in this world that is impossible for - good medicine, lots of money, a sound genuine plan (not sinister) and wisdom of the virtuous men.
quotes.transliteration:
auṣadhārthasumaṃtrāṇāṃ buddheścaiva mahātmanām |
asādhyaṃ nāsti lokeऽtra yadbrahmāṃḍasya madhyagam||

quotes.sanskritText:
मातृवत् परदारांश्च परद्रव्याणि लोष्टवत् ।
आत्मवत् सर्वभूतानि यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The right way to look at other people's wives is as if she is your mother, other people's wealth as if it is lump of mud, all other beings as if they were you.
quotes.transliteration:
mātṛvat paradārāṃśca paradravyāṇi loṣṭavat |
ātmavat sarvabhūtāni yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संतुष्यत्युत्तमः स्तुत्या धनेन महताऽधमः ।
प्रसीदंति जपैर्देवा बलिर्भूतविग्रहाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The Virtuous people are pleased by mere good words. A lowly person is mighty pleased with money. Gods are pleased with penance, while the demons are pleased by offering sacrifices.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃtuṣyatyuttamaḥ stutyā dhanena mahatāऽdhamaḥ |
prasīdaṃti japairdevā balirbhūtavigrahāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तावद्भयाद्धि भेतव्यं यावद्भयमनागतम् ।
आगतं तु भयं वीक्ष्य प्रहर्तव्यमभीतवत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must be afraid of troubles only until they have not occurred. Once you face the troubles, they must be dealt with in order to over come it effectively. No fear.
quotes.transliteration:
tāvadbhayāddhi bhetavyaṃ yāvadbhayamanāgatam |
āgataṃ tu bhayaṃ vīkṣya prahartavyamabhītavat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यपि च सुकृतकर्मणि दुर्नीतिश्चेत् श्रियं हरत्येव ।
तैलैः सदोपयुक्तां दीपशिखां विदलयति हि वातालिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if one has a lot of good karma on his side, if he follows the path of sins, then all his wealth will vanish. A wick will burn well when it has enough oil. But when the wind is strong, the flame dies even with oil.
quotes.transliteration:
satyapi ca sukṛtakarmaṇi durnītiścet śriyaṃ haratyeva |
tailaiḥ sadopayuktāṃ dīpaśikhāṃ vidalayati hi vātāliḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कर्तव्यमेव कर्तव्यं प्राणैः कंठगतैरपि ।
अकर्तव्यं न कर्तव्यं प्राणैः कंठगतैरपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must always do his duty as per Dharma even when his life is coming to an end. Similarly, even at that time - one must never do anything that is forbidden by Dharma.
quotes.transliteration:
kartavyameva kartavyaṃ prāṇaiḥ kaṃṭhagatairapi |
akartavyaṃ na kartavyaṃ prāṇaiḥ kaṃṭhagatairapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परदेशगोह्यनेकान्दृष्ट्वा मार्गान् भ्रमाकुलो भवति
स्वस्थानं प्रतिगंतुं तज्ञं पृष्ट्वैव निर्भ्रमो भवति ।
एवं मार्गान्विविधान् भ्रमावहान्संसरन् मनुष्योऽपि
लब्ध्वा गुरुं हि तज्ञं लब्धा पश्चात्स्वधामसुखरूपम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a traveller goes to a new city, he will be confused with many similar looking paths in that city. To reach his destination, he will have to consult and follow a local expert. Just like that, every man gets confused in this world regarding the path to be taken for complete happiness. He has to find and learn from an able teacher. Find the teacher, and it is easy to find the happiness (God).
quotes.transliteration:
paradeśagohyanekāndṛṣṭvā mārgān bhramākulo bhavati
svasthānaṃ pratigaṃtuṃ tajñaṃ pṛṣṭvaiva nirbhramo bhavati |
evaṃ mārgānvividhān bhramāvahānsaṃsaran manuṣyoऽpi
labdhvā guruṃ hi tajñaṃ labdhā paścātsvadhāmasukharūpam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्याधेस्तत्वपरिज्ञानं वेदनायश्च निग्रहः ।
एतद्वैद्यस्य वैद्यत्वं न वैद्यः प्रभुरायुषः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Diagnosing a illness, and alleviating pain are the expertise and duties of a doctor. But he is not the master of a person's longevity (or life).
quotes.transliteration:
vyādhestatvaparijñānaṃ vedanāyaśca nigrahaḥ |
etadvaidyasya vaidyatvaṃ na vaidyaḥ prabhurāyuṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जानीयात्संगरे भृत्यान् बांधवान् व्यसनागमे ।
आपत्कालेषु मित्राणि भार्यां च विभवक्षये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One knows the true character of a servant during war, of relatives when you get sick, of friends when you are in trouble and of your wife when you have lost all money (and fortune).
quotes.transliteration:
jānīyātsaṃgare bhṛtyān bāṃdhavān vyasanāgame |
āpatkāleṣu mitrāṇi bhāryāṃ ca vibhavakṣaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यो हि दत्वा गजश्रेष्टं कक्ष्यायां कुरुते मनः ।
रज्जुस्नेहेन किं तस्य त्यजतः कुंजरोत्तमम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is an irony that people set their eyes on the girdle after having let the elephant go. What good is the rope that ties the elephant after giving up on the great elephant has gone?
quotes.transliteration:
yo hi datvā gajaśreṣṭaṃ kakṣyāyāṃ kurute manaḥ |
rajjusnehena kiṃ tasya tyajataḥ kuṃjarottamam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गेहाधीशः सावधानश्चेत् चोरा न विशन्तिहि ।
अन्तः सौजन्यचोरोऽसद्विचारो न विशेत्तदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If the head of the family is careful, thieves will not be able to get into the house. This applies to thieves who rob the family of their virtuous ways by corrupting their thought with rogue ideas.
quotes.transliteration:
gehādhīśaḥ sāvadhānaścet corā na viśantihi |
antaḥ saujanyacoroऽsadvicāro na viśettadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्मिन् देशे न संमानो न मित्राणि न बांधवाः ।
न च विद्यागमः कश्चित्तं देशं परिवर्जयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never live in a country where he does not have respect, no friends, no relatives, no opportunities to study. He must leave such a state immediately.
quotes.transliteration:
yasmin deśe na saṃmāno na mitrāṇi na bāṃdhavāḥ |
na ca vidyāgamaḥ kaścittaṃ deśaṃ parivarjayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनंतरत्नप्रभवस्य यस्य
हिमं न सौभाग्यविलोपि जातं ।
एको हि दोषो गुणसन्निपाते
निमज्जतींदोः किरणेष्वांकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: ananta ratna prabhAvasya yasya The Himalaya has numerous virtuous qualities each one like a gem. His greatness is not concealed in spite of having snow all over him. His virtuous qualities are so many that it is not possible to see any vice that may exist. His glory is clearly evident in the moon light.
quotes.transliteration:
anaṃtaratnaprabhavasya yasya
himaṃ na saubhāgyavilopi jātaṃ |
eko hi doṣo guṇasannipāte
nimajjatīṃdoḥ kiraṇeṣvāṃkaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
छिंत्ते मोहं चित्प्रकर्षं प्रयुंक्ते
सूते सूक्तिं सूयते या पुमर्थान् ।
प्रीतिं कीर्तिं प्राप्तुकामेन सैषा
शाब्दी शुद्धिः शारदेवास्तु सेव्या ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Chinte moham chit prakarSham prayukte Good language can change lives. It let's you lose any attachment to worldly possessions. It illuminates your mind. It makes you tell good things. It helps you achieve your goal. It will help you get love and fame. using good language is equal to worshiping Goddess Sharada herself.
quotes.transliteration:
chiṃtte mohaṃ citprakarṣaṃ prayuṃkte
sūte sūktiṃ sūyate yā pumarthān |
prītiṃ kīrtiṃ prāptukāmena saiṣā
śābdī śuddhiḥ śāradevāstu sevyā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथाश्व रथहीनास्तु रथो वाश्वैर्यथा विना ।
एवं तपोऽप्यविद्यस्य विद्यवाऽप्यतपस्विनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like horses without chariot and chariot without horses are useless, knowledge and penance are useless without each other.
quotes.transliteration:
yathāśva rathahīnāstu ratho vāśvairyathā vinā |
evaṃ tapoऽpyavidyasya vidyavāऽpyatapasvinaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इदमस्खलितं धारय वारय परुषाक्षरा वाचः ।
एकः सकलजनानां जगति रिपुः परुषवाक् पुरुषः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Don't let this slip your mind. One must never use harsh words. If one uses it, he will be hated by the entire world.
quotes.transliteration:
idamaskhalitaṃ dhāraya vāraya paruṣākṣarā vācaḥ |
ekaḥ sakalajanānāṃ jagati ripuḥ paruṣavāk puruṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
समाश्रयबलादेव गरुडं यान्तमध्वनि ।
पिनाकपाणिस्थः कुशलं पृष्टवानहिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is the power of the host that a refugee inherits. Otherwise how can a snake say hello to an eagle and have a chit-chat? The snake in context is the one held by Lord Shiva as decoration around his neck and the eagle is Garuda.
quotes.transliteration:
samāśrayabalādeva garuḍaṃ yāntamadhvani |
pinākapāṇisthaḥ kuśalaṃ pṛṣṭavānahiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इदमेव हि पाण्डित्यं चातुर्यमिदमेव हि ।
इदमेव सुबुद्धित्त्वमायादल्पतरो व्ययः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This is scholarly wisdom. This is intelligence. This is called having a sound mind - Never let expenditure grow bigger than income.
quotes.transliteration:
idameva hi pāṇḍityaṃ cāturyamidameva hi |
idameva subuddhittvamāyādalpataro vyayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न विना पार्थिवो भृत्यैर्न भृत्याः पार्थिवं विना ।
तेषां च व्यवहारोऽयं परस्परनिबंधनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The King can not lead a king's life without the servants. The servants owe their livelyhood to the king. Their relationship is therefore mutually dependent - equally.
quotes.transliteration:
na vinā pārthivo bhṛtyairna bhṛtyāḥ pārthivaṃ vinā |
teṣāṃ ca vyavahāroऽyaṃ parasparanibaṃdhanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुष्टा भार्या शठं मित्रं भृत्यश्चोत्तरदायकः ।
ससर्पे च गृहे वासो मृत्युरेव न संशयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Death is certain (soon) for a person whose wife is wicked, whose friends cheat and whose servants talk back to him. He is in same level of danger as sleeping in a house which has snakes in it.
quotes.transliteration:
duṣṭā bhāryā śaṭhaṃ mitraṃ bhṛtyaścottaradāyakaḥ |
sasarpe ca gṛhe vāso mṛtyureva na saṃśayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कन्याश्च वरस्यापि यत्रोभयोर्भवेद्रतिः ।
तथा कन्यां वरो धीमान् वरयेद्वंशशुद्धये ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a man and woman love each other very much, a wise man will marry the woman to purify (make it better) his lineage.
quotes.transliteration:
kanyāśca varasyāpi yatrobhayorbhavedratiḥ |
tathā kanyāṃ varo dhīmān varayedvaṃśaśuddhaye ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जिह्वा दग्धा परान्नेन हस्तौ दग्धौ प्रतिग्रहात् ।
परस्त्रीभिर्मनो दग्धं कथं सिद्धिः कलौ युगे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the Epoch of Kali (present times), the tongue gets burnt by eating food given by others. The hands get burnt by taking money belonging to others. The mind is burnt by thinking about other women. What is the path to achievement then?
quotes.transliteration:
jihvā dagdhā parānnena hastau dagdhau pratigrahāt |
parastrībhirmano dagdhaṃ kathaṃ siddhiḥ kalau yuge ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुस्तकस्था हि या विद्या धनं यद् वान्यहस्तगम् ।
नोपकुर्याज्जनस्येह कार्यकाले समुपस्थिते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Knowledge that is present in a book and money lent to others are both useless when you need them. They can not be recalled at the very hour it is needed.
quotes.transliteration:
pustakasthā hi yā vidyā dhanaṃ yad vānyahastagam |
nopakuryājjanasyeha kāryakāle samupasthite ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सद्भक्तः श्रीकृष्णं प्रेयांसं बन्धुमात्मनो मनुते ।
न जगन्नाथं कृष्णं गोपीनाथं सदा विदुर्गोप्यः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A true devotee will always love God just like one of his family members. For the cow herding ladies, Krishna has always been their savior (Gopinatha) even though they knew that he was the master of the whole world (Jagannatha).
quotes.transliteration:
sadbhaktaḥ śrīkṛṣṇaṃ preyāṃsaṃ bandhumātmano manute |
na jagannāthaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ gopīnāthaṃ sadā vidurgopyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हित्वा धर्मं तथार्थं च कामं यस्तु निषेवते ।
स वृक्षाग्रे यथा सुप्तः पतितः प्रतिबुध्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who indulges in Kama having forgotten about dharma (morality) and even artha(wealth) will realize his mistake only after he is hurt. Just like a person who is sleeping on a branch of the tree. He will be woken up only when he falls.
quotes.transliteration:
hitvā dharmaṃ tathārthaṃ ca kāmaṃ yastu niṣevate |
sa vṛkṣāgre yathā suptaḥ patitaḥ pratibudhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपकर्तुं यथा स्वल्पः समर्थो न तथा महान् ।
प्रायः कूपस्तृषां हन्ति न कदापि तु वारिधिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wealth (great) person may not be able to help the same way as small person can do. When you are thirsty, a well can help you - not an ocean.
quotes.transliteration:
upakartuṃ yathā svalpaḥ samartho na tathā mahān |
prāyaḥ kūpastṛṣāṃ hanti na kadāpi tu vāridhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न परः पापमादत्ते परेषां पापकर्मणाम् ।
समयो रक्षितव्यस्तु संतश्चारित्रभूषणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If some one hurts a noble man, he should not hurt them back. A noble man will always do the right thing - even in the face of enemies. The live up to their reputation of being custodians of morality.
quotes.transliteration:
na paraḥ pāpamādatte pareṣāṃ pāpakarmaṇām |
samayo rakṣitavyastu saṃtaścāritrabhūṣaṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न कूपखननं युक्तं प्रदीप्ते वह्निना गृहे ।
चिंतनीया हि विपदामादावेव प्रतिक्रिया ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not wise to start digging a well when your house is on fire. One must be prepared for calamities well in advance. When they strike, one must only effect a plan that is already made.
quotes.transliteration:
na kūpakhananaṃ yuktaṃ pradīpte vahninā gṛhe |
ciṃtanīyā hi vipadāmādāveva pratikriyā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सतामदैन्यं वदनस्य शोभा
निर्लोभतांतर्वचसामयाञ्चा ।
कायस्य सत्सेव्यमसेवकत्वं
पाणेरुनुत्तानतलत्वमेव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: satAm adainyam vadanasya shobhA kAyasya satsevyam asevakatvam A virtuous man should never show (self) pity on his face. Mind is its best when it is free from greed. Words are best used when they are not used for begging. The body is best used when serving virtuous people or being free from servitude. The palm is best used when it is never looking upwards (begging). It must always look down (giving).
quotes.transliteration:
satāmadainyaṃ vadanasya śobhā
nirlobhatāṃtarvacasāmayāñcā |
kāyasya satsevyamasevakatvaṃ
pāṇerunuttānatalatvameva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मित्राणि शत्रुत्वमुपानयंती
मित्रत्वमर्थस्य वशाच्च शत्रून् ।
नीतिर्नयत्यस्मृत पूर्ववृत्तं
जन्मान्तरं जीवत एव पुंसः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When material goals become important, friends become foes and foes become friends. They easily forget their past to achieve the goals. It is as if they take a re-birth in the same lifetime.
quotes.transliteration:
mitrāṇi śatrutvamupānayaṃtī
mitratvamarthasya vaśācca śatrūn |
nītirnayatyasmṛta pūrvavṛttaṃ
janmāntaraṃ jīvata eva puṃsaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अकारणं रूपमकारणं कुलं ।
महत्सु नीचेषु च कर्म शोभते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not beauty or family that counts. It is just the work irrespective of whether it is done by nobility or a person of low birth.
quotes.transliteration:
akāraṇaṃ rūpamakāraṇaṃ kulaṃ |
mahatsu nīceṣu ca karma śobhate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुसुमशयनं पाषाणो वा प्रियं भवनं वनं
प्रतनुमसृणस्पर्शं वासस्त्वगप्यथ तारवी ।
सरसमशनं कुल्माषो वा धनानि तृणानि वा
शमसुखसुधापानक्षैभ्ये समं हि महात्मनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: For an ascetic who derives his satisfaction from killing desires, it does not matter if he sleeps on flowery bed or hard stone, lives in a palace or forest, smooth silky clothes or rags, sumptuous food or gruel, wealth or blades of grass.
quotes.transliteration:
kusumaśayanaṃ pāṣāṇo vā priyaṃ bhavanaṃ vanaṃ
pratanumasṛṇasparśaṃ vāsastvagapyatha tāravī |
sarasamaśanaṃ kulmāṣo vā dhanāni tṛṇāni vā
śamasukhasudhāpānakṣaibhye samaṃ hi mahātmanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कटु क्वणंतो मलदायकः खलाः
तुदंत्यलं बंधनशृंखला इव ।
मनस्तु साधुध्वनिभिः पदे पदे
हरंति संतो मणिनूपुरा इव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: manastu sAdhu dhvanibhiH pade pade Wicked people always speak harshly and dirty. Their words cause harsh noise and pain just like chains tied to your feet. On the other hand noble men are like anklets made of pearls - they make very pleasing sounds and never hurt.
quotes.transliteration:
kaṭu kvaṇaṃto maladāyakaḥ khalāḥ
tudaṃtyalaṃ baṃdhanaśṛṃkhalā iva |
manastu sādhudhvanibhiḥ pade pade
haraṃti saṃto maṇinūpurā iva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं दुःसहं नु साधूनां विदुषां किमपेक्षितम् ।
किमकार्यं कदर्याणां दुस्त्यजं किं धृतात्मनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is intolerable for the virtuous people? What will the scholars long for? What will a rogue think as "undoable"? What will self realized people find hard to give up?
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ duḥsahaṃ nu sādhūnāṃ viduṣāṃ kimapekṣitam |
kimakāryaṃ kadaryāṇāṃ dustyajaṃ kiṃ dhṛtātmanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अग्नौ दग्धं जले मग्नं हृतं तस्करपार्थिवैः ।
तत्सर्वं दानमित्याहुर्यदि क्लैब्यं न भाषते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What is lost to fire, water, thieves or kings will be accounted as 'donated' if we do not grieve over the lost items.
quotes.transliteration:
agnau dagdhaṃ jale magnaṃ hṛtaṃ taskarapārthivaiḥ |
tatsarvaṃ dānamityāhuryadi klaibyaṃ na bhāṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शत्रौ सांत्वं प्रतीकारः सर्वरोगेषु भेषजम् ।
मृत्योर्मृत्युंजयध्यानं दारिद्र्ये तु न किंचन ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even the enemy can be made to drop the hatred by adept diplomacy. Every disease has a medicine. Even death can be put away (temporarily) by chanting the Mrityunjaya mantra. But poverty has no solution.
quotes.transliteration:
śatrau sāṃtvaṃ pratīkāraḥ sarvarogeṣu bheṣajam |
mṛtyormṛtyuṃjayadhyānaṃ dāridrye tu na kiṃcana ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किंचित् कांतान्नपानादिकलिलं कोमलं गृहे ।
व्रणे कीट इवास्वाद्य वयः कार्यं न भस्मसात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not indulge too much in worldly pleasures like damsels, sweets and other eateries. If you stay at home all day savouring these pleasures, then you will be burning your lifetime into ashes.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃcit kāṃtānnapānādikalilaṃ komalaṃ gṛhe |
vraṇe kīṭa ivāsvādya vayaḥ kāryaṃ na bhasmasāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
श्वः कार्यमद्य कुर्वीत पूर्वाह्णे चापराह्णकम् ।
न हि प्रतीक्षते मृत्युः कृतमस्य न वा कृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Do it today what needs to be done tomorrow, in the morning what needs to be done in the afternoon. When it is time, death does not wait to see if you have done all your duties or not yet.
quotes.transliteration:
śvaḥ kāryamadya kurvīta pūrvāhṇe cāparāhṇakam |
na hi pratīkṣate mṛtyuḥ kṛtamasya na vā kṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
राजा तुष्टो हि भृत्यानामर्थमात्रं प्रयच्छति ।
ते तु संमानमात्रेण प्राणैरप्युपकुर्वते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a King likes the work of a servant, he just parts with money in the form of rewards. But, the servants on the other hand will be willing to lay down their lives in return of just the honor of being of service to the King. Isn't it an irony?
quotes.transliteration:
rājā tuṣṭo hi bhṛtyānāmarthamātraṃ prayacchati |
te tu saṃmānamātreṇa prāṇairapyupakurvate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यावद् भ्रियेत जठरं तावत् स्वत्वं हि देहिनाम्।
अधिकं योऽभिमन्येत स स्तेनो दंडमर्हति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must accumulate wealth as much needed to fill his stomach. If one accumulates more than that, he is stealing; and should be punished.
quotes.transliteration:
yāvad bhriyeta jaṭharaṃ tāvat svatvaṃ hi dehinām|
adhikaṃ yoऽbhimanyeta sa steno daṃḍamarhati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कन्यायाः वरसंपत्तिः पितुः प्रायः प्रयत्नतः ।
भाग्येषु शेषमायत्तं दृष्टपूर्वं न चान्यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who a girl marries depends a lot on her fathers efforts. There is also a lot of luck involved. From our previous experience, these are the only two factors that determines who a girl marries - father's effort and luck.
quotes.transliteration:
kanyāyāḥ varasaṃpattiḥ pituḥ prāyaḥ prayatnataḥ |
bhāgyeṣu śeṣamāyattaṃ dṛṣṭapūrvaṃ na cānyathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सा श्रीर्या न मदं कुर्यात्स सुखी तृष्णयोज्झितः ।
तन्मित्रं यत्र विश्वासः पुरुषः स जितेंद्रियः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Money is called wealth only when it does not intoxicate you. You can be happy only when you don't have any desires. He is a friend only when he can be trusted. One can be called as a man only when he can control his senses.
quotes.transliteration:
sā śrīryā na madaṃ kuryātsa sukhī tṛṣṇayojjhitaḥ |
tanmitraṃ yatra viśvāsaḥ puruṣaḥ sa jiteṃdriyaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विज्ञानगुणमहार्घे पुरुषे द्वेष्यत्वमपि रमणीयम् ।
जननिंदिते पुनर्जने प्रियत्वेनापि लज्जामहे ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If person is well learned and possesses good qualities, then being his enemy is also acceptable. But I would be ashamed to be a friend of a rogue who is hated by everyone.
quotes.transliteration:
vijñānaguṇamahārghe puruṣe dveṣyatvamapi ramaṇīyam |
jananiṃdite punarjane priyatvenāpi lajjāmahe ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लोकयात्रार्थमेवेह धर्मस्य नियमः कृतः ।
उभयत्र सुखोदर्कः इह चैव परत्र च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The rules are dharma are made thus to enable smooth functioning of this world. But following it will give you lot of happiness not only in this world, but also the afterlife.
quotes.transliteration:
lokayātrārthameveha dharmasya niyamaḥ kṛtaḥ |
ubhayatra sukhodarkaḥ iha caiva paratra ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथागदं वीर्यतममुपयुक्तं यदृच्छया ।
अजानतोऽप्यात्मगुणं कुर्यान्मंत्रोऽप्युदाहृतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A good medicine will be beneficial to the body even if consumed by accident. Just like that a hymn will do good to a person chanting it without knowing the true meaning of the same.
quotes.transliteration:
yathāgadaṃ vīryatamamupayuktaṃ yadṛcchayā |
ajānatoऽpyātmaguṇaṃ kuryānmaṃtroऽpyudāhṛtaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न गुह्यगोपने शक्तं न च दंशनिवारणे ।
शुनः पुच्छमिव व्यर्थं लोभिहस्तगतं धनं ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth that goes into the hands of a miser is of no use. It is like a tail of a dog – that can neither hide its private parts not can it keep away the flies.
quotes.transliteration:
na guhyagopane śaktaṃ na ca daṃśanivāraṇe |
śunaḥ pucchamiva vyarthaṃ lobhihastagataṃ dhanaṃ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ॠत्विक्पुरोहिताचार्यैर्मातुलातिथिसंश्रितैः
बालवृद्धातुरैर्वैद्यैर्ज्ञातिसंबंधिबांधवैः ।
मातापितृभ्यां जामीभिर्भ्रात्रा पुत्रेण भार्यया
दुहित्रा दासवर्गेण विवादं न समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never have a harsh argument with the following - a priest, a brahmin, teacher, uncle, guest, those whom you have given refuge, children, old people, patients , doctors, relatives of your father, son-in-law, relatives of mother, mother, father, sister, daughter-in-law, brother, son, wife, daughter and servants.
quotes.transliteration:
ṝtvikpurohitācāryairmātulātithisaṃśritaiḥ
bālavṛddhāturairvaidyairjñātisaṃbaṃdhibāṃdhavaiḥ |
mātāpitṛbhyāṃ jāmībhirbhrātrā putreṇa bhāryayā
duhitrā dāsavargeṇa vivādaṃ na samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वाणी रसवती यस्य भार्या प्रेमवती सती ।
लक्ष्मीर्दानवती यस्य सफलं तस्य जीवितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is lucky whose speech is always uttered in good taste, whose loving wife is chastely and whose wealth is used for charity.
quotes.transliteration:
vāṇī rasavatī yasya bhāryā premavatī satī |
lakṣmīrdānavatī yasya saphalaṃ tasya jīvitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एतावज्जन्मसाफल्यं देहिनामिह देहिषु ।
प्राणैरर्थैर्धिया वाचा श्रेय एवाचरेत्सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is only one way to be satisfied in life. Make use of your life, wealth, wisdom and words only to help others succeed.
quotes.transliteration:
etāvajjanmasāphalyaṃ dehināmiha dehiṣu |
prāṇairarthairdhiyā vācā śreya evācaretsadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दोषोऽपि गुणतां याति प्रभोर्भवति चेत्कृपा ।
अंगहीनोऽपि सूर्येण सारथ्येयोजितोऽरुणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It does not matter even if one has any handicap. Under an able master even those people can perform well. Surya has Aruna as his charioteer even though he is a cripple. He has never let down his master.
quotes.transliteration:
doṣoऽpi guṇatāṃ yāti prabhorbhavati cetkṛpā |
aṃgahīnoऽpi sūryeṇa sārathyeyojitoऽruṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कांतावियोगः स्वजनापमानो
रणस्य शेषः कुनृपस्य सेवा ।
दरिद्रभावो विषमा सभा च
विनाग्निमेते प्रदहंति कायम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following can burn our body without fire – departure of the wife, humiliation by our own people, unfinished battle, having to serve a bad king, poverty and a unfriendly assembly (of people who cannot decide right from wrong).
quotes.transliteration:
kāṃtāviyogaḥ svajanāpamāno
raṇasya śeṣaḥ kunṛpasya sevā |
daridrabhāvo viṣamā sabhā ca
vināgnimete pradahaṃti kāyam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न दानतुल्यं धनमन्यदस्ति
न सत्यतुल्यं व्रतमन्यदस्ति ।
न शीलतुल्यं शुभमन्यदस्ति
न क्षांतितुल्यं हितमन्यदस्ति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: na dAnatulayam dhanamanyadasti na shIlatulyam shubhamanyadasti There is no better wealth than which is spent in charity. No better penance than truth. Nothing more auspicious than character. Nothing more pleasing than patience.
quotes.transliteration:
na dānatulyaṃ dhanamanyadasti
na satyatulyaṃ vratamanyadasti |
na śīlatulyaṃ śubhamanyadasti
na kṣāṃtitulyaṃ hitamanyadasti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दिवाकराद्रक्षतियो गुहासु
लीनं दिवाभीतमिवांधकारं ।
क्षुद्रेऽपि नूनं शरणं प्रपन्ने
ममत्वमुच्चैःशिरसामतीव॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the Himalayas, there are caves where Sun rays have never entered. Inside, those afraid of sunlight - darkness and owls are safe. It is the noble quality of the virtuous people that protect even the weak (and lowly) when they ask for refuge.
quotes.transliteration:
divākarādrakṣatiyo guhāsu
līnaṃ divābhītamivāṃdhakāraṃ |
kṣudreऽpi nūnaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapanne
mamatvamuccaiḥśirasāmatīva||

quotes.sanskritText:
देहापत्यकलत्रादिष्वात्मसैन्येष्वसत्स्वपि ।
तेषां प्रमत्तो निधनं पश्यन्नपि न पश्यति॥
quotes.englishTranslation: This body, children, wives will not stay for ever. Neither will your personal army. Even after seeing them destroyed, you are not seeing the truth that none of it is permanent.
quotes.transliteration:
dehāpatyakalatrādiṣvātmasainyeṣvasatsvapi |
teṣāṃ pramatto nidhanaṃ paśyannapi na paśyati||

quotes.sanskritText:
न गणस्याग्रतो गच्छेत्सिद्धे कार्ये समं फलम् ।
यदि कार्यविपत्तिः स्यात् मुखरस्तत्र हन्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not go in front of a group (as a leader). If the work gets done, then every one gets equal profits. If it goes wrong, then people will kill the leader here. [Translator's note: This is perhaps relevant when it is an unknown group of people - like a mob that has no time to think about morality, This does not deter a good leader from leading 'his' men to glory.]
quotes.transliteration:
