na gaṇasyāgrato gacchetsiddhe kārye samaṃ phalam |
yadi kāryavipattiḥ syāt mukharastatra hanyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नेह यत्कर्म धर्माय न विरागाय कल्पते ।
न तीर्थपदसेवायै जीवन्नपि मृतो हि सः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Our actions must be to uphold Dharma, or to lead us to detachment (asceticism), or to serve noble wise men (who are worthy of service). If they do not meet any of the above, then we are as good as dead.
quotes.transliteration:
neha yatkarma dharmāya na virāgāya kalpate |
na tīrthapadasevāyai jīvannapi mṛto hi saḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नालंबते दैष्टिकतां न निषीदति पौरुषे ।
शब्दार्थौ सत्कविरिव द्वयं विद्वानपेक्षते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like a good poet will always try to get this work to be a good combination of words and its meaning - we should also depend on both our efforts and will of the providence. Relying on only one of them will not be appropriate.
quotes.transliteration:
nālaṃbate daiṣṭikatāṃ na niṣīdati pauruṣe |
śabdārthau satkaviriva dvayaṃ vidvānapekṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परतोषयिता न कश्चन
स्वगतो यस्य गुणोऽस्ति देहिनः ।
परदोषकथाभिरल्पकः
स्वजनं तोषयितुं किलेच्छति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: paratoShayitA na kashchana If one can not please other people by his own qualities, such a lowly person will attempt to please others by talking ill about other (noble) people (by highlighting other's faults).
quotes.transliteration:
paratoṣayitā na kaścana
svagato yasya guṇoऽsti dehinaḥ |
paradoṣakathābhiralpakaḥ
svajanaṃ toṣayituṃ kilecchati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कष्टं खलु मूर्खत्वं कष्टं खलु यौवनेषु दारिद्र्यं ।
कष्टात् कष्टतरं किं परगृहवासः परान्नं च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is difficult being stupid and poor while young. But the most difficulty is when you have to take refuge in someone else's house and eat his food.
quotes.transliteration:
kaṣṭaṃ khalu mūrkhatvaṃ kaṣṭaṃ khalu yauvaneṣu dāridryaṃ |
kaṣṭāt kaṣṭataraṃ kiṃ paragṛhavāsaḥ parānnaṃ ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्थानेष्टेव नियोक्तव्या भृत्याश्चाभरणानि च ।
न हि चूडामणिः पादे प्रभावामीति बध्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Servants and ornaments have to be placed at their appropriate positions. Will any one wear a precious gem (choodamani) on the foot.
quotes.transliteration:
sthāneṣṭeva niyoktavyā bhṛtyāścābharaṇāni ca |
na hi cūḍāmaṇiḥ pāde prabhāvāmīti badhyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपबर्हणेषु चैकं श्वेतं तूलं विचित्रवर्णेषु ।
तद्वत् कृष्णेऽकृष्णे दुष्टेऽदुष्टे नरे कृष्णः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The pillows may be of many different colors. But the cotton inside each one of them is the same - white. Just like that people (beings) may black or pale, wicked or virtuous - there is the same God residing in each of them.
quotes.transliteration:
upabarhaṇeṣu caikaṃ śvetaṃ tūlaṃ vicitravarṇeṣu |
tadvat kṛṣṇeऽkṛṣṇe duṣṭeऽduṣṭe nare kṛṣṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वे स्वर्गसुखार्थिनः क्रतुशतैः प्राज्यैर्यजंते जडाः
तेषां नाकपुरे प्रयाति विपुलः कालः क्षणार्धं यथा ।
क्षीणे पुण्यधने स्थितिर्न तु यथा वेश्यागृहे कामिनां
तस्मान्मोक्षसुखं समाश्रयत भोः सत्यं च नित्यं च युत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fools are those who do hundreds of rituals and rites to get to heaven. When they get there, the long time they spend seems like fraction of a second. Once their good deeds are spent, they get thrown out of heaven back to earth - much like the philanderer who get thrown out of a brothel when he has no money. We should understand this and strive to get permanent happiness by way of salvation only. That happiness is eternal and real.
quotes.transliteration:
sarve svargasukhārthinaḥ kratuśataiḥ prājyairyajaṃte jaḍāḥ
teṣāṃ nākapure prayāti vipulaḥ kālaḥ kṣaṇārdhaṃ yathā |
kṣīṇe puṇyadhane sthitirna tu yathā veśyāgṛhe kāmināṃ
tasmānmokṣasukhaṃ samāśrayata bhoḥ satyaṃ ca nityaṃ ca yut ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विद्यातपोऽधिकानां च प्रथमासनमुच्यते ।
पंक्तौ सह स्थितानां तु भोजनादि समं स्मृतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People who have attained greatness by having excess of education and penance must be seated ahead of every one. But once, people are all seated in the same row, they must all be served equally (in terms of food, respect etc...)
quotes.transliteration:
vidyātapoऽdhikānāṃ ca prathamāsanamucyate |
paṃktau saha sthitānāṃ tu bhojanādi samaṃ smṛtam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रोहते सायकैर्विद्धं वनं परशुना हतम् ।
वाचा दुरुक्तं भीभत्सं न संरोहति वाक्क्षतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: if a person is hit by an arrow, the wound will heal. Forests regrow after being razed with an axe. But if a person is hurt by the scary bad words, then the wound can not be healed.
quotes.transliteration:
rohate sāyakairviddhaṃ vanaṃ paraśunā hatam |
vācā duruktaṃ bhībhatsaṃ na saṃrohati vākkṣatam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वच्छंदवनजातेन शाकेनापि प्रपूर्यते ।
अस्य दग्धोदरस्यार्थे कः कुर्यात्पातकं महत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Hunger can be satiated by tubes and fruits that grow freely in the forests. Then why does one commit horrible sins just to satiate this burnt stomach?
quotes.transliteration:
svacchaṃdavanajātena śākenāpi prapūryate |
asya dagdhodarasyārthe kaḥ kuryātpātakaṃ mahat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लक्ष्मीर्वसति जिह्वाग्रे जिह्वाग्रे मित्रबांधवाः ।
जिह्वाग्रे बंधनं प्राप्तं जिह्वाग्रे मरणं ध्रुवम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Speech is a powerful tool. It can earn you money. It can get you friends and relatives. it can also cause equal harm if not used properly. It can get you arrested. It can even get you killed.
quotes.transliteration:
lakṣmīrvasati jihvāgre jihvāgre mitrabāṃdhavāḥ |
jihvāgre baṃdhanaṃ prāptaṃ jihvāgre maraṇaṃ dhruvam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बहुमायासमाकीर्णः नानादोषसमाकुलः ।
लुप्तधर्मक्रियाचारः घोरः कालो भविष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The coming times will be very scary. It will filled with various kinds of deceit. Sins will grow. People will follow Dharma less and less. (In Mahabharata, the following epoch of Kali is described thus)
quotes.transliteration:
bahumāyāsamākīrṇaḥ nānādoṣasamākulaḥ |
luptadharmakriyācāraḥ ghoraḥ kālo bhaviṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नास्ति कामसमो व्याधिः नास्ति मोहसमो रिपुः ।
नास्ति क्रोधसमो वह्निः नास्ति ज्ञानात्परं सुखम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no worse ailment than lust, no worse enemy as attachment, no fire more destructive than anger and no pleasure greater than knowledge.
quotes.transliteration:
nāsti kāmasamo vyādhiḥ nāsti mohasamo ripuḥ |
nāsti krodhasamo vahniḥ nāsti jñānātparaṃ sukham ||

quotes.sanskritText:
को न याति वशं लोके मुखे पिण्डेन पूरितः ।
मृदंगो मुखलेपेन करोति मधुरध्वनिम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Who in this world can not me brought under control by filling their mouths with food? (by bribing). When the Mridanga's face is smeared well, it starts making melodious sounds.
quotes.transliteration:
ko na yāti vaśaṃ loke mukhe piṇḍena pūritaḥ |
mṛdaṃgo mukhalepena karoti madhuradhvanim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एहि गच्छ पतोत्तिष्ठ वद मौनं समाचर ।
एवमाशाग्रहग्रस्तैः क्रीडंति धनिनोऽर्थिभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealthy people play with poor people who are bit by the 'desire' bug. They ask the poor people to - come here, go there, fall down, get up, speak, be silent - as they wish before giving them any money.
quotes.transliteration:
ehi gaccha patottiṣṭha vada maunaṃ samācara |
evamāśāgrahagrastaiḥ krīḍaṃti dhaninoऽrthibhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अर्थैरर्थाः निबध्यंते गजैर्वनगजा इव ।
न च कश्चित् कृते कार्ये कर्तारं समवेक्षते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wealth has to be used to get more wealth just like tame elephants are used to capture and tame the wild elephants. In this world, people will never remember the person they received help from after having benefited from them.
quotes.transliteration:
arthairarthāḥ nibadhyaṃte gajairvanagajā iva |
na ca kaścit kṛte kārye kartāraṃ samavekṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपकारिषु यः साधुः साधुत्वे तस्य को गुणः ।
अपकारिषु यः साधुः स साधुः सद्भिरुच्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you find a person helping others, he is mostly a virtuous person. there is nothing special there. But if you see a person not helping (hurting) others and he still a virtuous person, then that person is special and is showing real virtue.
quotes.transliteration:
upakāriṣu yaḥ sādhuḥ sādhutve tasya ko guṇaḥ |
apakāriṣu yaḥ sādhuḥ sa sādhuḥ sadbhirucyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सा भार्या या प्रियं ब्रूते स पुत्रो यत्र निर्वृत्तिः ।
तन्मित्रं यत्र विश्वासः सदेशो यत्र जीव्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: She is a wife who will keep the husband happy and does not speak harshly to him. A son is one who does not give troubles to his father. A friend is one who can be trusted. Your nation is one you live in (at present).
quotes.transliteration:
sā bhāryā yā priyaṃ brūte sa putro yatra nirvṛttiḥ |
tanmitraṃ yatra viśvāsaḥ sadeśo yatra jīvyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न पिता नात्मजो नात्मा न माता न सखीजनः ।
इह प्रेत्य च नारीणां पतिरेको गतिः सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Be it in this world or the afterlife, a lady can only depend on her man for anything - not father, not siblings, not herself, not mother and not her friends.
quotes.transliteration:
na pitā nātmajo nātmā na mātā na sakhījanaḥ |
iha pretya ca nārīṇāṃ patireko gatiḥ sadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हृदयं सदयं यस्य भाषितं सत्यभूषितम् ।
कायः परहितोपायः कलिः कुर्वीत तस्य किम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Kali (who can corrupt anyone) will not be able to do anything to a person who has compassion in his heart, truth in his words and uses his body to help others.
quotes.transliteration:
hṛdayaṃ sadayaṃ yasya bhāṣitaṃ satyabhūṣitam |
kāyaḥ parahitopāyaḥ kaliḥ kurvīta tasya kim ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पौरोहित्यं रजनिचरितं ग्रामणीत्वं नियोगो
माठापत्यं ह्यनृतवचनं साक्षिवादः परान्नम् ।
ब्रह्मद्वेषः खलजनरतिः प्राणिनां निर्दयत्वं
मा भूदेवं मम पशुपते जन्मजन्मांतरेऽपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: paurohityam rajani charitam grAmaNItvam niyogo Oh my lord who is the god of all beings, spare me from these in this and any further lives - being a leader, having to walk at night, being a village head, slavery, head of mutt, having to tell a lie, having to give testimony as a witness in any proceeding, living on other's mercy, enmity of a brahmin, friendship of wicked people, lacking compassion towards animals.
quotes.transliteration:
paurohityaṃ rajanicaritaṃ grāmaṇītvaṃ niyogo
māṭhāpatyaṃ hyanṛtavacanaṃ sākṣivādaḥ parānnam |
brahmadveṣaḥ khalajanaratiḥ prāṇināṃ nirdayatvaṃ
mā bhūdevaṃ mama paśupate janmajanmāṃtareऽpi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ईश्वराणां वचः सत्यं तथैवाचरितं क्वचित् ।
तेषां यत् स्ववचोयुक्तं बुद्धिमांस्तत् समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Whatever God says (in the Bhagavadgeetha) is truth. But his actions may not always be right. A wise man must observe when He has acted in accordance to his words and follow only that. (This verse may be in response to critics of Lord Krishna's questionable methods of fighting evil with deceit)
quotes.transliteration:
īśvarāṇāṃ vacaḥ satyaṃ tathaivācaritaṃ kvacit |
teṣāṃ yat svavacoyuktaṃ buddhimāṃstat samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अभ्यासस्तावद भक्तेर्यावन्नामश्रुतेस्तु समकालम् ।
नाश्रुनिपातोत्पुलकौ तौचेन्नावश्यकत्वमस्यकिल ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must practice the rituals of bhakti (devotion) until the point when tears roll out of the eyes every time naturally when he hears the name of God. But once such devotion is internalized, there is no need for any ritual.
quotes.transliteration:
abhyāsastāvada bhakteryāvannāmaśrutestu samakālam |
nāśrunipātotpulakau taucennāvaśyakatvamasyakila ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न भार्यया समं मित्रं न च धर्मो दयासमः ।
न स्वातंत्र्यसमं सौख्यं गार्हस्थ्यान्नाश्रमो वरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no better friend than a wife. No dharma better than compassion. Nothing better than being free. No state of life is better than running a household (being the man of the house).
quotes.transliteration:
na bhāryayā samaṃ mitraṃ na ca dharmo dayāsamaḥ |
na svātaṃtryasamaṃ saukhyaṃ gārhasthyānnāśramo varaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मुहूर्तमपि जीवेत नरः शुक्लेन कर्मणा ।
न कल्पमपि कृष्णेन लोकद्वयविरोधिना ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is acceptable to live just for a moment doing good work than live for epochs doing evil deeds that are against both worlds.
quotes.transliteration:
muhūrtamapi jīveta naraḥ śuklena karmaṇā |
na kalpamapi kṛṣṇena lokadvayavirodhinā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उत्तमं स्वार्जितं भुक्तं मध्यमं पितुरार्जितम् ।
कनिष्ठं भ्रातृवित्तं च स्त्रीवित्तमधमाधमम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A virtuous person will earn and spend his own money. A normal person will spend his father's money. A lowly person will spend his brother's money. A rogue among rogues will spend money that belongs to a lady.
quotes.transliteration:
uttamaṃ svārjitaṃ bhuktaṃ madhyamaṃ piturārjitam |
kaniṣṭhaṃ bhrātṛvittaṃ ca strīvittamadhamādhamam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः प्रीणयेत् सुचरितैः पितरं स पुत्रः
यद् भर्तुरेव हितमिच्छति तत्कलत्रम् ।
तन्मित्रमापदि सखे च समक्रियं यत्
एतत्त्रयं जगति पुण्यकृतो लभन्ते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: tat mitram aapadi sakhe cha samakriayam yat A good son is one who makes his father happy (by being obedient, follow dharma). A good wife is one who always keeps the interest of her husband in her mind. A good friend is one who does not change his mind when you are in trouble. Only people who have done good deeds in the past will get these in this world.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ prīṇayet sucaritaiḥ pitaraṃ sa putraḥ
yad bhartureva hitamicchati tatkalatram |
tanmitramāpadi sakhe ca samakriyaṃ yat
etattrayaṃ jagati puṇyakṛto labhante ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुर्वन्नपि व्यलीकानि यः प्रियः प्रिय एव सः ।
अशेषदोषदुष्टोऽपि कायः कस्य न वल्लभः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In spite of having innumerable defects, our loved ones still remain lovable. Just like that, everyone likes their own imperfect body.
quotes.transliteration:
kurvannapi vyalīkāni yaḥ priyaḥ priya eva saḥ |
aśeṣadoṣaduṣṭoऽpi kāyaḥ kasya na vallabhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संपन्नतरमेवान्नं दरिद्रा भुंजते सदा ।
क्षुत् स्वादुतां जनयति सा चाढ्येषु सुदुर्लभा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Poor people always eat tasty food. Their hunger puts taste in what ever they eat. This taste is elusive for the rich.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃpannataramevānnaṃ daridrā bhuṃjate sadā |
kṣut svādutāṃ janayati sā cāḍhyeṣu sudurlabhā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हस्तस्पर्शादिवांधेन विषमे पथि दावता ।
अनुमानप्रधानेन विनिपातो न दुर्लभः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A blind man finds his way by touching and feeling. Imagine him running on a treacherous course. He will often stumble and fall. It is a similar fate that awaits someone who undertakes a project based solely on a mere guess.
quotes.transliteration:
hastasparśādivāṃdhena viṣame pathi dāvatā |
anumānapradhānena vinipāto na durlabhaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सोऽर्थो यो हस्ते तन्मित्रं यन्निरंतरं व्यसने ।
तद्रूपं यत्र गुणाः तद्विज्ञानं यत्र धर्मः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is called wealth only when it is in your hands (liquid). He is a friend who stands by you always when you are in trouble. Beauty is complete only when there are good qualities in the person. It is knowledge only when it makes you follow the moral code of life (Dharma).
quotes.transliteration:
soऽrtho yo haste tanmitraṃ yanniraṃtaraṃ vyasane |
tadrūpaṃ yatra guṇāḥ tadvijñānaṃ yatra dharmaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निर्विषेणापि सर्पेण कर्तव्या महती फटा ।
विषं भवतु वा मा वा फटाटोपो भयंकरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even when the snake is not poisonous, it must have the ability to show off its hood and hiss. Regardless of the poison, the snake that displays its hood is scary. Appearances can save the day.
quotes.transliteration:
nirviṣeṇāpi sarpeṇa kartavyā mahatī phaṭā |
viṣaṃ bhavatu vā mā vā phaṭāṭopo bhayaṃkaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संति ह्यसाधवो लोके दुर्मैत्राश्छद्मवेषिणः ।
तेषामुदेत्यघं काले रोगः पातकिनामिव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are rogues in this world who become bad friends and who are roaming in disguise (of noble men). Their sin will catch up with them at appropriate time - like how disease catches up with other sinners.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃti hyasādhavo loke durmaitrāśchadmaveṣiṇaḥ |
teṣāmudetyaghaṃ kāle rogaḥ pātakināmiva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गोशतादपि गोक्षीरं प्रस्थं धान्यशतादपि ।
प्रासादादपि खट्टार्धं शेषाः परविभूतयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even if there are hundreds of cows, you need only very little milk. Even if you have hundreds of granaries, all you need a little food. Even if you have huge palace, all you need is half a bed to sleep in. Rest is to treated as if they belong to others.
quotes.transliteration:
gośatādapi gokṣīraṃ prasthaṃ dhānyaśatādapi |
prāsādādapi khaṭṭārdhaṃ śeṣāḥ paravibhūtayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शैले शैले न माणिक्यं मौक्तिकं न गजे गजे ।
साधवो न हि सर्वत्र चंदनं न वने वने ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Greatness cannot be found in all places. Every stone is not a gem. Every elephant will not have a gem on its head. Noble men cannot be found everywhere. Not every forest will have sandalwood.
quotes.transliteration:
śaile śaile na māṇikyaṃ mauktikaṃ na gaje gaje |
sādhavo na hi sarvatra caṃdanaṃ na vane vane ||

quotes.sanskritText:
असारे खलु संसारे सारमेतच्चतुष्टयम् ।
काश्यां वासः सतां सङ्गो गंगांभः शंभुसेवनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In this world without any real substance, there are only four things that matter - living in Kashi, befriending virtuous people, holy water of Ganga and worshiping Lord Shiva.
quotes.transliteration:
asāre khalu saṃsāre sārametaccatuṣṭayam |
kāśyāṃ vāsaḥ satāṃ saṅgo gaṃgāṃbhaḥ śaṃbhusevanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नरस्याभरणं रूपं रूपस्याभरणं गुणः ।
गुणस्याभरणं ज्ञानं ज्ञानस्याभरणं क्षमा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A man is decorated with his beauty. Beauty is decorated by good character. Good character is decorated by having the right knowledge. Knowledge is decorated by learning to forgive.
quotes.transliteration:
narasyābharaṇaṃ rūpaṃ rūpasyābharaṇaṃ guṇaḥ |
guṇasyābharaṇaṃ jñānaṃ jñānasyābharaṇaṃ kṣamā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गुणागुणज्ञेषु गुणा भवंति
ते निर्गुणं प्राप्य भवंति दोषाः ।
आस्वाद्यतोयाः प्रभवंति नद्यः
समुद्रमासाद्य भवन्त्यपेयाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If certain qualities are found in a person who can tell right from wrong, then he becomes virtuous. But if the same is found in a rogue, then it becomes a wickedness. Water in a river is so pleasant to drink. The same water becomes undrinkable when it reaches the sea.
quotes.transliteration:
guṇāguṇajñeṣu guṇā bhavaṃti
te nirguṇaṃ prāpya bhavaṃti doṣāḥ |
āsvādyatoyāḥ prabhavaṃti nadyaḥ
samudramāsādya bhavantyapeyāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उपदेशो हि मूर्खाणां प्रकोपाय न शांतये ।
पयः पानं भुजंगानां केवलं विषवर्धनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Advice is of no use to a fool. Instead of placating him, it will only anger him more. If you let a snake drink milk, it will only lead to more venom.
quotes.transliteration:
upadeśo hi mūrkhāṇāṃ prakopāya na śāṃtaye |
payaḥ pānaṃ bhujaṃgānāṃ kevalaṃ viṣavardhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सौवर्णानि सरोजानि निर्मातुं संति शिल्पिनः ।
तत्र सौरभनिर्माणे चतुरश्चतुराननः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sculptors make easily make a golden lotus. But only the Creator (God) can put fragrance into it.
quotes.transliteration:
sauvarṇāni sarojāni nirmātuṃ saṃti śilpinaḥ |
tatra saurabhanirmāṇe caturaścaturānanaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्थितासनस्था शयिता पराङ्मुखी
स्वलंकृता वाप्य नलंकृता वा ।
निरीक्ष्यमाणा प्रमादा सुदुर्बलं
मनुष्यमालेख्यगताऽपि कर्षति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A lady may be sitting, standing, or sleeping on a bed with her face away from you, well decorated or not, while she is waiting for someone, or lost her mind, or even in a picture - she can attract a man if he is weak.
quotes.transliteration:
sthitāsanasthā śayitā parāṅmukhī
svalaṃkṛtā vāpya nalaṃkṛtā vā |
nirīkṣyamāṇā pramādā sudurbalaṃ
manuṣyamālekhyagatāऽpi karṣati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
रायः कलत्रं पशवः सुतादयो
गृहा मही कुंजरकोशभूतयः ।
सर्वेऽर्थकामाः क्षणभंगुरायुषः
कुर्वन्ति मर्त्यस्य कियत्प्रियं चलाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: How much can all the material pleasures like money, wife, animals, children, house, land, elephants, wealth and other such things that can be bought with money? Life itself is ephemeral, lasting for less than a second. What can these impermanent things do to make us happy?
quotes.transliteration:
rāyaḥ kalatraṃ paśavaḥ sutādayo
gṛhā mahī kuṃjarakośabhūtayaḥ |
sarveऽrthakāmāḥ kṣaṇabhaṃgurāyuṣaḥ
kurvanti martyasya kiyatpriyaṃ calāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नित्यमास्यं शुचि स्त्रीणां शकुनैः फलपातने ।
प्रसवे च शुचिर्वत्सः श्वा मृगग्रहणे शुचिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A woman's face is always clean. Fruits that the birds drop (after eating half) are not to be considered dirty. The udder of a cow that is sucked by a calf is clean. If a dog brings the hunted meat, it is also considered clean - not dirty.
quotes.transliteration:
nityamāsyaṃ śuci strīṇāṃ śakunaiḥ phalapātane |
prasave ca śucirvatsaḥ śvā mṛgagrahaṇe śuciḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
केचिदज्ञानतो नष्टाः केचिन्नष्टाः प्रमादतः ।
केचित् ज्ञानावलेपेन केचिन्नष्टैस्तु नाशिताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Some losses happen due to ignorance. Some due to a hot mind. Some losses happen because we illusion of knowledge. Some losses will destroy us altogether.
quotes.transliteration:
kecidajñānato naṣṭāḥ kecinnaṣṭāḥ pramādataḥ |
kecit jñānāvalepena kecinnaṣṭaistu nāśitāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सिन्धौ यद्वन्मुक्ताः सन्त्येव परं जिघृक्षुभिर्यत्नैः
यावल्लाभं मज्जन रूपः कार्योनिपुणबुद्धिभिस्तद्वत् ।
परमेश्वरोहि नित्यं जगतिव्याप्तः परोक्षविधया वा
तस्य च साक्षात्कारो भवति हि यत्नैः श्रवणमननाद्यैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Everyone is aware that there are pearls in the ocean. But only those who make the effort to dive and use their intelligence to search for it will get them. Similarly, God is everywhere in this creation (world). He is there, not in the form that we expect him to be. But if you want to see him as he is (and realize) then you have to make the effort - by listening to his stories, contemplating on his form and existence, so on.
quotes.transliteration:
sindhau yadvanmuktāḥ santyeva paraṃ jighṛkṣubhiryatnaiḥ
yāvallābhaṃ majjana rūpaḥ kāryonipuṇabuddhibhistadvat |
parameśvarohi nityaṃ jagativyāptaḥ parokṣavidhayā vā
tasya ca sākṣātkāro bhavati hi yatnaiḥ śravaṇamananādyaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
देशे देशे कलत्राणि देशे देशे च बांधवाः ।
तं तु देशं न पश्यामि यत्र भ्राता सहोदरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One may get wives in different places (countries) you visit. You may even get new relatives and friends. But I have not yet seen a country where one can get a brother (born from the same womb). (So, Ayodhya is special to me - as told by Rama).
quotes.transliteration:
deśe deśe kalatrāṇi deśe deśe ca bāṃdhavāḥ |
taṃ tu deśaṃ na paśyāmi yatra bhrātā sahodaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सत्यश्चाद्य प्रवादोऽयं लौकिकः प्रतिभाति मा ।
पितृन् समनुजायन्ते नरा मातारमङ्गनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not seeming to be true what people say - Sons will inherit the qualities of the father and daughters that of the mother. (As told by Sumantra to Kaikeyi)
quotes.transliteration:
satyaścādya pravādoऽyaṃ laukikaḥ pratibhāti mā |
pitṛn samanujāyante narā mātāramaṅganāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
जितरोषरया महाधियः
सपदिक्रोधजितो लघुर्जनः ।
विजितेन जितस्य दुर्मते
र्मतिमद्भिः सह का विरोधिता ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: vijetena jitasya durmater Great wise men have overcome anger permanently. Simpler men have been consumed/conquered due because they succumbed to anger. There can never be a fair contest between the winners and the conquered or those who have overcome anger and those who can not.
quotes.transliteration:
jitaroṣarayā mahādhiyaḥ
sapadikrodhajito laghurjanaḥ |
vijitena jitasya durmate
rmatimadbhiḥ saha kā virodhitā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
छिन्नोऽपि रोहति तरुः क्षीणोऽप्युपचीयते चंद्रः ।
इति विमृशंतः सन्तः संतप्यन्ते न विप्लुता लोके ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A tree grows even after being cut. The moon grows back after diminishing. Wise noble men observe this and realize that tough times are not permanent. Hence they will not feel sad in those times.
quotes.transliteration:
chinnoऽpi rohati taruḥ kṣīṇoऽpyupacīyate caṃdraḥ |
iti vimṛśaṃtaḥ santaḥ saṃtapyante na viplutā loke ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाभ्युत्थानक्रिया यत्र नालापा मधुराक्षराः ।
गुणदोषकथा नैव तत्र हर्म्ये न गम्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not visit a house where the guests are welcomed sitting down (not standing up in respect), where there are no sweet words spoken and where there is no harmless gossip.
quotes.transliteration:
nābhyutthānakriyā yatra nālāpā madhurākṣarāḥ |
guṇadoṣakathā naiva tatra harmye na gamyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुलीनैः सह संपर्कः पण्डितैः सह मित्रताम् ।
ज्ञातिभिश्च समं मेलं कुर्वाणो न विनश्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He shall not be ever destroyed who has acquaintance with people of powerful families, friendship with scholars and networks among knowledgeable people.
quotes.transliteration:
kulīnaiḥ saha saṃparkaḥ paṇḍitaiḥ saha mitratām |
jñātibhiśca samaṃ melaṃ kurvāṇo na vinaśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संपूर्णकुंभो न करोति शब्दं
अर्धोघटो घोषमुपैति नूनम् ।
विद्वान्कुलीनो न करोति गर्वं
जल्पन्ति मूढास्तु गुणैर्विहीनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: sampUrNa kumbho na karoti shabdam A pot that is filled completely will not make any sound. A half filled pot will make a lot of noise. Virtuous scholars born to good families will not show off their talent. A fool without any good qualities will however talk a lot.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃpūrṇakuṃbho na karoti śabdaṃ
ardhoghaṭo ghoṣamupaiti nūnam |
vidvānkulīno na karoti garvaṃ
jalpanti mūḍhāstu guṇairvihīnāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृतानां स्वामिनः कार्ये भृत्यानामनुवर्तिनाम् ।
भवेत्स्वर्गेऽक्षयो वासः कीर्तिश्च धरणीतले ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a servant continues his master's work after death and follows him (also dies) then he will have unlimited stay in heaven. On this earth, he will have unlimited fame.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtānāṃ svāminaḥ kārye bhṛtyānāmanuvartinām |
bhavetsvargeऽkṣayo vāsaḥ kīrtiśca dharaṇītale ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्याच्चेदीशदिदृक्षा नाम्नि श्रधां दृढां सदा कुरुत ।
सत्यासत्य विवेकं नित्यं चानेन तत्त्वविज्ञानम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you want to be able to see God with your own eyes, you will first need to believe firmly in his name and think about him always. You should always be able to separate the truth from false. Using that intellect, one must try to realise the truth that will lead us to God.
quotes.transliteration:
syāccedīśadidṛkṣā nāmni śradhāṃ dṛḍhāṃ sadā kuruta |
satyāsatya vivekaṃ nityaṃ cānena tattvavijñānam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हितोपदेशं शृणुयात् कुर्वीत च यथोदितम् ।
विदुरोक्तमकृत्वाभूत्कौरवः शोकशल्यभाक् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: You must listen to people when they are advising for your own well being - and do as they say. Kaurava listened to Vidura, but did not do as he said. As a result he met with lot of grief.
quotes.transliteration:
hitopadeśaṃ śṛṇuyāt kurvīta ca yathoditam |
viduroktamakṛtvābhūtkauravaḥ śokaśalyabhāk ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुतो वा नूतनं वस्तु वयमुत्प्रेक्षितुं क्षमाः ।
वाचो विन्यासवैचित्र्यमात्रमत्र विचार्यताम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One can not write an absolutely new piece all by himself. What needs to be told (in a work) is pretty much known. How it can be told differently is where the talent lies in. A literary work has to be judged on that.
quotes.transliteration:
kuto vā nūtanaṃ vastu vayamutprekṣituṃ kṣamāḥ |
vāco vinyāsavaicitryamātramatra vicāryatām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कस्य न शोको यः स्यात्
अक्रोधः किं सुखं तुष्टिः ।
को राजा रन्जनकृत्
कश्च श्वा नीचसेवको यः स्यात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kasya na shoko yaH syAt Who does not have worries? A person who does not get angry. Who is happy? He who is satisfied. Who is the king? Who takes good care of the subjects. Who is a dog? He who serves a wicked man.
quotes.transliteration:
kasya na śoko yaḥ syāt
akrodhaḥ kiṃ sukhaṃ tuṣṭiḥ |
ko rājā ranjanakṛt
kaśca śvā nīcasevako yaḥ syāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दैवेन ते हतधियो भवतः प्रसंगात्
सर्वाशुभोपशमनाद्वि मुखेन्द्रिया ये।
कुर्वन्ति कामसुखलेशलवाय दीना
लोभाभिभूतमनसोऽकुशलानि शश्वत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: daivena te hatadhiyo bhavataH prasangAt Who are weak? Those whose mind gets corrupted easily (perhaps due to influence of fate). Those who lose faith in god (who can set right everything) and go the other way. Those who get greedy even for a small portion of material pleasures. Those who will always do the wrong thing. They are the people who are really weak.
quotes.transliteration:
daivena te hatadhiyo bhavataḥ prasaṃgāt
sarvāśubhopaśamanādvi mukhendriyā ye|
kurvanti kāmasukhaleśalavāya dīnā
lobhābhibhūtamanasoऽkuśalāni śaśvat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनादायी व्ययं कुर्यादसहायी रणप्रियः ।
आतुरः सर्वभक्षी च नरः शीघ्रं विनश्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These people are going to get destroyed soon - one who spends more than he earns, one who goes to war without support, a patient who does not watch his diet.
quotes.transliteration:
anādāyī vyayaṃ kuryādasahāyī raṇapriyaḥ |
āturaḥ sarvabhakṣī ca naraḥ śīghraṃ vinaśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सुखदुःखे समे स्यातां जंतूनां कर्महेतुके ।
मूर्ध्नि स्थितानां केशानां भवेतां स्नेहछेदने ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There will be happiness and sadness according to our actions. We should not expect only one kind of emotions always. The hair on our head will get the nourishment of oil on one day and get mercilessly cut on another day.
quotes.transliteration:
sukhaduḥkhe same syātāṃ jaṃtūnāṃ karmahetuke |
mūrdhni sthitānāṃ keśānāṃ bhavetāṃ snehachedane ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथामयोंऽगे समुपेक्षितो नृभि-
र्न शक्यते रूढपदश्चिकित्सितुम् ।
यथेंद्रियग्राम उपेक्षितस्तथा
रिपुर्महान् बद्धबलो न चाल्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following things when neglected will grow into monstrous proportions and become unconquerable - disease in our body, attraction of the group of our sense organs, an enemy.
quotes.transliteration:
yathāmayoṃऽge samupekṣito nṛbhi-
rna śakyate rūḍhapadaścikitsitum |
yatheṃdriyagrāma upekṣitastathā
ripurmahān baddhabalo na cālyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विदुषां वदनाद्वचः सहसा यांति नो बहिः ।
याताश्चेन्न परांचंति द्विरदानां रदा इव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Scholars rarely utter words because they think too much before uttering any word. But once they utter, they will not take it back. Elephants grow tusks very late. But once they do, they look magnificent.
quotes.transliteration:
viduṣāṃ vadanādvacaḥ sahasā yāṃti no bahiḥ |
yātāścenna parāṃcaṃti dviradānāṃ radā iva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पिपीलिकार्जितं धान्यं मक्षिकासंचितं मधु ।
लुब्धेन संचितं द्रव्यं समूलं च विनश्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following will be destroyed without any trace - grains stored by an ant, honey by a bee and wealth hoarded by a miser.
quotes.transliteration:
pipīlikārjitaṃ dhānyaṃ makṣikāsaṃcitaṃ madhu |
lubdhena saṃcitaṃ dravyaṃ samūlaṃ ca vinaśyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
फलं कतकवृक्षस्य यद्यप्यंबुप्रसादकं ।
न नाम ग्रहणादेव तस्य वारि प्रसीदति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The seeds of the clearing nut plant (Strychnos Potatorum) is known to clear the water of suspended particles. But mere uttering its name will not do it. Knowledge is similar - it has to be applied, not just recited.
quotes.transliteration:
phalaṃ katakavṛkṣasya yadyapyaṃbuprasādakaṃ |
na nāma grahaṇādeva tasya vāri prasīdati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संस्नाप्यापि करी चेन्मुक्तो मलिनः क्षणाद् भवति
स्नानान्ते यदिबद्धस्तिष्ठेदमलः तथैव सत्सङ्गात् ।
जातं परमपवित्रं मनः पुनर्वो विषयिसहवासात्
मलिनं स्यात्तच्चेशे बद्धं तिष्ठेत्सुनिर्मलं नूनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: After washing the elephant, if it is left free, it will get muddy in a second. But if you keep it restrained, then it will remain clean. Similarly, our mind becomes clean when it comes in contact with a virtuous person. But if you talk to rogue later, it will again become polluted. However, if you can restrain it by thinking about God at all times, then it can kept clean and pure.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃsnāpyāpi karī cenmukto malinaḥ kṣaṇād bhavati
snānānte yadibaddhastiṣṭhedamalaḥ tathaiva satsaṅgāt |
jātaṃ paramapavitraṃ manaḥ punarvo viṣayisahavāsāt
malinaṃ syāttacceśe baddhaṃ tiṣṭhetsunirmalaṃ nūnam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
परस्वभावकर्माणि न प्रशंसेन्न गर्हयेत् ।
विश्वमेकात्मकं पश्यन् प्रकृत्या पुरुषेण च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When one observes other people's nature or work, he should neither praise or berate. One must always have the unified view of the world that it is made up of Prakruti and run by Purusha. Individual players have no independent role - and hence should not be accredited for the work.
quotes.transliteration:
parasvabhāvakarmāṇi na praśaṃsenna garhayet |
viśvamekātmakaṃ paśyan prakṛtyā puruṣeṇa ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उशना वेद यच्छास्त्रं यच्च वेद बृहस्पतिः ।
स्वभावेनैव तच्छास्त्रं स्त्रीबुद्धौ सुप्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All the wisdom and intelligence attributed to Shukracharya and Bruhaspati is already present naturally in a woman.
quotes.transliteration:
uśanā veda yacchāstraṃ yacca veda bṛhaspatiḥ |
svabhāvenaiva tacchāstraṃ strībuddhau supratiṣṭhitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रियो नरपतिर्वह्निर्विषं युक्त्या विषेवितम् ।
स्वार्थाय यदि वा दुःखसंभारायैव केवलम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Women, king, fire and poison - all must be used in moderation. If not, they will be cause for pain and misery.
quotes.transliteration:
striyo narapatirvahnirviṣaṃ yuktyā viṣevitam |
svārthāya yadi vā duḥkhasaṃbhārāyaiva kevalam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यस्मिन् रुष्टे भयं नास्ति तुष्टे नैव धनागमः ।
निग्रहानुग्रहौ न स्तः स रुष्टः किं करिष्यति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you are angry and no one is scared, then it is useless. Similarly if you are pleased and no one benefits from it, it is again a waste. There is no value attached to your anger or being pleased if does not mean anything to any one.
quotes.transliteration:
yasmin ruṣṭe bhayaṃ nāsti tuṣṭe naiva dhanāgamaḥ |
nigrahānugrahau na staḥ sa ruṣṭaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा खरश्चंदनभारवाही
भारस्य वेत्ता न तु चंदनस्य ।
तथैव लोका बहुशास्त्रयुक्ताः
धर्मेण हीनाः पशुभिः समानाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a donkey is carrying a sack of sandalwood, it knows about the weight only - not about the greatness of sandalwood. Similarly, there are many people in this world who have studied many scriptures and never got a hang of its real meaning. If a person does not follow dharma even after so much study, is is as good as the donkey.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā kharaścaṃdanabhāravāhī
bhārasya vettā na tu caṃdanasya |
tathaiva lokā bahuśāstrayuktāḥ
dharmeṇa hīnāḥ paśubhiḥ samānāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षते प्रहारा निपतंत्यभीक्ष्णं
धनक्षये दीप्यति जठराग्निः ।
आपत्सु वैराणि समुल्लसंति
छिद्रेष्वनर्था बहुलीभवंति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: aapatsu vairANi samullAsanti A wound gets hurt again and again before healing. You feel more hungry when he has no money. All your enemies rejoice (and perhaps unite) when you are in trouble. When your life is being torn apart, you commit more blunders.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣate prahārā nipataṃtyabhīkṣṇaṃ
dhanakṣaye dīpyati jaṭharāgniḥ |
āpatsu vairāṇi samullasaṃti
chidreṣvanarthā bahulībhavaṃti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सहस्व श्रियमन्येषां यद्यपि त्वयि नास्ति सा ।
अन्यत्रापि सतीं लक्ष्मीं कुशला भुंजते सदा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you see wealth (that you don't have) with others, don't feel jealous. Just live with it. You must understand that if you have done good things in the past, then you will be able to enjoy the wealth even if it does not belong to you.
quotes.transliteration:
sahasva śriyamanyeṣāṃ yadyapi tvayi nāsti sā |
anyatrāpi satīṃ lakṣmīṃ kuśalā bhuṃjate sadā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आश्रमांस्तुलया सर्वान् धृतानाहुर्मनीषिणः ।
एकतश्च त्रयो राजन् गृहस्थाश्रम एकतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh! King, wise men have weighed the different phases of life in a balance. They have found one to be as heavy as the other three put together. Grihasthaashrama - the phase where one runs his household independently is equal to brahmacharya (student hood), vanaprastha (forest dwelling, retirement) and sanyasa (total renouncement) all put together.
quotes.transliteration:
āśramāṃstulayā sarvān dhṛtānāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ |
ekataśca trayo rājan gṛhasthāśrama ekataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
लभेत वा प्रार्थयिता न वा श्रियम् ।
श्रिया दुरापः कथमीप्सितो जनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: People who wish for wealth may or may not get it. But if wealth wishes for some people, has it ever not got it?
quotes.transliteration:
labheta vā prārthayitā na vā śriyam |
śriyā durāpaḥ kathamīpsito janaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संमार्जनोपांजनेन सेकेलोल्लेखनेन च ।
गवां च परिवासेन भूमिः शुध्यति पंचभिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There are five ways you can keep the ground clean - by sweeping dirt off it, by smearing it with cow-dung, by sprinkling water over it, by scraping the top layer and by letting cows stand there.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃmārjanopāṃjanena sekelollekhanena ca |
gavāṃ ca parivāsena bhūmiḥ śudhyati paṃcabhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हरिभक्तिः केवलतो वेदान्तग्रन्थवाचनाभ्यासात्
नभवेद् यत्पञ्चाङ्गे कदा कदा कुत्र वृष्टिरिति ।
तत्कोष्टकं सुलिखितं तेषां पुञ्जान्नचैक जलबिन्दुः
तद्वद्भक्तिप्राप्तौ तदुक्तगुणभाक स्वयं भूयात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: hari bhaktiH kevalato vedaanta grantha vaachana abhyaasaat Devotion towards God can not be got from reading a lot of books and scriptures. A lot of scholars may get together and create the panchanga (calendar) which accurately tells when and where it would rain. All of them put together can not force even a drop of water from the sky. Just like that reading a lot of books is not enough for realization - one has to have strong devotion in God to realize the truth.
quotes.transliteration:
haribhaktiḥ kevalato vedāntagranthavācanābhyāsāt
nabhaved yatpañcāṅge kadā kadā kutra vṛṣṭiriti |
tatkoṣṭakaṃ sulikhitaṃ teṣāṃ puñjānnacaika jalabinduḥ
tadvadbhaktiprāptau taduktaguṇabhāka svayaṃ bhūyāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मारारिति परेशं संबोधयितुं जनः सुखं मनुते ।
योग्यं तन्नह्यन्यन्मातुः परदैवतं तथा सुखदम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: No wonder people find it pleasing to address God as 'Mother'. They do so, because God does everything that a mother does - protect, provide and love. There is nobody better deserved than God to be called as 'Mother'
quotes.transliteration:
mārāriti pareśaṃ saṃbodhayituṃ janaḥ sukhaṃ manute |
yogyaṃ tannahyanyanmātuḥ paradaivataṃ tathā sukhadam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्त्रियो रत्नान्यथो विद्या धर्मं शौचं सुभाषितम् ।
विविधानि च शिल्पानि समादेयानि सर्वतः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One can collect these items from all places, any time - women (as brides), gems, knowledge, dharma, righteousness, cleanliness, a good saying and a good sculpture.
quotes.transliteration:
striyo ratnānyatho vidyā dharmaṃ śaucaṃ subhāṣitam |
vividhāni ca śilpāni samādeyāni sarvataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यद्युच्चैरपि न परानुपकुरुषे तन्मृषैव ते जन्म ।
यदि नैष जनितवर्षः को नाम पयोधरोत्कर्षः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you do not help people in spite of being highly placed, then your whole life is a grand waste. What is the use of cloud going so high in the sky if it does not rain?
quotes.transliteration:
yadyuccairapi na parānupakuruṣe tanmṛṣaiva te janma |
yadi naiṣa janitavarṣaḥ ko nāma payodharotkarṣaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रशमश्च क्षमा चैव आर्जवं प्रियवादिता ।
असामर्थ्यफला ह्येते निर्गुणेषु सतां गुणाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Austerity, forgiveness, righteousness, pleasant talking are all considered signs of weakness by the wicked. In fact, these are all the excellent qualities of noble men.
quotes.transliteration:
praśamaśca kṣamā caiva ārjavaṃ priyavāditā |
asāmarthyaphalā hyete nirguṇeṣu satāṃ guṇāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मृत्योर्बिभेषि किं बाल न स भीतं विमुंचति ।
अद्यवाब्दशतांते वा मृत्युर्वै प्राणिनां ध्रुवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Oh boy! why are you afraid of death? Death does not spare those who are afraid. It may happen right now or after a hundred years. But it is certain.
quotes.transliteration:
mṛtyorbibheṣi kiṃ bāla na sa bhītaṃ vimuṃcati |
adyavābdaśatāṃte vā mṛtyurvai prāṇināṃ dhruvaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अंजनस्य क्षयं दृष्ट्वा वल्मीकस्य च संचयं ।
अवंध्यं दिवसं कुर्यात् दानाध्ययनकर्मसु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person should learn about impermanence from these two. Collyrium applied to the eyelashes get washed away easily. Ants build the anthill only to let a snake live in it. After having seen this, one should not waste his time every day. He must engage in charity (giving) and introspective study.
quotes.transliteration:
aṃjanasya kṣayaṃ dṛṣṭvā valmīkasya ca saṃcayaṃ |
avaṃdhyaṃ divasaṃ kuryāt dānādhyayanakarmasu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रथमे नार्जिता विद्या द्वितीये नार्जितं धनम् ।
तृतीये नार्जितं पुण्यं चतुर्थे किं करिष्यसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you did not study in your early life, not earn wealth in your youth, not do noble deeds during old age - then how are you going to face death honorably?
quotes.transliteration:
prathame nārjitā vidyā dvitīye nārjitaṃ dhanam |
tṛtīye nārjitaṃ puṇyaṃ caturthe kiṃ kariṣyasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न चागमादृते धर्मस्तर्केण व्यवतिष्ठते ।
ॠषीणामपि यज्ज्ञानं तदप्यागमपूर्वकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Dharma does not stand solely on basis of debate and logic. It has to explained on the basis of what is there in the Vedas. Even the knowledge of all the sages is derived from the Vedas.
quotes.transliteration:
na cāgamādṛte dharmastarkeṇa vyavatiṣṭhate |
ṝṣīṇāmapi yajjñānaṃ tadapyāgamapūrvakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न चातिप्रणयः कार्यः कर्तव्यो प्रणयश्च ते ।
उभयं हि महान् दोषस्तस्मादंतरदृग्भव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not be too close to the king. Nor should one ignore him. Both strategies are flawed and are dangerous. Hence one must be wise and employ a middle path.
quotes.transliteration:
na cātipraṇayaḥ kāryaḥ kartavyo praṇayaśca te |
ubhayaṃ hi mahān doṣastasmādaṃtaradṛgbhava ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यः कस्मादपि पुरुषात्संप्राप्तः सः प्रभोः सदुपदेशः ।
वृष्टिजलं कस्मादपि मार्गादायातु विष्णुपदतस्तत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a good advice comes to you from any one, it should not matter who it came from. You should assume it came from the Lord himself. You may get rain water in different ways, but it all has the same source - from the foot of the Lord Vishnu.
quotes.transliteration:
yaḥ kasmādapi puruṣātsaṃprāptaḥ saḥ prabhoḥ sadupadeśaḥ |
vṛṣṭijalaṃ kasmādapi mārgādāyātu viṣṇupadatastat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यं मातापितरौ क्लेशं सहेते संभवे नृणाम् ।
न तस्य निष्कृतिः शक्या कर्तुं वर्षशतैरपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must never forget the pains that parents take to bring us to this world. One can not repay the debt even by working for a hundred years.
quotes.transliteration:
yaṃ mātāpitarau kleśaṃ sahete saṃbhave nṛṇām |
na tasya niṣkṛtiḥ śakyā kartuṃ varṣaśatairapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
व्यसने क्लिश्यमानं हि यो मित्रं नाभिपद्यते ।
अनुनीय यथाशक्ति तं नृशंसं विदुर्बुधाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a friend is in trouble, it is one's duty to say a few consoling words and help him as much as possible. One who does not do that is considered as cruel by wise men.
quotes.transliteration:
vyasane kliśyamānaṃ hi yo mitraṃ nābhipadyate |
anunīya yathāśakti taṃ nṛśaṃsaṃ vidurbudhāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पुराणमित्येव न साधु सर्वं
न चापि काव्यं नवमित्यवद्यम् ।
संतः परीक्ष्यान्यतरद्भजन्ते
मूढः परप्रत्ययनेयबुद्धिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: purANamiti eva na sAdhu sarvam Nothing is to be considered great just because it is old. Similarly, not all things new are to be considered lightly. Wise men will weigh each work and treat them appropriately. Only fools follow others.
quotes.transliteration:
purāṇamityeva na sādhu sarvaṃ
na cāpi kāvyaṃ navamityavadyam |
saṃtaḥ parīkṣyānyataradbhajante
mūḍhaḥ parapratyayaneyabuddhiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
समाजेभ्यः सुमनसां सुभाषितमयं मधु ।
यावज्जीवं विचिन्वंति साधवो मधुपा इव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man must accumulate good sayings from other wise men as long as he lives. Subhashitas as sweet as honey. And we must be as tireless as bee to collect them.
quotes.transliteration:
samājebhyaḥ sumanasāṃ subhāṣitamayaṃ madhu |
yāvajjīvaṃ vicinvaṃti sādhavo madhupā iva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न व्याधयो नापि यमः प्राप्तुं श्रेयः प्रतीक्षते ।
यावदेव भवेत् कल्पस्तावच्छ्रेयः समाचरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Illness or Death do not wait for a man to accumulate good Karma by doing good things over time. So, do not wait longer. Keep on doing good as and when such opportunity arises.
quotes.transliteration:
na vyādhayo nāpi yamaḥ prāptuṃ śreyaḥ pratīkṣate |
yāvadeva bhavet kalpastāvacchreyaḥ samācaret ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्थित्यतिक्रांतिभीरूणि स्वच्छान्याकुलितान्यपि ।
तोयानि तोयराशीनां मनांसि च मनस्विनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Minds of great people are like water in the sea. Even under tumultuous conditions it does not breach its limits and does not get muddy when shaken.
quotes.transliteration:
sthityatikrāṃtibhīrūṇi svacchānyākulitānyapi |
toyāni toyarāśīnāṃ manāṃsi ca manasvinām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वंशभवो गुणवानपि
संगविशेषेण पूज्यते पुरुषः ।
न हि तुंबीफलविकलो
वीणादंडः प्रयाति महिमानम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: vamshabhavo guNavAn api na hi tumbIphalavikalo It is not enough to be born in a good family or have unique qualities. One gets respected only when he is in company of noble people. The stick (made of bamboo) of the veena does not get any respect even with the strings(unique qualities) if it is not attached to the acoustic drum.
quotes.transliteration:
vaṃśabhavo guṇavānapi
saṃgaviśeṣeṇa pūjyate puruṣaḥ |
na hi tuṃbīphalavikalo
vīṇādaṃḍaḥ prayāti mahimānam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नोदन्वानर्थितामेति सदांभोभिः प्रपूर्यते ।
आत्मा तु पात्रानां नेयः पात्रमायांति संपदः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The mighty ocean never asks for anything. Still, it is always filled with water. One must make himself worthy first. Wealth will automatically follow.
quotes.transliteration:
nodanvānarthitāmeti sadāṃbhobhiḥ prapūryate |
ātmā tu pātrānāṃ neyaḥ pātramāyāṃti saṃpadaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संग्रहैकपरः प्रायः समुद्रोऽपि रसातले ।
दातारं जलदं पश्य गर्जंतं भुवनोपरि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One who collects will always be at the bottom - like the ocean the collector of gems and precious stones is condemned to the rasatala world. On the other hand the one who gives it to others will always be on top - like the clouds that roar on top of the world.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃgrahaikaparaḥ prāyaḥ samudroऽpi rasātale |
dātāraṃ jaladaṃ paśya garjaṃtaṃ bhuvanopari ||

quotes.sanskritText:
शनैः पंथाः शनैः कंथा शनैः पर्वतमस्तके ।
शनैर्विद्या शनैर्वित्तं पंचैतानि शनैः शनैः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must walk along a long path slowly. A fine cloth must be weaved very slowly. One must slowly climb the mountain. Education must happen slowly. Wealth is also accumulated slowly. These are the five things that have to be done very slowly.
quotes.transliteration:
śanaiḥ paṃthāḥ śanaiḥ kaṃthā śanaiḥ parvatamastake |
śanairvidyā śanairvittaṃ paṃcaitāni śanaiḥ śanaiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
गर्जति शरदि न वर्षति
वर्षति वर्षासु निःस्वनो मेघः ।
नीचो वदति न कुरुते
न वदति सुजनः करोत्येव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: garjati sharadi na varShati nIcho vadati na kurute The clouds of Sharath season only roar - they do not shower. On the other hand, those in the Varsha season only shower and not roar. A lowly man will only talk and never act. A virtuous person will act and not bother to talk.
quotes.transliteration:
garjati śaradi na varṣati
varṣati varṣāsu niḥsvano meghaḥ |
nīco vadati na kurute
na vadati sujanaḥ karotyeva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
बंधुः को नाम दुष्टानां कुप्येत्को नातियाचितः ।
को न तृप्यति वित्तेन कुकृते को न पंडितः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Does a wicked man have any friends? Who will not get angry at a persistent beggar? Who can not be satiated by money? Who is not an expert at sin?
quotes.transliteration:
baṃdhuḥ ko nāma duṣṭānāṃ kupyetko nātiyācitaḥ |
ko na tṛpyati vittena kukṛte ko na paṃḍitaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भक्ता भक्त समाजे गुणानुवादैस्सदा रमन्तेऽत्र ।
धूमपलोको य्द्वन्नैकाकी*किन्तु समविषयेषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Devotees will always flock together with other devotees and sing/talk about God's qualities. This increases their devotion also. It is not as natural as people addicted to smoking flock together and enjoy each others company. Their addiction also grows in like minded company.
quotes.transliteration:
bhaktā bhakta samāje guṇānuvādaissadā ramanteऽtra |
dhūmapaloko ydvannaikākī*kintu samaviṣayeṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
काक आह्वयते काकान् याचको न तु याचकान् ।
काकयाचकयोर्मध्ये वरं काको न याचकः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When a crow finds food, it calls out for other crows. A beggar does not do the same. He is greedy and wants it all for himself. So, if you compare a crow and a beggar, a crow is better.
quotes.transliteration:
kāka āhvayate kākān yācako na tu yācakān |
kākayācakayormadhye varaṃ kāko na yācakaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्रीडन्तीं भगिनीं स्वां सिंहमुखं धारयन्भीमः
आक्रन्दयति पुनस्तां कुरुते विभयां स्वरूपदर्शनतः ।
एवं स्यात् परमात्मा मायावृतिधृक् भयं तथा शोकं
जनयत्यावृतिहीनः सदा प्रियात्मा परम सुखदश्च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The big brother goes in front of his little sister wearing a Lion's mask. She will be scared and starts crying. At that moment, he will remove the mask and show his pleasant face which will drive away all fear from the sister as she feels secured. Just like that God creates this illusion and generates a lot of fear and grief in our lives. But when we can see him in real form, there will be no fear and endless happiness.
quotes.transliteration:
krīḍantīṃ bhaginīṃ svāṃ siṃhamukhaṃ dhārayanbhīmaḥ
ākrandayati punastāṃ kurute vibhayāṃ svarūpadarśanataḥ |
evaṃ syāt paramātmā māyāvṛtidhṛk bhayaṃ tathā śokaṃ
janayatyāvṛtihīnaḥ sadā priyātmā parama sukhadaśca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दोषभीतेरनारंभस्तत्कापुरुषलक्षणम् ।
कैरजीर्णभयाद्भृतभोजनं परिहीयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is cowardly not to start an industry out of fear of failing. Does any one stop eating good food being afraid of indigestion?
quotes.transliteration:
doṣabhīteranāraṃbhastatkāpuruṣalakṣaṇam |
kairajīrṇabhayādbhṛtabhojanaṃ parihīyate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तत्त्वमात्मस्थमज्ञात्वा मूढः शास्त्रेषु मुह्यति ।
गोपः कक्षगते छागे कूपे पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The truth is hidden within oneself. Fools don't see it and search for it in the scriptures. Just like the story of a shepherd who has the sheep in his arms and looks for it in the well.
quotes.transliteration:
tattvamātmasthamajñātvā mūḍhaḥ śāstreṣu muhyati |
gopaḥ kakṣagate chāge kūpe paśyati durmatiḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निर्गुणः शोभते नैव विपुलाडंबरोऽपि ना ।
आपातरम्यपुष्टिश्रीशोभितः शाल्मलिर्यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you don't have good qualities, then any amount of decoration will not make you look attractive. It will be like a silk cotton tree that has beautiful flowers and fruits to look but useless.
quotes.transliteration:
nirguṇaḥ śobhate naiva vipulāḍaṃbaroऽpi nā |
āpātaramyapuṣṭiśrīśobhitaḥ śālmaliryathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्वावंभसि निवेष्टव्यौ गले बद्ध्वा दृढां शिलाम् ।
धनवंतमदातारं दरिद्रं चातपस्विनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: These two should be thrown into the sea after tying heavy stones tightly to their necks - a wealthy person who does not do charity, a poor guy who does not do penance.
quotes.transliteration:
dvāvaṃbhasi niveṣṭavyau gale baddhvā dṛḍhāṃ śilām |
dhanavaṃtamadātāraṃ daridraṃ cātapasvinam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धन्यानामुत्तमं दाक्ष्यं धनानामुत्तमं श्रुतम् ।
लाभानाम् श्रेय आरोग्यं सुखानां तुष्टिरुत्तमा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Of all the qualities, efficiency is the best. Of all the wealth, knowledge is the best. Of all the profits, health is the best. Of all the happiness, being satisfied is the best.
quotes.transliteration:
dhanyānāmuttamaṃ dākṣyaṃ dhanānāmuttamaṃ śrutam |
lābhānām śreya ārogyaṃ sukhānāṃ tuṣṭiruttamā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
एतावानेव पुरुषः कृतं यस्मिन् न नश्यति ।
यावच्च कुर्यादन्योऽस्य कुर्याद्बहुगुणं ततः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He is a real man who remembers the help done to him by others. He will make sure that it does not end there. He will always return more help than he received.
quotes.transliteration:
etāvāneva puruṣaḥ kṛtaṃ yasmin na naśyati |
yāvacca kuryādanyoऽsya kuryādbahuguṇaṃ tataḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वायुर्यथा घनानीकं तृणं तूलं रजांसि च ।
संयोज्याक्षिपते भूयस्तथा भूतानि भूतकृत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A strong wind can scatter clouds, grass, cotton and sand together to different directions. The creator can do the same with all the beings (his creations)
quotes.transliteration:
vāyuryathā ghanānīkaṃ tṛṇaṃ tūlaṃ rajāṃsi ca |
saṃyojyākṣipate bhūyastathā bhūtāni bhūtakṛt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनो धावति सर्वत्र मदोन्मत्तगजेंद्रवत् ।
ज्ञानांकुशसमा बुद्धिः तस्य निश्चलते मनः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Mind is like an elephant in heat - it runs in every direction. Intellect is like the hook used by the mahout to control and steady the elephant. We must also use our wisdom to control our minds.
quotes.transliteration:
mano dhāvati sarvatra madonmattagajeṃdravat |
jñānāṃkuśasamā buddhiḥ tasya niścalate manaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यदि सत्संगनिरतो भविष्यसि भविष्यसि ।
तथाऽसज्जनगोष्ठीषु पतिष्यसि पतिष्यसि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If you always stay in the company of virtuous people, you will live and prosper. If you fall into bad company, you will definitely fall.
quotes.transliteration:
yadi satsaṃganirato bhaviṣyasi bhaviṣyasi |
tathāऽsajjanagoṣṭhīṣu patiṣyasi patiṣyasi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
माता गुरुतरा भूमेः खात् पितोच्चतरस्तथा ।
मनः शीघ्रतरं वाताच्चिंता बहुतरी तृणात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Mother's weight (importance) is more than that of the earth. Father's place should be held higher than the sky. Mind moves faster than wind. Worries outnumber the blades of grass.
quotes.transliteration:
mātā gurutarā bhūmeḥ khāt pitoccatarastathā |
manaḥ śīghrataraṃ vātācciṃtā bahutarī tṛṇāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
तुल्यवर्णच्छदः कृष्णः कोकिलैः सह संगतः ।
केन व्याख्यायते काकः स्वयं यदि भाषते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A Crow can mingle with nightingales since they have the same plumage – black. No one will notice the difference until the crow starts to speak by itself. Only a fool would speak and reveal his ignorance in a congregation of wise men.
quotes.transliteration:
tulyavarṇacchadaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ kokilaiḥ saha saṃgataḥ |
kena vyākhyāyate kākaḥ svayaṃ yadi bhāṣate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धर्मो जितो ह्यधर्मेण जितः सत्योऽनृतेन च ।
जिता भृत्यैस्तु राजानः स्त्रीभिश्च पुरुषा जिताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the epoch of kali (the present age), Dharma will be overcome by immorality, Truth will be overshadowed by lies, servants will overthrow the kings and women will be in control of the men.
quotes.transliteration:
dharmo jito hyadharmeṇa jitaḥ satyoऽnṛtena ca |
jitā bhṛtyaistu rājānaḥ strībhiśca puruṣā jitāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
काष्ठादग्निर्जायते मथ्यमानात्
भूमिस्तोयं खन्यमाना ददाति ।
सोत्साहानां नास्त्यसाध्यं नराणां
मार्गारब्धाः सर्वयत्नाः फलंति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Fire can be made from just twigs and nothing else by rubbing together. If one digs the earth with effort, he will get water. If a person has enough enthusiasm, nothing is impossible. When efforts are put in the right direction, then success is guaranteed.
quotes.transliteration:
kāṣṭhādagnirjāyate mathyamānāt
bhūmistoyaṃ khanyamānā dadāti |
sotsāhānāṃ nāstyasādhyaṃ narāṇāṃ
mārgārabdhāḥ sarvayatnāḥ phalaṃti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विवाहकाले रतिसंप्रयोगे
प्राणात्यये सर्वधनापहारे ।
विप्रस्य चार्थे ह्यनृतं वदेत
पंचानृतान्याहुरपातकानि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: vivAhakAle rati samprayoge viprasya cha arthe hi anRutam vadeta There are five instances when uttering a lie does not attract sin - during a wedding (to make it happen), romantic banter, when one's life is threatened, when you have lost all money and to help a (poor) brahmin. [translator's note: http://wordsofwisdom.in/subhashitani/2010/07/28/]
quotes.transliteration:
vivāhakāle ratisaṃprayoge
prāṇātyaye sarvadhanāpahāre |
viprasya cārthe hyanṛtaṃ vadeta
paṃcānṛtānyāhurapātakāni ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न द्विषंति न याचंते परनिंदां न कुर्वते ।
अनाहूता न चायांति तेनाश्मनोऽपि देवताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: They don't hate any one, don't beg, don't berate anyone, don't go to places uninvited. For these reasons, stones can also be treated like Gods.
quotes.transliteration:
na dviṣaṃti na yācaṃte paraniṃdāṃ na kurvate |
anāhūtā na cāyāṃti tenāśmanoऽpi devatāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अनर्घ्यमपि माणिक्यं हेमाश्रयमपेक्षते ।
अनाश्रयान शोभन्ते पण्डिता वनिता लताः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Even a very precious gem needs the support of gold to be made into a wearable ornament. Just like that the following things are weak without proper support - scholars, women and creepers.
quotes.transliteration:
anarghyamapi māṇikyaṃ hemāśrayamapekṣate |
anāśrayāna śobhante paṇḍitā vanitā latāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न जानपदिकं दुःखमेकः शोचितुमर्हति ।
अप्यभावेन युज्येत तच्चास्य निवर्तते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When there is any danger or trouble for the entire country, it is not wise (not possible) to think and find a solution alone. Even if one person gave all he has to the solve it, it will not be enough.
quotes.transliteration:
na jānapadikaṃ duḥkhamekaḥ śocitumarhati |
apyabhāvena yujyeta taccāsya nivartate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यत्र स्त्री यत्र कितवो बालो यत्रानुशासिता ।
मज्जंति तेऽवशा राजन् नद्यामश्मप्लवा इव ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: If a kingdom is administered by a woman, a rumormonger or a kid, then that kingdom will sink like a boat made of stone.
quotes.transliteration:
yatra strī yatra kitavo bālo yatrānuśāsitā |
majjaṃti teऽvaśā rājan nadyāmaśmaplavā iva ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न हायनैर्न पलितैः न वित्तेन न बंधुभिः ।
ॠषयश्चक्रिरे धर्मं योऽनूचानः स नो महान् ॥
त्
quotes.englishTranslation: It is not considered Dharma just because old people, people with white hair, rich people or your relatives said so. The sages have observed what is considered moral from ages and spoken that to be the Dharma. We value that the most.
quotes.transliteration:
na hāyanairna palitaiḥ na vittena na baṃdhubhiḥ |
ṝṣayaścakrire dharmaṃ yoऽnūcānaḥ sa no mahān ||
t

quotes.sanskritText:
वीणेव श्रोत्रहीनस्य लोलाक्षीव विचक्षुषः ।
व्यसोः कुसुममालेव श्रीः कदर्यस्य निष्फला ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Just like melody of veena is lost on someone who can't hear, a beauty of a damsel is lost on a blind person and garlands of flower is lost on a dead body - wealth loses its purpose when it accumulates with a miser.
quotes.transliteration:
vīṇeva śrotrahīnasya lolākṣīva vicakṣuṣaḥ |
vyasoḥ kusumamāleva śrīḥ kadaryasya niṣphalā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
किं तेन जातु जातेन मातुर्यौवनहारिणा ।
आरोहति न यः स्वस्य वंशस्याग्रे ध्वजो यथा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is about people who can not do enough good deeds to cause the uplift of the entire family – just like the flag on the mast. What is the use of such a person being born? They take birth only to spoil the youth of their mother.
quotes.transliteration:
kiṃ tena jātu jātena māturyauvanahāriṇā |
ārohati na yaḥ svasya vaṃśasyāgre dhvajo yathā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कन्या वरयते रूपं माता वित्तं पिता श्रुतम् ।
बांधवाः कुलमिच्छंति मिष्टान्नमितरे जनाः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A girl looks for beauty in her match. Mother wants to make sure that he is wealthy. Father looks for education. Relatives will look for good family so that they can make new acquaintances. Others look for good meal with lots of sweets.
quotes.transliteration:
kanyā varayate rūpaṃ mātā vittaṃ pitā śrutam |
bāṃdhavāḥ kulamicchaṃti miṣṭānnamitare janāḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सेवितव्यो महावृक्षः फलछायासमन्वितः ।
यदि दैवात्फलं नास्ति छाया केन निवार्यते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must always take shelter of a big tree with lots of fruits and shade. Just in case due to bad luck, there are no fruits - still you will have the shade. Who can take that way from you?
quotes.transliteration:
sevitavyo mahāvṛkṣaḥ phalachāyāsamanvitaḥ |
yadi daivātphalaṃ nāsti chāyā kena nivāryate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
अफलानि दुरंतानि समव्ययफलानि च ।
अशक्यानि च वस्तूनि नारभेत विचक्षणः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise men will not take up a task that will definitely fail, end in destruction, yields results that barely break-even or already known to be impossible.
quotes.transliteration:
aphalāni duraṃtāni samavyayaphalāni ca |
aśakyāni ca vastūni nārabheta vicakṣaṇaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
नाप्राप्यमभिवांछंति नष्टं नेच्छंति शोचितुम् ।
आपत्स्वपि न मुह्यन्ति नराः पंडितबुद्धयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wise and learned men will never desire for things that are not to be had. They will not grieve over loss. They will not turn their backs on troubles - they face it head on.
quotes.transliteration:
nāprāpyamabhivāṃchaṃti naṣṭaṃ necchaṃti śocitum |
āpatsvapi na muhyanti narāḥ paṃḍitabuddhayaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षिपेद्वाक्यशरान्घोरान्न पारुष्यविषप्लुतान् ।
वाक्पारुष्यरुषा चक्रे भीमः कुरुकुलक्षयम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must not throw arrows dipped in the venom of harsh speech at any one. It can only lead to destruction of self. It is the harsh words of Duryodhana that angered Bheema to eventually destroy all the Kauravas.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣipedvākyaśarānghorānna pāruṣyaviṣaplutān |
vākpāruṣyaruṣā cakre bhīmaḥ kurukulakṣayam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षमया रोचते लक्ष्मीर्ब्राह्मी सौरी यथा प्रभा ।
क्षमिणामाशु भगवांस्तुष्यते हरिरीश्वरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: He who forgives, will glow with brilliance similar to that of the pleasant morning Sun. Even the all powerful God will be pleased very soon with people who forgive.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣamayā rocate lakṣmīrbrāhmī saurī yathā prabhā |
kṣamiṇāmāśu bhagavāṃstuṣyate harirīśvaraḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दौर्मंत्र्यान्नृपतिर्विनश्यति यतिः संगात् सुतो लालनात्
विप्रोऽनध्ययनात् कुलं कुतनयात् शीलं खलोपासनात् ।
ह्रीर्मद्यादनवेक्षणादपि कृषिः स्नेहः प्रवासाश्रयात्
मैत्री चाप्रणयात् समृद्धिरनयात् त्यागात् प्रमादाद्धनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A king gets destroyed by his unfair policies. A sage - by contact (attachment). A kid - by pampering. A brahmin - by not studying continuously. A family - by a bad son. Character - by befriending rogues. Mind - by liquor. Agriculture - by not monitoring it. Friendship - by traveling together to a far off place. Good friendship - by falling in love. Prosperity - by going against Dharma. Wealth - by spending recklessly. These are the ways the above objects get destroyed.
quotes.transliteration:
daurmaṃtryānnṛpatirvinaśyati yatiḥ saṃgāt suto lālanāt
viproऽnadhyayanāt kulaṃ kutanayāt śīlaṃ khalopāsanāt |
hrīrmadyādanavekṣaṇādapi kṛṣiḥ snehaḥ pravāsāśrayāt
maitrī cāpraṇayāt samṛddhiranayāt tyāgāt pramādāddhanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
मनस्यन्यद् वचस्यन्यत्कार्यमन्यद् दुरात्मनाम् ।
मनस्येकं वचस्येकं कर्मण्येकं महात्मनाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Wicked people have one thing on their mind, another in the speech and a different thing in their actions. Virtuous people have the same thing in their mind, in their words and in their actions.
quotes.transliteration:
manasyanyad vacasyanyatkāryamanyad durātmanām |
manasyekaṃ vacasyekaṃ karmaṇyekaṃ mahātmanām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उष्ट्राणां च विवाहेषु गीतं गायन्ति गर्दभाः ।
परस्परं प्रशम्सन्ति अहो रूपमहो ध्वनिः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In the wedding of camels, the donkeys are singing. They are praising each other. Oh! What a beauty! What a voice!
quotes.transliteration:
uṣṭrāṇāṃ ca vivāheṣu gītaṃ gāyanti gardabhāḥ |
parasparaṃ praśamsanti aho rūpamaho dhvaniḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
उदकुम्भे ह्युदकुम्भं ग्राम्यावहन्ति निजशिरसि
विवदन्तेऽप्यन्योन्यं तथाऽपि नाघोजलं पतति ।
सन्मार्गविप्रवसितः स्थितौहि कस्यामपि वसन्नेवं
सन्मार्गादच्युतिकृत् कृत्यं संलक्ष्यचेह वर्तेत ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: udakumbhe hi udakumbham grAmyaa vahanti nija shirasi In the village ladies can carry pots of water stacked on top of one another with ease. While they carry, they can talk to each other and it seems as though the pots are not bothering them. Not a drop of water spills over too. We must go about out worldly existence like that. One must not stray from the path of morality even when carrying a lot of load and balancing responsibilities.
quotes.transliteration:
udakumbhe hyudakumbhaṃ grāmyāvahanti nijaśirasi
vivadanteऽpyanyonyaṃ tathāऽpi nāghojalaṃ patati |
sanmārgavipravasitaḥ sthitauhi kasyāmapi vasannevaṃ
sanmārgādacyutikṛt kṛtyaṃ saṃlakṣyaceha varteta ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कल्याणी बत गाथेयं लौकिकी प्रतिभाति मे ।
एति जीवन्तमानंदो नरं वर्षाशतादपि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is perhaps true what people say about patience. Good times will come to people if they stay alive and wait patiently.
quotes.transliteration:
kalyāṇī bata gātheyaṃ laukikī pratibhāti me |
eti jīvantamānaṃdo naraṃ varṣāśatādapi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यमाजीवंति पुरुषं सर्वभूतानु संजय ।
पक्वं द्रुममिवासाद्य तस्य जीवितमर्थवत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sanjaya, Only his life is worth living who helps all other beings just like a tree with lot of fruits. (as told by Vidula to her son Sanjaya)
quotes.transliteration:
yamājīvaṃti puruṣaṃ sarvabhūtānu saṃjaya |
pakvaṃ drumamivāsādya tasya jīvitamarthavat ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आहवे व्यसने चैव दुर्भिक्षे शत्रुविग्रहे ।
राजद्वारे श्मशाने च यस्तिष्ठति स बांधवः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A real friend is one who stands by you all the time - in a battle field, in troubled times, in poverty, when captured by enemies, at the door of a king (being summoned), in the cemetery.
quotes.transliteration:
āhave vyasane caiva durbhikṣe śatruvigrahe |
rājadvāre śmaśāne ca yastiṣṭhati sa bāṃdhavaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दारेषु किंचित्स्वजनेषु किंचि-
द्गोप्यं वयस्येषु सुतेषु किंचित् ।
युक्तं न वा युक्तमिदं विचिंत्य
वदेद्विपश्चिन्महतोऽनुरोधात् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: yuktaM na vA yuktam idam vicintya One must hide a little secrets from wife, relatives, friends or children. Not everyone need to be told everything. A wise man must think 'to tell or not to tell' a lot according to the task at hand.
quotes.transliteration:
dāreṣu kiṃcitsvajaneṣu kiṃci-
dgopyaṃ vayasyeṣu suteṣu kiṃcit |
yuktaṃ na vā yuktamidaṃ viciṃtya
vadedvipaścinmahatoऽnurodhāt ||

quotes.sanskritText:
न धर्मशास्त्रं पठतीति कारणं
न चापि वेदाध्ययनं दुरात्मनः ।
स्वभाव एवात्र तथातिरिच्यते
यथा प्रकृत्या मधुरं गवां पयः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: svabhAva eva atra tathA atirichyate A rogue is rogue for ever. It doe not matter if he is studying the moral code of life, or the vedas. He will stay true to his innate nature. Just like it is natural for cow's milk to be sweet, it is natural for them to wicked.
quotes.transliteration:
na dharmaśāstraṃ paṭhatīti kāraṇaṃ
na cāpi vedādhyayanaṃ durātmanaḥ |
svabhāva evātra tathātiricyate
yathā prakṛtyā madhuraṃ gavāṃ payaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सद्भावेन जयेन्मित्रं सद्भावेन च बांधवान् ।
स्त्रीभृत्यान् दानमानाभ्यां दाक्षिण्येनेतरं जनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Friends and relatives should be won over by righteous conduct. Women and servants by gifts and respect. Propriety should used to win over the rest of the people.
quotes.transliteration:
sadbhāvena jayenmitraṃ sadbhāvena ca bāṃdhavān |
strībhṛtyān dānamānābhyāṃ dākṣiṇyenetaraṃ janam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
विदैवज्ञं ग्रामं विबुधविधुरां भूपतिसभां
मुखं श्रुत्या जीनं मनुजपतिशून्यं च विषयम् ।
अनाचारन् दारानपहरिकथं काव्यमपि च
प्रवक्तृत्वापेतं गुरुमपि सुबुद्धिः परिहरेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A wise man will avoid these: a village without a person who understands God (to approach when troubled), a kings court that does not have wise advisors, face of someone who has not studied scriptures, a kingdom that does not have any king, a wife who does not follow Dharma and a poem that is not a story of God (in someway or other) and a teacher who does not teach well.
quotes.transliteration:
vidaivajñaṃ grāmaṃ vibudhavidhurāṃ bhūpatisabhāṃ
mukhaṃ śrutyā jīnaṃ manujapatiśūnyaṃ ca viṣayam |
anācāran dārānapaharikathaṃ kāvyamapi ca
pravaktṛtvāpetaṃ gurumapi subuddhiḥ pariharet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
षडेते ह्यवमन्यंते नित्यं पूर्वोपकारिणं
आचार्यं शिक्षिताः शिष्याः कृतदाराश्च मातरम् ।
नारीं विगतकामास्तु कृतार्थश्च प्रयोजकम्
नावं निस्तीर्णकांतारा आतुराश्च चिकित्सकम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following six people are known to forget those who helped them before - students forgetting their teachers, daughters forgetting about mothers after marrying, a mendicant who has no interest in sensual pleasures forgetting the ladies who gave him pleasures, any beneficiary of help forgetting the benefactor, people forgetting the boatman after crossing the river and patients forgetting about the doctor after being treated.
quotes.transliteration:
ṣaḍete hyavamanyaṃte nityaṃ pūrvopakāriṇaṃ
ācāryaṃ śikṣitāḥ śiṣyāḥ kṛtadārāśca mātaram |
nārīṃ vigatakāmāstu kṛtārthaśca prayojakam
nāvaṃ nistīrṇakāṃtārā āturāśca cikitsakam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पश्य कर्मवशात् प्राप्तं भोज्यकालेऽपि भोजनम् ।
हस्तोद्यमं विना वक्त्रे प्रविशेन्न कथंचन ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Without action, there will be nothing in this world. Even when you sit to have food, the food will not enter your mouth without the work of the hands.
quotes.transliteration:
paśya karmavaśāt prāptaṃ bhojyakāleऽpi bhojanam |
hastodyamaṃ vinā vaktre praviśenna kathaṃcana ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स जीवति गुणा यस्य धर्मो यस्य स जीवति ।
गुणधर्मविहीनो यो निष्फलं तस्य जीवितम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One must have righteous qualities and follow dharma. Only then he can be said to have lived. If one is devoid of both righteousness and dharma, his life is wasted.
quotes.transliteration:
sa jīvati guṇā yasya dharmo yasya sa jīvati |
guṇadharmavihīno yo niṣphalaṃ tasya jīvitam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कपिरपि च कापिशायनमदमत्तो वृश्चिकेन संदष्टः ।
अपि च पिशाचग्रस्तः किं ब्रूवो वैकृतं तस्य ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: It is first of all a monkey. On top it, it is drunk. A scorpion has stung it. To add to the chaos, it is possessed. One can only imagine what can happen. It is the state of the human world today - men, intoxicated by wealth, stung by ignorance and possessed by greed.
quotes.transliteration:
kapirapi ca kāpiśāyanamadamatto vṛścikena saṃdaṣṭaḥ |
api ca piśācagrastaḥ kiṃ brūvo vaikṛtaṃ tasya ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पात्रापात्रविवेकोऽस्ति धेनुपन्नगयोरिव ।
तृणात्संजायते क्षीरं क्षीरात्संजायते विषम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is definitely visible difference between good and the bad - just like the difference between a cow and a snake. One eats grass and gives milk. The other drinks the same milk and gives out venom.
quotes.transliteration:
pātrāpātravivekoऽsti dhenupannagayoriva |
tṛṇātsaṃjāyate kṣīraṃ kṣīrātsaṃjāyate viṣam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
खगा वीतफलं वृक्षं भुक्त्वा चातिथयो गृहं ।
दग्धं मृगस्तथारण्यं जारो भुक्त्वा रतां स्त्रियम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: No one sticks around once a thing is no longer useful. Birds abandon a tree that has emptied the fruits. Guests leave the hosts when he has nothing to offer. A herd of deer will just move on and abandon a forest that was burnt down. A philander leaves a woman after satiating his urges.
quotes.transliteration:
khagā vītaphalaṃ vṛkṣaṃ bhuktvā cātithayo gṛhaṃ |
dagdhaṃ mṛgastathāraṇyaṃ jāro bhuktvā ratāṃ striyam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
संभोजनं संकथनं संप्रश्नोऽथ समागमाः ।
ज्ञातिभिः सह कार्याणि न विरोधः कदाचन ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: One should always welcome interactions with wise men - having food together, listening to them, asking thought provoking questions, visiting their home. One should never harbor any enmity towards them.
quotes.transliteration:
saṃbhojanaṃ saṃkathanaṃ saṃpraśnoऽtha samāgamāḥ |
jñātibhiḥ saha kāryāṇi na virodhaḥ kadācana ||

quotes.sanskritText:
सर्वेषामपि जन्तूनामिह स्वात्मैव वल्लभः ।
इतरेऽपत्यवित्ताद्यास्तद्वल्लभतयैव हि ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: All the beings of this world love themselves the most. All other attachments - children, wealth and others have their root in loving themselves.
quotes.transliteration:
sarveṣāmapi jantūnāmiha svātmaiva vallabhaḥ |
itareऽpatyavittādyāstadvallabhatayaiva hi ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्रियुप्राया वृत्तिः विनयमधुरो वाचि नियमः
प्रकृत्या कल्याणी मतिरनवगीतः परिचयः ।
पुरो वा पश्चाद्वा तदिदमविपर्यासितरसं
रहस्यं साधूनामनुपधि विशुद्धं विजयते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Virtuous people alway behave pleasantly. Their words are always humble and sweet. By nature they do good. Once they become friends, the friendship never wanes. Their behaviour today is as it was before (under changing circumstances). Righteousness is the secret of their good life.
quotes.transliteration:
priyuprāyā vṛttiḥ vinayamadhuro vāci niyamaḥ
prakṛtyā kalyāṇī matiranavagītaḥ paricayaḥ |
puro vā paścādvā tadidamaviparyāsitarasaṃ
rahasyaṃ sādhūnāmanupadhi viśuddhaṃ vijayate ||

quotes.sanskritText:
यथा चतुर्भिः कनकं परीक्ष्यते
निघर्षणच्छेदन तापताडनैः ।
तथा चतुर्भिः पुरुषः परीक्ष्यते
श्रुतेन शीलेन कुलेन कर्मणा ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The quality of gold is determined by four types of test - scratching against touch stone, cutting, heating and beating. The quality of man should also be tested in four dimensions - education, character, family (bring up) and his actions.
quotes.transliteration:
yathā caturbhiḥ kanakaṃ parīkṣyate
nigharṣaṇacchedana tāpatāḍanaiḥ |
tathā caturbhiḥ puruṣaḥ parīkṣyate
śrutena śīlena kulena karmaṇā ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कोऽन्धो योऽकार्यरतः
को बधिरो यो हितानि न शृणोति ।
को मूको यः काले
प्रियाणि वक्तुं न जानाति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: ko andho yo akArya rataH Who is blind? He who is involved in bad actions. Who is deaf? He who does not listen to good advice. Who is dumb? He who cannot speak pleasant words at appropriate time.
quotes.transliteration:
koऽndho yoऽkāryarataḥ
ko badhiro yo hitāni na śṛṇoti |
ko mūko yaḥ kāle
priyāṇi vaktuṃ na jānāti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
धनमेव धरणिवलये कलयति सफलायुषं पुरुषम् ।
यदि जीवति धनरहितः कथय मृतः कीदृशो भवति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: In some way, wealth is what makes ones life on this earth worthwhile. If you say a person without wealth also lives, then I beg to see how dead people are like. Aren't people without money as good as dead here?
quotes.transliteration:
dhanameva dharaṇivalaye kalayati saphalāyuṣaṃ puruṣam |
yadi jīvati dhanarahitaḥ kathaya mṛtaḥ kīdṛśo bhavati ||

quotes.sanskritText:
निजदोषावृतमनसामतिसुंदरमेव भाति विपरीतम् ।
पश्यति पित्तोपहतः शशिशुभ्रं शंखमपि पीतम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Those who are imperfect themselves, will always see fault in others. Even if a caunch shell is as white as the moon, a person with jaundice will see it yellow (pale).
quotes.transliteration:
nijadoṣāvṛtamanasāmatisuṃdarameva bhāti viparītam |
paśyati pittopahataḥ śaśiśubhraṃ śaṃkhamapi pītam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
द्यूतं पुस्तकवाद्ये च नाटकेषु च सक्तता ।
स्त्रीयस्तंद्री च निद्रा च विद्याविघ्नकराणि षट् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: The following six are known to be greatest obstacles to learning – gambling, addicted to reading books (novels) or listening to music, watching plays, women, laziness and sleep.
quotes.transliteration:
dyūtaṃ pustakavādye ca nāṭakeṣu ca saktatā |
strīyastaṃdrī ca nidrā ca vidyāvighnakarāṇi ṣaṭ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
आदाय वारि परितः सरितां मुखेभ्यः
किं नाम साधितमनेन महार्णवेन ।
क्षारीकृतं च वडवा दहने हुतं च
पाताळमूलकुहरे विनिवेशितं च ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: What did the ocean do with all fresh water that it collected from the mouths of all rivers? It made it salty, fed it to the underground fire and distributed among the underworld dwellers. Even if you give something magnificent to wicked people, they will misuse it.
quotes.transliteration:
ādāya vāri paritaḥ saritāṃ mukhebhyaḥ
kiṃ nāma sādhitamanena mahārṇavena |
kṣārīkṛtaṃ ca vaḍavā dahane hutaṃ ca
pātāळmūlakuhare viniveśitaṃ ca ||

quotes.sanskritText:
क्षांतितुल्यं तपो नास्ति संतोषान्न सुखं परम् ।
नास्ति तृष्णासमो व्याधिर्न च धर्मो दयापरः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: There is no greater penance than to forgive; no greater happiness than having food to eat; no greater disease than desire and no greater dharma (moral code for life) than being compassionate.
quotes.transliteration:
kṣāṃtitulyaṃ tapo nāsti saṃtoṣānna sukhaṃ param |
nāsti tṛṣṇāsamo vyādhirna ca dharmo dayāparaḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
प्राणस्यान्नमिदं सर्वं प्रजापतिरकल्पयत् ।
स्थावरं जंगमं चैव सर्वं प्राणस्य भोजनम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: God created objects in this world for life to sustain by itself. He created moving objects, static objects all to satisfy the demands of 'life'.
quotes.transliteration:
prāṇasyānnamidaṃ sarvaṃ prajāpatirakalpayat |
sthāvaraṃ jaṃgamaṃ caiva sarvaṃ prāṇasya bhojanam ||

quotes.sanskritText:
कुत्र विधेयो यत्नः
विद्याभ्यासे सदौषधे दाने ।
अवधीरणा क्व कार्या
खलपरयोषित् परधनेषु ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: kutra vidheyo yatnaH Where must we be spending time and effort? Education, a good medicine and charity. What should we avoid? Rogues, other's wives and other people's money.
quotes.transliteration:
kutra vidheyo yatnaḥ
vidyābhyāse sadauṣadhe dāne |
avadhīraṇā kva kāryā
khalaparayoṣit paradhaneṣu ||

quotes.sanskritText:
वनेऽपि सिंहा मृगमांसभक्ष्या
बुभुक्षिता नैव तृणं चरंति ।
एवं कुलीना व्यसनाभिभूता
न नीतिमार्गं परिलंघयंति ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A lion that is used to eating meat after hunting deer and other creatures will not eat grass even when it is hungry. Just like that, even when troubled by miseries, a person of noble birth (and intentions) will never stray from the path of morality.
quotes.transliteration:
vaneऽpi siṃhā mṛgamāṃsabhakṣyā
bubhukṣitā naiva tṛṇaṃ caraṃti |
evaṃ kulīnā vyasanābhibhūtā
na nītimārgaṃ parilaṃghayaṃti ||

quotes.sanskritText:
दुःखोपघाते शारीरे मानसे चाप्युपस्थिते ।
यस्मिन्न शक्यते कर्तुं यत्नस्तं नानुचिंतयेत् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: When there is some grief to the mind or any wound to the body that we cannot do anything about – one must just stop thinking about it. That is best way to deal with the unsolvable problem.
quotes.transliteration:
duḥkhopaghāte śārīre mānase cāpyupasthite |
yasminna śakyate kartuṃ yatnastaṃ nānuciṃtayet ||

quotes.sanskritText:
ईशोऽस्मत्पोषणकृत् स्वीकुर्मो नहितावताऽस्य सदयत्वं
सर्वस्यापि पितुस्तत्कर्मैवावश्यकं विदुः सर्वे ।
सहिचेदस्मान्भक्तान् विषयविषाध्वनः परावृत्य
सन्मार्गे निजसुखदो नस्यादन्यस्ततः सदय इह ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: God provides for us all. This is accepted. But that does not make him kind and compassionate. Everybody knows that every father does the same for his children - provide and protect. However, God also saves us devotees from the path of material pleasures and puts us on the good path of Dharma. This is what makes him truly kind and compassionate.
quotes.transliteration:
īśoऽsmatpoṣaṇakṛt svīkurmo nahitāvatāऽsya sadayatvaṃ
sarvasyāpi pitustatkarmaivāvaśyakaṃ viduḥ sarve |
sahicedasmānbhaktān viṣayaviṣādhvanaḥ parāvṛtya
sanmārge nijasukhado nasyādanyastataḥ sadaya iha ||

quotes.sanskritText:
भूमिं छादयितुं सर्वां कुतश्चर्म भविश्यति ।
उपानच्चर्ममात्रेण छन्ना भवति मेदिनी ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Where can one find so much leather to cover the entire earth (to walk on)? Instead if you wear a leather foot wear and walk around - you will feel as though the whole earth is covered with leather.
quotes.transliteration:
bhūmiṃ chādayituṃ sarvāṃ kutaścarma bhaviśyati |
upānaccarmamātreṇa channā bhavati medinī ||

quotes.sanskritText:
स्वल्पापि साधुसंपद् भोज्य
महतां न पृथ्व्यपि खलश्रीः ।
सारसमेव पयस्तृषमपनयति
न यदसां पत्युः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: svalpa api sAdhu sampad bhojya Even if the virtuous people have very less wealth, they deserve much more. A wicked person on the other hand may own the entire earth, but they don't deserve it. Just like water from a small pond can quench thirst but the whole ocean cannot.
quotes.transliteration:
svalpāpi sādhusaṃpad bhojya
mahatāṃ na pṛthvyapi khalaśrīḥ |
sārasameva payastṛṣamapanayati
na yadasāṃ patyuḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
पद्यं यद्यपि विद्यते बहु सतां हृद्यं विगद्यं न तत्
गद्यं च प्रतिपद्यते न विजहत्पद्यं बुधास्वाद्यताम् ।
आदत्ते हि तयोः प्रयोग उभयोरामोदभूमोदयं
संगः कस्य हि न स्वदेत मनसे माध्वीकमृद्वीकयोः ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: padyam yadyapi vodyate bahu satAm hRudyam vigadyam na tat Similarly scholars do not like a lot of prose without any poems in between. It is more pleasurable only when both of them are used together. Who will not like a dish that has mixed both honey and sweet grapes?
quotes.transliteration:
padyaṃ yadyapi vidyate bahu satāṃ hṛdyaṃ vigadyaṃ na tat
gadyaṃ ca pratipadyate na vijahatpadyaṃ budhāsvādyatām |
ādatte hi tayoḥ prayoga ubhayorāmodabhūmodayaṃ
saṃgaḥ kasya hi na svadeta manase mādhvīkamṛdvīkayoḥ ||

quotes.sanskritText:
हालाहलं खलु पिपासति कौतुकेन
कालानलं परिचुचुंबिषति प्रकामम् ।
व्यालाधिपं च यतते परितब्धुमद्धा
यो दुर्जनं वशयितुं तनुते मनीषाम् ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: A person who wants to get a rogue under his control (or befriend him) is taking a huge risk. Just like, curiosity to drink poison, playing with fire, hugging the king of snakes (takShaka).
quotes.transliteration:
hālāhalaṃ khalu pipāsati kautukena
kālānalaṃ paricucuṃbiṣati prakāmam |
vyālādhipaṃ ca yatate paritabdhumaddhā
yo durjanaṃ vaśayituṃ tanute manīṣām ||

quotes.sanskritText:
इक्ष्वाकुशर्यातिययातिराममांधातृनाभागनृगांबरीषाः।
एते सकोशाः पुरुषाः सराष्ट्राः नष्टाः शरीरैः क्रतुभिर्धरन्ते ॥
quotes.englishTranslation: Sharyati, Yayati, Rama, Mandhatru, Nabhaga, Nruga and Ambaresha - these are the names of great kings who ruled great kingdoms with great wealth. All of them got destroyed along with their bodies, wealth and kingdoms. They are alive today only because of their actions and fame.
quotes.transliteration:
ikṣvākuśaryātiyayātirāmamāṃdhātṛnābhāganṛgāṃbarīṣāḥ|
ete sakośāḥ puruṣāḥ sarāṣṭrāḥ naṣṭāḥ śarīraiḥ kratubhirdharante ||
